text.NRSV: An account of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: The Lineage Roll of Jesus Christ,—Son of David, Son of Abraham.
text.KJV: The booke of the generation of Iesus Christ, the sonne of Dauid, the sonne of Abraham.
text.NABRE: I. The Infancy Narrative Chapter 1 The Genealogy of Jesus. The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.
text.DRB: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.

text.NRSV: Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
text.Rotherham: Abraham begat Isaac, and Isaac begat Jacob, and Jacob, begat Judah and his brethren;
text.KJV: Abraham begate Isaac, and Isaac begate Iacob, and Iacob begate Iudas and his brethren.
text.NABRE: Abraham became the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.
text.DRB: Abraham begat Isaac, And Isaac begat Jacob. And Jacob begat Judas and his brethren:

text.NRSV: and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Aram,
text.Rotherham: And Judah begat Perez and Zarah of Tamah, and Perez begat Hezron, and Hezron begat Ram;
text.KJV: And Iudas begate Phares and Zara of Thamar, and Phares begate Esrom, and Esrom begate Aram.
text.NABRE: Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez became the father of Hezron, Hezron the father of Ram,
text.DRB: And Judas begat Phares and Zaram of Thamar. And Phares begat Esron. And Esron begat Aram.

text.NRSV: and Aram the father of Aminadab, and Aminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
text.Rotherham: And Ram begat Amminadab, and Amminadab begat Nashon, and Nashon begat Salmon;
text.KJV: And Aram begate Aminadab, and Aminadab begate Naasson, and Naasson begate Salmon.
text.NABRE: Ram the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon, Nahshon the father of Salmon,
text.DRB: And Aram begat Aminadab. And Aminadab begat Naasson. And Naasson begat Salmon.

text.NRSV: and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
text.Rotherham: And Salmon begat Boaz of Rahab, and Boaz begat Obed of Ruth, and Obed begat Jesse;
text.KJV: And Salmon begat Boos of Rachab, and Boos begate Obed of Ruth, and Obed begate Iesse.
text.NABRE: Salmon the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz became the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed became the father of Jesse,
text.DRB: And Salmon begat Booz of Rahab. And Booz begat Obed of Ruth. And Obed begat Jesse.

text.NRSV: and Jesse the father of King David. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
text.Rotherham: And Jesse begat David the King. And David begat Solomon of her [that had been the wife] of Uriah;
text.KJV: And Iesse begate Dauid the King, & Dauid the King begat Solomon of her that had bin the wife of Urias.
text.NABRE: Jesse the father of David the king. David became the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
text.DRB: And Jesse begat David the king. And David the king begat Salomon of her that was the wife of Urias.

text.NRSV: and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
text.Rotherham: And Solomon begat Rehoboam, and Rehoboam begat Abijah, and Abijah begat Asa;
text.KJV: And Solomon begat Roboam, and Roboam begate Abia, and Abia begate Asa.
text.NABRE: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, Abijah the father of Asaph.
text.DRB: And Salomon begat Roboam. And Roboam begat Abia. And Abia begat Asa.

text.NRSV: and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
text.Rotherham: And Asa begat Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat begat Joram, and Joram begat Uzziah;
text.KJV: And Asa begate Iosaphat, and Iosaphat begate Ioram, and Ioram begate Ozias.
text.NABRE: Asaph became the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, Joram the father of Uzziah.
text.DRB: And Asa begat Josaphat. And Josaphat begat Joram. And Joram begat Ozias.

text.NRSV: and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
text.Rotherham: And Uzziah begat Jotham, and Jotham begat Ahaz, and Ahaz begat Hezekiah:
text.KJV: And Ozias begat Ioatham, and Ioatham begate Achas, and Achas begate Ezekias.
text.NABRE: Uzziah became the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, Ahaz the father of Hezekiah.
text.DRB: And Ozias begat Joatham. And Joatham begat Achaz. And Achaz begat Ezechias.

text.NRSV: and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos, and Amos the father of Josiah,
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah begat Manasseh, and Manasseh begat Amon, and Amon begat Josiah;
text.KJV: And Ezekias begate Manasses, and Manasses begate Amon, and Amon begate Iosias.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amos, Amos the father of Josiah.
text.DRB: And Ezechias begat Manasses. And Manasses begat Amon. And Amon begat Josias.

text.NRSV: and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: And Josiah begat Jechoniah, and his brethren,—upon the removal to Babylon.
text.KJV: And Iosias begate Iechonias and his brethren, about the time they were caried away to Babylon.
text.NABRE: Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers at the time of the Babylonian exile.
text.DRB: And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren in the Transmigration of Babylon.

text.NRSV: And after the deportation to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Salathiel, and Salathiel the father of Zerubbabel,
text.Rotherham: And, after the removal to Babylon, Jechoniah begat Shealtiel, and Shealtiel begat Zerubbabel;
text.KJV: And after they were brought to Babylon, Iechonias begat Salathiel, and Salathiel begate Zorobabel.
text.NABRE: After the Babylonian exile, Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel, Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
text.DRB: And after the Transmigration of Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel. And Salathiel begat Zorobabel.

text.NRSV: and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
text.Rotherham: And Zerubbabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begat Azor;
text.KJV: And Zorobabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begate Azor.
text.NABRE: Zerubbabel the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim, Eliakim the father of Azor,
text.DRB: And Zorobabel begat Abiud. And Abiud begat Eliacim. And Eliacim begat Azor.

text.NRSV: and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
text.Rotherham: And Azor begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud;
text.KJV: And Azor begat Sadoc, & Sadoc begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud.
text.NABRE: Azor the father of Zadok. Zadok became the father of Achim, Achim the father of Eliud,
text.DRB: And Azor begat Sadoc. And Sadoc begat Achim. And Achim begat Eliud.

text.NRSV: and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
text.Rotherham: And Eliud begat Eleazar, and Eleazar begat Matthan, and Matthan begat Jacob;—
text.KJV: And Eliud begate Eleazar, and Eleazar begate Matthan, and Matthan begate Iacob.
text.NABRE: Eliud the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan, Matthan the father of Jacob,
text.DRB: And Eliud begat Eleazar. And Eleazar begat Mathan. And Mathan begat Jacob.

text.NRSV: and Jacob the father of Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob begat Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus,—who is called Christ.
text.KJV: And Iacob begate Ioseph the husband of Mary, of whom was borne Iesus, who is called Christ.
text.NABRE: Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary. Of her was born Jesus who is called the Messiah.
text.DRB: And Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary: of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

text.NRSV: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David to the deportation to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the deportation to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
text.Rotherham: So then, all the generations from Abraham unto David, are, fourteen, generations, and, from David unto the removal to Babylon, fourteen, generations; and, from the removal to Babylon unto the Christ, fourteen, generations.
text.KJV: So all the generations from Abraham to Dauid, are fourteene generations: and from Dauid vntill the carying away into Babylon, are foureteene generations: and from the carying away into Babylon vnto Christ, are fourteene generations.
text.NABRE: Thus the total number of generations from Abraham to David is fourteen generations; from David to the Babylonian exile, fourteen generations; from the Babylonian exile to the Messiah, fourteen generations. The Birth of Jesus.
text.DRB: Therefore all the generations from Abraham unto David, fourteen generations. And from David to the Transmigration of Babylon, fourteen generations. And from the Transmigration of Babylon unto Christ, fourteen generations.

text.NRSV: Now the birth of Jesus the Messiah took place in this way. When his mother Mary had been engaged to Joseph, but before they lived together, she was found to be with child from the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Now the birth, of [Jesus] Christ, was, thus: His mother Mary having been betrothed to Joseph,—before they came together, she was found with child by [the] Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: ¶ Now the birth of Iesus Christ was on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Ioseph (before they came together) shee was found with childe of the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: Now this is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about. When his mother Mary was betrothed to Joseph, but before they lived together, she was found with child through the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: And the generation of Christ was in this wise. When his mother Mary was spoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found to be with child, by the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: Her husband Joseph, being a righteous man and unwilling to expose her to public disgrace, planned to dismiss her quietly.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Joseph her husband, being, righteous, and yet unwilling to expose her,—intended, privately, to divorce her.
text.KJV: Then Ioseph her husband being a iust man, and not willing to make her a publique example, was minded to put her away priuily.
text.NABRE: Joseph her husband, since he was a righteous man, yet unwilling to expose her to shame, decided to divorce her quietly.
text.DRB: Whereupon Joseph her husband for that he was a just man, & would not put her to open shame: was minded secretly to dismiss her.

text.NRSV: But just when he had resolved to do this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: But, when, these things, he had pondered, lo! a messenger of the Lord, by dream, appeared to him, saying,—Joseph, son of David! do not fear to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for, that which, in her, hath been begotten, is of [the], Holy, Spirit.
text.KJV: But while hee thought on these things, behold, the Angel of the Lord appeared vnto him in a dreame, saying, Ioseph thou sonne of Dauid, feare not to take vnto thee Mary thy wife: for that which is conceiued in her, is of the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: Such was his intention when, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary your wife into your home. For it is through the holy Spirit that this child has been conceived in her.
text.DRB: But as he was thus thinking, behold the Angel of our Lord appeared to him in sleep saying: Joseph son of David, fear not to take Mary thy wife, for that which is born in her, is of the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: She will bear a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus,—for, he, will save his people from their sins.
text.KJV: And she shall bring forth a sonne, and thou shalt call his Name Iesus: for hee shall saue his people from their sinnes.
text.NABRE: She will bear a son and you are to name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text.DRB: And she shall bring forth a Son: and thou shalt call his name Jesus. For he shall save his people from their sins.

text.NRSV: All this took place to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
text.Rotherham: But, all this, hath come to pass, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord, through the prophet, saying:
text.KJV: (Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the Prophet, saying,
text.NABRE: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
text.DRB: And all this was done that it might be fulfilled which our Lord spake by the Prophet saying.

text.NRSV: “Look, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall name him Emmanuel,” which means, “God is with us.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! a Virgin, shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son,—and they shall call his name Emmanuel; which is, being translated, God with us.
text.KJV: Behold, a Uirgin shall be with childe, and shall bring foorth a sonne, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted, is, God with vs.)
text.NABRE: “Behold, the virgin shall be with child and bear a son, and they shall name him Emmanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text.DRB: Behold a Virgin shall be with child, & bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.

text.NRSV: When Joseph awoke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him; he took her as his wife,
text.Rotherham: And Joseph, awaking, from his sleep, did as the messenger of the Lord directed him,—and took unto him his wife;
text.KJV: Then Ioseph, being raised from sleepe, did as the Angel of the Lord had bidden him, & tooke vnto him his wife:
text.NABRE: When Joseph awoke, he did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him and took his wife into his home.
text.DRB: And Joseph rising up from sleep, did as the Angel of our Lord commanded him, & took his wife.

text.NRSV: but had no marital relations with her until she had borne a son; and he named him Jesus.
text.Rotherham: and knew her not, until she had brought forth a son,—and he called his name Jesus.
text.KJV: And knewe her not, till shee had brought forth her first borne sonne, and he called his name Iesus.
text.NABRE: He had no relations with her until she bore a son, and he named him Jesus.
text.DRB: And he knew her not till she brought forth her first born Son: & called his name Jesus.

text.NRSV: In the time of King Herod, after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: Now, when, Jesus, was born in Bethlehem of Judaea, in the days of Herod the king, lo! wise men from eastern parts, came into Jerusalem:
text.KJV: Now when Iesus was borne in Bethlehem of Iudea, in the dayes of Herod the king, behold, there came Wise men from the East to Hierusalem,
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Visit of the Magi. When Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, in the days of King Herod, behold, magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem,
text.DRB: When Jesus therefore was born in Bethlehem of Juda in the days of Herod the king, behold there came Sages from the East to Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: asking, “Where is the child who has been born king of the Jews? For we observed his star at its rising, and have come to pay him homage.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Where is he that hath been born king of the Jews? for we saw his star in the east, and have come to bow down to him.
text.KJV: Saying, Where is he that is borne King of the Iewes? for we haue seene his Starre in the East, and are come to worship him.
text.NABRE: saying, “Where is the newborn king of the Jews? We saw his star at its rising and have come to do him homage.”
text.DRB: Saying, where is he that is born King of the Jews? For we have seen his star in the East, and are come to adore him.

text.NRSV: When King Herod heard this, he was frightened, and all Jerusalem with him;
text.Rotherham: But, when King Herod heard, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him;
text.KJV: When Herod the king had heard these things, he was troubled, and all Hierusalem with him.
text.NABRE: When King Herod heard this, he was greatly troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
text.DRB: And Herod the king Hearing this, was troubled, & all Jerusalem with him.

text.NRSV: and calling together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
text.Rotherham: and, assembling all the high-priests and scribes of the people, he was enquiring of them—Where is, the Christ, to be born?
text.KJV: And when he had gathered all the chiefe Priests and Scribes of the people together, hee demanded of them where Christ should be borne.
text.NABRE: Assembling all the chief priests and the scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Messiah was to be born.
text.DRB: And assembling together all the high Priests and the Scribes of the people, he inquired of them where Christ should be born.

text.NRSV: They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for so it has been written by the prophet:
text.Rotherham: And, they, said to him, In Bethlehem of Judaea,—for, so, is it written through the prophet:
text.KJV: And they said vnto him, In Bethlehem of Iudea: For thus it is written by the Prophet;
text.NABRE: They said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for thus it has been written through the prophet:
text.DRB: But they said to him: In Bethlehem of Juda. For so it is written by the Prophet:

text.NRSV: ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means least, art thou, among the governors of Judah,—For, out of thee, shall one come forth to govern, Who shall shepherd my people Israel.
text.KJV: And thou Bethlehem in the land of Iuda, art not the least among the Princes of Iuda: for out of thee shall come a Gouernour, that shall rule my people Israel.
text.NABRE: ‘And you, Bethlehem, land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; since from you shall come a ruler, who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”
text.DRB: And thou Bethlehem the land of Juda, art not the least among the Princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come forth the Captain that shall rule my people Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Herod secretly called for the wise men and learned from them the exact time when the star had appeared.
text.Rotherham: Then Herod, privately, calling the wise men, ascertained from them the time of the appearing star;
text.KJV: Then Herod, when he had priuily called the Wise men, enquired of them diligently what time the Starre appeared:
text.NABRE: Then Herod called the magi secretly and ascertained from them the time of the star’s appearance.
text.DRB: Then Herod secretly calling the Sages, learned diligently of them the time of the star which appeared to them;

text.NRSV: Then he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, “Go and search diligently for the child; and when you have found him, bring me word so that I may also go and pay him homage.”
text.Rotherham: and sending them to Bethlehem, said—Go search out accurately concerning the child,—and, as soon as ye find it, bring report unto me, that, I also, may come and bow myself down to it.
text.KJV: And he sent them to Bethlehem, and said, Goe, and search diligently for the yong child, and when ye haue found him, bring me word againe, that I may come and worship him also.
text.NABRE: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and search diligently for the child. When you have found him, bring me word, that I too may go and do him homage.”
text.DRB: and sending them into Bethlehem, said: Go, & inquire diligently of the child; and when you shall find him, make report to me, that I also may come and adore him.

text.NRSV: When they had heard the king, they set out; and there, ahead of them, went the star that they had seen at its rising, until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text.Rotherham: Now, they, hearing the king, went, and lo! the star which they had seen in the east, was going before them, until it went and stood over where the child was;
text.KJV: When they had heard the King, they departed, and loe, the Starre which they saw in the East, went before them, till it came and stood ouer where the young childe was.
text.NABRE: After their audience with the king they set out. And behold, the star that they had seen at its rising preceded them, until it came and stopped over the place where the child was.
text.DRB: Who having heard the king, went their way; and behold the star which they had seen in the East, went before them, until it came and stood over, where the child was.

text.NRSV: When they saw that the star had stopped, they were overwhelmed with joy.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy;
text.KJV: When they saw the Starre, they reioyced with exceeding great ioy.
text.NABRE: They were overjoyed at seeing the star,
text.DRB: And seeing the star they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.

text.NRSV: On entering the house, they saw the child with Mary his mother; and they knelt down and paid him homage. Then, opening their treasure chests, they offered him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
text.Rotherham: and, coming into the house, they saw the child, with Mary its mother,—and, falling down, prostrated themselves unto it, and, opening their treasures, offered unto it gifts—gold and frankincense and myrrh;
text.KJV: ¶ And when they were come into the house, they saw the yong child with Mary his mother, and fell downe, and worshipped him: and when they had opened their treasures, they presented vnto him gifts, gold, and frankincense, and myrrhe.
text.NABRE: and on entering the house they saw the child with Mary his mother. They prostrated themselves and did him homage. Then they opened their treasures and offered him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
text.DRB: And entering into the house, they found the child with Mary his mother, & falling down adored him; and opening their treasures, they offered to him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

text.NRSV: And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they left for their own country by another road.
text.Rotherham: and, being instructed by dream, not to return unto Herod, by another way, retired they unto their own country.
text.KJV: And being warned of God in a dreame, that they should not returne to Herode, they departed into their owne countrey another way.
text.NABRE: And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed for their country by another way.
text.DRB: And having received an answer in sleep that they should not return to Herod, they went back another way into their country.

text.NRSV: Now after they had left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you; for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.”
text.Rotherham: Now, when they had retired, lo! a messenger of the Lord, appeareth by dream unto Joseph, saying, Arise, take unto thee the child and its mother, and flee into Egypt, and be there, until I tell thee,—for Herod is about to seek the child, to destroy it.
text.KJV: And when they were departed, behold, the Angel of the Lord appeareth to Ioseph in a dreame, saying, Arise and take the young childe, and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and bee thou there vntill I bring thee word: for Herode will seeke the young childe, to destroy him.
text.NABRE: The Flight to Egypt. When they had departed, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Rise, take the child and his mother, flee to Egypt, and stay there until I tell you. Herod is going to search for the child to destroy him.”
text.DRB: And after they were departed, Behold an Angel of our Lord appeared in sleep to Joseph, saying: Arise, & take the child and his mother, and fly into Ægypt; and be there until I shall tell thee. For it will come to pass that Herod will seek the child to destroy him.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother by night, and went to Egypt,
text.Rotherham: And, he, arising, took unto him the child and its mother, by night, and retired into Egypt;
text.KJV: When he arose, he tooke the yong childe and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt:
text.NABRE: Joseph rose and took the child and his mother by night and departed for Egypt.
text.DRB: Who arose, & took the child and his mother by night, and retired into Ægypt: and he was there until the death of Herod:

text.NRSV: and remained there until the death of Herod. This was to fulfill what had been spoken by the Lord through the prophet, “Out of Egypt I have called my son.”
text.Rotherham: and was there, until the death of Herod,—that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt, called I my son.
text.KJV: And was there vntill the death of Herode, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the Prophet, saying, Out of Egypt haue I called my sonne.
text.NABRE: He stayed there until the death of Herod, that what the Lord had said through the prophet might be fulfilled, “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
text.DRB: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of our Lord by the Prophet, saying: Out of Ægypt have I called my Son.

text.NRSV: When Herod saw that he had been tricked by the wise men, he was infuriated, and he sent and killed all the children in and around Bethlehem who were two years old or under, according to the time that he had learned from the wise men.
text.Rotherham: Then Herod, seeing that he had been mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly enraged,—and sent and slew all the male children that were in Bethlehem, and in all its bounds, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had ascertained from the wise men.
text.KJV: ¶ Then Herode, when hee saw that hee was mocked of the Wise men, was exceeding wroth, and sent foorth, and slewe all the children that were in Bethlehem, and in all the coasts thereof, from two yeeres olde and vnder, according to the time, which he had diligently enquired of the Wise men.
text.NABRE: The Massacre of the Infants. When Herod realized that he had been deceived by the magi, he became furious. He ordered the massacre of all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity two years old and under, in accordance with the time he had ascertained from the magi.
text.DRB: Then Herod perceiving that he was deluded by the Sages, was exceeding angry; and sending murdered all the men children that were in Bethlehem, & in all the borders thereof, from two year old and under, according to the time which he had diligently sought out of the Sages.

text.NRSV: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken through the prophet Jeremiah:
text.Rotherham: Then was fulfilled, that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying:
text.KJV: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Ieremie the Prophet, saying,
text.NABRE: Then was fulfilled what had been said through Jeremiah the prophet:
text.DRB: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the Prophet saying:

text.NRSV: “A voice was heard in Ramah, wailing and loud lamentation, Rachel weeping for her children; she refused to be consoled, because they are no more.”
text.Rotherham: A voice, in Ramah, was heard, weeping and great mourning,—Rachel weeping for her children, and would not be comforted,—because they are not.
text.KJV: In Rama was there a voice heard, lamentation, and weeping, and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.
text.NABRE: “A voice was heard in Ramah, sobbing and loud lamentation; Rachel weeping for her children, and she would not be consoled, since they were no more.”
text.DRB: A voice in Rama was heard, crying out & much wailing: Rachel bewailing her children, & would not be comforted, because they are not.

text.NRSV: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord suddenly appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt and said,
text.Rotherham: But, when Herod was, dead, lo! a messenger of the Lord, appeareth by dream unto Joseph in Egypt;
text.KJV: ¶ But when Herode was dead, behold, an Angel of the Lord appeareth in a dreame to Ioseph in Egypt,
text.NABRE: The Return from Egypt. When Herod had died, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt
text.DRB: But when Herod was dead, behold an Angel of our Lord appeared in sleep to Joseph in Ægypt.

text.NRSV: “Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were seeking the child’s life are dead.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Arise! and take unto thee the child and its mother, and be journeying into the land of Israel,—for they are dead, who were seeking the life of the child.
text.KJV: Saying, Arise, and take the yong childe and his mother, and goe into the land of Israel: for they are dead which sought the yong childes life.
text.NABRE: and said, “Rise, take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel, for those who sought the child’s life are dead.”
text.DRB: saying: Arise, and take the child & his mother, and go into the land of Israel. For they are dead that sought the life of the child.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, he, arising, took unto him the child and its mother, and entered into the land of Israel.
text.KJV: And he arose, and tooke the yong childe and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.
text.NABRE: He rose, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
text.DRB: Who arose, & took the child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.

text.NRSV: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And after being warned in a dream, he went away to the district of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: Hearing, however, that, Archelaus, was reigning over Judaea instead of his father Herod, he was afraid, thither, to go,—and so, being instructed by dream, he retired into the parts of Galilee;
text.KJV: But when he heard that Archelaus did reigne in Iudea in the roome of his father Herod, hee was afraid to goe thither: notwithstanding, beeing warned of God in a dreame, he turned aside into the parts of Galilee:
text.NABRE: But when he heard that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go back there. And because he had been warned in a dream, he departed for the region of Galilee.
text.DRB: But hearing that Archelaus reigned in Jewry for Herod his father, he feared to go thither: and being warned in sleep retired into the quarters of Galilee.

text.NRSV: There he made his home in a town called Nazareth, so that what had been spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled, “He will be called a Nazorean.”
text.Rotherham: and came and fixed his dwelling in a city called Nazareth,—that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets—A Nazarene, shall he be called.
text.KJV: And hee came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophets, He shalbe called a Nazarene.
text.NABRE: He went and dwelt in a town called Nazareth, so that what had been spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled, “He shall be called a Nazorean.”
text.DRB: And coming he dwelt in a city called Nazareth: that it might be fulfilled which was said by the Prophets: That he shall be called a Nazarite.

text.NRSV: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness of Judea, proclaiming,
text.Rotherham: Now, in those days, came John the Immerser, proclaiming in the wilderness of Judaea;
text.KJV: In those daies came Iohn the Baptist, preaching in the wildernesse of Iudea,
text.NABRE: II. The Proclamation of the Kingdom Chapter 3 The Preaching of John the Baptist. In those days John the Baptist appeared, preaching in the desert of Judea
text.DRB: And in those days cometh John the Baptist preaching in the desert of Jewry,

text.NRSV: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
text.Rotherham: saying, Repent ye,—for the kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near.
text.KJV: And saying, Repent yee: for the kingdome of heauen is at hand.
text.NABRE: [and] saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand!”
text.DRB: & saying: Do penance: for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.

text.NRSV: This is the one of whom the prophet Isaiah spoke when he said, “The voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.’”
text.Rotherham: For, this, is he who was spoken of through Isaiah the prophet, saying, A voice, of one crying aloud! In the wilderness, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths.
text.KJV: For this is he that was spoken of by the Prophet Esaias, saying, The voyce of one crying in the wildernes, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
text.NABRE: It was of him that the prophet Isaiah had spoken when he said: “A voice of one crying out in the desert, ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths.’”
text.DRB: For this is he that was spoken of by Esay the Prophet, saying: A voice of one crying in the desert, prepare ye the way of our Lord, make straight his paths.

text.NRSV: Now John wore clothing of camel’s hair with a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
text.Rotherham: But John, himself, had his raiment of camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins,—while, his food, was locusts and wild honey.
text.KJV: And the same Iohn had his raiment of camels haire, and a leatherne girdle about his loynes, and his meate was locusts and wilde hony.
text.NABRE: John wore clothing made of camel’s hair and had a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
text.DRB: And the said John had his garment of camels' hair, and a girdle of a skin about his loins: and his meat was locusts & wild honey.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Jerusalem and all Judea were going out to him, and all the region along the Jordan,
text.Rotherham: Then, were going forth unto him—Jerusalem, and all Judaea, and all the country round about the Jordan:
text.KJV: Then went out to him Hierusalem, and all Iudea, and all the region round about Iordane,
text.NABRE: At that time Jerusalem, all Judea, and the whole region around the Jordan were going out to him
text.DRB: Then went forth to him Jerusalem and all Jewry, and all the country about Jordan:

text.NRSV: and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.Rotherham: and were being immersed in the Jordan river, by him, openly confessing their sins.
text.KJV: And were baptized of him in Iordane, confessing their sinnes.
text.NABRE: and were being baptized by him in the Jordan River as they acknowledged their sins.
text.DRB: & were baptized of him in Jordan, confessing their sins.

text.NRSV: But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
text.Rotherham: But, seeing, many of the Pharisees and Sadducees, coming unto his immersion, he said to them,—Broods of vipers! who suggested to you, to be fleeing from the coming wrath?
text.KJV: ¶ But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to his Baptisme, he said vnto them, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
text.NABRE: When he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
text.DRB: And seeing many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his Baptism, he said to them. Ye viper's brood, who hath shewed you to flee from the wrath to come?

text.NRSV: Bear fruit worthy of repentance.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth, therefore, fruit worthy of repentance;
text.KJV: Bring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance.
text.NABRE: Produce good fruit as evidence of your repentance.
text.DRB: Yield therefore fruit worthy of penance.

text.NRSV: Do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our ancestor’; for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.
text.Rotherham: and think not to be saying within yourselves,—As our father, we have, Abraham; for, I say unto you, that God is able, out of these stones, to raise up children unto Abraham.
text.KJV: And thinke not to say within your selues, Wee haue Abraham to our father: For I say vnto you, that God is able of these stones to raise vp children vnto Abraham.
text.NABRE: And do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I tell you, God can raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
text.DRB: And delight not to say within yourselves, we have Abraham to our father. For I tell you that God is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

text.NRSV: Even now the ax is lying at the root of the trees; every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.Rotherham: Already also, the axe, unto the root of the trees, is being laid,—every tree, therefore, not bringing forth good fruit, is to be hewn down, and, into fire, to be cast.
text.KJV: And now also the axe is layd vnto the root of the trees: Therefore euery tree which bringeth not foorth good fruite, is hewen downe, and cast into the fire.
text.NABRE: Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees. Therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.DRB: For now the axe is put to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doth not yield good fruit, shall be cut down, & cast into the fire.

text.NRSV: “I baptize you with water for repentance, but one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
text.Rotherham: I, indeed, am immersing you, in water, unto repentance,—but, he who, after me, cometh is, mightier than I, whose, sandals, I am not worthy to bear, he, will immerse you, in Holy Spirit and fire:
text.KJV: I indeed baptize you with water vnto repentance: but he that commeth after mee, is mightier then I, whose shooes I am not worthy to beare, hee shall baptize you with the holy Ghost, and with fire.
text.NABRE: I am baptizing you with water, for repentance, but the one who is coming after me is mightier than I. I am not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the holy Spirit and fire.
text.DRB: I indeed baptize you in water unto penance, but he that shall come after me, is stronger than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to bear, he shall baptize you in the Holy Ghost & fire.

text.NRSV: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and will gather his wheat into the granary; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
text.Rotherham: Whose fan is in his hand, and he will clear out his threshing-floor,—and will gather his wheat into the granary, but, the chaff, will he burn up with fire unquenchable.
text.KJV: Whose fanne is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floore, and gather his wheat into the garner: but wil burne vp the chaffe with vnquenchable fire.
text.NABRE: His winnowing fan is in his hand. He will clear his threshing floor and gather his wheat into his barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.” The Baptism of Jesus.
text.DRB: Whose fan is in his hand, and he shall clean purge his floor: and he will gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan, to be baptized by him.
text.Rotherham: Then, cometh Jesus, from Galilee to the Jordan, unto John,—to be immersed by him.
text.KJV: ¶ Then commeth Iesus from Galilee to Iordane, vnto Iohn, to be baptized of him:
text.NABRE: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan to be baptized by him.
text.DRB: Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to Jordan, unto John, to be baptized of him.

text.NRSV: John would have prevented him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, would have hindered him, saying—I, have, need, by thee, to be immersed,—and dost, thou, come unto me?
text.KJV: But Iohn forbade him, saying, I haue need to bee baptized of thee, and commest thou to me?
text.NABRE: John tried to prevent him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and yet you are coming to me?”
text.DRB: But John stayed him, saying: I ought to be baptized of thee, and comest thou to me?

text.NRSV: But Jesus answered him, “Let it be so now; for it is proper for us in this way to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he consented.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus answering, said unto him, Suffer [me] even now,—for, thus, it becometh us, to fulfil, all righteousness; then, he suffered him.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said vnto him, Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becommeth vs to fulfill all righteousnesse. Then he suffered him.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “Allow it now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed him.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to him: Suffer me for this time. For so it becometh us to fulfill all justice. Then he suffered him.

text.NRSV: And when Jesus had been baptized, just as he came up from the water, suddenly the heavens were opened to him and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, having been immersed, straightway, went up from the water,—and lo! the heavens were opened and he saw the Spirit of God, descending like a dove coming upon him;
text.KJV: And Iesus, when hee was baptized, went vp straightway out of the water: and loe, the heauens were opened vnto him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a doue, and lighting vpon him.
text.NABRE: After Jesus was baptized, he came up from the water and behold, the heavens were opened [for him], and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove [and] coming upon him.
text.DRB: And Jesus being baptized, forthwith came out of the water: and lo the Heavens were opened to him: & he saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, & coming upon him.

text.NRSV: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, the Beloved, with whom I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and lo! a voice out of the heavens,—saying, This, is my Son, the Beloved, in whom I delight.
text.KJV: And loe, a voice from heauen, saying, This is my beloued Sonne, in whom I am well pleased.
text.NABRE: And a voice came from the heavens, saying, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.”
text.DRB: And behold a voice from Heaven saying: This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus was led up into the wilderness, by the Spirit, to be tempted by the adversary;
text.KJV: Then was Iesus led vp of the Spirit into the wildernesse, to bee tempted of the deuill.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 The Temptation of Jesus. Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the desert to be tempted by the devil.
text.DRB: Then Jesus was led of the Spirit into the desert, to be tempted of the devil.

text.NRSV: He fasted forty days and forty nights, and afterwards he was famished.
text.Rotherham: and, fasting forty days and forty nights,—afterwards, he hungered.
text.KJV: And when hee had fasted forty dayes and forty nights, hee was afterward an hungred.
text.NABRE: He fasted for forty days and forty nights, and afterwards he was hungry.
text.DRB: And when he had fasted forty days and forty nights, afterward he was hungry.

text.NRSV: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, the tempter said to him,—If thou art God’s, Son, speak! that, these stones, may become, loaves.
text.KJV: And when the tempter came to him, hee said, If thou be the sonne of God, command that these stones bee made bread.
text.NABRE: The tempter approached and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command that these stones become loaves of bread.”
text.DRB: And the tempter approached and said to him: If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.

text.NRSV: But he answered, “It is written, ‘One does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.’”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said, It is written,—Not on bread alone, shall man live, but on every declaration coming forth through the mouth of God.
text.KJV: But he answered, and said, It is written, Man shall not liue by bread alone, but by euery word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “It is written: ‘One does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes forth from the mouth of God.’”
text.DRB: Who answered and said: It is written, Not in bread alone doth man live, but in every word that proceedeth from the mouth of God.

text.NRSV: Then the devil took him to the holy city and placed him on the pinnacle of the temple,
text.Rotherham: Then, the adversary taketh him with him, unto the holy city,—and he set him upon the pinnacle of the temple;
text.KJV: Then the deuill taketh him vp into the holy Citie, and setteth him on a pinacle of the Temple,
text.NABRE: Then the devil took him to the holy city, and made him stand on the parapet of the temple,
text.DRB: Then the devil took him up into the holy city, and set him upon the pinnacle of the Temple,

text.NRSV: saying to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down; for it is written, ‘He will give his angels charge over you,’ and ‘On their hands they will bear you up, so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.’”
text.Rotherham: and saith to him—If thou art God’s, Son, cast thyself down,—for it is written, To his messengers, will he give charge concerning thee; and, on hands, will they bear thee up, lest once thou strike, against a stone, thy foot.
text.KJV: And saith vnto him, If thou bee the Sonne of God, cast thy selfe downe: For it is written, He shall giue his Angels charge concerning thee, & in their handes they shall beare thee vp, lest at any time thou dash thy foote against a stone.
text.NABRE: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down. For it is written: ‘He will command his angels concerning you’ and ‘with their hands they will support you, lest you dash your foot against a stone.’”
text.DRB: and said to him: If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down, for it is written: That he will give his Angels charge of thee, & in their hands shall they hold thee up, lest perhaps thou knock thy foot against a stone.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Again it is written, ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said to him, Again, it is written,—Thou shalt not put to the test, the Lord thy God.
text.KJV: Iesus said vnto him, It is written againe, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Again it is written, ‘You shall not put the Lord, your God, to the test.’”
text.DRB: Jesus said to him again: It is written, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor;
text.Rotherham: Again, the adversary taketh him with him, into an exceeding high mountain,—and pointeth out to him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory;
text.KJV: Againe the Deuill taketh him vp into an exceeding high mountaine, and sheweth him all the kingdomes of the world, and the glory of them:
text.NABRE: Then the devil took him up to a very high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in their magnificence,
text.DRB: Again the devil took him up into a very high mountain: and he shewed him all the kingdoms of the world, and the glory of them,

text.NRSV: and he said to him, “All these I will give you, if you will fall down and worship me.”
text.Rotherham: and said to him, All these things, will I give thee,—if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
text.KJV: And saith vnto him, All these things will I giue thee, if thou wilt fall downe and worship me.
text.NABRE: and he said to him, “All these I shall give to you, if you will prostrate yourself and worship me.”
text.DRB: and said to him: All these will I give thee, if falling down thou wilt adore me.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Away with you, Satan! for it is written, ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve only him.’”
text.Rotherham: Then saith Jesus unto him, Withdraw, Satan! for it is written,—The Lord thy God, shalt thou worship, and, to him alone, render divine service.
text.KJV: Then saith Iesus vnto him, Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him onely shalt thou serue.
text.NABRE: At this, Jesus said to him, “Get away, Satan! It is written: ‘The Lord, your God, shall you worship and him alone shall you serve.’”
text.DRB: Then Jesus saith to him: Avaunt Satan; for it is written, The Lord thy God shalt thou adore, and him only shalt thou serve.

text.NRSV: Then the devil left him, and suddenly angels came and waited on him.
text.Rotherham: Then, the adversary leaveth him,—and lo! messengers came near, and began ministering unto him.
text.KJV: Then the deuill leaueth him, and behold, Angels came and ministred vnto him.
text.NABRE: Then the devil left him and, behold, angels came and ministered to him. The Beginning of the Galilean Ministry.
text.DRB: Then the devil left him; and behold Angels came, and ministered to him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing that, John, had been delivered up, he retired into Galilee;
text.KJV: ¶ Now when Iesus had heard that Iohn was cast into prison, he departed into Galilee.
text.NABRE: When he heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.
text.DRB: And when Jesus had heard that John was delivered up, he retired into Galilee :

text.NRSV: He left Nazareth and made his home in Capernaum by the sea, in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali,
text.Rotherham: and, forsaking Nazareth, he came and fixed his dwelling in Capernaum, which was by the lake,—within the bounds of Zebulun and Naphtali:
text.KJV: And leauing Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is vpon the Sea coast, in the borders of Zabulon and Nephthali:
text.NABRE: He left Nazareth and went to live in Capernaum by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali,
text.DRB: and leaving the city Nazareth, came and dwelt in Capharnaum a sea town, in the borders of Zabulon & Nephthali;

text.NRSV: so that what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled:
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:
text.KJV: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the Prophet, saying,
text.NABRE: that what had been said through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled:
text.DRB: that it might be fulfilled which was said by Esay the Prophet.

text.NRSV: “Land of Zebulun, land of Naphtali, on the road by the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles—
text.Rotherham: Land of Zebulun, and land of Naphtali, the lake-way across the Jordan,—Galilee of the nations,
text.KJV: The land of Zabulon, and the land of Nephthali, by the way of the Sea beyond Iordane, Galilee of the Gentiles:
text.NABRE: “Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the way to the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles,
text.DRB: Land of Zabulon & land of Nephthali, the way of the sea beyond Jordan of Galilee, of the Gentiles:

text.NRSV: the people who sat in darkness have seen a great light, and for those who sat in the region and shadow of death light has dawned.”
text.Rotherham: The people that was sitting in darkness, a great light, beheld,—and, on them who were sitting in land and shade of death, Light rose on them.
text.KJV: The people which sate in darkenesse, saw great light: and to them which sate in the region and shadow of death, light is sprung vp.
text.NABRE: the people who sit in darkness have seen a great light, on those dwelling in a land overshadowed by death light has arisen.”
text.DRB: the people that sat in darkness, hath seen great light: & to them that sat in a country of the shadow of death, light is risen to them.

text.NRSV: From that time Jesus began to proclaim, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
text.Rotherham: From that time, began Jesus to be making proclamation, and saying,—Repent ye, for the kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near.
text.KJV: ¶ From that time Iesus began to preach, and to say, Repent, for the kingdome of heauen is at hand.
text.NABRE: From that time on, Jesus began to preach and say, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.” The Call of the First Disciples.
text.DRB: From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say: Do penance, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.

text.NRSV: As he walked by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea—for they were fishermen.
text.Rotherham: And, walking round beside the sea of Galilee, he saw two brethren—Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,—casting a large fishing-net into the sea, for they were fishers;
text.KJV: ¶ And Iesus walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon, called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the Sea (for they were fishers)
text.NABRE: As he was walking by the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon who is called Peter, and his brother Andrew, casting a net into the sea; they were fishermen.
text.DRB: And Jesus walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea (for they were fishers)

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fish for people.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them, Come after me,—and I will make you fishers, of men.
text.KJV: And he saith vnto them, Follow mee: and I will make you fishers of men.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you fishers of men.”
text.DRB: and he saith to them: Come ye after me, and I will make you to be fishers of men.

text.NRSV: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, straightway, leaving the nets, followed him.
text.KJV: And they straightway left their nets, and followed him.
text.NABRE: At once they left their nets and followed him.
text.DRB: But they incontinent leaving the nets, followed him.

text.NRSV: As he went from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John, in the boat with their father Zebedee, mending their nets, and he called them.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward from thence, he saw other two brethren—James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother—in the boat with Zebedee their father, putting in order their nets,—and he called them.
text.KJV: And going on from thence, hee sawe other two brethren, Iames the sonne of Zebedee, and Iohn his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, mending their nets: and he called them.
text.NABRE: He walked along from there and saw two other brothers, James, the son of Zebedee, and his brother John. They were in a boat, with their father Zebedee, mending their nets. He called them,
text.DRB: And going forward from thence, he saw other two brethren, James of Zebedee, and John his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, repairing their nets: and he called them.

text.NRSV: Immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, straightway, leaving the boat and their father, followed him.
text.KJV: And they immediatly left the shippe and their father, and followed him.
text.NABRE: and immediately they left their boat and their father and followed him. Ministering to a Great Multitude.
text.DRB: And they forthwith left their nets and father and followed him.

text.NRSV: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom and curing every disease and every sickness among the people.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was going round throughout all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the glad-message of the kingdom,—and curing every disease, and every infirmity, among the people.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their Synagogues, and preaching the Gospel of the kingdome, and healing all maner of sickenesse, and all maner of disease among the people.
text.NABRE: He went around all of Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and curing every disease and illness among the people.
text.DRB: And Jesus went round about all Galilee, teaching in their Synagogues, & preaching the Gospel of the Kingdom: and healing every malady, and every infirmity, in the people.

text.NRSV: So his fame spread throughout all Syria, and they brought to him all the sick, those who were afflicted with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, and paralytics, and he cured them.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the report of him into all Syria; and they brought unto him all who were sick, with divers diseases and tortures, distressed, demonized and lunatic and paralyzed,—and he cured them.
text.KJV: And his fame went thorowout all Syria: and they brought vnto him all sicke people that were taken with diuerse diseases and torments, and those which were possessed with deuils, and those which were lunaticke, and those that had the palsie, and he healed them.
text.NABRE: His fame spread to all of Syria, and they brought to him all who were sick with various diseases and racked with pain, those who were possessed, lunatics, and paralytics, and he cured them.
text.DRB: And the bruit of him went into all Syria, and they presented to him all that were ill at ease, diversely taken with diseases and torments, and such as were possest, and lunatics, and sick of the palsy, and he cured them:

text.NRSV: And great crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And there followed him large multitudes—from Galilee, and Decapolis, and Jerusalem, and Judaea, and across the Jordan.
text.KJV: And there followed him great great multitudes of people, from Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Hierusalem, and from Iudea, and from beyond Iordane.
text.NABRE: And great crowds from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, and Judea, and from beyond the Jordan followed him.
text.DRB: And much people followed him from Galilee, and Decapolis, and Jerusalem, and from Jury and from beyond Jordan.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up the mountain; and after he sat down, his disciples came to him.
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain,—and, when he had taken a seat, his disciples came unto him;
text.KJV: And seeing the multitudes, he went vp into a mountaine: and when he was set, his disciples came vnto him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 The Sermon on the Mount. When he saw the crowds, he went up the mountain, and after he had sat down, his disciples came to him.
text.DRB: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his Disciples came unto him,

text.NRSV: Then he began to speak, and taught them, saying:
text.Rotherham: and, opening his mouth, he began teaching them, saying:—
text.KJV: And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,
text.NABRE: He began to teach them, saying:
text.DRB: and opening his mouth he taught them, saying.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the destitute, in spirit; for, theirs, is the kingdom of the heavens;
text.KJV: Blessed are the poore in spirit: for theirs is the kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: The Beatitudes “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.DRB: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who mourn; for, they, shall be comforted:
text.KJV: Blessed are they that mourne: for they shall be comforted.
text.NABRE: Blessed are they who mourn, for they will be comforted.
text.DRB: Blessed are the meek: for they shall possess the land.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the meek; for, they, shall inherit the earth:
text.KJV: Blessed are the meeke: for they shall inherit the earth.
text.NABRE: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the land.
text.DRB: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who hunger and thirst for righteousness; for, they, shall be filled:
text.KJV: Blessed are they which doe hunger and thirst after righteousnesse: for they shall be filled.
text.NABRE: Blessed are they who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be satisfied.
text.DRB: Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after justice: for they shall have their fill.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the merciful, for they will receive mercy.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the merciful; for, they, shall receive mercy:
text.KJV: Blessed are the mercifull: for they shall obtaine mercie.
text.NABRE: Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.
text.DRB: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the pure, in heart; for, they, shall, see God:
text.KJV: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
text.NABRE: Blessed are the clean of heart, for they will see God.
text.DRB: Blessed are the clean of heart: for they shall see God.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
text.Rotherham: Happy, the peacemakers; for, they, shall be, called sons of God:
text.KJV: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall bee called the children of God.
text.NABRE: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
text.DRB: Blessed are the peace-makers: for they shall be called the children of God.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness’ sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Happy, they who have been persecuted for righteousness’ sake; for, theirs, is the kingdom of the heavens.
text.KJV: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousnesse sake: for theirs is the kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: Blessed are they who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text.DRB: Blessed are they that suffer persecution for justice: for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you when people revile you and persecute you and utter all kinds of evil against you falsely on my account.
text.Rotherham: Happy, are ye, whensoever they may reproach you and persecute you, and say every evil thing against you, falsely, for my sake:
text.KJV: Blessed are ye, when men shall reuile you, and persecute you, and shal say all manner of euill against you falsly for my sake.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you when they insult you and persecute you and utter every kind of evil against you [falsely] because of me.
text.DRB: Blessed are ye when they shall revile you, and persecute you, & speak all that naught is against you, untruly, for my sake:

text.NRSV: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice and exult, because, your reward, is great in the heavens; for, so, persecuted they the prophets who were before you.
text.KJV: Reioyce, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heauen: For so persecuted they the Prophets which were before you.
text.NABRE: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward will be great in heaven. Thus they persecuted the prophets who were before you. The Similes of Salt and Light.
text.DRB: be glad & rejoice, for your reward is very great in Heaven. For so they persecuted the Prophets, that were before you.

text.NRSV: “You are the salt of the earth; but if salt has lost its taste, how can its saltiness be restored? It is no longer good for anything, but is thrown out and trampled under foot.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are the salt of the earth; but, if the salt become tasteless, wherewith shall it be salted? it is good, for nothing, any more, save, being cast out, to be trampled on by men.
text.KJV: ¶ Yee are the salt of the earth: But if the salt haue lost his sauour, wherewith shall it bee salted? It is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be troden vnder foote of men.
text.NABRE: “You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its taste, with what can it be seasoned? It is no longer good for anything but to be thrown out and trampled underfoot.
text.DRB: You are the salt of the earth.

text.NRSV: “You are the light of the world. A city built on a hill cannot be hid.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are the light of the world: it is impossible for a city to be hid, on the top of a mountain, lying.
text.KJV: Yee are the light of the world. A citie that is set on an hill, cannot be hid.
text.NABRE: You are the light of the world. A city set on a mountain cannot be hidden.
text.DRB: But if the salt leese his virtue, wherewith shall it be salted? It is good for nothing any more but to be cast forth, and to be trodden of men.

text.NRSV: No one after lighting a lamp puts it under the bushel basket, but on the lampstand, and it gives light to all in the house.
text.Rotherham: Neither light they a lamp, and place it under the measure; but upon the lampstand, and it giveth light to all that are in the house.
text.KJV: Neither doe men light a candle, and put it vnder a bushell: but on a candlesticke, and it giueth light vnto all that are in the house.
text.NABRE: Nor do they light a lamp and then put it under a bushel basket; it is set on a lampstand, where it gives light to all in the house.
text.DRB: You are the light of the world. A city cannot be hid, situated on a mountain.

text.NRSV: In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: In like manner, let your light shine before men,—that they may see your good works,—and glorify your Father who is in the heavens.
text.KJV: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good workes, and glorifie your father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: Just so, your light must shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your heavenly Father.
text.DRB: Neither do men light a candle and put it under a bushel, but upon a candlestick, that it may shine to all that are in the house.

text.NRSV: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the law or the prophets; I have come not to abolish but to fulfill.
text.Rotherham: Do not think, that I came to pull down the law, or the prophets,—I came not to pull down, but to fulfil.
text.KJV: ¶ Thinke not that I am come to destroy the lawe or the Prophets. I am not come to destroy, but to fulfill.
text.NABRE: Teaching About the Law. “Do not think that I have come to abolish the law or the prophets. I have come not to abolish but to fulfill.
text.DRB: So let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in Heaven.

text.NRSV: For truly I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not one letter, not one stroke of a letter, will pass from the law until all is accomplished.
text.Rotherham: For, verily, I say unto you, until the heaven and the earth shall pass away, one least letter, or one point, may in nowise pass away from the law, till all be accomplished.
text.KJV: For verily I say vnto you, Till heauen and earth passe, one iote or one title, shall in no wise passe from the law, till all be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, not the smallest letter or the smallest part of a letter will pass from the law, until all things have taken place.
text.DRB: Do not think that I am come to break the Law, or the Prophets. I am not come to break, but to fulfill.

text.NRSV: Therefore, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments, and teaches others to do the same, will be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever does them and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever, therefore, shall relax one of these commandments, the least, and teach men so, shall be called, least, in the kingdom of the heavens; but, whosoever shall do and teach, the same, shall be called, great, in the kingdom of the heavens.
text.KJV: Whosoeuer therfore shall breake one of these least commaundements, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdome of heauen: but whosoeuer shall doe, and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whoever breaks one of the least of these commandments and teaches others to do so will be called least in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever obeys and teaches these commandments will be called greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text.DRB: For assuredly I say unto you, till Heaven and earth pass, one jot, or one tittle shall not pass of the Law, till all be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: For I say unto you, that, unless your righteousness exceed that of the Scribes and Pharisees, in nowise, may ye enter into the kingdom of the heavens.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, That except your righteousnesse shall exceede the righteousnesse of the Scribes and Pharisees, yee shall in no case enter into the kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: I tell you, unless your righteousness surpasses that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will not enter into the kingdom of heaven. Teaching About Anger.
text.DRB: He therefore that shall break one of these least commandments, and shall so teach men, shall be called the least in the Kingdom of Heaven. But he that shall do and teach, he shall be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not murder’; and ‘whoever murders shall be liable to judgment.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, to them of olden time,—Thou shalt not commit murder, and, whosoever shall commit murder, shall be, liable, to judgment.
text.KJV: ¶ Yee haue heard, that it was saide by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill: and, Whosoeuer shall kill, shalbe in danger of the iudgement.
text.NABRE: “You have heard that it was said to your ancestors, ‘You shall not kill; and whoever kills will be liable to judgment.’
text.DRB: For I tell you, that unless your justice abound more than that of the Scribes and Pharisees, you shall not enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: But I say to you that if you are angry with a brother or sister, you will be liable to judgment; and if you insult a brother or sister, you will be liable to the council; and if you say, ‘You fool,’ you will be liable to the hell of fire.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, every one who is angry with his brother, shall be, liable, to judgment,—and, whosoever shall say to his brother, Worthless one!, shall be, liable, to the high council; and, whosoever shall say, Rebel!, shall be, liable, unto the fiery gehenna.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, that whosoeuer is angry with his brother without a cause, shall be in danger of the Iudgement: and whosoeuer shall say to his brother, Racha, shal be in danger of the counsell: but whosoeuer shall say, Thou foole, shalbe in danger of hell fire.
text.NABRE: But I say to you, whoever is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment, and whoever says to his brother, ‘Raqa,’ will be answerable to the Sanhedrin, and whoever says, ‘You fool,’ will be liable to fiery Gehenna.
text.DRB: You have heard that it was said to them of old: Thou shalt not kill. And whoso killeth, shall be in danger of judgement.

text.NRSV: So when you are offering your gift at the altar, if you remember that your brother or sister has something against you,
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, thou be bearing thy gift towards the altar, and, there, shouldst remember that, thy brother, hath aught against thee,
text.KJV: Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there remembrest that thy brother hath ought against thee:
text.NABRE: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar, and there recall that your brother has anything against you,
text.DRB: But I say to you, that whosoever is angry with his brother, shall be in danger of judgment. And whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of a council. And whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be guilty of the Hell of fire.

text.NRSV: leave your gift there before the altar and go; first be reconciled to your brother or sister, and then come and offer your gift.
text.Rotherham: leave, there, thy gift before the altar, and withdraw,—first, be reconciled unto thy brother, and, then, coming, be offering thy gift.
text.KJV: Leaue there thy gift before the altar, and goe thy way, first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.
text.NABRE: leave your gift there at the altar, go first and be reconciled with your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
text.DRB: If therefore thou offer thy gift at the Altar, and there thou remember that thy brother hath ought against thee;

text.NRSV: Come to terms quickly with your accuser while you are on the way to court with him, or your accuser may hand you over to the judge, and the judge to the guard, and you will be thrown into prison.
text.Rotherham: Be making agreement with thine adversary, quickly, while thou art with him, in the way,—lest once thine adversary deliver thee up unto the judge, and the judge, unto the officer, and, into prison, thou be cast.
text.KJV: Agree with thine aduersarie quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him: least at any time the aduersarie deliuer thee to the iudge, and the iudge deliuer thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.
text.NABRE: Settle with your opponent quickly while on the way to court with him. Otherwise your opponent will hand you over to the judge, and the judge will hand you over to the guard, and you will be thrown into prison.
text.DRB: leave there thy offering before the Altar, and go first to be reconciled to thy brother: and then coming thou shalt offer thy gift.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, you will never get out until you have paid the last penny.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto thee, In nowise, mayest thou come out from thence, until thou pay the last halfpenny.
text.KJV: Uerily I say vnto thee, thou shalt by no meanes come out thence, till thou hast payd the vttermost farthing.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, you will not be released until you have paid the last penny.
text.DRB: Be at agreement with thy adversary betimes, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest perhaps the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, Thou shalt not commit adultery:
text.KJV: ¶ Yee haue heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit adulterie.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Adultery. “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
text.DRB: Amen I say to thee, thou shalt not go out from thence till thou repay the last farthing.

text.NRSV: But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, Every one who looketh on a woman so as to covet her, already, hath committed adultery with her, in his heart.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, That whosoeuer looketh on a woman to lust after her, hath committed adulterie with her already in his heart.
text.NABRE: But I say to you, everyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
text.DRB: You have heard that it was said to them of old: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

text.NRSV: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
text.Rotherham: And, if, thy right eye, is causing thee to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from thee,—for it profiteth thee, that, one of thy members, should perish, and not, thy whole body, be cast into gehenna.
text.KJV: And if thy right eie offend thee, plucke it out, and cast it from thee. For it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
text.NABRE: If your right eye causes you to sin, tear it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one of your members than to have your whole body thrown into Gehenna.
text.DRB: But I say to you, that whosoever shall see a woman to lust after her, hath already committed adultery with her in his heart.

text.NRSV: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away; it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to go into hell.
text.Rotherham: And, if, thy right hand, is causing thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from thee,—for it profiteth thee, that, one of thy members, should perish, and not, thy whole body, into gehenna, depart.
text.KJV: And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee. For it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
text.NABRE: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one of your members than to have your whole body go into Gehenna.
text.DRB: And if thy right eye scandalize thee, pluck it out, & cast it from thee. For it is expedient for thee that one of thy limbs perish, rather than thy whole body be cast into hell.

text.NRSV: “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.’
text.Rotherham: It was said, moreover, Whosoever shall divorce his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement;
text.KJV: It hath beene said, Whosoeuer shall put away his wife, let him giue her a writing of diuorcement.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Divorce. “It was also said, ‘Whoever divorces his wife must give her a bill of divorce.’
