text.DRB: And if thy right hand scandalize thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is expedient for thee that one of thy limbs perish rather than that thy whole body go into Hell.

text.NRSV: But I say to you that anyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of unchastity, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, that, Everyone who divorceth his wife—saving for unfaithfulness, causeth her to be made an adulteress,—[and, whosoever shall marry a divorced woman, committeth adultery].
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, that whosoeuer shall put away his wife, sauing for the cause of fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoeuer shall marie her that is diuorced, committeth adulterie.
text.NABRE: But I say to you, whoever divorces his wife (unless the marriage is unlawful) causes her to commit adultery, and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
text.DRB: It was said also, whosoever shall dismiss his wife, let him give her a bill of divorcement.

text.NRSV: “Again, you have heard that it was said to those of ancient times, ‘You shall not swear falsely, but carry out the vows you have made to the Lord.’
text.Rotherham: Again, ye have heard that it was said, to them of olden time, Thou shalt not swear falsely,—but shalt render unto the Lord, thine oaths.
text.KJV: ¶ Againe, yee haue heard that it hath beene said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forsweare thy selfe, but shalt performe vnto the Lord thine othes.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Oaths. “Again you have heard that it was said to your ancestors, ‘Do not take a false oath, but make good to the Lord all that you vow.’
text.DRB: But I say to you, whosoever shall dismiss his wife, excepting the cause of fornication, maketh her to commit adultery: And he that shall marry her that is dismissed; committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Do not swear at all, either by heaven, for it is the throne of God,
text.Rotherham: But, I, tell you—not to swear, at all: Neither by heaven, because it is the, throne of God,—
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, Sweare not at all, neither by heauen, for it is Gods throne:
text.NABRE: But I say to you, do not swear at all; not by heaven, for it is God’s throne;
text.DRB: Again you have heard that it was said to them of old, Thou shalt not commit perjury: but thou shall perform thy oaths to our Lord.

text.NRSV: or by the earth, for it is his footstool, or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
text.Rotherham: Nor by the earth, because it is his, footstool; nor by Jerusalem, because, it is the, city, of the Great King;
text.KJV: Nor by the earth, for it is his footstoole: neither by Hierusalem, for it is the citie of the great king.
text.NABRE: nor by the earth, for it is his footstool; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
text.DRB: But I say to you not to swear at all: neither by heaven, because it is the throne of God: neither by the earth, because it is the foot-stool of his feet: neither by Jerusalem, because it is the city of the great King.

text.NRSV: And do not swear by your head, for you cannot make one hair white or black.
text.Rotherham: Nor, by thine own head, mayest thou swear, because thou art not able to make, one hair, white or black.
text.KJV: Neither shalt thou sweare by thy head, because thou canst not make one haire white or blacke.
text.NABRE: Do not swear by your head, for you cannot make a single hair white or black.
text.DRB: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.

text.NRSV: Let your word be ‘Yes, Yes’ or ‘No, No’; anything more than this comes from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: But let your word be, Yea, yea,—Nay, nay; and, what goeth beyond these, is, of evil.
text.KJV: But let your communication bee Yea, yea: Nay, nay: For whatsoeuer is more then these, commeth of euill.
text.NABRE: Let your ‘Yes’ mean ‘Yes,’ and your ‘No’ mean ‘No.’ Anything more is from the evil one.
text.DRB: Let your talk be yea, yea: no, no: and that which is over & above these, is of evil.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said,—Eye for eye, and tooth for tooth.
text.KJV: ¶ Yee haue heard that it hath beene said, An eie for an eie, and a tooth for a tooth.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Retaliation. “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.’
text.DRB: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Do not resist an evildoer. But if anyone strikes you on the right cheek, turn the other also;
text.Rotherham: But, I, tell you, not to resist evil,—Nay, whoever is smiting thee on the right cheek, turn to him, the other also;
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, that yee resist not euill: but whosoeuer shall smite thee on thy right cheeke, turne to him the other also.
text.NABRE: But I say to you, offer no resistance to one who is evil. When someone strikes you on [your] right cheek, turn the other one to him as well.
text.DRB: But I say to you not to resist evil: but if one strike thee on thy right cheek, turn to him also the other:

text.NRSV: and if anyone wants to sue you and take your coat, give your cloak as well;
text.Rotherham: And, him who is desiring thee to be judged, and to take, thy tunic, let him have, thy mantle also.
text.KJV: And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coate, let him haue thy cloake also.
text.NABRE: If anyone wants to go to law with you over your tunic, hand him your cloak as well.
text.DRB: and to him that will contend with thee in judgement, and take away thy coat, let go thy cloak also unto him.

text.NRSV: and if anyone forces you to go one mile, go also the second mile.
text.Rotherham: And, whoever shall impress thee one mile, go with him two:
text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall compell thee to goe a mile, goe with him twaine.
text.NABRE: Should anyone press you into service for one mile, go with him for two miles.
text.DRB: and whosoever will force thee one mile, go with him other twain.

text.NRSV: Give to everyone who begs from you, and do not refuse anyone who wants to borrow from you.
text.Rotherham: To him who is asking thee, give; and, him who is desiring from thee to borrow, do not thou turn away.
text.KJV: Giue to him that asketh thee: and from him that would borrow of thee, turne not thou away.
text.NABRE: Give to the one who asks of you, and do not turn your back on one who wants to borrow. Love of Enemies.
text.DRB: He that asketh of thee, give to him: and to him that would borrow of thee, turn not away.

text.NRSV: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
text.Rotherham: Ye have heard, that it was said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.
text.KJV: ¶ Yee haue heard, that it hath beene said, Thou shalt loue thy neighbour, and hate thine enemie:
text.NABRE: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
text.DRB: You have heard that it was said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, & hate thine enemy.

text.NRSV: But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
text.Rotherham: But, I, say unto you, Be loving your enemies, and praying for them who are persecuting you:
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, Loue your enemies, blesse them that curse you, doe good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully vse you, and persecute you:
text.NABRE: But I say to you, love your enemies, and pray for those who persecute you,
text.DRB: But I say to you, love your enemies, do good to them that hate you: and pray for them that persecute and abuse you:

text.NRSV: so that you may be children of your Father in heaven; for he makes his sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the righteous and on the unrighteous.
text.Rotherham: That ye may become sons of your Father who is in the heavens: because, his sun, he maketh arise on evil and good, and sendeth rain, on just and unjust.
text.KJV: That yee may be the children of your father which is in heauen: for he maketh his sunne to rise on the euill and on the good, and sendeth raine on the iust, and on the vniust.
text.NABRE: that you may be children of your heavenly Father, for he makes his sun rise on the bad and the good, and causes rain to fall on the just and the unjust.
text.DRB: that you may be the children of your father which is in heaven, who maketh his sun to rise upon good & bad, and raineth upon just and unjust.

text.NRSV: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
text.Rotherham: For, if ye love them that love you, what reward have ye? are not, even the tax-collectors, the same thing, doing?
text.KJV: For if yee loue them which loue you, what reward haue yee? Doe not euen the Publicanes the same?
text.NABRE: For if you love those who love you, what recompense will you have? Do not the tax collectors do the same?
text.DRB: For if you love them that love you, what reward shall you have? Do not also the Publicans this?

text.NRSV: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing than others? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?
text.Rotherham: And, if ye salute your brethren only, what, more than common, are ye doing? are not, even the nations, the same thing, doing?
text.KJV: And if yee salute your brethren only, what do you more then others? Doe not euen the Publicanes so?
text.NABRE: And if you greet your brothers only, what is unusual about that? Do not the pagans do the same?
text.DRB: And if you salute your brethren only, what do you more, do not also the Heathen this?

text.NRSV: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, shall become, perfect: as, your heavenly Father, is perfect.
text.KJV: Be yee therefore perfect, euen as your father, which is in heauen, is perfect.
text.NABRE: So be perfect, just as your heavenly Father is perfect.
text.DRB: Be you perfect therefore, as also your heavenly Father is perfect.

text.NRSV: “Beware of practicing your piety before others in order to be seen by them; for then you have no reward from your Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: [But] take heed, that ye do not, your righteousness, before men, to be gazed at by them,—otherwise at least, reward, have ye none, with your Father who is in the heavens.
text.KJV: Take heed that yee doe not your almes before men, to bee seene of them: otherwise yee haue no reward of your father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Teaching About Almsgiving. “[But] take care not to perform righteous deeds in order that people may see them; otherwise, you will have no recompense from your heavenly Father.
text.DRB: Take good heed that you do not your justice before men, to be seen of them: otherwise reward you shall not have with your father which is in heaven.

text.NRSV: “So whenever you give alms, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may be praised by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, thou mayest be doing an alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, just as, the hypocrites, do in the synagogues and in the streets—that they may be glorified by men,—Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.
text.KJV: Therefore, when thou doest thine almes, doe not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites doe, in the Synagogues, and in the streetes, that they may haue glory of men. Uerily, I say vnto you, they haue their reward.
text.NABRE: When you give alms, do not blow a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets to win the praise of others. Amen, I say to you, they have received their reward.
text.DRB: Therefore when thou doest an alms-deed, sound not a trumpet before thee, as the Hypocrites do in the Synagogues and in the streets, that they may be honoured of men: Amen I say to you, they have received their reward.

text.NRSV: But when you give alms, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
text.Rotherham: But, thou, when doing an alms, let not, thy left hand, know what thy right hand is doing;
text.KJV: But when thou doest almes, let not thy left hand know, what thy right doeth:
text.NABRE: But when you give alms, do not let your left hand know what your right is doing,
text.DRB: But when thou doest an alms-deed, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:

text.NRSV: so that your alms may be done in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: that thine alms may be in secret,—and, thy Father, who seeth in secret, will give it back to thee.
text.KJV: That thine almes may be in secret: And thy father which seeth in secret, himselfe shall reward thee openly.
text.NABRE: so that your almsgiving may be secret. And your Father who sees in secret will repay you.
text.DRB: that thy alms-deed may be in secret, and thy father which seeth in secret, will repay thee.

text.NRSV: “And whenever you pray, do not be like the hypocrites; for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and at the street corners, so that they may be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye may be praying, ye shall not be as the hypocrites, because they love, in the synagogues, and at the corners of the broad ways, to take their stand and pray, that they may shine before men; Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.
text.KJV: ¶ And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are: for they loue to pray standing in the Synagogues, and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seene of men. Uerily I say vnto you, they haue their reward.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Prayer. “When you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, who love to stand and pray in the synagogues and on street corners so that others may see them. Amen, I say to you, they have received their reward.
text.DRB: And when ye pray, you shall not be as the hypocrites, that love to stand and pray in the Synagogues & corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men: Amen I say to you, they have received their reward.

text.NRSV: But whenever you pray, go into your room and shut the door and pray to your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, when thou wouldest pray, enter into thy closet, and, fastening thy door, pray unto thy Father who is in secret,—and, thy Father who seeth in secret, will reward thee.
text.KJV: But thou when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy doore, pray to thy father which is in secret, and thy father which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
text.NABRE: But when you pray, go to your inner room, close the door, and pray to your Father in secret. And your Father who sees in secret will repay you.
text.DRB: But thou when thou shalt pray, enter into thy chamber, and having shut the door, pray to thy father in secret: and thy father which seeth in secret, will repay thee.

text.NRSV: “When you are praying, do not heap up empty phrases as the Gentiles do; for they think that they will be heard because of their many words.
text.Rotherham: And, being at prayer, use not vain repetitions, just like the nations,—for they think, that, in their much speaking, they shall be hoard;
text.KJV: But when yee pray, vse not vaine repetitions, as the heathen doe. For they thinke that they shall be heard for their much speaking.
text.NABRE: In praying, do not babble like the pagans, who think that they will be heard because of their many words.
text.DRB: And when you are praying, speak not much, as the Heathen. For they think that in their much-speaking they may be heard.

text.NRSV: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
text.Rotherham: do not, therefore, make yourselves like them, for [God] your Father knoweth of what things ye have, need, before ye ask him.
text.KJV: Be not yee therefore like vnto them: For your father knoweth what things yee haue neede of, before yee aske him.
text.NABRE: Do not be like them. Your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
text.DRB: Be not you therefore like to them, for your father knoweth what is needful for you, before you ask him.

text.NRSV: “Pray then in this way: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name.
text.Rotherham: Thus, therefore pray, ye: Our Father, who art in the heavens,—Hallowed be thy name,
text.KJV: After this maner therefore pray yee: Our father which art in heauen, hallowed be thy name.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Prayer. “This is how you are to pray: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name,
text.DRB: Thus therefore shall you pray. Our Father which art in heaven, sanctified be thy name.

text.NRSV: Your kingdom come. Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Come may thy kingdom,—Accomplished be thy will, as in heaven, also on earth:
text.KJV: Thy kingdome come. Thy will be done, in earth, as it is in heauen.
text.NABRE: your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as in heaven.
text.DRB: Let thy Kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, in earth also.

text.NRSV: Give us this day our daily bread.
text.Rotherham: Our needful bread, give us, this day;
text.KJV: Giue vs this day our daily bread.
text.NABRE: Give us today our daily bread;
text.DRB: Give us today our supersubstantial bread.

text.NRSV: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
text.Rotherham: And forgive us our debts, as, we also, have forgiven our debtors;
text.KJV: And forgiue vs our debts, as we forgiue our debters.
text.NABRE: and forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors;
text.DRB: And forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors,

text.NRSV: And do not bring us to the time of trial, but rescue us from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: And bring us not into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
text.KJV: And lead vs not into temptation, but deliuer vs from euill: For thine is the kingdome, and the power, and the glory, for euer, Amen.
text.NABRE: and do not subject us to the final test, but deliver us from the evil one.
text.DRB: And lead us not into tentation. But deliver us from evil. Amen.

text.NRSV: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you;
text.Rotherham: For, if ye forgive men their faults, Your Father who is in the heavens, will forgive, even you;
text.KJV: For, if yee forgiue men their trespasses, your heauenly father will also forgiue you.
text.NABRE: If you forgive others their transgressions, your heavenly Father will forgive you.
text.DRB: For if you will forgive men their offences, your heavenly father will forgive you also your offences.

text.NRSV: but if you do not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
text.Rotherham: But, if ye forgive not men [their faults], neither will your Father forgive, your faults.
text.KJV: But, if yee forgiue not men their trespasses, neither will your father forgiue your trespasses.
text.NABRE: But if you do not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your transgressions.
text.DRB: But if you will not forgive men, neither will your father forgive you your offences.

text.NRSV: “And whenever you fast, do not look dismal, like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces so as to show others that they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye may he fasting, become not ye, as the hypocrites, of sullen countenance,—for they darken their looks, that they may appear, unto men, to be fasting: Verily, I say unto you, they are getting back their reward.
text.KJV: ¶ Moreouer, when yee fast, be not as the Hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appeare vnto men to fast: Uerily I say vnto you, they haue their reward.
text.NABRE: Teaching About Fasting. “When you fast, do not look gloomy like the hypocrites. They neglect their appearance, so that they may appear to others to be fasting. Amen, I say to you, they have received their reward.
text.DRB: And when you fast, be not as the hypocrites, sad. For they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Amen I say to you, that they have received their reward.

text.NRSV: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
text.Rotherham: But, when, thou, art fasting, anoint thy head, and, thy face, wash,—
text.KJV: But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face:
text.NABRE: But when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,
text.DRB: But thou when thou doest fast, anoint thy head, and wash thy face:

text.NRSV: so that your fasting may be seen not by others but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
text.Rotherham: that thou do not appear, unto men, to be fasting, but to thy Father who is in secret,—and, thy Father who seeth in secret, will reward thee.
text.KJV: That thou appeare not vnto men to fast, but vnto thy father which is in secret: and thy father which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
text.NABRE: so that you may not appear to others to be fasting, except to your Father who is hidden. And your Father who sees what is hidden will repay you.
text.DRB: that thou appear not to men to fast, but to thy father which is in secret: and thy father which seeth in secret, will repay thee.

text.NRSV: “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust consume and where thieves break in and steal;
text.Rotherham: Be not laying up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where, moth and rust, do tarnish, and where, thieves, dig through and steal;
text.KJV: ¶ Lay not vp for your selues treasures vpon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where theeues breake thorow, and steale.
text.NABRE: Treasure in Heaven. “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and decay destroy, and thieves break in and steal.
text.DRB: Heap not up to yourselves treasures on the earth: where the rust and moth do corrupt, & where thieves dig through and steal.

text.NRSV: but store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consumes and where thieves do not break in and steal.
text.Rotherham: but be laying up or yourselves treasures in heaven, where, neither moth nor rust, doth tarnish, and where, thieves, dig not through nor steal:
text.KJV: But lay vp for your selues treasures in heauen, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, & where theeues doe not breake thorow, nor steale.
text.NABRE: But store up treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor decay destroys, nor thieves break in and steal.
text.DRB: But heap up to yourselves treasures in heaven: where neither the rust nor moth doth corrupt, and where thieves do not dig through nor steal.

text.NRSV: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
text.Rotherham: for, where, thy treasure, is, there, will be, thy heart [also].
text.KJV: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
text.NABRE: For where your treasure is, there also will your heart be. The Light of the Body.
text.DRB: For where thy treasure is, there is thy heart also.

text.NRSV: “The eye is the lamp of the body. So, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light;
text.Rotherham: The lamp of the body, is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body, shall be, lighted up;
text.KJV: The light of the body is the eye: If therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shalbe full of light.
text.NABRE: “The lamp of the body is the eye. If your eye is sound, your whole body will be filled with light;
text.DRB: The candle of thy body is thine eye. If thine eye be simple, thy whole body shall be lightsome.

text.NRSV: but if your eye is unhealthy, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!
text.Rotherham: But, if thine eye be, evil, thy whole body, shall be, in the dark;—if therefore, the light which is in thee, is, darkness, the darkness, how great!
text.KJV: But if thine eye be euill, thy whole body shall be full of darknesse. If therfore the light that is in thee be darkenesse, how great is that darkenesse?
text.NABRE: but if your eye is bad, your whole body will be in darkness. And if the light in you is darkness, how great will the darkness be.
text.DRB: But if thine eye be naught: thy whole body shall be darksome. If then the light that is in thee, be darkness: the darkness itself how great shall it be?

text.NRSV: “No one can serve two masters; for a slave will either hate the one and love the other, or be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
text.Rotherham: No one, unto two masters, can be in service; for either, the one, he will hate, and, the other ,love, or, one, he will hold to, and, the other, despise: Ye cannot be in service, unto God, and, unto Riches.
text.KJV: ¶ No man can serue two masters: for either he will hate the one and loue the other, or else hee will holde to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serue God and Mammon.
text.NABRE: God and Money. “No one can serve two masters. He will either hate one and love the other, or be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon. Dependence on God.
text.DRB: No man can serve two masters. For either he will hate the one, and love the other: or he will sustain the one, and contemn the other. You cannot serve God and Mammon.

text.NRSV: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing?
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I say unto you: Be not anxious for your life, what ye shall eat [or what ye shall drink],—or for your body, what ye shall put on: Is not, the life, more than, the food? And, the body, than, the raiment?
text.KJV: Therfore I say vnto you, Take no thought for your life, what yee shall eate, or what ye shall drinke, nor yet for your body, what yee shall put on: Is not the life more then meate? and the body then raiment?
text.NABRE: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat [or drink], or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food and the body more than clothing?
text.DRB: Therefore I say to you, be not careful for your life, what you shall eat, neither for your body what raiment you shall put on. Is not the life more than the meat: and the body more than the raiment?

text.NRSV: Look at the birds of the air; they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
text.Rotherham: Observe intently, the birds of the heaven,—that they neither sow, nor reap, nor gather into barns, and yet, your heavenly Father, feedeth, them: Are no, ye, much better than, they?
text.KJV: Behold the foules of the aire: for they sow not, neither do they reape, nor gather into barnes, yet your heauenly father feedeth them. Are yee not much better then they?
text.NABRE: Look at the birds in the sky; they do not sow or reap, they gather nothing into barns, yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are not you more important than they?
text.DRB: Behold the fowls of the air, that they sow not, neither reap, nor gather into barns: and your heavenly father feedeth them.

text.NRSV: And can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your span of life?
text.Rotherham: But who from among you, being anxious, can add to his stature one cubit?
text.KJV: Which of you by taking thought, can adde one cubite vnto his stature?
text.NABRE: Can any of you by worrying add a single moment to your life-span?
text.DRB: And which of you by caring, can add to his stature one cubit?

text.NRSV: And why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they neither toil nor spin,
text.Rotherham: And, about clothing, why are ye anxious? Consider well the lilies of the field, how they grow,—they toil not neither do they spin;
text.KJV: And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the lillies of the field, how they grow: they toile not, neither doe they spinne.
text.NABRE: Why are you anxious about clothes? Learn from the way the wild flowers grow. They do not work or spin.
text.DRB: And for raiment why are you careful? Consider the lilies of the field how they grow: they labour not, neither do they spin.

text.NRSV: yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not clothed like one of these.
text.Rotherham: And yet, I say unto you, not even Solomon, in all his glory, was arrayed like, one of these!
text.KJV: And yet I say vnto you, that euen Solomon in all his glory, was not arayed like one of these.
text.NABRE: But I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was clothed like one of them.
text.DRB: But I say to you, that neither Salomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these.

text.NRSV: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is alive today and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith?
text.Rotherham: Now, if the grass of the field—which to-day, is, and, to-morrow, into an oven, is cast—God thus adorneth, not much rather, you, little of faith?
text.KJV: Wherefore, if God so clothe the grasse of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the ouen: shall he not much more clothe you, O yee of little faith?
text.NABRE: If God so clothes the grass of the field, which grows today and is thrown into the oven tomorrow, will he not much more provide for you, O you of little faith?
text.DRB: And if the grass of the field, which today is, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, God doth so clothe: how much more you, O ye of very small faith?

text.NRSV: Therefore do not worry, saying, ‘What will we eat?’ or ‘What will we drink?’ or ‘What will we wear?’
text.Rotherham: Do not then be anxious saying, What shall we eat? or What shall we drink? or Wherewithal shall we be arrayed?
text.KJV: Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eate? or, what shall we drinke? or wherewithall shall wee be clothed?
text.NABRE: So do not worry and say, ‘What are we to eat?’ or ‘What are we to drink?’ or ‘What are we to wear?’
text.DRB: Be not careful therefore, saying, what shall we eat, or what shall we drink, or wherewith shall we be covered?

text.NRSV: For it is the Gentiles who strive for all these things; and indeed your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.
text.Rotherham: For, all these things, the nations, seek after,—for your heavenly Father, knoweth, that ye are needing, all these things.
text.KJV: (For after all these things doe the Gentiles seeke:) for your heauenly father knoweth that ye haue neede of all these things.
text.NABRE: All these things the pagans seek. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
text.DRB: for all these things the Heathen do seek after. For your father knoweth that you need all these things.

text.NRSV: But strive first for the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
text.Rotherham: But be seeking first, the kingdom and its righteousness,—and, all these things, shall be added unto you.
text.KJV: But seeke ye first the kingdome of God, and his righteousnesse, and all these things shalbe added vnto you.
text.NABRE: But seek first the kingdom [of God] and his righteousness, and all these things will be given you besides.
text.DRB: Seek therefore first the Kingdom of God, and the justice of him, and all these things shall be given you besides.

text.NRSV: “So do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will bring worries of its own. Today’s trouble is enough for today.
text.Rotherham: Do not, then, be anxious for the morrow; for the morrow, will be anxious, for itself: Sufficient for the day, is the evil thereof.
text.KJV: Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of it selfe: sufficient vnto the day is the euill thereof.
text.NABRE: Do not worry about tomorrow; tomorrow will take care of itself. Sufficient for a day is its own evil.
text.DRB: Be not careful therefore for the morrow; for the morrow day will be careful for itself. Sufficient for the day is the evil thereof.

text.NRSV: “Do not judge, so that you may not be judged.
text.Rotherham: Judge not, that ye be not judged;—
text.KJV: Iudge not, that ye be not iudged.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Judging Others. “Stop judging, that you may not be judged.
text.DRB: Judge not, that you be not judged.

text.NRSV: For with the judgment you make you will be judged, and the measure you give will be the measure you get.
text.Rotherham: For, with what judgment ye judge, shall ye be judged,—and, with what measure ye mete, shall it be measured unto you.
text.KJV: For with what iudgment ye iudge, yee shall be iudged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you againe.
text.NABRE: For as you judge, so will you be judged, and the measure with which you measure will be measured out to you.
text.DRB: For in what judgement you judge, you shall be judged: and in what measure you mete, it shall be measured to you again.

text.NRSV: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: Why, moreover, beholdest thou the mote, in the eye of thy brother,—while, the beam in thine own eye, thou dost not consider?
text.KJV: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brothers eye, but considerest not the beame that is in thine owne eye?
text.NABRE: Why do you notice the splinter in your brother’s eye, but do not perceive the wooden beam in your own eye?
text.DRB: And why seest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye: and the beam that is in thine own eye thou seest not?

text.NRSV: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while the log is in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: Or how wilt thou say unto thy brother, Let me cast the mote out of thine eye,—when lo! a beam, is in thine own eye?
text.KJV: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let mee pull out the mote out of thine eye, and beholde, a beame is in thine owne eye?
text.NABRE: How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me remove that splinter from your eye,’ while the wooden beam is in your eye?
text.DRB: Or how sayest thou to thy brother: Let me cast out the mote of thine eye; and behold a beam is in thine own eye?

text.NRSV: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
text.Rotherham: Hypocrite! cast first, out of thine own eye, the beam,—and, then, shalt thou see clearly to cast the mote, out of the eye of thy brother.
text.KJV: Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beame out of thine owne eye: and then shalt thou see clearely to cast out the mote out of thy brothers eye.
text.NABRE: You hypocrite, remove the wooden beam from your eye first; then you will see clearly to remove the splinter from your brother’s eye.
text.DRB: Hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see to cast out the mote out of thy brother's eye.

text.NRSV: “Do not give what is holy to dogs; and do not throw your pearls before swine, or they will trample them under foot and turn and maul you.
text.Rotherham: Do not give what is holy unto dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine,—lest once they trample, them, down with their feet, and, turning, tear, you.
text.KJV: ¶ Giue not that which is holy vnto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearles before swine: lest they trample them vnder their feete, and turne againe and rent you.
text.NABRE: Pearls Before Swine. “Do not give what is holy to dogs, or throw your pearls before swine, lest they trample them underfoot, and turn and tear you to pieces.
text.DRB: Give not that which is holy to dogs: neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest perhaps they tread them with their feet, & turning, all to tear you.

text.NRSV: “Ask, and it will be given you; search, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text.Rotherham: Be asking, and it shall be given you, Be seeking, and ye shall find,—Be knocking, and it shall be opened unto you.
text.KJV: ¶ Aske, and it shalbe giuen you: seeke, and ye shall finde: knocke, and it shalbe opened vnto you.
text.NABRE: The Answer to Prayers. “Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
text.DRB: Ask, and it shall be given you: seek, and you shall find, knock, & it shall be opened to you.

text.NRSV: For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who searches finds, and for everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever asketh, receiveth, and, he that seeketh, findeth,—and, to him that knocketh, shall it be opened.
text.KJV: For euery one that asketh, receiueth: and he that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shalbe opened.
text.NABRE: For everyone who asks, receives; and the one who seeks, finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
text.DRB: For every one that asketh, receiveth: and that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.

text.NRSV: Is there anyone among you who, if your child asks for bread, will give a stone?
text.Rotherham: Or what man, from among yourselves, whom his son shall ask for a loaf,—a stone, will give him?
text.KJV: Or what man is there of you, whom if his sonne aske bread, will hee giue him a stone?
text.NABRE: Which one of you would hand his son a stone when he asks for a loaf of bread,
text.DRB: Or what man is there of you, whom if his child shall ask bread, will he reach him a stone?

text.NRSV: Or if the child asks for a fish, will give a snake?
text.Rotherham: Or, a fish also, shall ask,—a serpent, will give him?
text.KJV: Or if he aske a fish, will hee giue him a serpent?
text.NABRE: or a snake when he asks for a fish?
text.DRB: Or if he shall ask him fish, will he reach him a serpent?

text.NRSV: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
text.Rotherham: If then, ye, being, evil, know how, good gifts, to be giving unto your children, how much more, will, your Father who is in the heavens, give good things to them that ask, him?
text.KJV: If ye then being euill, know how to giue good giftes vnto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heauen, giue good things to them that aske him?
text.NABRE: If you then, who are wicked, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
text.DRB: If you then being naught, know how to give good gifts to your children: how much more will your Father which is in Heaven, give good things to them that ask him?

text.NRSV: “In everything do to others as you would have them do to you; for this is the law and the prophets.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever things, therefore, ye would, that men be doing unto you, so, be, ye also, doing, unto them,—for, this, is the law and the prophets.
text.KJV: Therefore all things whatsoeuer ye would that men should doe to you, doe ye euen so to them: for this is the Law and the Prophets.
text.NABRE: The Golden Rule. “Do to others whatever you would have them do to you. This is the law and the prophets.
text.DRB: All things therefore whatsoever you will that men do to you, do you also to them. For this is the Law and the Prophets.

text.NRSV: “Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the road is easy that leads to destruction, and there are many who take it.
text.Rotherham: Enter ye in at, the narrow gate; because broad and roomy is the way that leadeth unto destruction,—and, many, are they who enter thereby:
text.KJV: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate, for wide is the gate, and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which goe in thereat:
text.NABRE: The Narrow Gate. “Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the road broad that leads to destruction, and those who enter through it are many.
text.DRB: Enter ye by the narrow gate: because broad is the gate, and large is the way that leadeth to perdition, and many there be that enter by it.

text.NRSV: For the gate is narrow and the road is hard that leads to life, and there are few who find it.
text.Rotherham: Because, narrow, is the gate, and, confined, the way, that leadeth unto life,—and, few, are they who find, it.
text.KJV: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way which leadeth vnto life, and few there be that finde it.
text.NABRE: How narrow the gate and constricted the road that leads to life. And those who find it are few. False Prophets.
text.DRB: How narrow is the gate, and strait is the way that leadeth to life: & few there are that find it!

text.NRSV: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
text.Rotherham: Beware of false prophets, who come unto you in clothing of sheep,—while, within, they are ravening wolves.
text.KJV: ¶ Beware of false prophets which come to you in sheepes clothing, but inwardly they are rauening wolues.
text.NABRE: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but underneath are ravenous wolves.
text.DRB: Take ye great heed of false Prophets, which come to you in the clothing of sheep, but inwardly are ravening wolves.

text.NRSV: You will know them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thorns, or figs from thistles?
text.Rotherham: By their fruits, shall ye find, them, out,—unless perhaps men gather—from thorns, grapes! or, from thistles, figs!
text.KJV: Yee shall knowe them by their fruits: Doe men gather grapes of thornes, or figges of thistles?
text.NABRE: By their fruits you will know them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
text.DRB: By their fruits you shall know them. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?

text.NRSV: In the same way, every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
text.Rotherham: So, every good tree, fine fruit, produceth,—whereas, the worthless tree, evil fruit, produceth:
text.KJV: Euen so, euery good tree bringeth forth good fruit: but a corrupt tree bringeth forth euill fruit.
text.NABRE: Just so, every good tree bears good fruit, and a rotten tree bears bad fruit.
text.DRB: Even so every good tree yieldeth good fruits, and the evil tree yieldeth evil fruits.

text.NRSV: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
text.Rotherham: It is, impossible, for a, good tree, to be bearing, evil fruit, neither doth, a worthless tree, produce, fine fruit.
text.KJV: A good tree cannot bring forth euil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
text.NABRE: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a rotten tree bear good fruit.
text.DRB: Agood tree cannot yield evil fruits, neither an evil tree yield good fruits.

text.NRSV: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.Rotherham: Every tree that beareth not fine fruit, is hewn down, and, into fire, is cast.
text.KJV: Euery tree that bringeth not forth good fruit, is hewen downe, and cast into the fire.
text.NABRE: Every tree that does not bear good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
text.DRB: Every tree that yieldeth not good fruit, shall be cut down, and shall be cast into fire.

text.NRSV: Thus you will know them by their fruits.
text.Rotherham: After all then, by their fruits, shall ye find, them, out.
text.KJV: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
text.NABRE: So by their fruits you will know them.
text.DRB: Therefore by their fruits you shall know them.

text.NRSV: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord! Lord! shall enter into the kingdom of the heavens,—but he that doeth the will of my Father who is in the heavens.
text.KJV: ¶ Not euery one that saith vnto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdome of heauen: but he that doth the will of my father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: The True Disciple. “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father in heaven.
text.DRB: Not every one that sayeth to me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the Kingdom of Heaven: but he that doth the will of my Father which is in Heaven, he shall enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: On that day many will say to me, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many deeds of power in your name?’
text.Rotherham: Many, will say unto me, in, that, day, Lord! Lord! did we not, in thy name, prophesy, and, in thy name, cast, demons, out,—and, in thy name, many works of power, perform?
text.KJV: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, haue we not prophecied in thy name? and in thy name haue cast out deuils? and in thy name done many wonderfull works?
text.NABRE: Many will say to me on that day, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name? Did we not drive out demons in your name? Did we not do mighty deeds in your name?’
text.DRB: Many shall say to me in that day: Lord, Lord, have not we prophesied in thy name, and in thy name cast out devils, and in thy name wrought many miracles?

text.NRSV: Then I will declare to them, ‘I never knew you; go away from me, you evildoers.’
text.Rotherham: And, then, will I confess unto them, Never, have I acknowledged you,—Depart from me, ye workers of lawlessness!
text.KJV: And then wil I professe vnto them, I neuer knew you: Depart from me, ye that worke iniquity.
text.NABRE: Then I will declare to them solemnly, ‘I never knew you. Depart from me, you evildoers.’
text.DRB: And then I will confess unto them, That I never knew you: depart from me you that work iniquity.

text.NRSV: “Everyone then who hears these words of mine and acts on them will be like a wise man who built his house on rock.
text.Rotherham: Every one, therefore, who heareth [these] my words, and doeth them, shall be likened to a prudent man, who built his house upon the rock;
text.KJV: ¶ Therefore, whosoeuer heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I wil liken him vnto a wise man, which built his house vpon a rocke:
text.NABRE: The Two Foundations. “Everyone who listens to these words of mine and acts on them will be like a wise man who built his house on rock.
text.DRB: Every one therefore that heareth these words, and doeth them, shall be likened to a wise man that built his house upon a rock,

text.NRSV: The rain fell, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on rock.
text.Rotherham: And the rain descended, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and rushed against that house, and it fell not; for it had been founded upon the rock.
text.KJV: And the raine descended, and the floods came, and the windes blew, and beat vpon that house: and it fell not, for it was founded vpon a rocke.
text.NABRE: The rain fell, the floods came, and the winds blew and buffeted the house. But it did not collapse; it had been set solidly on rock.
text.DRB: and the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat against that house, and it fell not, for it was founded upon a rock.

text.NRSV: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not act on them will be like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
text.Rotherham: And every one who heareth these my words, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand;
text.KJV: And euery one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall bee likened vnto a foolish man, which built his house vpon the sand:
text.NABRE: And everyone who listens to these words of mine but does not act on them will be like a fool who built his house on sand.
text.DRB: And every one that heareth these my words, and doeth them not, shall be like a foolish man that built his house upon the sand.

text.NRSV: The rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell—and great was its fall!”
text.Rotherham: And the rain descended, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and dashed against that house, and it fell; and, the fall thereof, was, great.
text.KJV: And the raine descended, and the floods came, and the windes blew, and beat vpon that house, and it fell, and great was the fall of it.
text.NABRE: The rain fell, the floods came, and the winds blew and buffeted the house. And it collapsed and was completely ruined.”
text.DRB: and the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat against that house, and it fell, & the fall thereof was great.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were astounded at his teaching,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended these words, with astonishment were the multitudes being struck at his teaching;
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when Iesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine.
text.NABRE: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were astonished at his teaching,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when Jesus had fully ended these words, the multitude were in admiration upon his doctrine.

text.NRSV: for he taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
text.Rotherham: for he was teaching them as one having, authority, and not as their Scribes.
text.KJV: For he taught them as one hauing authoritie, and not as the Scribes.
text.NABRE: for he taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
text.DRB: For he was teaching them as having power, and not as their Scribes and Pharisees.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had come down from the mountain, great crowds followed him;
text.Rotherham: And when he came down from the mountain, there followed him large multitudes.
text.KJV: When he was come downe from the Mountaine, great multitudes folowed him.
text.NABRE: III. Ministry and Mission in Galilee Chapter 8 The Cleansing of a Leper. When Jesus came down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.
text.DRB: And when he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him:

text.NRSV: and there was a leper who came to him and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! a leper, coming near, began to bow down to him saying,—Lord! if thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me.
text.KJV: And behold, there came a leper, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, If thou wilt, thou canst make me cleane.
text.NABRE: And then a leper approached, did him homage, and said, “Lord, if you wish, you can make me clean.”
text.DRB: And behold a Leper came and adored him saying: Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

text.NRSV: He stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I do choose. Be made clean!” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
text.Rotherham: And, stretching forth the hand, he touched him, saying,—I am willing, be cleansed! And, straightway, cleansed was his leprosy.
text.KJV: And Iesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will, bee thou cleane. And immediately his leprosie was cleansed.
text.NABRE: He stretched out his hand, touched him, and said, “I will do it. Be made clean.” His leprosy was cleansed immediately.
text.DRB: And Jesus stretching forth his hand, touched him, saying: I will. Be thou made clean. And forthwith, his leprosy was made clean.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him, Mind! tell, no one,—but withdraw, thyself, shew to the priest, and offer the gift which Moses directed, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: And Iesus saith vnto him, See thou tell no man, but go thy way, shew thy selfe to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimonie vnto them.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you tell no one, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses prescribed; that will be proof for them.” The Healing of a Centurion’s Servant.
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to him: See thou tell nobody: but go, shew thyself to the Priest, and offer the gift which Moyses commanded for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: When he entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, appealing to him
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered into Capernaum, there came near unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
text.KJV: ¶ And when Iesus was entred into Capernaum, there came vnto him a Centurion, beseeching him,
text.NABRE: When he entered Capernaum, a centurion approached him and appealed to him,
text.DRB: And when he was entered into Capharnaum, there came to him a Centurion, beseeching him,

text.NRSV: and saying, “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible distress.”
text.Rotherham: and saying, Lord! my servant, is laid prostrate in the house, a paralytic, fearfully tortured.
text.KJV: And saying, Lord, my seruant lieth at home sicke of the palsie, grieuously tormented.
text.NABRE: saying, “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, suffering dreadfully.”
text.DRB: and saying: Lord my boy lieth at home sick of the palsy, and is sore tormented.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “I will come and cure him.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, I, will come and cure him.
text.KJV: And Iesus saith vnto him, I will come, and heale him.
text.NABRE: He said to him, “I will come and cure him.”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to him: I will come, and cure him.

text.NRSV: The centurion answered, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof; but only speak the word, and my servant will be healed.
text.Rotherham: But the centurion, answering, said, Lord! I am of no consideration, that, under my roof, thou shouldest enter,—but, only say with a word, and healed shall be my servant.
text.KJV: The Centurion answered, and said, Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest come vnder my roofe: but speake the word onely, and my seruant shalbe healed.
text.NABRE: The centurion said in reply, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you enter under my roof; only say the word and my servant will be healed.
text.DRB: And the Centurion making answer, said: Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof: but only say the word, & my boy shall be healed.

text.NRSV: For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and the slave does it.”
text.Rotherham: For, I also, am a man [set] under authority, having under myself, soldiers,—and I say to this one, Go! and he goeth, and to another, Come! and he cometh, and, to my servant, Do this! and he doeth it.
text.KJV: For I am a man vnder authority, hauing souldiers vnder me: and I say to this man, Goe, and he goeth: and to another, Come, and he commeth: and to my seruant, Doe this, and he doth it.
text.NABRE: For I too am a person subject to authority, with soldiers subject to me. And I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come here,’ and he comes; and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
text.DRB: For I also am a man subject to authority, having under me soldiers; and I say to this go, and he goeth, and to another, come, and he cometh; and to my servant, do this, and he doeth it.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard him, he was amazed and said to those who followed him, “Truly I tell you, in no one in Israel have I found such faith.
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus, hearing, marveled, and said to them that followed,—Verily, I say unto you, With no one in Israel, such faith as this, have I found.
text.KJV: When Iesus heard it, he marueiled, and said to them that followed, Uerely, I say vnto you, I haue not found so great faith, no not in Israel.
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed and said to those following him, “Amen, I say to you, in no one in Israel have I found such faith.
text.DRB: And Jesus hearing this, marveled: and said to them that followed him: Amen I say to you, I have not found so great faith in Israel.

text.NRSV: I tell you, many will come from east and west and will eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you, Many from east and west, will have come, and shall recline with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, in the kingdom of the heavens;
text.KJV: And I say vnto you, that many shall come from the East and West, and shal sit downe with Abraham, and Isaac, & Iacob, in the kingdome of heauen:
text.NABRE: I say to you, many will come from the east and the west, and will recline with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob at the banquet in the kingdom of heaven,
text.DRB: And I say to you that many shall come from the East and West, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, & Jacob in the Kingdom of Heaven:

text.NRSV: while the heirs of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, the sons of the kingdom, shall be cast forth into the darkness outside,—There, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
text.KJV: But the children of the kingdome shall be cast out into outer darkenesse: there shalbe weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: but the children of the kingdom will be driven out into the outer darkness, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.”
text.DRB: but the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out into the exterior darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: And to the centurion Jesus said, “Go; let it be done for you according to your faith.” And the servant was healed in that hour.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Withdraw! and, as thou hast believed, be it done for thee. And healed was the servant in that hour.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto the Centurion, Go thy way, and as thou hast beleeued, so be it done vnto thee. And his seruant was healed in the self same houre.
text.NABRE: And Jesus said to the centurion, “You may go; as you have believed, let it be done for you.” And at that very hour [his] servant was healed. The Cure of Peter’s Mother-in-Law.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to the Centurion: Go, and as thou hast believed, be it done to thee. And the boy was healed in the same hour.

text.NRSV: When Jesus entered Peter’s house, he saw his mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever;
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, coming into the house of Peter, saw his mother-in-law laid prostrate and in a fever,
text.KJV: ¶ And when Iesus was come into Peters house, hee saw his wiues mother laid, and sicke of a feuer:
text.NABRE: Jesus entered the house of Peter, and saw his mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
text.DRB: And when Jesus was come into Peter's house, he saw his wife's mother laid, and in a fit of a fever:

text.NRSV: he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she got up and began to serve him.
text.Rotherham: and he touched her hand, and the fever left her,—and she arose, and began ministering unto him.
text.KJV: And he touched her hand, and the feuer left her: and she arose, and ministred vnto them.
text.NABRE: He touched her hand, the fever left her, and she rose and waited on him.
text.DRB: and he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she arose, and ministered to him.

text.NRSV: That evening they brought to him many who were possessed with demons; and he cast out the spirits with a word, and cured all who were sick.
text.Rotherham: But, when, evening, came, they brought unto him many demonized,—and he cast out the spirits with a word, and, all who were sick, he cured:
text.KJV: ¶ When the Euen was come, they brought vnto him many that were possessed with deuils: and hee cast out the spirits with his worde, and healed all that were sicke,
text.NABRE: Other Healings. When it was evening, they brought him many who were possessed by demons, and he drove out the spirits by a word and cured all the sick,
text.DRB: And when evening was come, they brought to him many that had devils: and he cast out the spirits with a word: and all that were ill at ease he cured:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah, “He took our infirmities and bore our diseases.”
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,—Himself, our weaknesses, took, and, diseases, bare.
text.KJV: That it might be fulfilled which which was spoken by Esaias the Prophet, saying, Himselfe tooke our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
text.NABRE: to fulfill what had been said by Isaiah the prophet: “He took away our infirmities and bore our diseases.” The Would-be Followers of Jesus.
text.DRB: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken, by Esay the Prophet, saying: He took our infirmities, and bare our diseases.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus saw great crowds around him, he gave orders to go over to the other side.
text.Rotherham: Now Jesus, seeing a multitude about him, gave orders to depart unto the other side.
text.KJV: ¶ Now when Iesus saw great multitudes about him, hee gaue commaundement to depart vnto the other side.
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw a crowd around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side.
text.DRB: And Jesus seeing great multitudes about him, commanded to go beyond the water.

text.NRSV: A scribe then approached and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
text.Rotherham: And a certain scribe, coming near, said unto him,—Teacher; I will follow thee, whithersoever Thou goest.
text.KJV: And a certaine Scribe came, and said vnto him, Master, I will follow thee whithersoeuer thou goest.
text.NABRE: A scribe approached and said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
text.DRB: And a certain Scribe came, and said to him: Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou shalt go.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him: The foxes, have, dens, and, the birds of the heavens, nests,—but, the Son of Man, hath not where, his head he may recline.
text.KJV: And Iesus saith vnto him, The Foxes haue holes, and the birds of the ayre haue nests: but the sonne of man hath not where to lay his head.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Foxes have dens and birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to rest his head.”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to him: The foxes have holes, and the fowls of the air nests: but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.

text.NRSV: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
text.Rotherham: And, another of the disciples, saith unto him,—Lord! suffer me, first, to depart, and bury my father.
text.KJV: And another of his Disciples said vnto him, Lord, suffer me first to goe, and bury my father.
text.NABRE: Another of [his] disciples said to him, “Lord, let me go first and bury my father.”
text.DRB: And another of his Disciples said to him: Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, saith unto him,—Be following me, and leave, the dead, to bury, their own dead.
text.KJV: But Iesus said vnto him, Follow me, & let the dead, bury their dead.
text.NABRE: But Jesus answered him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their dead.”
text.DRB: But Jesus said to him: Follow me, and let the dead bury the dead.

text.NRSV: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, when he, entered, into a boat, his disciples followed him.
text.KJV: ¶ And when he was entred into a ship, his Disciples followed him.
text.NABRE: The Calming of the Storm at Sea. He got into a boat and his disciples followed him.
text.DRB: And when he entered into the boat, his Disciples followed him:

text.NRSV: A windstorm arose on the sea, so great that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but he was asleep.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a great squall, arose in the sea, so that, the boat, was being covered, by the waves,—but, he, was sleeping.
text.KJV: And behold, there arose a great tempest in the Sea, insomuch that the ship was couered with the waues: but he was asleepe.
text.NABRE: Suddenly a violent storm came up on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by waves; but he was asleep.
text.DRB: and lo a great tempest arose in the sea, so that the boat was covered with waves, but he slept.

text.NRSV: And they went and woke him up, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, they aroused him, saying, Master! save, we perish!
text.KJV: And his Disciples came to him, and awoke, saying, Lord, saue vs: we perish.
text.NABRE: They came and woke him, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”
text.DRB: And they came to him, and raised him, saying: Lord, save us, we perish.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, you of little faith?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a dead calm.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them, Why, are ye, fearful, little of faith? Then, aroused, he rebuked the winds and the sea,—and it became a great calm.
text.KJV: And he saith vnto them, Why are yee fearefull, O yee of litle faith? Then hee arose, and rebuked the winds and the Sea, and there was a great calme.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Why are you terrified, O you of little faith?” Then he got up, rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was great calm.
text.DRB: And he saith to them: Why are you fearful, O ye of little faith? Then rising up he commanded the winds, and the sea, and there ensued a great calm.

text.NRSV: They were amazed, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?”
text.Rotherham: But, the men, marveled, saying, Whence is this one,—that, both the winds and the sea, unto him, give ear?
text.KJV: But the men marueiled, saying, What maner of man is this, that euen the winds and the Sea obey him?
text.NABRE: The men were amazed and said, “What sort of man is this, whom even the winds and the sea obey?”
text.DRB: Moreover the men marveled, saying: What an one is this, for the winds and the sea obey him.

text.NRSV: When he came to the other side, to the country of the Gadarenes, two demoniacs coming out of the tombs met him. They were so fierce that no one could pass that way.
text.Rotherham: And, when he, came, unto the other side, into the country of the Gadarenes, there met him, two men demonized, out of the tombs, coming forth,—fierce exceedingly, so that no one could pass that way;
text.KJV: ¶ And when hee was come to the other side, into the countrey of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with deuils, comming out of the tombes, exceeding fierce, so that no man might passe by that way.
text.NABRE: The Healing of the Gadarene Demoniacs. When he came to the other side, to the territory of the Gadarenes, two demoniacs who were coming from the tombs met him. They were so savage that no one could travel by that road.
text.DRB: And when he was come beyond the water, into the country of the Gerasens, there met him two that had devils, coming forth out of the sepulchres, exceeding fierce, so that none could pass by that way.

text.NRSV: Suddenly they shouted, “What have you to do with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
text.Rotherham: and lo! they cried aloud, saying, What have we in common with thee, O Son of God? Hast thou come hither, before the right time, to torment us?
text.KJV: And behold, they cryed out, saying, What haue we to doe with thee, Iesus thou sonne of God? Art thou come hither to torment vs befor þ time?
text.NABRE: They cried out, “What have you to do with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the appointed time?”
text.DRB: And behold they cried saying: What is between us & thee Jesus the Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time.

text.NRSV: Now a large herd of swine was feeding at some distance from them.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, far from them, a herd of many swine, feeding;
text.KJV: And there was a good way off from them, an heard of many swine, feeding.
text.NABRE: Some distance away a herd of many swine was feeding.
text.DRB: And there was not far from them an herd of many swine feeding.

text.NRSV: The demons begged him, “If you cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.”
text.Rotherham: and, the demons, began to beseech him, saying, If thou dost cast us out, send us away, into the herd of swine.
text.KJV: So the deuils besought him, saying, If thou cast vs out, suffer vs to goe away into the herd of swine.
text.NABRE: The demons pleaded with him, “If you drive us out, send us into the herd of swine.”
text.DRB: And the devils besought him saying: If thou cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Go!” So they came out and entered the swine; and suddenly, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the water.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, Withdraw! So, they, going out, departed into the swine,—and lo! all the herd rushed down the cliff, into the sea, and died in the waters.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Goe. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and behold, the whole herd of swine ranne violently downe a steepe place into the Sea, and perished in the waters.
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “Go then!” They came out and entered the swine, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea where they drowned.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Go. But they going forth went into the swine, and behold the whole herd went with a violence, headlong into the sea: and they died in the waters.

text.NRSV: The herdsmen ran off, and on going into the town, they told the whole story about what had happened to the demoniacs.
text.Rotherham: And, they who were feeding them, fled,—and, departing into the city, reported all things, and what concerned them who had been demonized.
text.KJV: And they that kept them, fled, and went their waies into the citie, and told euery thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the deuils.
text.NABRE: The swineherds ran away, and when they came to the town they reported everything, including what had happened to the demoniacs.
text.DRB: And the swineherds fled: and coming into the city, told all, and of them that had been possessed of devils.

text.NRSV: Then the whole town came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their neighborhood.
text.Rotherham: And lo! all the city, came out to meet Jesus,—and, seeing him, they besought that he would pass on from their bounds.
text.KJV: And behold, the whole citie came out to meete Iesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that hee would depart out of their coasts.
text.NABRE: Thereupon the whole town came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him they begged him to leave their district.
text.DRB: And behold the whole city went out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him, they besought him that he would pass from their quarters.

text.NRSV: And after getting into a boat he crossed the sea and came to his own town.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into a boat, he crossed over, and came into his own city.
text.KJV: And hee entred into a ship, and passed ouer, and came into his owne citie.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 The Healing of a Paralytic. He entered a boat, made the crossing, and came into his own town.
text.DRB: And entering into a boat, he passed over the water, and came into his own city.

text.NRSV: And just then some people were carrying a paralyzed man lying on a bed. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! they were bringing unto him a paralytic, on a couch, laid prostrate; and Jesus, seeing, their faith, said to the paralytic,—Take courage! child, forgiven are thy sins.
text.KJV: And behold, they brought to him a man sicke of the palsie, lying on a bed: and Iesus seeing their faith, said vnto the sicke of the palsie, Sonne, be of good cheere, thy sinnes be forgiuen thee.
text.NABRE: And there people brought to him a paralytic lying on a stretcher. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Courage, child, your sins are forgiven.”
text.DRB: And behold they brought to him one sick of the palsy lying in bed. And Jesus seeing their faith, said to the sick of the palsy: Have a good heart son, thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! certain of the scribes, said within themselves,—This man, speaketh profanely!
text.KJV: And behold, certaine of the Scribes said within themselues, This man blasphemeth.
text.NABRE: At that, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man is blaspheming.”
text.DRB: And behold certain of the Scribes said within themselves: He blasphemeth.

text.NRSV: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, knowing, their inward thoughts, said,—To what end are ye cherishing evil thoughts within your hearts?
text.KJV: And Iesus knowing their thoughts, said, Wherefore thinke yee euill in your hearts?
text.NABRE: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and said, “Why do you harbor evil thoughts?
text.DRB: And Jesus seeing their thoughts, said: Wherefore think you evil in your hearts?

text.NRSV: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
text.Rotherham: For which is easier—To say, Forgiven are thy sins,—or to say, Rise and be walking!
text.KJV: For whether is easier to say, Thy sinnes be forgiuen thee: or to say, Arise, and walke?
text.NABRE: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
text.DRB: Whether is easier, to say, thy sins are forgiven thee: or to say, arise and walk.

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he then said to the paralytic—“Get up, take your bed and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know, that the Son of Man hath, authority, upon the earth, to be forgiving sins, then, saith he to the paralytic,—Rise! take up thy couch, and withdraw unto thy house.
text.KJV: But that yee may know that the sonne of man hath power on earth to forgiue sinnes, (Then saith hee to the sicke of the palsie) Arise, take vp thy bed, and goe vnto thine house.
text.NABRE: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he then said to the paralytic, “Rise, pick up your stretcher, and go home.”
text.DRB: But that you may know that the Son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins, (then said he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go into thy house.

text.NRSV: And he got up and went to his home.
text.Rotherham: And, rising, he departed unto his house.
text.KJV: And he arose, and departed to his house.
text.NABRE: He rose and went home.
text.DRB: And he arose, and went into his house.

text.NRSV: When the crowds saw it, they were filled with awe, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to human beings.
text.Rotherham: Now the multitudes, seeing, were struck with fear, and glorified the God who had given authority, such as this, unto men.
text.KJV: But when the multitudes saw it, they marueiled, & glorified God, which had giuen such power vnto men.
text.NABRE: When the crowds saw this they were struck with awe and glorified God who had given such authority to human beings. The Call of Matthew.
text.DRB: And the multitudes seeing it, were afraid, and glorified God that gave such power to men.

text.NRSV: As Jesus was walking along, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, passing aside from thence, saw a man presiding over the tax-office, called, Matthew, and saith unto him,—Be following me. And, arising, he followed him.
text.KJV: ¶ And as Iesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man named Matthew, sitting at the receite of custome: and he saith vnto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
text.NABRE: As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the customs post. He said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.DRB: And when Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man sitting in the custom-house, named Matthew; And he saith to him: Follow me. And he arose up, and followed him.

text.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in the house, many tax collectors and sinners came and were sitting with him and his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was reclining in the house, that lo! many tax-collectors and sinners, came, and were reclining together with Jesus and his disciples.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, as Iesus sate at meate in the house, behold, many publicanes and sinners, came and sate downe with him and his Disciples.
text.NABRE: While he was at table in his house, many tax collectors and sinners came and sat with Jesus and his disciples.
text.DRB: And it came to pass as he was sitting at meat in the house, behold many Publicans and sinners came, and sat down with Jesus and his Disciples.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, observing it, began to say unto his disciples,—Wherefore, with tax-collectors and sinners, doth your Teacher eat?
text.KJV: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said vnto his disciples, Why eateth your master with publicanes & sinners.
text.NABRE: The Pharisees saw this and said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.DRB: And the Pharisees seeing it, said to his disciples: why doth your master eat with Publicans and sinners?

text.NRSV: But when he heard this, he said, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
text.Rotherham: And, he, hearing it, said,—No need have the strong, of a physician, but they who are sick.
text.KJV: But when Iesus heard that, hee said vnto them, They that be whole neede not a Physicion, but they that are sicke.
text.NABRE: He heard this and said, “Those who are well do not need a physician, but the sick do.
text.DRB: But Jesus hearing it, said: They that are in health, need not a physician, but they that are ill at ease.

text.NRSV: Go and learn what this means, ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
text.Rotherham: But go ye, and learn what this meaneth,—Mercy, I desire, and not, sacrifice; For I came, not to call the righteous, but sinners.
text.KJV: But goe ye and learne what that meaneth, I will haue mercy and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
text.NABRE: Go and learn the meaning of the words, ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ I did not come to call the righteous but sinners.”
