text.DRB: But go your ways & learn what it is, I will mercy, & not sacrifice. For I am not come to call the just, but sinners.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.Rotherham: Then come near unto him the disciples of John, saying,—Wherefore do, we, and the Pharisees, fast, whereas, thy disciples, fast not?
text.KJV: ¶ Then came to him the disciples of Iohn, saying, Why doe we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?
text.NABRE: The Question About Fasting. Then the disciples of John approached him and said, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast [much], but your disciples do not fast?”
text.DRB: Then came to him the Disciples of John, saying: Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but thy Disciples do not fast?

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them, can they? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them,—Can, the sons of the bridechamber, mourn, so long as, the bridegroom, is, with them? But days will come, when the bridegroom, shall be taken from them, and, then, will they fast.
text.KJV: And Iesus saide vnto them, Can the children of the bride-chamber mourne, as long as the bridegrome is with them? But the dayes will come when the bridegrome shall bee taken from them, and then shall they fast.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: Can the children of the Bridegroom mourn, as long as the Bridegroom is with them? But the days will come when the Bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then they shall fast.

text.NRSV: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak, for the patch pulls away from the cloak, and a worse tear is made.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, no one, layeth on a patch of unshrunk cloth, upon an old garment,—for the shrinking of it teareth away from the garment, and, a worse rent, is made.
text.KJV: No man putteth a piece of new cloth vnto an olde garment: for that which is put in to fill it vp, taketh from the garment, & the rent is made worse.
text.NABRE: No one patches an old cloak with a piece of unshrunken cloth, for its fullness pulls away from the cloak and the tear gets worse.
text.DRB: And nobody putteth a piece of raw cloth to an old garment. For he taketh away the piecing thereof from the garment, and there is made a greater rent.

text.NRSV: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins; otherwise, the skins burst, and the wine is spilled, and the skins are destroyed; but new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved.”
text.Rotherham: Neither pour they new wine into old skins: otherwise at least, the skins are burst, and, the wine, runneth out, and, the skins, are spoiled,—but they pour new wine into unused skins, and, both, are together preserved.
text.KJV: Neither doe men put new wine into old bottels: else the bottels breake, and the wine runneth out, and the bottels perish: but they put new wine into new bottels, and both are preserued.
text.NABRE: People do not put new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise the skins burst, the wine spills out, and the skins are ruined. Rather, they pour new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.”
text.DRB: Neither do they put new wine into old bottles. Otherwise the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish. But new wine they put into new bottles: and both are preserved together.

text.NRSV: While he was saying these things to them, suddenly a leader of the synagogue came in and knelt before him, saying, “My daughter has just died; but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
text.Rotherham: While, these things, he was speaking unto them, lo! a [certain] ruler, came, and began bowing down to him, saying,—My daughter, just now died! But come, and lay thy hand upon, her, and she shall live.
text.KJV: ¶ While hee spake these things vnto them, beholde, there came a certaine ruler and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is euen now dead: but come, and lay thy hand vpon her, and she shall liue.
text.NABRE: The Official’s Daughter and the Woman with a Hemorrhage. While he was saying these things to them, an official came forward, knelt down before him, and said, “My daughter has just died. But come, lay your hand on her, and she will live.”
text.DRB: As he was speaking this unto them, behold a certain Governor approached, and adored him, saying: Lord, my daughter is even now dead; but come, lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.

text.NRSV: And Jesus got up and followed him, with his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, arising, was following him, also his disciples.
text.KJV: And Iesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
text.NABRE: Jesus rose and followed him, and so did his disciples.
text.DRB: And Jesus rising up followed him, and his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Then suddenly a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his cloak,
text.Rotherham: And lo! a woman, having a flow of blood twelve years, coming near behind, touched the fringe of his mantle.
text.KJV: (¶ And behold, a woman which was diseased with an issue of blood twelue yeeres, came behinde him, and touched the hemme of his garment.
text.NABRE: A woman suffering hemorrhages for twelve years came up behind him and touched the tassel on his cloak.
text.DRB: And behold a woman which was troubled with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment.

text.NRSV: for she said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be made well.”
text.Rotherham: For she kept saying within herself,—If only I touch his mantle, I shall be made well!
text.KJV: For she said within her selfe, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.
text.NABRE: She said to herself, “If only I can touch his cloak, I shall be cured.”
text.DRB: For she said within herself: If I shall touch only his garment, I shall be safe.

text.NRSV: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, “Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well.” And instantly the woman was made well.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, turning and seeing her, said,—Take courage! daughter, Thy faith, hath made thee well. And the woman was made well, from that hour.
text.KJV: But Iesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, bee of good comfort, thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that houre.)
text.NABRE: Jesus turned around and saw her, and said, “Courage, daughter! Your faith has saved you.” And from that hour the woman was cured.
text.DRB: But Jesus turning and seeing her, said: Have a good heart daughter, thy faith hath made thee safe. And the woman became whole from that hour.

text.NRSV: When Jesus came to the leader’s house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, coming, into the house of the ruler, and seeing the flute-players and the multitude in confusion, was saying:
text.KJV: And when Iesus came into the rulers house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
text.NABRE: When Jesus arrived at the official’s house and saw the flute players and the crowd who were making a commotion,
text.DRB: And when Jesus was come into the house of the Governor, & saw minstrels and the multitude keeping a stir,

text.NRSV: he said, “Go away; for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him.
text.Rotherham: Give place! for the maiden died not, but is sleeping. And they began to deride him.
text.KJV: He said vnto them, Giue place, for the mayd is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorne.
text.NABRE: he said, “Go away! The girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they ridiculed him.
text.DRB: he said: Depart, for the wench is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.

text.NRSV: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl got up.
text.Rotherham: But, when the multitude had been put forth, he went in, and grasped her hand,—and the maiden arose.
text.KJV: But when the people were put foorth, he went in, and tooke her by the hand: and the mayd arose.
text.NABRE: When the crowd was put out, he came and took her by the hand, and the little girl arose.
text.DRB: And when the multitude was put forth, he entered in, and held her hand. And the maid arose.

text.NRSV: And the report of this spread throughout that district.
text.Rotherham: And forth went this report, into the whole of that land.
text.KJV: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
text.NABRE: And news of this spread throughout all that land. The Healing of Two Blind Men.
text.DRB: And this bruit went forth into all that country.

text.NRSV: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, crying loudly, “Have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: And, as Jesus, was passing aside from thence, there followed him, two blind men, crying aloud and saying,—Have mercy on us, O Son of David!
text.KJV: ¶ And when Iesus departed thence, two blinde men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou sonne of Dauid, haue mercy on vs.
text.NABRE: And as Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed [him], crying out, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text.DRB: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, there followed him two blind men crying and saying: Have mercy on us, O Son of David.

text.NRSV: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him; and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?” They said to him, “Yes, Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered the house, the blind men came unto him,—and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye, that I can, do this? They say unto him, Yea, Lord!
text.KJV: And when he was come into the house, the blinde men came to him: and Iesus saith vnto them, Beleeue ye that I am able to doe this? They said vnto him, Yea, Lord.
text.NABRE: When he entered the house, the blind men approached him and Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I can do this?” “Yes, Lord,” they said to him.
text.DRB: And when he was come to the house, the blind came to him. And Jesus saith to them: Do you believe, that I can do this unto you? They say to him: Yea Lord.

text.NRSV: Then he touched their eyes and said, “According to your faith let it be done to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith, be it done unto you.
text.KJV: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith, bee it vnto you.
text.NABRE: Then he touched their eyes and said, “Let it be done for you according to your faith.”
text.DRB: Then he touched their eyes, saying: According to your faith, be it done to you.

text.NRSV: And their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly ordered them, “See that no one knows of this.”
text.Rotherham: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus, sternly charged them, saying, Mind! let no one know!
text.KJV: And their eyes were opened: and Iesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
text.NABRE: And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them sternly, “See that no one knows about this.”
text.DRB: And their eyes were opened, and Jesus threatened them, saying: See that no man know it.

text.NRSV: But they went away and spread the news about him throughout that district.
text.Rotherham: They, however, going forth, made him known throughout the whole of that land.
text.KJV: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that countrey.
text.NABRE: But they went out and spread word of him through all that land.
text.DRB: But they went forth, and bruited him in all that country.

text.NRSV: After they had gone away, a demoniac who was mute was brought to him.
text.Rotherham: And, as, they, were going forth, lo! there was brought to him, a dumb man, demonized.
text.KJV: ¶ As they went out, beholde, they brought to him a dumbe man possessed with a deuill.
text.NABRE: The Healing of a Mute Person. As they were going out, a demoniac who could not speak was brought to him,
text.DRB: And when they were gone forth, behold they brought him a dumb man, possessed with a devil.

text.NRSV: And when the demon had been cast out, the one who had been mute spoke; and the crowds were amazed and said, “Never has anything like this been seen in Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And, the demon being cast out, the dumb man spoke; and the multitudes marvelled, saying,—Never, was it seen thus, in Israel.
text.KJV: And when the deuil was cast out, the dumbe spake, and the multitudes marueiled, saying, It was neuer so seene in Israel.
text.NABRE: and when the demon was driven out the mute person spoke. The crowds were amazed and said, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.”
text.DRB: And after the devil was cast out, the dumb man spake, and the multitudes marveled saying: Never was the like seen in Israel.

text.NRSV: But the Pharisees said, “By the ruler of the demons he casts out the demons.”
text.Rotherham: [But, the Pharisees, beg to say, In the ruler of the demons, is he casting out the demons.]
text.KJV: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out the deuils through the prince of the deuils.
text.NABRE: But the Pharisees said, “He drives out demons by the prince of demons.”
text.DRB: But the Pharisees said: In the prince of devils he casteth out devils.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and curing every disease and every sickness.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was going round all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the glad-message of the kingdom,—and curing every disease, and every infirmity.
text.KJV: And Iesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their Synagogues, and preaching the Gospel of the kingdome, and healing euery sickenesse, and euery disease among the people.
text.NABRE: The Compassion of Jesus. Jesus went around to all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom, and curing every disease and illness.
text.DRB: And Jesus went about all the cities, and towns, teaching in their Synagogues, and preaching the Gospel of the Kingdom, and curing every disease, and every infirmity.

text.NRSV: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the multitudes, he was moved with compassion concerning them, because they were torn and thrown down, like sheep hating no shepherd.
text.KJV: ¶ But when he saw the multitudes, he was moued with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheepe hauing no shepheard.
text.NABRE: At the sight of the crowds, his heart was moved with pity for them because they were troubled and abandoned, like sheep without a shepherd.
text.DRB: And seeing the multitudes, he pitied them; because they were vexed, and lay like sheep that have not a shepherd.

text.NRSV: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto his disciples, The harvest, indeed, is great, but, the labourers, few;
text.KJV: Then saith he vnto his disciples, The haruest truely is plenteous, but the labourers are few.
text.NABRE: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is abundant but the laborers are few;
text.DRB: Then he saith to his Disciples: The harvest surely is great, but the workmen are few.

text.NRSV: therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”
text.Rotherham: Beg ye, therefore, of the Lord of the harvest,—That he would thrust forth labourers, into his harvest.
text.KJV: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the haruest, that hee will send foorth labourers into his haruest.
text.NABRE: so ask the master of the harvest to send out laborers for his harvest.”
text.DRB: Pray therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth workmen into his harvest.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus summoned his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to cure every disease and every sickness.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near his twelve disciples, he gave them authority over impure spirits,—so as to be casting them out, and curing every disease and every infirmity.
text.KJV: And when hee had called vnto him his twelue disciples, he gaue them power against vncleane spirits, to cast them out, and to heale all maner of sickenesse, and all maner of disease.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 The Mission of the Twelve. Then he summoned his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to drive them out and to cure every disease and every illness.
text.DRB: And having called his twelve disciples together, he gave them power over unclean spirits, that they should cast them out, & should cure all manner of disease, & all manner of infirmity.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, also known as Peter, and his brother Andrew; James son of Zebedee, and his brother John;
text.Rotherham: Now, the twelve apostles’ names, are these:—first Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,—and James, the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother;
text.KJV: Now the names of the twelue Apostles are these: The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, Iames the sonne of Zebedee, and Iohn his brother:
text.NABRE: The names of the twelve apostles are these: first, Simon called Peter, and his brother Andrew; James, the son of Zebedee, and his brother John;
text.DRB: And the names of the twelve Apostles be these: The first, Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother,

text.NRSV: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
text.Rotherham: Philip, and Bartholomew,—Thomas, and Matthew, the tax-collector; James, the son of Alphaeus, and Thadaeus;
text.KJV: Philip, and Bartholomew, Thomas, and Matthew the Publicane, Iames the sonne of Alpheus, and Lebbeus, whose surname was Thaddeus:
text.NABRE: Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James, the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddeus;
text.DRB: James of Zebedee, and John his brother, Philip and Barthlemew, Thomas and Matthew the publican, and James of Alphæus, and Thaddæus,

text.NRSV: Simon the Cananaean, and Judas Iscariot, the one who betrayed him.
text.Rotherham: Simon, the zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who also delivered him up.
text.KJV: Simon the Canaanite, and Iudas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
text.NABRE: Simon the Cananean, and Judas Iscariot who betrayed him.
text.DRB: Simon Cananæus, & Judas Iscariote, who also betrayed him.

text.NRSV: These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: “Go nowhere among the Gentiles, and enter no town of the Samaritans,
text.Rotherham: These twelve, Jesus sent forth, charging them, saying:—Into any way to the nations, do not depart,—and, into any city of Samaritans, do not enter;
text.KJV: These twelue Iesus sent foorth, and commanded them, saying, Goe not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:
text.NABRE: The Commissioning of the Twelve. Jesus sent out these twelve after instructing them thus, “Do not go into pagan territory or enter a Samaritan town.
text.DRB: These twelve did Jesus send; commanding them, saying: Into the way of the Gentiles go ye not, and into the cities of the Samaritans enter ye not:

text.NRSV: but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: But be going, rather, unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
text.KJV: But goe rather to the lost sheepe of the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: Go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
text.DRB: but go rather to the sheep that are perished of the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: As you go, proclaim the good news, ‘The kingdom of heaven has come near.’
text.Rotherham: And, as ye go, proclaim, saying, The kingdom of the heavens hath drawn near!
text.KJV: And as yee goe, preach, saying, The kingdome of heauen is at hand:
text.NABRE: As you go, make this proclamation: ‘The kingdom of heaven is at hand.’
text.DRB: And going preach, saying: That the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.

text.NRSV: Cure the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons. You received without payment; give without payment.
text.Rotherham: Be curing, the sick, raising, the dead,—cleansing, lepers, casting out, demons: freely, ye have, received, freely, give.
text.KJV: Heale the sicke, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out deuils: freely ye haue receiued, freely giue.
text.NABRE: Cure the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, drive out demons. Without cost you have received; without cost you are to give.
text.DRB: Cure the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out devils: gratis you have received, gratis give ye.

text.NRSV: Take no gold, or silver, or copper in your belts,
text.Rotherham: Ye may procure—neither gold, nor silver, nor copper, for your belts,—
text.KJV: Prouide neither gold, nor siluer, nor brasse in your purses:
text.NABRE: Do not take gold or silver or copper for your belts;
text.DRB: Do not possess gold, nor silver, nor money in your purses:

text.NRSV: no bag for your journey, or two tunics, or sandals, or a staff; for laborers deserve their food.
text.Rotherham: neither satchel for journey, nor two tunics, nor sandals, nor staff; for, worthy, is the labourer, of his maintenance.
text.KJV: Nor scrippe for your iourney, neither two coats, neither shooes, nor yet staues: (for the workeman is worthy of his meat.)
text.NABRE: no sack for the journey, or a second tunic, or sandals, or walking stick. The laborer deserves his keep.
text.DRB: not a scrip for the way, neither two coats, neither shoes, neither rod. For the workman is worthy of his meat.

text.NRSV: Whatever town or village you enter, find out who in it is worthy, and stay there until you leave.
text.Rotherham: And, into whatsoever city or village ye enter, search out who in it is, worthy, and, there, abide, till ye go forth.
text.KJV: And into whatsoeuer city or towne ye shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy, and there abide till yee goe thence.
text.NABRE: Whatever town or village you enter, look for a worthy person in it, and stay there until you leave.
text.DRB: And into whatsoever city or town you shall enter, inquire who in it is worthy: and there tarry till you go forth.

text.NRSV: As you enter the house, greet it.
text.Rotherham: And, as ye enter the house, salute it;
text.KJV: And when ye come into an house, salute it.
text.NABRE: As you enter a house, wish it peace.
text.DRB: And when ye enter into the house, salute it, saying: Peace be to this house.

text.NRSV: If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
text.Rotherham: And, if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it, but, if it be not worthy, let your peace, unto you, return.
text.KJV: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come vpon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace returne to you.
text.NABRE: If the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it; if not, let your peace return to you.
text.DRB: And if so be that house be worthy, your peace shall come upon it. But if it be not worthy, your peace shall return to you.

text.NRSV: If anyone will not welcome you or listen to your words, shake off the dust from your feet as you leave that house or town.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall neither welcome you, nor hear your words, as ye go forth outside that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet:
text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall not receiue you, nor heare your words: when yee depart out of that house, or city, shake off the dust of your feete.
text.NABRE: Whoever will not receive you or listen to your words—go outside that house or town and shake the dust from your feet.
text.DRB: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words; going forth out of the house or the city shake off the dust from your feet.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you,—More tolerable, will it be, for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for, that, city!
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of iudgment, then for that citie.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, it shall be more tolerable for the land of the Sodomites and Gomorrheans in the day of judgement, than for that city.

text.NRSV: “See, I am sending you out like sheep into the midst of wolves; so be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I, send you forth as sheep amidst wolves; become ye therefore—prudent, as serpents, and simple, as doves.
text.KJV: ¶ Behold, I send you foorth as sheepe in the middest of wolues: be yee therefore wise as serpents, and harmelesse as doues.
text.NABRE: Coming Persecutions. “Behold, I am sending you like sheep in the midst of wolves; so be shrewd as serpents and simple as doves.
text.DRB: Behold I send you as sheep in the midst of wolves. Be ye therefore wise as serpents, and simple as doves.

text.NRSV: Beware of them, for they will hand you over to councils and flog you in their synagogues;
text.Rotherham: But beware of men; for they will deliver you up into high-councils, and, in their synagogues, will they scourge you,—
text.KJV: But beware of men: for they will deliuer you vp to the Councils, and they will scourge you in their Synagogues,
text.NABRE: But beware of people, for they will hand you over to courts and scourge you in their synagogues,
text.DRB: And take heed of men. For they will deliver you up in Councils, and in their Synagogues they will scourge you.

text.NRSV: and you will be dragged before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them and the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: And, before both governor and kings, shall ye be brought, for my sake—for a witness to them and the nations.
text.KJV: And yee shall be brought before Gouernours and Kings for my sake, for a testimonie against them, and the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: and you will be led before governors and kings for my sake as a witness before them and the pagans.
text.DRB: And to presidents and to kings shall you be led for my sake, in testimony to them and the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: When they hand you over, do not worry about how you are to speak or what you are to say; for what you are to say will be given to you at that time;
text.Rotherham: And, when they deliver you up, be not anxious how or what ye shall speak,—for it shall be given you in that hour what ye shall speak;
text.KJV: But when they deliuer you vp, take no thought, how or what ye shall speake, for it shall bee giuen you in that same houre what ye shall speake.
text.NABRE: When they hand you over, do not worry about how you are to speak or what you are to say. You will be given at that moment what you are to say.
text.DRB: But when they shall deliver you up, take no thought how or what to speak: for it shall be given you in that hour what to speak.

text.NRSV: for it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
text.Rotherham: For it is not, ye, who are speaking, but, the Spirit of your Father, that is speaking in you.
text.KJV: For it is not yee that speake, but the Spirit of your Father, which speaketh in you.
text.NABRE: For it will not be you who speak but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
text.DRB: For it is not you that speak, but the Spirit of your Father that speaketh in you.

text.NRSV: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;
text.Rotherham: And, brother, will deliver up, brother, unto death, and, father, child,—and, children, will rise up, against parents, and will put them to death.
text.KJV: And the brother shall deliuer vp the brother to death, and the father the childe: and the children shall rise vp against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.
text.NABRE: Brother will hand over brother to death, and the father his child; children will rise up against parents and have them put to death.
text.DRB: The brother also shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the Son: and the children shall rise up against the parents, and shall work their death,

text.NRSV: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be hated by all, because of my name,—but, he that endureth throughout, the same, shall be saved.
text.KJV: And yee shall be hated of all men for my Names sake: but he that endureth to the end, shalbe saued.
text.NABRE: You will be hated by all because of my name, but whoever endures to the end will be saved.
text.DRB: and you shall be odious to all men for my name: but he that shall persevere unto the end, he shall be saved.

text.NRSV: When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next; for truly I tell you, you will not have gone through all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
text.Rotherham: And, when they persecute you in this city, flee into another,—for, verily, I say unto you, in nowise shall ye finish the cities of Israel, till, he Son of Man, come.
text.KJV: But when they persecute you in this citie, flee ye into another: for verely I say vnto you, ye shall not haue gone ouer the cities of Israel, till the Sonne of man be come.
text.NABRE: When they persecute you in one town, flee to another. Amen, I say to you, you will not finish the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
text.DRB: And when they shall persecute you in this city, flee into another. Amen I say to you, you shall not finish all the cities of Israel, till the Son of man come.

text.NRSV: “A disciple is not above the teacher, nor a slave above the master;
text.Rotherham: A, disciple, is not above, the teacher, nor, a servant, above, his lord:
text.KJV: The disciple is not aboue his master, nor the seruant aboue his lord.
text.NABRE: No disciple is above his teacher, no slave above his master.
text.DRB: The disciple is not above the master, nor the servant above his lord.

text.NRSV: it is enough for the disciple to be like the teacher, and the slave like the master. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more will they malign those of his household!
text.Rotherham: Sufficient for the disciple, that he become, as his teacher, and, the servant, as, his lord. If, the master of the house, Beelzebul, they called, how much more, the men of his house!
text.KJV: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the seruant as his Lord: If they haue called the Master of the house of Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?
text.NABRE: It is enough for the disciple that he become like his teacher, for the slave that he become like his master. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more those of his household!
text.DRB: It sufficeth the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the goodman of the house Beelzebub, how much more them of his household?

text.NRSV: “So have no fear of them; for nothing is covered that will not be uncovered, and nothing secret that will not become known.
text.Rotherham: Then do not fear them,—for, nothing hath been covered, which shall not be, uncovered, and, hidden, which shall not be, made known.
text.KJV: Feare them not therefore: for there is nothing couered, that shall not be reueiled; and hidde, that shall not be knowen.
text.NABRE: Courage Under Persecution. “Therefore do not be afraid of them. Nothing is concealed that will not be revealed, nor secret that will not be known.
text.DRB: Therefore fear ye not them. For nothing is hid, that shall not be revealed: and secret, that shall not be known.

text.NRSV: What I tell you in the dark, utter in the light; and what you hear whispered, proclaim from the housetops.
text.Rotherham: What I am saying to you in the darkness, tell ye in the light,—and, what [whispered] into the ear ye are hearing, proclaim ye on the housetops.
text.KJV: What I tell you in darkenesse, that speake yee in light: and what yee heare in the eare, that preach yee vpon the house tops.
text.NABRE: What I say to you in the darkness, speak in the light; what you hear whispered, proclaim on the housetops.
text.DRB: That which I speak to you in the dark, speak ye in the light: and that which you hear in the ear, preach ye upon the house tops.

text.NRSV: Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
text.Rotherham: And be not in fear, by reason of them that are killing the body,—and, the soul, are not able to kill. But fear, rather, him who is able, both soul and body, to destroy in gehenna!
text.KJV: And feare not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soule: but rather feare him which is able to destroy both soule and body in hell.
text.NABRE: And do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather, be afraid of the one who can destroy both soul and body in Gehenna.
text.DRB: And fear ye not them that kill the body, and are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him that can destroy both soul and body into Hell.

text.NRSV: Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from your Father.
text.Rotherham: Are not, two sparrows, for a farthing, sold? And, one from among them, shall not fall upon the ground, without your Father;
text.KJV: Are not two Sparrowes solde for a farthing? And one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
text.NABRE: Are not two sparrows sold for a small coin? Yet not one of them falls to the ground without your Father’s knowledge.
text.DRB: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing: and not one of them shall fall upon the ground without your father?

text.NRSV: And even the hairs of your head are all counted.
text.Rotherham: But, even the hairs of, your, head, have all been numbered.
text.KJV: But the very haires of your head are all numbred.
text.NABRE: Even all the hairs of your head are counted.
text.DRB: But your very hairs of the head are all numbered.

text.NRSV: So do not be afraid; you are of more value than many sparrows.
text.Rotherham: Then be not in fear—than many sparrows, better are, ye!
text.KJV: Feare yee not therefore, ye are of more value then many Sparrowes.
text.NABRE: So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
text.DRB: Fear not therefore: better are you than many sparrows.

text.NRSV: “Everyone therefore who acknowledges me before others, I also will acknowledge before my Father in heaven;
text.Rotherham: Every one therefore who shall confess me before men, I also, will confess, him, before my Father who is in the heavens;
text.KJV: Whosoeuer therefore shall confesse mee before men, him will I confesse also before my Father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: Everyone who acknowledges me before others I will acknowledge before my heavenly Father.
text.DRB: Every one therefore that shall confess me before men, I also will confess him before my Father which is in Heaven.

text.NRSV: but whoever denies me before others, I also will deny before my Father in heaven.
text.Rotherham: But, whoever shall deny me before men, I also, will deny, him, before my Father who is in the heavens.
text.KJV: But whosoeuer shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: But whoever denies me before others, I will deny before my heavenly Father.
text.DRB: But he that shall deny me before men, I also will deny him before my Father which is in Heaven.

text.NRSV: “Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.
text.Rotherham: Do not think, that I came to thrust peace upon the earth,—I came not to thrust, peace, but, a sword;
text.KJV: Thinke not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.
text.NABRE: Jesus: A Cause of Division. “Do not think that I have come to bring peace upon the earth. I have come to bring not peace but the sword.
text.DRB: Do not ye think that I came to send peace into the earth: I came not to send peace, but the sword.

text.NRSV: For I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law;
text.Rotherham: For I came to set at variance—a man, against, his father, and, a daughter, against, her mother, and, a bride, against, her mother-in-law;
text.KJV: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, & the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
text.NABRE: For I have come to set a man ‘against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law;
text.DRB: For I came to separate man against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.

text.NRSV: and one’s foes will be members of one’s own household.
text.Rotherham: And, a man foes, are, they of his own house.
text.KJV: And a mans foes shalbe they of his owne houshold.
text.NABRE: and one’s enemies will be those of his household.’
text.DRB: And a man's enemies, they of his own household.

text.NRSV: Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me;
text.Rotherham: He that loveth father or mother above me, is not, worthy, of me,—and, he that loveth son or daughter above me, is not, worthy, of me;
text.KJV: He that loueth father or mother more then me, is not worthy of me: and he that loueth sonne or daughter more then me, is not worthy of me.
text.NABRE: The Conditions of Discipleship. “Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me, and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me;
text.DRB: He that loveth father or mother more than me, is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter above me, is not worthy of me.

text.NRSV: and whoever does not take up the cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
text.Rotherham: And, he that doth not take his cross and follow after me, is not, worthy, of me.
text.KJV: And he that taketh not his crosse, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
text.NABRE: and whoever does not take up his cross and follow after me is not worthy of me.
text.DRB: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth me, is not worthy of me.

text.NRSV: Those who find their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake will find it.
text.Rotherham: He that hath found his life, shall lose it,—and, he that hath lost his life, for my sake, shall find it.
text.KJV: He that findeth his life, shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake, shall find it.
text.NABRE: Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
text.DRB: He that hath found his life, shall lose it: and he that hath lost his life for me, shall find it.

text.NRSV: “Whoever welcomes you welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me.
text.Rotherham: He that giveth welcome unto you, unto me, giveth welcome, and, he that, unto me, giveth welcome, giveth welcome—unto him that sent me forth.
text.KJV: ¶ He that receiueth you, receiueth me: and he that receiueth mee, receiueth him that sent me.
text.NABRE: Rewards. “Whoever receives you receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
text.DRB: He that receiveth you, receiveth me: and he that receiveth me, receiveth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: Whoever welcomes a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward; and whoever welcomes a righteous person in the name of a righteous person will receive the reward of the righteous;
text.Rotherham: He that giveth welcome unto a prophet, in the name of a prophet, the reward of a prophet, shall receive; and, he that giveth welcome unto a righteous man, in the name of a righteous man, the reward of a righteous man, shall receive;—
text.KJV: He that receiueth a Prophet in the name of a Prophet, shall receiue a Prophets reward: and he that receiueth a righteous man, in the name of a righteous man, shal receiue a righteous mans reward.
text.NABRE: Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward, and whoever receives a righteous man because he is righteous will receive a righteous man’s reward.
text.DRB: He that receiveth a Prophet in the name of a Prophet, shall receive the reward of a Prophet, and he that receiveth a just man in the name of a just man, shall receive the reward of a just man.

text.NRSV: and whoever gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the name of a disciple—truly I tell you, none of these will lose their reward.”
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall give to drink—unto one of these little ones—a cup of cold water only, in the name of a disciple, Verily, I say unto you, in nowise, shall lose his reward!
text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall giue to drinke vnto one of these litle ones, a cup of cold water onely, in the name of a disciple, verily I say vnto you, hee shall in no wise lose his reward.
text.NABRE: And whoever gives only a cup of cold water to one of these little ones to drink because he is a disciple—amen, I say to you, he will surely not lose his reward.”
text.DRB: And whosoever shall give drink to one of these little ones a cup of cold water, only in the name of a disciple, amen I say to you, he shall not lose his reward.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and proclaim his message in their cities.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished giving instructions to his twelve disciples, he passed on from thence, to be teaching and proclaiming in their cities.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when Iesus had made an end of commaunding his twelue Disciples, hee departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 When Jesus finished giving these commands to his twelve disciples, he went away from that place to teach and to preach in their towns.
text.DRB: And it came to pass: when Jesus had done commanding his twelve Disciples, he passed from thence, to teach & preach in their cities.

text.NRSV: When John heard in prison what the Messiah was doing, he sent word by his disciples
text.Rotherham: Now, John, hearing in the prison the works of the Christ, sending through his disciples,
text.KJV: Now when Iohn had heard in the prison the workes of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
text.NABRE: IV. Opposition from Israel The Messengers from John the Baptist. When John heard in prison of the works of the Messiah, he sent his disciples to him
text.DRB: And when John had heard in prison the works of Christ; sending two of his Disciples, he said to him:

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?”
text.Rotherham: said unto him, Art, thou, the coming one? or, a different one, are we to expect?
text.KJV: And said vnto him, Art thou hee that should come? Or doe wee looke for another?
text.NABRE: with this question, “Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another?”
text.DRB: Art thou he that art to come, or look we for another?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John what you hear and see:
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—Go report unto John what ye do hear and see:—
text.KJV: Iesus answered and saide vnto them, Go and shew Iohn againe those things which we doe heare and see:
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Go and tell John what you hear and see:
text.DRB: And Jesus making answer said to them: Go and report to John what you have heard and seen.

text.NRSV: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor have good news brought to them.
text.Rotherham: The, blind, recover sight, and, the lame, walk, lepers, are cleansed, and, the deaf, hear,—and, the dead, are raised, and, the destitute, are told the joyful tidings;
text.KJV: The blind receiue their sight, and the lame walke, the lepers are cleansed, and the deafe heare, the dead are raised vp, and the poore haue the Gospel preached to them.
text.NABRE: the blind regain their sight, the lame walk, lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor have the good news proclaimed to them.
text.DRB: The blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are made clean, the deaf hear, the dead rise again, to the poor the Gospel is preached.

text.NRSV: And blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is he, whosoever shall not find cause of stumbling, in me.
text.KJV: And blessed is he, whoseouer shal not be offended in me.
text.NABRE: And blessed is the one who takes no offense at me.” Jesus’ Testimony to John.
text.DRB: and blessed is he that shall not be scandalized in me.

text.NRSV: As they went away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to look at? A reed shaken by the wind?
text.Rotherham: But, as these were going their way, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes, concerning John,—What went ye forth into the wilderness to gaze at? A reed, by a wind, shaken?
text.KJV: ¶ And as they departed, Iesus began to say vnto the multitudes concerning Iohn, what went ye out into the wildernesse to see? a reede shaken with the winde?
text.NABRE: As they were going off, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John, “What did you go out to the desert to see? A reed swayed by the wind?
text.DRB: And when they went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes of John: What went you out into the desert to see? a reed shaken with the wind?

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? Someone dressed in soft robes? Look, those who wear soft robes are in royal palaces.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A man, in soft clothing, arrayed? Lo! they who soft clothing do wear, are in the houses of kings.
text.KJV: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they that weare soft cloathing, are in kings houses.
text.NABRE: Then what did you go out to see? Someone dressed in fine clothing? Those who wear fine clothing are in royal palaces.
text.DRB: But what went you out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Behold they that are clothed in soft garments, are in kings' houses.

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.Rotherham: But why went ye forth? A prophet, to see? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.
text.KJV: But what went ye out for to see? A Prophet? yea, I say vnto you, and more then a Prophet.
text.NABRE: Then why did you go out? To see a prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.DRB: But what went you out to see? A Prophet? Yea I tell you and more than a Prophet.

text.NRSV: This is the one about whom it is written, ‘See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he, concerning whom it is written,—Lo! I, send my messenger before thy face, who shall make ready thy way before thee!
text.KJV: For this is he of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
text.NABRE: This is the one about whom it is written: ‘Behold, I am sending my messenger ahead of you; he will prepare your way before you.’
text.DRB: For this is he of whom it is written: Behold I send mine Angel before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, among those born of women no one has arisen greater than John the Baptist; yet the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—There hath not arisen, among such as are born of women, a greater than John the Immerser,—yet, he that is less in the kingdom of the heavens, is, greater than he.
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, Among them that are borne of women, there hath not risen a greater then Iohn the Baptist: notwithstanding, hee that is least in the kingdome of heauen, is greater then he.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, among those born of women there has been none greater than John the Baptist; yet the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, there hath not risen among the born of women a greater than John the Baptist: yet he that is the lesser in the Kingdom of Heaven, is greater than he.

text.NRSV: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence, and the violent take it by force.
text.Rotherham: But, from the days of John the Immerser, until even now, the kingdom of the heavens, is being invaded, and, invaders, are, seizing upon it.
text.KJV: And from the dayes of Iohn the Baptist, vntill now, the kingdome of heauen suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.
text.NABRE: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven suffers violence, and the violent are taking it by force.
text.DRB: And from the days of John the Baptist until now, the Kingdom of Heaven suffereth violence, and the violent bear it away.

text.NRSV: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John came;
text.Rotherham: For, all the prophets and the law, until John, did prophesy;
text.KJV: For all the Prophets, and the Law prophecied vntill Iohn.
text.NABRE: All the prophets and the law prophesied up to the time of John.
text.DRB: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied unto John:

text.NRSV: and if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye are willing to accept it, he, is Elijah—the one destined to come.
text.KJV: And if ye wil receiue it, this is Elias which was for to come.
text.NABRE: And if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah, the one who is to come.
text.DRB: and if you will receive it, he is Elias that is for to come.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears listen!
text.Rotherham: He that hath ears, let him hear!
text.KJV: Hee that hath eares to heare, let him heare.
text.NABRE: Whoever has ears ought to hear.
text.DRB: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: “But to what will I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to one another,
text.Rotherham: But, unto what, shall I liken this generation? It is, like, unto children, sitting the market-places, who, calling unto the others,
text.KJV: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like vnto children, sitting in the markets, and calling vnto their fellowes,
text.NABRE: “To what shall I compare this generation? It is like children who sit in marketplaces and call to one another,
text.DRB: And whereunto shall I esteem this Generation to be like? It is like to children sitting in the market place,

text.NRSV: ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we wailed, and you did not mourn.’
text.Rotherham: say, We played the flute to you, and ye danced not,—we sang a lament, and ye beat not the breast.
text.KJV: And saying, we haue piped vnto you, and ye haue not danced: wee haue mourned vnto you, and ye haue not lamented.
text.NABRE: ‘We played the flute for you, but you did not dance, we sang a dirge but you did not mourn.’
text.DRB: which crying to their companions, say: we have piped to you, and you have not danced: we have lamented, and you have not mourned.

text.NRSV: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon’;
text.Rotherham: For, John, came, neither eating nor drinking, and they say—A demon, he hath!
text.KJV: For Iohn came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, he hath a deuill.
text.NABRE: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they said, ‘He is possessed by a demon.’
text.DRB: For John came neither eating nor drinking; and they say: He hath a devil.

text.NRSV: the Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ Yet wisdom is vindicated by her deeds.”
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man, came, eating and drinking, and they say, Lo! a gluttonous man and a wine-drinker! a friend of, tax-collectors, and sinners! And yet wisdom hath been justified by her works.
text.KJV: The sonne of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a wine bibber, a friend of publicanes and sinners: but wisedom is iustified of her children.
text.NABRE: The Son of Man came eating and drinking and they said, ‘Look, he is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.’ But wisdom is vindicated by her works.”
text.DRB: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say: Behold a man that is a glutton and a wine-drinker, a friend of Publicans and sinners. And wisdom is justified of her children.

text.NRSV: Then he began to reproach the cities in which most of his deeds of power had been done, because they did not repent.
text.Rotherham: Then, began he to upbraid the cities, in which had been done his noblest mighty works; because they repented not:—
text.KJV: ¶ Then began he to vpbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not.
text.NABRE: Reproaches to Unrepentant Towns. Then he began to reproach the towns where most of his mighty deeds had been done, since they had not repented.
text.DRB: Then began he to upbraid the cities, wherein were done the most of his miracles, for that they had not done penance.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
text.Rotherham: Alas for thee, Chorazin! Alas for thee, Bethsaida! Because, if, in Tyre and Zidon, had been done the works of power, which were done in you, of old, in sackcloth and ashes, had they repented.
text.KJV: Woe vnto thee Chorazin, woe vnto thee Bethsaida: for if the mightie workes which were done in you, had bene done in Tyre and Sidon, they would haue repented long agoe in sackcloth and ashes.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty deeds done in your midst had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would long ago have repented in sackcloth and ashes.
text.DRB: Woe be to thee Corozain, woe be to thee Beth-saida: for if in Tyre & Sidon had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in you, they had done penance in hair-cloth & ashes long ago.

text.NRSV: But I tell you, on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you, For Tyre and Zidon, more tolerable, will it be, in the day of judgment, than for you.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, It shall bee more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of iudgement, then for you.
text.NABRE: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
text.DRB: But nevertheless, I say to you, it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgement, than for you.

text.NRSV: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades. For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Capernaum! Unto heaven, shalt thou be uplifted? Unto hades, thou shall be brought down; because, if, in Sodom, had been done the works of power, which were done in thee, it would, in that case, have remained until this day.
text.KJV: And thou Capernaum, which art exalted vnto heauen, shalt be brought downe to hell: For if the mighty works which haue beene done in thee, had bin done in Sodome, it would haue remained vntil this day.
text.NABRE: And as for you, Capernaum: ‘Will you be exalted to heaven? You will go down to the netherworld.’ For if the mighty deeds done in your midst had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
text.DRB: And thou Capharnaum, shalt thou be exalted up to Heaven? thou shalt come down even unto Hell. For if in Sodom had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in thee, perhaps it had remained unto this day.

text.NRSV: But I tell you that on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom than for you.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you—For the land of Sodom, more tolerable, will it be in a day of judgment, than, for thee.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom, in þ day of iudgment, then for thee.
text.NABRE: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”
text.DRB: But notwithstanding I say to you, that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgement, than for thee.

text.NRSV: At that time Jesus said, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the intelligent and have revealed them to infants;
text.Rotherham: In that season, answered Jesus, and said—I openly give praise unto thee, Father, Lord of heaven and earth,—in that thou hast hid these things from the wise and discerning, and hast revealed them unto babes;
text.KJV: ¶ At that time Iesus answered, and said, I thanke thee, O Father, Lord of heauen and earth, because thou hast hid these things frō the wise & prudent, & hast reueiled them vnto babes.
text.NABRE: The Praise of the Father. At that time Jesus said in reply, “I give praise to you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, for although you have hidden these things from the wise and the learned you have revealed them to the childlike.
text.DRB: At that time Jesus answered and said: I confess to thee O Father Lord of Heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to little ones.

text.NRSV: yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
text.Rotherham: Yea, O Father! That, so, it hath become, a delight, before thee.
text.KJV: Euen so, Father, for so it seemed good in thy sight.
text.NABRE: Yes, Father, such has been your gracious will.
text.DRB: Yea Father; for so hath it well pleased thee.

text.NRSV: All things have been handed over to me by my Father; and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
text.Rotherham: All things, unto me, have been delivered up by my Father; and, no one, fully knoweth the Son, save the rather, neither doth any one fully know, the Father, save, the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son, may be minded, to reveal, him.
text.KJV: All things are deliuered vnto me of my father: and no man knoweth the sonne but the father: neither knoweth any man the father, saue the sonne, and hee to whomsoeuer the sonne will reueile him.
text.NABRE: All things have been handed over to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wishes to reveal him.
text.DRB: All things are delivered me of my Father. And no man knoweth the Son but the Father: neither doth any know the Father, but the Son, and to whom it shall please the Son to reveal.

text.NRSV: “Come to me, all you that are weary and are carrying heavy burdens, and I will give you rest.
text.Rotherham: Come unto me! all ye that toil and are burdened, and, I, will give you rest:
text.KJV: ¶ Come vnto me all yee that labour, and are heauy laden, and I will giue you rest.
text.NABRE: The Gentle Mastery of Christ. “Come to me, all you who labor and are burdened, and I will give you rest.
text.DRB: Come ye to me all that labour, and are burdened, and I will refresh you.

text.NRSV: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
text.Rotherham: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me,—because, meek, am I and lowly, in heart, and ye shall find rest unto your souls;
text.KJV: Take my yoke vpon you, and learne of me, for I am meeke and lowly in heart: and yee shall find rest vnto your soules.
text.NABRE: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am meek and humble of heart; and you will find rest for yourselves.
text.DRB: Take up my yoke upon you, and learn of me, because I am meek, and humble of heart: and you shall find rest to your souls.

text.NRSV: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
text.Rotherham: For, my yoke, is easy, and, my burden, light.
text.KJV: For my yoke is easie, and my burden is light.
text.NABRE: For my yoke is easy, and my burden light.”
text.DRB: For my yoke is sweet, and my burden light.

text.NRSV: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the sabbath; his disciples were hungry, and they began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
text.Rotherham: In that season, went Jesus, on, the sabbath, through the cornfields,—and, his disciples, hungered, and began to pluck ears of corn, and to eat.
text.KJV: At that time, Iesus went on the Sabbath day thorow the corne, & his Disciples were an hungred, and beganne to pluck the eares of corne, and to eate.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Picking Grain on the Sabbath. At that time Jesus was going through a field of grain on the sabbath. His disciples were hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.
text.DRB: AT that time Jesus went through the corn on the Sabboth: and his Disciples being hungry, began to pluck the ears, and to eat.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees saw it, they said to him, “Look, your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: But, the Pharisees, observing it, said unto him, Lo! thy disciples, are doing what is not allowed to do, on sabbath.
text.KJV: But when the Pharises saw it, they said vnto him, Behold, thy Disciples doe that which is not lawfull to doe vpon the Sabbath day.
text.NABRE: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, “See, your disciples are doing what is unlawful to do on the sabbath.”
text.DRB: And the Pharisees seeing them, said to him: Lo, thy Disciples do that which is not lawful for them to do on the Sabboth-days.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them, have ye never read what, David, did, when he hungered, and they who were with him? how
text.KJV: But he said vnto them, Haue yee not read what Dauid did when hee was an hungred, and they that were with him,
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry,
text.DRB: But he said to them: Have you not read what David did when he was an hungered, and they that were with him:

text.NRSV: He entered the house of God and ate the bread of the Presence, which it was not lawful for him or his companions to eat, but only for the priests.
text.Rotherham: he entered into the house of God and, the presence-bread, did eat, which it was not, allowable, for him to eat, nor for them who were with him,—save for the priests, alone?
text.KJV: How he entred into the house of God, and did eate the shew bread, which was not lawfull for him to eate, neither for them which were with him, but, only for the Priests?
text.NABRE: how he went into the house of God and ate the bread of offering, which neither he nor his companions but only the priests could lawfully eat?
text.DRB: how he entered into the house of God, and did eat the loaves of proposition, which it was not lawful for him to eat, nor for them that were with him, but for Priests only?

text.NRSV: Or have you not read in the law that on the sabbath the priests in the temple break the sabbath and yet are guiltless?
text.Rotherham: Or have ye not read, in the law, that, on the sabbaths, the priests, in the temple, the sabbath, profane, and are, blameless?
text.KJV: Or haue yee not read in the law, how that on the Sabbath dayes the Priests in the Temple profane the Sabbath, and are blamelesse?
text.NABRE: Or have you not read in the law that on the sabbath the priests serving in the temple violate the sabbath and are innocent?
text.DRB: Or have ye not read in the Law, that on Sabboth-days the Priests in the temple do break the Sabboth, & are without blame?

text.NRSV: I tell you, something greater than the temple is here.
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you,—Something greater than the Temple, is here!
text.KJV: But I saye vnto you, that in this place is one greater then the Temple.
text.NABRE: I say to you, something greater than the temple is here.
text.DRB: But I tell you that there is here a greater than the temple.

text.NRSV: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy and not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the guiltless.
text.Rotherham: If, however, ye had known what this meaneth—Mercy, I desire, and not, sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the blameless;
text.KJV: But if yee had knowen what this meaneth, I will haue mercy, and not sacrifice, yee would not haue condemned the guiltlesse.
text.NABRE: If you knew what this meant, ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned these innocent men.
text.DRB: And if you did know what it is, I will mercy, and not Sacrifice: you would never have condemned the innocents.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: For, the Son of Man, is, Lord of the Sabbath.
text.KJV: For the sonne of man is Lord euen of the Sabbath day.
text.NABRE: For the Son of Man is Lord of the sabbath.”
text.DRB: For the Son of man is Lord of the Sabboth also.

text.NRSV: He left that place and entered their synagogue;
text.Rotherham: And, passing on from thence, he came into their synagogue;
text.KJV: And when hee was departed thence, he went into their Synagogue.
text.NABRE: The Man with a Withered Hand. Moving on from there, he went into their synagogue.
text.DRB: And when he had passed from thence, he came into their Synagogue.

text.NRSV: a man was there with a withered hand, and they asked him, “Is it lawful to cure on the sabbath?” so that they might accuse him.
text.Rotherham: and lo! a man having, a withered hand, and they questioned him, saying, Is it allowable, on the sabbath, to heal? that they might accuse him.
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, there was a man which had his hand withered, and they asked him, saying, Is it lawfull to heale on the Sabbath dayes? that they might accuse him.
text.NABRE: And behold, there was a man there who had a withered hand. They questioned him, “Is it lawful to cure on the sabbath?” so that they might accuse him.
text.DRB: And behold there was a man which had a withered hand, and they asked him saying: Whether is it lawful to cure on the Sabboths? that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Suppose one of you has only one sheep and it falls into a pit on the sabbath; will you not lay hold of it and lift it out?
text.Rotherham: And said unto them, What man, from among yourselves, [shall there be],—Who shall have one sheep, and, if this should fall, on the sabbath, into a pit, will not lay hold of it, and raise it?
text.KJV: And hee said vnto them, What man shal there be among you, that shall haue one sheepe: and if it fall into a pit on the Sabbath day, will hee not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Which one of you who has a sheep that falls into a pit on the sabbath will not take hold of it and lift it out?
text.DRB: But he said to them: What man shall there be of you, that shall have one sheep: and if the same fall into a ditch on the Sabboths, will he not take hold and lift it up?

text.NRSV: How much more valuable is a human being than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: How much better, then, a man, than, a sheep? So that it is allowable, on the sabbath, nobly, to act.
text.KJV: How much then is a man better then a shepe? Wherfore it is lawfull to doe well on the Sabbath dayes.
text.NABRE: How much more valuable a person is than a sheep. So it is lawful to do good on the sabbath.”
text.DRB: How much better is a man more than a sheep? Therefore it is lawful on the Sabboths to do a good deed.

text.NRSV: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and it was restored, as sound as the other.
text.Rotherham: Then saith he unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand! And he stretched it forth,—and it was restored, whole, as the other.
text.KJV: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand: and hee stretched it forth, and it was restored whole, like as the other.
text.NABRE: Then he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and it was restored as sound as the other.
text.DRB: Then he saith to the man: Stretch forth thy hand, and he stretched it forth, and it was restored to health even as the other.

text.NRSV: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how to destroy him.
text.Rotherham: And the Pharisees, going forth, took, counsel, against him, to the end that, him, they might destroy.
text.KJV: ¶ Then the Pharises went out, and held a counsell against him, how they might destroy him.
text.NABRE: But the Pharisees went out and took counsel against him to put him to death. The Chosen Servant.
text.DRB: And the Pharisees going forth made a consultation against him, how they might destroy him.

text.NRSV: When Jesus became aware of this, he departed. Many crowds followed him, and he cured all of them,
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, taking note, retired from thence,—and many followed him, and he cured them all;
text.KJV: But when Iesus knew it, hee withdrew himselfe from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all,
text.NABRE: When Jesus realized this, he withdrew from that place. Many [people] followed him, and he cured them all,
text.DRB: But Jesus knowing it, retired from thence: and many followed him, and he cured them all.

text.NRSV: and he ordered them not to make him known.
text.Rotherham: and straitly charged them, lest they should make him, manifest:
text.KJV: And charged them that they should not make him knowen:
text.NABRE: but he warned them not to make him known.
text.DRB: and he charged them that they should not disclose him.

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:—
text.KJV: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the Prophet, saying,
text.NABRE: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through Isaiah the prophet:
text.DRB: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esay the Prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “Here is my servant, whom I have chosen, my beloved, with whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my servant, whom I have chosen, My beloved, in whom, my soul, delighteth,—I will put my Spirit upon him, and, justice, unto the nations, will he report:
text.KJV: Behold, my seruant whom I haue chosen, my beloued in whom my soule is well pleased: I will put my spirit vpon him, and he shall shew iudgement to the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved in whom I delight; I shall place my spirit upon him, and he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.
text.DRB: Behold my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved in whom my soul hath well liked. I will put my spirit upon him, and judgement to the Gentiles shall he shew.

text.NRSV: He will not wrangle or cry aloud, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
text.Rotherham: He will not strive, nor will he cry out, nor shall any hear, in the broadways, his voice:
text.KJV: He shall not striue, nor cry, neither shall any man heare his voice in the streets.
text.NABRE: He will not contend or cry out, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
text.DRB: He shall not contend, nor cry out, neither shall any man hear in the streets his voice.

text.NRSV: He will not break a bruised reed or quench a smoldering wick, until he brings justice to victory.
text.Rotherham: A bruised cane, will he not break, and, a smoking wick, will he not quench,—until he urge on, justice, to victory,
text.KJV: A bruised reed shal he not breake, and smoking flaxe shall he not quench, till he send forth iudgment vnto victory.
text.NABRE: A bruised reed he will not break, a smoldering wick he will not quench, until he brings justice to victory.
text.DRB: The reed bruised he shall not break, & smoking flax he shall not extinguish: till he cast forth judgement unto victory.

text.NRSV: And in his name the Gentiles will hope.”
text.Rotherham: And, in his name, shall nations hope.
text.KJV: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.
text.NABRE: And in his name the Gentiles will hope.” Jesus and Beelzebul.
text.DRB: And in his name the Gentiles shall hope.

text.NRSV: Then they brought to him a demoniac who was blind and mute; and he cured him, so that the one who had been mute could speak and see.
text.Rotherham: Then they brought unto him, one demonized, blind and dumb,—and he cured him, so that the dumb did speak and see.
text.KJV: ¶ Then was brought vnto him one possessed with a deuill, blinde, and dumbe: and hee healed him, insomuch that the blinde and dumbe both spake and saw.
text.NABRE: Then they brought to him a demoniac who was blind and mute. He cured the mute person so that he could speak and see.
text.DRB: Then was offered to him one possessed with a devil, blind and dumb: and he cured him, so that he spake and saw.

text.NRSV: All the crowds were amazed and said, “Can this be the Son of David?”
text.Rotherham: And all the multitudes were beside themselves, and were saying, Can, this one, be, the Son of David?
text.KJV: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is this the sonne of Dauid?
text.NABRE: All the crowd was astounded, and said, “Could this perhaps be the Son of David?”
text.DRB: And all the multitudes were amazed, and said: Whether this be the Son of David?

text.NRSV: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “It is only by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons, that this fellow casts out the demons.”
text.Rotherham: But, the Pharisees, hearing it, said, This one, doth not cast out the demons, save in Beelzebul ruler of the demons.
text.KJV: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doeth not cast out deuils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the deuils.
text.NABRE: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “This man drives out demons only by the power of Beelzebul, the prince of demons.”
text.DRB: But the Pharisees hearing it, said: This fellow casteth not out devils but in Beelzebub the prince of the devils.

text.NRSV: He knew what they were thinking and said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste, and no city or house divided against itself will stand.
text.Rotherham: And, knowing their inward thoughts, he said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself, is laid waste,—and, no city or house divided against itself, will stand;
text.KJV: And Iesus knew their thoughts, and said vnto them, Euery kingdome diuided against it selfe, is brought to desolation: and euery citie or house diuided against it selfe, shall not stand.
text.NABRE: But he knew what they were thinking and said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and no town or house divided against itself will stand.
text.DRB: And Jesus knowing their cogitations, said to them: Every kingdom divided against itself shall be made desolate: and every city or house divided against itself, shall not stand.

text.NRSV: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself; how then will his kingdom stand?
text.Rotherham: And, if, Satan, is casting, Satan, out, against himself, hath he become divided,—How then shall, his kingdom, stand?
text.KJV: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is diuided against himselfe; how shall then his kingdome stand?
text.NABRE: And if Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself; how, then, will his kingdom stand?
text.DRB: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself: how then shall his kingdom stand?

text.NRSV: If I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your own exorcists cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
text.Rotherham: And, if, I, in Beelzebul, am casting out the demons, In whom are, your sons, casting them out? Wherefore, they, shall be, judges of you.
text.KJV: And if I by Beelzebub cast out deuils, by whom doe your children cast them out? Therefore they shall be your Iudges.
text.NABRE: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your own people drive them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
text.DRB: And if I in Beelzebub cast out devils, your children in whom do they cast out? Therefore they shall be your judges.

text.NRSV: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out demons, then the kingdom of God has come to you.
text.Rotherham: But, if, in God’s Spirit, I, am casting out the demons, then doubtless hath come upon you unawares, the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: But if I cast out deuils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdome of God is come vnto you.
text.NABRE: But if it is by the Spirit of God that I drive out demons, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
text.DRB: But if I in the Spirit of God do cast out devils, then is the Kingdom of God come upon you.

text.NRSV: Or how can one enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property, without first tying up the strong man? Then indeed the house can be plundered.
text.Rotherham: Or how can one enter the house of the mighty and, seize his goods, unless, first, he bind the mighty one? and, then, his house, he will plunder.
text.KJV: Or else, how can one enter into a strong mans house, & spoile his goods, except hee first binde the strong man, and then he will spoile his house.
text.NABRE: How can anyone enter a strong man’s house and steal his property, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.
text.DRB: Or how can a man enter into the house of the strong, and rifle his vessels, unless he first bind the strong? and then he will rifle his house.

text.NRSV: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
text.Rotherham: He that is no with me, is, against me,—and, he that gathereth not with me, scattereth
text.KJV: He that is not with me, is against me: and hee that gathereth not with me, scattereth abroad.
text.NABRE: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
text.DRB: He that is not with me, is against me: and he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.

text.NRSV: Therefore I tell you, people will be forgiven for every sin and blasphemy, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, I say unto you, All sin and profane speaking, shall be forgiven unto men,—but, the speaking profanely of the Spirit, shall not be forgiven;
