text.KJV: ¶ Wherefore I say vnto you, All maner of sinne and blasphemie shall be forgiuen vnto men: but the blasphemie against the holy Ghost, shall not bee forgiuen vnto men.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I say to you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven people, but blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
text.DRB: Therefore I say to you: Every sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven men, but the blasphemy of the Spirit shall not be forgiven.

text.NRSV: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him,—but, whosoever shall speak against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, either in this age, or the coming.
text.KJV: And whosoeuer speaketh a word against the sonne of man, it shall be forgiuen him: but whosoeuer speaketh against the holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiuen him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
text.NABRE: And whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven; but whoever speaks against the holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the age to come.
text.DRB: And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but he that shall speak against the Holy-Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him neither in this world, nor in the world to come.

text.NRSV: “Either make the tree good, and its fruit good; or make the tree bad, and its fruit bad; for the tree is known by its fruit.
text.Rotherham: Either make the tree good, and its fruit good, or make the tree worthless, and its fruit worthless; for, from the fruit, the tree is known.
text.KJV: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good: Or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: For the tree is knowen by his fruit.
text.NABRE: A Tree and Its Fruits. “Either declare the tree good and its fruit is good, or declare the tree rotten and its fruit is rotten, for a tree is known by its fruit.
text.DRB: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good: or make the tree evil, and his fruit evil. For of the fruit the tree is known.

text.NRSV: You brood of vipers! How can you speak good things, when you are evil? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
text.Rotherham: Broods of vipers! How can ye speak, good things, being, evil? For, out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaketh.
text.KJV: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being euil, speake good things? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
text.NABRE: You brood of vipers, how can you say good things when you are evil? For from the fullness of the heart the mouth speaks.
text.DRB: You vipers' broods, how can you speak good things, whereas you are evil? For of the aboundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.

text.NRSV: The good person out of a good treasure brings forth good, and the evil person out of an evil treasure brings forth evil.
text.Rotherham: The good man, out of the good treasure, putteth forth, good things; and, the evil man, out of the evil treasure, putteth forth, evil things.
text.KJV: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart, bringeth foorth good things: and an euill man out of the euill treasure, bringeth foorth euill things.
text.NABRE: A good person brings forth good out of a store of goodness, but an evil person brings forth evil out of a store of evil.
text.DRB: Agood man out of a good treasure bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of an evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.

text.NRSV: I tell you, on the day of judgment you will have to give an account for every careless word you utter;
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you, That, every useless expression that men shall utter, they shall render, concerning it, an account, in a day of judgment;
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, That euery idle word that men shall speake, they shall giue accompt thereof in the day of Iudgement.
text.NABRE: I tell you, on the day of judgment people will render an account for every careless word they speak.
text.DRB: But I say unto you, that every idle word that men shall speak, they shall render an account for it in the day of judgement.

text.NRSV: for by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”
text.Rotherham: For, by thy words, shalt thou be, justified, and, by thy words, shalt thou be condemned.
text.KJV: For by thy wordes thou shalt bee iustified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
text.NABRE: By your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.” The Demand for a Sign.
text.DRB: For of thy words thou shalt be justified, and of thy words thou shalt be condemned.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, “Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you.”
text.Rotherham: Then, answered him, certain of the Scribes and Pharisees, saying, Teacher! we desire of thee, a sign, to behold.
text.KJV: ¶ Then certaine of the Scribes, and of the Pharisees, answered, saying, Master, we would see a signe from thee.
text.NABRE: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, “Teacher, we wish to see a sign from you.”
text.DRB: Then answered him certain of the Scribes and Pharisees, saying: Master, we would see a sign from thee.

text.NRSV: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them, A wicked and adulterous generation, a sign, doth seek, and, a sign, will not be, given, it, save the sign of Jonah the prophet.
text.KJV: But hee answered, and said to them, An euill and adulterous generation seeketh after a signe, and there shall no signe be giuen to it, but the signe of the Prophet Ionas.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “An evil and unfaithful generation seeks a sign, but no sign will be given it except the sign of Jonah the prophet.
text.DRB: Who answered, and said to them: The wicked and adulterous Generation seeketh a sign: and a sign shall not be given it, but the sign of Jonas the Prophet.

text.NRSV: For just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the sea monster, so for three days and three nights the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth.
text.Rotherham: For, just as was Jonah in the belly of the sea-monster three days and three nights, so, will be the Son of Man, in the heart of the earth, three days and three nights.
text.KJV: For as Ionas was three dayes and three nights in the whales belly: so shal the sonne of man be three daies and three nights in the heart of the earth.
text.NABRE: Just as Jonah was in the belly of the whale three days and three nights, so will the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.
text.DRB: For as Jonas was in the whale's belly three days and three nights; so shall the Son of man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

text.NRSV: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the proclamation of Jonah, and see, something greater than Jonah is here!
text.Rotherham: Men of Nineveh, will rise up in the judgment, with this generation, and will condemn it,—because they repented into the proclamation of Jonah; and lo! something greater than Jonah, here.
text.KJV: The men of Nineue shall rise in iudgement with this generation, and shall condemne it, because they repented at the preaching of Ionas, and behold, a greater then Ionas is here.
text.NABRE: At the judgment, the men of Nineveh will arise with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and there is something greater than Jonah here.
text.DRB: The men of Ninivee shall rise in the judgement with this Generation, and shall condemn it: because they did penance at the preaching of Jonas. And behold more than Jonas here.

text.NRSV: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon, and see, something greater than Solomon is here!
text.Rotherham: The queen of the south, will arise in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it—because she came out of the ends of the earth, to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and lo! something greater than Solomon, here.
text.KJV: The Queene of the South shall rise vp in the iudgement with this generation, and shall condemne it: for she came from the vttermost parts of the earth to heare the wisedome of Solomon, and behold, a greater then Solomon is here.
text.NABRE: At the judgment the queen of the south will arise with this generation and condemn it, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and there is something greater than Solomon here. The Return of the Unclean Spirit.
text.DRB: The queen of the South shall rise in the judgement with this Generation, and shall condemn it: because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Salomon, and behold more than Salomon here.

text.NRSV: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through waterless regions looking for a resting place, but it finds none.
text.Rotherham: But, when the impure spirit goeth out of the man, it passeth through waterless places, seeking rest,—and findeth it not.
text.KJV: When the vncleane spirit is gone out of a man, hee walketh thorow dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
text.NABRE: “When an unclean spirit goes out of a person it roams through arid regions searching for rest but finds none.
text.DRB: And when an unclean spirit shall go out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth not.

text.NRSV: Then it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’ When it comes, it finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
text.Rotherham: Then, it saith, Into my house, will I return whence I came out,—and, coming, findeth it empty [and] swept and adorned.
text.KJV: Then he saith, I will returne into my house from whence I came out; And when he is come, he findeth it emptie, swept, and garnished.
text.NABRE: Then it says, ‘I will return to my home from which I came.’ But upon returning, it finds it empty, swept clean, and put in order.
text.DRB: Then he saith: I will return into my house whence I came out. And coming he findeth it vacant, swept with besoms, and trimmed.

text.NRSV: Then it goes and brings along seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and live there; and the last state of that person is worse than the first. So will it be also with this evil generation.”
text.Rotherham: Then, it goeth, and taketh along with itself, seven diverse spirits, more wicked than itself,—and, entering, abideth there; and, the last state of that man, becometh, worse than the first. So, shall it be, with this, wicked, generation.
text.KJV: Then goeth he, and taketh with himselfe seuen other spirits more wicked then himselfe, and they enter in and dwell there: And the last state of that man is worse then the first. Euen so shal it be also vnto this wicked generation.
text.NABRE: Then it goes and brings back with itself seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they move in and dwell there; and the last condition of that person is worse than the first. Thus it will be with this evil generation.” The True Family of Jesus.
text.DRB: Then goeth he, and taketh with him seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last of that man be made worse than the first. So shall it be also to this wicked Generation.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and his brothers were standing outside, wanting to speak to him.
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking unto the multitudes, lo! his mother and brethren, were standing without, seeking to speak with him.
text.KJV: ¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speake with him.
text.NABRE: While he was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and his brothers appeared outside, wishing to speak with him.
text.DRB: As he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold his mother and his brethren stood without, seeking to speak to him.

text.NRSV: Someone told him, “Look, your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you.”
text.Rotherham: [And one said to him, Lo! thy mother and thy brethren, without, are standing, seeking, to speak, with thee.]
text.KJV: Then one saide vnto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speake with thee.
text.NABRE: [Someone told him, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, asking to speak with you.”]
text.DRB: And one said unto him: Behold thy mother and thy brethren stand without, seeking thee.

text.NRSV: But to the one who had told him this, Jesus replied, “Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto him that was telling him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
text.KJV: But he answered, and said vnto him that told him, Who is my mother? And who are my brethren?
text.NABRE: But he said in reply to the one who told him, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”
text.DRB: But he answering him that told him, said: Who is my mother, and who are my brethren?

text.NRSV: And pointing to his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
text.Rotherham: And, stretching forth his hand towards his disciples, he said, Lo! my mother and my brethren!
text.KJV: And hee stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold, my mother and my brethren.
text.NABRE: And stretching out his hand toward his disciples, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text.DRB: And stretching forth his hand upon his Disciples, he said: Behold my mother and my brethren.

text.NRSV: For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall do the will of my Father who is in the heavens, he, is my, brother, and sister, and mother.
text.KJV: For whosoeuer shall doe the will of my Father which is in heauen, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.
text.NABRE: For whoever does the will of my heavenly Father is my brother, and sister, and mother.”
text.DRB: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father, that is in Heaven: he is my brother, and sister, and mother.

text.NRSV: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat beside the sea.
text.Rotherham: On that day, Jesus, going out of the house, was sitting near the sea:
text.KJV: The same day went Iesus out of the house, and sate by the sea side.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 The Parable of the Sower. On that day, Jesus went out of the house and sat down by the sea.
text.DRB: The same day Jesus going out of the house, sat by the sea side.

text.NRSV: Such great crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat there, while the whole crowd stood on the beach.
text.Rotherham: and there were gathered unto him, large multitudes, so that, he, into a boat, entered, and was sitting, and, all the multitude, on the beach was standing.
text.KJV: And great multitudes were gathered together vnto him, so that hee went into a ship, and sate, and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
text.NABRE: Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down, and the whole crowd stood along the shore.
text.DRB: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, in so much that he went up into a boat & sat: and all the multitude stood in the shore;

text.NRSV: And he told them many things in parables, saying: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto them many things, in parables, saying: Lo! the sower went forth to sow,—
text.KJV: And hee spake many things vnto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went foorth to sow.
text.NABRE: And he spoke to them at length in parables, saying: “A sower went out to sow.
text.DRB: and he spake to them many things in parables, saying: Behold the Sower went forth to sow.

text.NRSV: And as he sowed, some seeds fell on the path, and the birds came and ate them up.
text.Rotherham: and, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell by the pathway, and, the birds, came, and devoured it;
text.KJV: And when he sowed, some seedes fell by the wayes side, and the foules came, and deuoured them vp.
text.NABRE: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and birds came and ate it up.
text.DRB: And whiles he soweth, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air did come and eat it.

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell on rocky ground, where they did not have much soil, and they sprang up quickly, since they had no depth of soil.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell on the rocky places, where it had not much earth,—and, straightway, it sprang up, because if had no depth of earth;
text.KJV: Some fell vpon stony places, where they had not much earth: and foorth with they sprung vp, because they had no deepenesse of earth.
text.NABRE: Some fell on rocky ground, where it had little soil. It sprang up at once because the soil was not deep,
text.DRB: Other some also fell upon rocky places, where they had not much earth: and they shot up incontinent, because they had not deepness of earth,

text.NRSV: But when the sun rose, they were scorched; and since they had no root, they withered away.
text.Rotherham: and, the sun arising, it was scorched, and, because it had no root, it withered away;
text.KJV: And when the Sunne was vp, they were scorched: and because they had not root, they withered away.
text.NABRE: and when the sun rose it was scorched, and it withered for lack of roots.
text.DRB: and after the sun was up, they parched: and because they had not root, they withered.

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell upon the thorns, and the thorns came up, and choked it;
text.KJV: And some fell among thorns: and the thornes sprung vp, & choked them.
text.NABRE: Some seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it.
text.DRB: And other fell among thorns: and the thorns grew and choked them.

text.NRSV: Other seeds fell on good soil and brought forth grain, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
text.Rotherham: But, some, fell upon the good ground, and did yield fruit,—this, indeed a hundred fold, and, that, sixty, and, the other, thirty.
text.KJV: But other fell into good ground, and brought foorth fruit, some an hundred folde, some sixtie folde, some thirty folde.
text.NABRE: But some seed fell on rich soil, and produced fruit, a hundred or sixty or thirtyfold.
text.DRB: And other some fell upon good ground: and they yielded fruit, the one an hundredfold, the other threescore, and another thirty.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears listen!”
text.Rotherham: He that hath ears, let him hear.
text.KJV: Who hath eares to heare, let him heare.
text.NABRE: Whoever has ears ought to hear.”
text.DRB: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: Then the disciples came and asked him, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, coming near, said to him, Wherefore, in parables, art thou speaking to them?
text.KJV: And the disciples came, and sayd vnto him, Why speakest thou vnto them in parables?
text.NABRE: The Purpose of Parables. The disciples approached him and said, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”
text.DRB: And his Disciples came and said to him: Why speakest thou to them in parables?

text.NRSV: He answered, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said, Because, unto you, hath it been given, to get to know the sacred secrets of the kingdom of the heavens,—whereas, unto them, hath it not been given.
text.KJV: He answered, and said vnto them, Because it is giuen vnto you to know the mysteries of the kingdome of heauen, but to them it is not giuen.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “Because knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been granted to you, but to them it has not been granted.
text.DRB: Who answered and said unto them: Because to you it is given to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of Heaven: but to them it is not given.

text.NRSV: For to those who have, more will be given, and they will have an abundance; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever hath, it shall be given, to him, and he shall be made to abound,—but, whoever hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken from him?
text.KJV: For whosoeuer hath, to him shall be giuen, and he shall haue more abundance: but whosoeuer hath not, from him shall be taken away, euen that hee hath.
text.NABRE: To anyone who has, more will be given and he will grow rich; from anyone who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
text.DRB: For he that hath, to him shall be given, and he shall abound: but he that hath not, from him shall be taken away that also which he hath.

text.NRSV: The reason I speak to them in parables is that ‘seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.’
text.Rotherham: For this reason, in parables, unto them, do I speak,—because, seeing, they see not, and, hearing, they hear not,—neither do they understand.
text.KJV: Therefore speake I to then in parables: because they seeing, see not: and hearing, they heare not, neither doe they vnderstand.
text.NABRE: This is why I speak to them in parables, because ‘they look but do not see and hear but do not listen or understand.’
text.DRB: Therefore in parables I speak to them: because seeing they see not, and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand:

text.NRSV: With them indeed is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah that says: ‘You will indeed listen, but never understand, and you will indeed look, but never perceive.
text.Rotherham: And, again is being fulfilled in them, the prophecy of Isaiah, which saith,—They shall, surely hear, and yet will not understand, and, surely see, and yet not perceive;
text.KJV: And in them is fulfilled the prophecie of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall heare, and shall not vnderstand: and seeing yee shall see, and shall not perceiue.
text.NABRE: Isaiah’s prophecy is fulfilled in them, which says: ‘You shall indeed hear but not understand, you shall indeed look but never see.
text.DRB: and the Prophecy of Esay is fulfilled in them, which saith: With hearing shall you hear; and you shall not understand: and seeing shall you see, and you shall not see.

text.NRSV: For this people’s heart has grown dull, and their ears are hard of hearing, and they have shut their eyes; so that they might not look with their eyes, and listen with their ears, and understand with their heart and turn—and I would heal them.’
text.Rotherham: For the heart of this people hath become dense, and, with their ears, heavily have they heard, and, their eyes, have they closed,—lest, once they should see with their eyes, and, with their ears, should hear, and, with their hearts, should understand, and return; when I would certainly heal them.
text.KJV: For this peoples heart is waxed grosse, and their eares are dull of hearing, and their eyes they haue closed, lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and heare with their eares, and should vnderstand with their heart, and should be conuerted, and I should heale them.
text.NABRE: Gross is the heart of this people, they will hardly hear with their ears, they have closed their eyes, lest they see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart and be converted, and I heal them.’ The Privilege of Discipleship.
text.DRB: For the heart of this people is waxed gross, and with their ears they have heavily heard, and their eyes they have shut: lest at any time they may see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart and be converted, and I may heal them.

text.NRSV: But blessed are your eyes, for they see, and your ears, for they hear.
text.Rotherham: But happy are, your, eyes, that they see, and your ears, that they hear;
text.KJV: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your eares, for they heare.
text.NABRE: “But blessed are your eyes, because they see, and your ears, because they hear.
text.DRB: But blessed are your eyes because they do see, and your ears because they do hear.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see, but did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but did not hear it.
text.Rotherham: For, verily, I say unto you—Many prophets and righteous men, have coveted to see what ye see, and have not seen, and to hear what ye hear, and have not heard.
text.KJV: For verely I say vnto you, that many Prophets, and righteous men haue desired to see those things which yee see, and haue not seene them: and to heare those things which ye heare, and haue not heard them.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it. The Explanation of the Parable of the Sower.
text.DRB: For amen I say to you, that many Prophets and just men have desired to see the things that you see, and have not seen them: and to hear the things that you hear, and have not heard them.

text.NRSV: “Hear then the parable of the sower.
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye, then, the parable of him that sowed:—
text.KJV: ¶ Heare ye therefore the parable of the sower.
text.NABRE: “Hear then the parable of the sower.
text.DRB: Hear you therefore the parable of the sower.

text.NRSV: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what is sown in the heart; this is what was sown on the path.
text.Rotherham: When anyone heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, the wicked one cometh, and catcheth up that which hath been sown in his heart,—this, is he, by the pathway, sown.
text.KJV: When any one heareth the word of the kingdome, and vnderstandeth it not, then commeth the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sowen in his heart: this is hee which receiued seede by the way side.
text.NABRE: The seed sown on the path is the one who hears the word of the kingdom without understanding it, and the evil one comes and steals away what was sown in his heart.
text.DRB: Every one that heareth the word of the Kingdom and understandeth not, there cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart: this is he that was sown by the way side.

text.NRSV: As for what was sown on rocky ground, this is the one who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy;
text.Rotherham: And, he on the rocky places sown, the same, is he that, heareth the word, and, straightway, with joy, receiveth it;
text.KJV: But he that receiued the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, & anon with ioy receiueth it:
text.NABRE: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and receives it at once with joy.
text.DRB: And he that was sown upon rocky places: this is he that heareth the word, and incontinent receiveth it with joy,

text.NRSV: yet such a person has no root, but endures only for a while, and when trouble or persecution arises on account of the word, that person immediately falls away.
text.Rotherham: yet hath he no root in himself, but is, only for a season,—and, there arising, tribulation or persecution because of the word, straightway, he findeth cause of stumbling.
text.KJV: Yet hath hee not root in himselfe, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
text.NABRE: But he has no root and lasts only for a time. When some tribulation or persecution comes because of the word, he immediately falls away.
text.DRB: yet hath he not root in himself, but is for a time: and when there falleth tribulation and persecution for the word, he is by and by scandalized.

text.NRSV: As for what was sown among thorns, this is the one who hears the word, but the cares of the world and the lure of wealth choke the word, and it yields nothing.
text.Rotherham: And, he among the thorns sown, the same, is he that, heareth the word,—and, the anxiety of the age and the deceit of riches, choke up the word, and, unfruitful, it becometh.
text.KJV: He also that receiued seed among the thorns, is he that heareth the word, and the care of this world, and the deceitfulnesse of riches choke the word, and he becommeth vnfruitfull.
text.NABRE: The seed sown among thorns is the one who hears the word, but then worldly anxiety and the lure of riches choke the word and it bears no fruit.
text.DRB: And he that was sown among thorns, this is he that heareth the word, and the carefulness of this world and the deceitfulness of riches choket up the word, and he becometh fruitless.

text.NRSV: But as for what was sown on good soil, this is the one who hears the word and understands it, who indeed bears fruit and yields, in one case a hundredfold, in another sixty, and in another thirty.”
text.Rotherham: But, he on the good ground sown, the same, is he who doth hear and understand the word, who, indeed, beareth fruit and produceth,—this, a hundred, and, that, sixty, and, the other, thirty.
text.KJV: But he that receiued seed into the good ground, is hee that heareth the word, and vnderstandeth it, which also beareth fruit, and bringeth foorth, some an hundred fold, some sixtie, some thirty.
text.NABRE: But the seed sown on rich soil is the one who hears the word and understands it, who indeed bears fruit and yields a hundred or sixty or thirtyfold.”
text.DRB: But he that was sown upon good ground: this is he that heareth the word, and understandeth, and bringeth fruit, and yieldeth some an hundred-fold, and another threescore, and another thirty.

text.NRSV: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to someone who sowed good seed in his field;
text.Rotherham: Another parable, put he before them, saying—The kingdom of the heavens hath become like a man sowing good seed in his field;
text.KJV: ¶ Another parable put he forth vnto them, saying; The kingdome of heauen is likened vnto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
text.NABRE: The Parable of the Weeds Among the Wheat. He proposed another parable to them. “The kingdom of heaven may be likened to a man who sowed good seed in his field.
text.DRB: Another parable he proposed to them, saying: The Kingdom of Heaven is resembled to a man that sowed good seed in his field.

text.NRSV: but while everybody was asleep, an enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and then went away.
text.Rotherham: and, while men were sleeping, his enemy came, and sowed over darnel, in among the wheat,—and away he went.
text.KJV: But while men slept, his enemy came & sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
text.NABRE: While everyone was asleep his enemy came and sowed weeds all through the wheat, and then went off.
text.DRB: But when men were a sleep, his enemy came and oversowed cockle among the wheat, and went his way.

text.NRSV: So when the plants came up and bore grain, then the weeds appeared as well.
text.Rotherham: And, when the blade shot up, and brought forth, fruit, then, appeared, the darnel also.
text.KJV: But when the blade was sprung vp, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
text.NABRE: When the crop grew and bore fruit, the weeds appeared as well.
text.DRB: And when the blade was shot up, and had brought forth fruit, then appeared also the cockle.

text.NRSV: And the slaves of the householder came and said to him, ‘Master, did you not sow good seed in your field? Where, then, did these weeds come from?’
text.Rotherham: And the servants of the householder, coming near, said to him,—Sir! was it not, good seed, thou didst sow in thy field? Whence then hath it, darnel?
text.KJV: So the seruants of the housholder came, and said vnto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seede in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
text.NABRE: The slaves of the householder came to him and said, ‘Master, did you not sow good seed in your field? Where have the weeds come from?’
text.DRB: And the servants of the goodman of the house coming said to him: Sir, didst thou not sow good seed in thy field? Whence then hath it cockle?

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘An enemy has done this.’ The slaves said to him, ‘Then do you want us to go and gather them?’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—An enemy, hath, done this. And they say, unto him—Wilt thou, then, that we go and collect it?
text.KJV: He said vnto them, An enemy hath done this. The seruants said vnto him, Wilt thou then that we goe and gather them vp?
text.NABRE: He answered, ‘An enemy has done this.’ His slaves said to him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’
text.DRB: And he said to them: The enemy-man hath done this. And the servants said to him: Wilt thou we go and gather it up?

text.NRSV: But he replied, ‘No; for in gathering the weeds you would uproot the wheat along with them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, saith—Nay! lest, at any time, while collecting the darnel, ye uproot, along with it, the wheat:
text.KJV: But he said, Nay: lest while yee gather vp the tares, ye root vp also the wheat with them.
text.NABRE: He replied, ‘No, if you pull up the weeds you might uproot the wheat along with them.
text.DRB: And he said: No lest perhaps gathering up the cockle, you may root up the wheat also together with it.

text.NRSV: Let both of them grow together until the harvest; and at harvest time I will tell the reapers, Collect the weeds first and bind them in bundles to be burned, but gather the wheat into my barn.’”
text.Rotherham: Suffer both to grow together until the harvest, and at, harvest time, I will say unto the reapers,—Collect ye first, the darnel, and bind it into bundles, with a view to the burning it up; but, the wheat, be gathering it into my barn.
text.KJV: Let both grow together vntil the haruest: and in the time of haruest, I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and binde them in bundels to burne them: but gather the wheat into my barne.
text.NABRE: Let them grow together until harvest; then at harvest time I will say to the harvesters, “First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles for burning; but gather the wheat into my barn.”’” The Parable of the Mustard Seed.
text.DRB: Suffer both to grow until the harvest, and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers: Gather up first the cockle, and bind it into bundles to burn, but the wheat gather ye into my barn.

text.NRSV: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and sowed in his field;
text.Rotherham: Another parable, put he before them, saying—The kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field;
text.KJV: ¶ Another parable put he foorth vnto them, saying, The kingdome of heauen is like to a graine of mustard seed, which a man tooke, and sowed in his field.
text.NABRE: He proposed another parable to them. “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a person took and sowed in a field.
text.DRB: Another parable he proposed unto them, saying: The Kingdom of Heaven is like to a mustard-seed, which a man took and sowed in his field.

text.NRSV: it is the smallest of all the seeds, but when it has grown it is the greatest of shrubs and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and make nests in its branches.”
text.Rotherham: which, indeed, is, less, than all seeds, but, when grown, is greater than garden-plants, and becometh a tree,—so that the birds of heaven come, and lodge among its branches.
text.KJV: Which indeed is the least of al seeds: but when it is growen, it is the greatest among herbes, and becommeth a tree: so that the birds of the aire come and lodge in the branches thereof.
text.NABRE: It is the smallest of all the seeds, yet when full-grown it is the largest of plants. It becomes a large bush, and the ‘birds of the sky come and dwell in its branches.’”
text.DRB: Which is the least surely of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is greater than all herbs, and is made a tree, so that the fowls of the air come, and dwell in the branches thereof.

text.NRSV: He told them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed in with three measures of flour until all of it was leavened.”
text.Rotherham: Another parable, [spake he unto them]:—The kingdom of the heavens is like, unto leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour until, the whole, was leavened.
text.KJV: ¶ Another parable spake he vnto them, The kingdome of heauen is like vnto leauen, which a woman tooke, and hid in three measures of meale, till the whole was leauened.
text.NABRE: The Parable of the Yeast. He spoke to them another parable. “The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed with three measures of wheat flour until the whole batch was leavened.”
text.DRB: Another parable he spake to them: The Kingdom of Heaven is like to leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, until the whole was leavened.

text.NRSV: Jesus told the crowds all these things in parables; without a parable he told them nothing.
text.Rotherham: All these things, spake Jesus in parables, unto the multitudes, and, without a parable, was he speaking, nothing, unto them:
text.KJV: All these things spake Iesus vnto the multitude in parables, and without a parable spake hee not vnto them:
text.NABRE: The Use of Parables. All these things Jesus spoke to the crowds in parables. He spoke to them only in parables,
text.DRB: All these things Jesus spake in parables to the multitudes, and without parables he did not speak to them:

text.NRSV: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet: “I will open my mouth to speak in parables; I will proclaim what has been hidden from the foundation of the world.”
text.Rotherham: that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through the prophet, saying—I will open, in parables, my mouth, I will bring up things hidden from the foundation!
text.KJV: That it might bee fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables, I wil vtter things which haue bin kept secret from the foundation of the world.
text.NABRE: to fulfill what had been said through the prophet: “I will open my mouth in parables, I will announce what has lain hidden from the foundation [of the world].”
text.DRB: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet saying: I will open my mouth in parables, I will utter things hidden from the foundation of the world.

text.NRSV: Then he left the crowds and went into the house. And his disciples approached him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the weeds of the field.”
text.Rotherham: Then, dismissing the multitudes, he went into the house, and his disciples came near to him, saying—Make quite plain to us the parable of the darnel of the field.
text.KJV: Then Iesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came vnto him, saying, Declare vnto vs the parable of the tares of the field.
text.NABRE: The Explanation of the Parable of the Weeds. Then, dismissing the crowds, he went into the house. His disciples approached him and said, “Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.”
text.DRB: Then having dismissed the multitudes, he came into the house, and his Disciples came unto him, saying: Expound us the parable of the cockle of the field.

text.NRSV: He answered, “The one who sows the good seed is the Son of Man;
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—He that soweth the good seed, is the Son of Man;
text.KJV: He answered, and said vnto them, Hee that soweth the good seed, is the sonne of man.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “He who sows good seed is the Son of Man,
text.DRB: Who made answer & said to them: He that soweth the good seed, is the Son of man.

text.NRSV: the field is the world, and the good seed are the children of the kingdom; the weeds are the children of the evil one,
text.Rotherham: And, the field, is the world, and, the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom,—and, the darnel seeds, are the sons of the evil one;
text.KJV: The field is the world. The good seed, are the children of the kingdome: but the tares are the children of the wicked one.
text.NABRE: the field is the world, the good seed the children of the kingdom. The weeds are the children of the evil one,
text.DRB: And the field, is the world. And the good seed: these are the children of the kingdom. And the cockle: are the children of the wicked one.

text.NRSV: and the enemy who sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.
text.Rotherham: And, the enemy that sowed them, is the adversary, and, the harvest, is, the conclusion of an age, and, the reapers, are, messengers.
text.KJV: The enemie that sowed them, is the deuill. The haruest, is the ende of the world. And the reapers are the Angels.
text.NABRE: and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.
text.DRB: And the enemy that sowed them, is the devil. But the harvest, is the end of the world. And the reapers, are the Angels.

text.NRSV: Just as the weeds are collected and burned with fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
text.Rotherham: Just, therefore, as collected is the darnel, and, with fire is burned, so, will it be in the conclusion of the age:—
text.KJV: As therefore the tares are gathered and burnt in the fire: so shall it be in the end of this world.
text.NABRE: Just as weeds are collected and burned [up] with fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
text.DRB: Even as cockle therefore is gathered up, and burnt with fire: so shall it be in the end of the world.

text.NRSV: The Son of Man will send his angels, and they will collect out of his kingdom all causes of sin and all evildoers,
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man will send forth his messengers, and they will collect, out of his kingdom, all the causes of stumbling, and the doers of lawlessness,
text.KJV: The Sonne of man shall send forth his Angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdome all things that offend, and them which doe iniquitie:
text.NABRE: The Son of Man will send his angels, and they will collect out of his kingdom all who cause others to sin and all evildoers.
text.DRB: The Son of man shall send his Angels, and they shall gather out of his Kingdom all scandals, and them that work iniquity:

text.NRSV: and they will throw them into the furnace of fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: and will cast them into the furnace of fire: there, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
text.KJV: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wayling and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: They will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.
text.DRB: and shall cast them into the furnace of fire: There shall be weeping & gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Let anyone with ears listen!
text.Rotherham: Then, the righteous, will shine forth as the sun, in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears, let him hear!
text.KJV: Then shall the righteous shine foorth as the Sunne, in the kingdome of their father. Who hath eares to heare, let him heare.
text.NABRE: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Whoever has ears ought to hear. More Parables.
text.DRB: Then shall the just shine as the sun, in the Kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: “The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field, which someone found and hid; then in his joy he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
text.Rotherham: The kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a treasure hid in the field, which a man, finding, hid,—and, by reason of his joy, withdraweth and selleth whatsoever he hath, and buyeth that field.
text.KJV: ¶ Againe, the kingdome of heauen is like vnto treasure hid in a field: the which when a man hath found, hee hideth, and for ioy thereof goeth and selleth all that hee hath, and buyeth that field.
text.NABRE: “The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure buried in a field, which a person finds and hides again, and out of joy goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
text.DRB: The Kingdom of Heaven is like a treasure hidden in a field. Which a man having found, did hide it, and for joy thereof goeth, and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.

text.NRSV: “Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls;
text.Rotherham: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is, like, unto, a merchant, seeking beautiful pearls,—
text.KJV: ¶ Againe, the kingdome of heauen is like vnto a marchant man, seeking goodly pearles:
text.NABRE: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant searching for fine pearls.
text.DRB: Again the Kingdom of Heaven is like to a merchant man, seeking good pearls.

text.NRSV: on finding one pearl of great value, he went and sold all that he had and bought it.
text.Rotherham: and, finding one very precious pearl, departing, he at once sold all things, whatsoever he had, and bought it.
text.KJV: Who when hee had found one pearle of great price, he went and solde all that he had, and bought it.
text.NABRE: When he finds a pearl of great price, he goes and sells all that he has and buys it.
text.DRB: And having found one precious pearl, he went his way, and sold all that he had, and bought it.

text.NRSV: “Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was thrown into the sea and caught fish of every kind;
text.Rotherham: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like unto, a large drag-net, cast into the sea and gathering of every kind,—
text.KJV: ¶ Againe, the kingdome of heauen is like vnto a net that was cast into the sea, and gathered of euery kind,
text.NABRE: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net thrown into the sea, which collects fish of every kind.
text.DRB: Again the Kingdom of Heaven is like to a net cast into the sea, and gathering together of all kind of fishes.

text.NRSV: when it was full, they drew it ashore, sat down, and put the good into baskets but threw out the bad.
text.Rotherham: which, when it was filled, they dragged up on the beach, and, sitting down, collected the good into vessels, but, the worthless, forth they cast.
text.KJV: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sate downe, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
text.NABRE: When it is full they haul it ashore and sit down to put what is good into buckets. What is bad they throw away.
text.DRB: Which, when it was filled, drawing it forth, and sitting by the shore, they chose out the good into vessels, but the bad they did cast out.

text.NRSV: So it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come out and separate the evil from the righteous
text.Rotherham: So, will it be in the conclusion of the age: The messengers will come forth, and separate the wicked from among the righteous;
text.KJV: So shall it be at the ende of the world: the Angels shal come forth, and seuer the wicked from among the iust,
text.NABRE: Thus it will be at the end of the age. The angels will go out and separate the wicked from the righteous
text.DRB: So shall it be in the consummation of the world. The Angels shall go forth, and shall separate the evil from among the just.

text.NRSV: and throw them into the furnace of fire, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: and will cast them into the furnace of fire: there, will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
text.KJV: And shal cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing, and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: and throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.
text.DRB: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: “Have you understood all this?” They answered, “Yes.”
text.Rotherham: Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea!
text.KJV: Iesus saith vnto them, Haue ye vnderstood all these things? They say vnto him, Yea, Lord.
text.NABRE: Treasures New and Old. “Do you understand all these things?” They answered, “Yes.”
text.DRB: Have ye understood all these things? They say to him, Yea.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Therefore every scribe who has been trained for the kingdom of heaven is like the master of a household who brings out of his treasure what is new and what is old.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Wherefore, every scribe, discipled unto the kingdom of the heavens, is like unto a householder, who putteth forth out of his treasure, things new and old.
text.KJV: Then said he vnto them, Therefore euery Scribe which is instructed vnto the kingdom of heauen, is like vnto a man that is an housholder, which bringeth foorth out of his treasure things new and old.
text.NABRE: And he replied, “Then every scribe who has been instructed in the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings from his storeroom both the new and the old.”
text.DRB: He said unto them: Therefore every Scribe instructed in the Kingdom of Heaven, is like to a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure new things and old.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had finished these parables, he left that place.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these parables, he removed from thence;
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, that when Iesus had finished these parables, hee departed thence.
text.NABRE: When Jesus finished these parables, he went away from there.
text.DRB: And it came to pass: when Jesus had ended these parables, he passed from thence.

text.NRSV: He came to his hometown and began to teach them in their synagogue, so that they were astounded and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom and these deeds of power?
text.Rotherham: and, coming into his own city, began teaching them in their synagogue, so that with astonishment were they being struck, and were saying—Whence, hath, this one, this wisdom, and the mighty works?
text.KJV: And when hee was come into his owne countrey, he taught them in their Synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this wisedome, and these mighty works?
text.NABRE: V. Jesus, the Kingdom, and the Church The Rejection at Nazareth. He came to his native place and taught the people in their synagogue. They were astonished and said, “Where did this man get such wisdom and mighty deeds?
text.DRB: And coming into his own country, he taught them in their Synagogues, so that they marveled, and said: How came this fellow by this wisdom and virtues?

text.NRSV: Is not this the carpenter’s son? Is not his mother called Mary? And are not his brothers James and Joseph and Simon and Judas?
text.Rotherham: Is not, this one, the carpenter’s, son? Is not, his mother, called Mary, and are not his brethren—James and Joseph, and Simon, and Judas?
text.KJV: Is not this the Carpenters sonne? Is not his mother called Marie? and his brethren, Iames, and Ioses, and Simon, and Iudas?
text.NABRE: Is he not the carpenter’s son? Is not his mother named Mary and his brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?
text.DRB: Is not this the carpenter's son? Is not his mother called Mary, and his brethren James, and Joseph, and Simon, and Jude:

text.NRSV: And are not all his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all this?”
text.Rotherham: and, his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence, then hath, this one, all these things?
text.KJV: And his sisters, are they not all with vs? whence then hath this man all these things?
text.NABRE: Are not his sisters all with us? Where did this man get all this?”
text.DRB: and his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence therefore hath he all these things?

text.NRSV: And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them, “Prophets are not without honor except in their own country and in their own house.”
text.Rotherham: And they began to find cause of stumbling in him. But, Jesus, said unto them—A prophet is not without honour, save in his city, and in his house.
text.KJV: And they were offended in him. But Iesus said vnto them, A Prophet is not without honour, saue in his owne countrey, and in his owne house.
text.NABRE: And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his native place and in his own house.”
text.DRB: And they were scandalized in him. But Jesus said to them: There is not a Prophet without honour but in his own country, and in his own house.

text.NRSV: And he did not do many deeds of power there, because of their unbelief.
text.Rotherham: And he did not, there, many mighty works, because of their unbelief.
text.KJV: And hee did not many mighty workes there, because of their vnbeliefe.
text.NABRE: And he did not work many mighty deeds there because of their lack of faith.
text.DRB: And he wrought not many miracles there because of their incredulity.

text.NRSV: At that time Herod the ruler heard reports about Jesus;
text.Rotherham: In that season, heard Herod the tetrarch, the fame of Jesus:
text.KJV: At that time Herod the Tetrarch heard of the fame of Iesus,
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Herod’s Opinion of Jesus. At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the reputation of Jesus
text.DRB: AT that time Herod the Tetrarch heard the fame of Jesus:

text.NRSV: and he said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has been raised from the dead, and for this reason these powers are at work in him.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto his servants—This, is John the Immerser,—he hath arisen from the dead, for this cause, are the powers working mightily within him.
text.KJV: And said vnto his seruants, This is Iohn the Baptist, hee is risen from the dead, and therfore mighty workes doe shew foorth themselues in him.
text.NABRE: and said to his servants, “This man is John the Baptist. He has been raised from the dead; that is why mighty powers are at work in him.”
text.DRB: and said to his servants: This is John the Baptist: he is risen from the dead, & therefore virtues work in him.

text.NRSV: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
text.Rotherham: For, Herod, seizing John, had bound him, and, in prison, put him away,—because of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother;
text.KJV: ¶ For Herode had layd hold on Iohn, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias sake, his brother Philips wife.
text.NABRE: The Death of John the Baptist. Now Herod had arrested John, bound [him], and put him in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip,
text.DRB: For Herod apprehended John and bound him, & put him into prison because of Herodias, his brother's wife.

text.NRSV: because John had been telling him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
text.Rotherham: for John had been saying to him, It is not allowed thee, to have her.
text.KJV: For Iohn said vnto him, It is not lawfull for thee to haue her.
text.NABRE: for John had said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
text.DRB: For John said unto him: It is not lawful for thee to have her.

text.NRSV: Though Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded him as a prophet.
text.Rotherham: And desiring, to kill, him, he feared the multitude, because, as a prophet, they held him.
text.KJV: And when he would haue put him to death, hee feared the multitude, because they counted him as a Prophet.
text.NABRE: Although he wanted to kill him, he feared the people, for they regarded him as a prophet.
text.DRB: And willing to put him to death, he feared the People: because they esteemed him as a Prophet.

text.NRSV: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before the company, and she pleased Herod
text.Rotherham: But, a birthday feast of Herod taking place, the daughter of Herodias danced in the midst, and pleased Herod;
text.KJV: But when Herods birth day was kept, the daughter of Herodias daunced before them, and pleased Herode.
text.NABRE: But at a birthday celebration for Herod, the daughter of Herodias performed a dance before the guests and delighted Herod
text.DRB: But on Herod's birth-day, the daughter of Herodias danced before them: and pleased Herod.

text.NRSV: so much that he promised on oath to grant her whatever she might ask.
text.Rotherham: wherefore, with an oath, he promised to give her, whatsoever she should ask for herself;
text.KJV: Whereupon he promised with an oath, to giue her whatsoeuer she would aske.
text.NABRE: so much that he swore to give her whatever she might ask for.
text.DRB: Whereupon he promised with an oath, to give her whatsoever she would ask of him.

text.NRSV: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter.”
text.Rotherham: and, she, being led on by her mother,—Give me (saith she) here, upon a charger, the head of John the Immerser.
text.KJV: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Giue me heere Iohn Baptists head in a charger.
text.NABRE: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”
text.DRB: But she being instructed before of her mother saith: Give me here in a dish the head of John the Baptist.

text.NRSV: The king was grieved, yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he commanded it to be given;
text.Rotherham: And the king, though grieved, yet, because of the oaths and the guests, ordered it to be given;
text.KJV: And the king was sorie: neuerthelesse for the othes sake, and them which sate with him at meate, he commanded it to be giuen her:
text.NABRE: The king was distressed, but because of his oaths and the guests who were present, he ordered that it be given,
text.DRB: And the king was stroken sad: yet because of his oath, and for them that sat with him at table, he commanded it to be given.

text.NRSV: he sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
text.Rotherham: and sent and beheaded John in the prison.
text.KJV: And he sent, and beheaded Iohn in the prison.
text.NABRE: and he had John beheaded in the prison.
text.DRB: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.

text.NRSV: The head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, who brought it to her mother.
text.Rotherham: And his head was brought upon a charger, and given unto the maiden, and she brought it to her mother.
text.KJV: And his head was brought in a charger, and giuen to the Damsell: and she brought it to her mother.
text.NABRE: His head was brought in on a platter and given to the girl, who took it to her mother.
text.DRB: And his head was brought in a dish: and it was given to the damsel, and she brought it to her mother.

text.NRSV: His disciples came and took the body and buried it; then they went and told Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And his disciples, going near, bare away the corpse, and buried him, and came and brought tidings unto Jesus.
text.KJV: And his Disciples came, and took vp the body, and buried it, and went and told Iesus.
text.NABRE: His disciples came and took away the corpse and buried him; and they went and told Jesus. The Return of the Twelve and the Feeding of the Five Thousand.
text.DRB: And his Disciples came and took the body, and buried it: and came and told Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat to a deserted place by himself. But when the crowds heard it, they followed him on foot from the towns.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, hearing it, retired from thence in a boat, into a desert place, apart,—and the multitudes, hearing of it, followed him on foot from the cities.
text.KJV: ¶ When Iesus heard of it, he departed thence by ship, into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard thereof, they followed him on foote, out of the cities.
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard of it, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. The crowds heard of this and followed him on foot from their towns.
text.DRB: Which when Jesus had heard, he retired from thence by boat, into a desert place apart, and the multitudes having heard of it, followed him on foot out of the cities.

text.NRSV: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them and cured their sick.
text.Rotherham: And, coming forth, he saw a great multitude,—and was moved with compassion over them, and cured their sick.
text.KJV: And Iesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was mooued with compassion toward them, and he healed their sicke.
text.NABRE: When he disembarked and saw the vast crowd, his heart was moved with pity for them, and he cured their sick.
text.DRB: And he coming forth saw a great multitude, and pitied them, and cured their diseased.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now late; send the crowds away so that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.”
text.Rotherham: And, evening, arriving, the disciples came unto him, saying—The place is, a desert, and, the hour, hath already passed,—dismiss the multitudes, that they may go away into the villages, and buy themselves food.
text.KJV: ¶ And when it was euening, his Disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may goe into the villages, and buy themselues victuals.
text.NABRE: When it was evening, the disciples approached him and said, “This is a deserted place and it is already late; dismiss the crowds so that they can go to the villages and buy food for themselves.”
text.DRB: And when it was evening, his Disciples came unto him, saying: It is a desert place, and the hour is now past: dismiss the multitudes, that going into the towns, they may buy themselves victuals.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “They need not go away; you give them something to eat.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them, No need, have they to go away,—give, ye, them to eat.
text.KJV: But Iesus said vnto them, They neede not depart; giue yee them to eate.
text.NABRE: [Jesus] said to them, “There is no need for them to go away; give them some food yourselves.”
text.DRB: But Jesus said to them: They have no need to go: give ye them to eat.

text.NRSV: They replied, “We have nothing here but five loaves and two fish.”
text.Rotherham: But, they, say unto him—We have nothing here, save five loaves and two fishes.
text.KJV: And they say vnto him, We haue heere but fiue loaues, and two fishes.
text.NABRE: But they said to him, “Five loaves and two fish are all we have here.”
text.DRB: They answered him: We have not here, but five loaves, and two fishes.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Bring them here to me.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Bring, them, to me, here.
text.KJV: He said, Bring them hither to me.
text.NABRE: Then he said, “Bring them here to me,”
text.DRB: Who said to them: Bring them hither to me.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
text.Rotherham: And, giving orders that the multitudes should recline upon the grass,—taking the five loaves and the two fishes,—looking up into the heaven, he blessed; and, breaking, gave, the disciples, the loaves, and the disciples unto the multitude.
text.KJV: And hee commanded the multitude to sit downe on the grasse, & tooke the fiue loaues, and the two fishes, and looking vp to heauen, hee blessed, and brake, and gaue the loaues to his Disciples, and the Disciples to the multitude.
text.NABRE: and he ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he said the blessing, broke the loaves, and gave them to the disciples, who in turn gave them to the crowds.
text.DRB: And when he had commanded the multitude to sit down upon the grass, he took the five loaves and the two fishes, and looking up unto Heaven he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his Disciples, and the Disciples to the multitudes.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled; and they took up what was left over of the broken pieces, twelve baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they did all eat, and were filled,—and they took up the remainder of the broken pieces, twelve baskets, full.
text.KJV: And they did all eat, & were filled: and they tooke vp of the fragments that remained twelue baskets full.
text.NABRE: They all ate and were satisfied, and they picked up the fragments left over —twelve wicker baskets full.
text.DRB: And they did all eat, and had their fill. And they took the leavings, twelve full baskets of the fragments.

text.NRSV: And those who ate were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
text.Rotherham: And, they who did eat, were about five thousand, men, besides women and children.
text.KJV: And they that had eaten, were about fiue thousand men, beside women and children.
text.NABRE: Those who ate were about five thousand men, not counting women and children. The Walking on the Water.
text.DRB: And the number of them that did eat was, five thousand men, beside women and children.

text.NRSV: Immediately he made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
text.Rotherham: And [straightway] constrained he the disciples to enter into a boat, and be going before him unto the other side, while he dismissed the multitudes.
text.KJV: ¶ And straightway Iesus constrained his Disciples to get into a ship, and to goe before him vnto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
text.NABRE: Then he made the disciples get into the boat and precede him to the other side, while he dismissed the crowds.
text.DRB: And forthwith Jesus commanded his Disciples to go up into the boat, and to go before him over the water, till he dismissed the multitudes.

text.NRSV: And after he had dismissed the crowds, he went up the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone,
text.Rotherham: And, dismissing the multitudes, he went up into the mountain, apart, to pray,—and when, evening, came, alone, was he, there.
text.KJV: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went vp into a mountaine apart to pray: and when the euening was come, he was there alone:
text.NABRE: After doing so, he went up on the mountain by himself to pray. When it was evening he was there alone.
text.DRB: And having dismissed the multitude, he ascended into a mountain alone to pray. And when it was evening, he was there alone.

text.NRSV: but by this time the boat, battered by the waves, was far from the land, for the wind was against them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the boat, still many furlongs from the land, was holding off, being distressed by the waves,—for, the wind, was, contrary.
text.KJV: But the ship was now in the midst of the Sea, tossed with waues: for the wind was contrary.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile the boat, already a few miles offshore, was being tossed about by the waves, for the wind was against it.
text.DRB: But the boat in the midst of the sea was tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.

text.NRSV: And early in the morning he came walking toward them on the sea.
text.Rotherham: And, in the fourth watch of the night, he came unto them, walking upon the sea.
text.KJV: And in the fourth watch of the night, Iesus went vnto them, walking on the Sea.
text.NABRE: During the fourth watch of the night, he came toward them, walking on the sea.
text.DRB: And in the fourth watch of the night, he came unto them walking upon the sea.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified, saying, “It is a ghost!” And they cried out in fear.
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, seeing him, upon the lake walking, were troubled,—saying—It is, a ghost; and, by reason of their fear, they cried out.
text.KJV: And when the Disciples saw him walking on the Sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit: and they cried out for feare.
text.NABRE: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea they were terrified. “It is a ghost,” they said, and they cried out in fear.
text.DRB: And seeing him upon the sea walking, they were troubled saying: That it is a ghost: & for fear they cried out.

text.NRSV: But immediately Jesus spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus spake unto them, saying—Take courage! it is, I,—be not afraid.
text.KJV: But straightway Iesus spake vnto them, saying, Be of good cheere: it is I, be not afraid.
text.NABRE: At once [Jesus] spoke to them, “Take courage, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.DRB: And immediately Jesus spake unto them, saying: Have confidence: it is I, fear ye not.

text.NRSV: Peter answered him, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Peter said unto him,—Lord! if it is, thou, bid me come unto thee, upon the waters.
text.KJV: And Peter answered him, and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come vnto thee on the water.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him in reply, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the water.”
text.DRB: And Peter making answer said: Lord if it be thou, bid me come to thee upon the waters.

text.NRSV: He said, “Come.” So Peter got out of the boat, started walking on the water, and came toward Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Come! And, descending from the boat, Peter walked upon the waters, and came unto Jesus.
text.KJV: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come downe out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Iesus.
text.NABRE: He said, “Come.” Peter got out of the boat and began to walk on the water toward Jesus.
text.DRB: And he said, Come. And Peter descending out of the boat, walked upon the water to come to Jesus.

text.NRSV: But when he noticed the strong wind, he became frightened, and beginning to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
text.Rotherham: But, seeing the wind, he was affrighted, and, beginning to sink, cried out, saying—Lord! save me!
text.KJV: But when he saw the wind boysterous, he was afraid: and beginning to sinke, he cried, saying, Lord saue me.
text.NABRE: But when he saw how [strong] the wind was he became frightened; and, beginning to sink, he cried out, “Lord, save me!”
text.DRB: But seeing the wind rough, he was afraid: and when he began to be drowned, he cried out saying: Lord, save me.

text.NRSV: Jesus immediately reached out his hand and caught him, saying to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus, stretching forth his hand, laid hold upon him and saith unto him—O little-of-faith! why didst thou doubt?
text.KJV: And immediately Iesus stretched foorth his hand, and caught him, and said vnto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
text.NABRE: Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand and caught him, and said to him, “O you of little faith, why did you doubt?”
text.DRB: And incontinent Jesus stretching forth his hand took hold of him, and said unto him: O thou of little faith, why didst thou doubt?

text.NRSV: When they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came up into the boat, the wind abated.
text.KJV: And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.
text.NABRE: After they got into the boat, the wind died down.
text.DRB: And when they were gone up into the boat, the wind ceased.

text.NRSV: And those in the boat worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: And, they in the boat, bowed down to him, saying—Truly, God’s Son, thou art!
text.KJV: Then they that were in the ship, came and worshipped him, saying, Of a trueth thou art the sonne of God.
text.NABRE: Those who were in the boat did him homage, saying, “Truly, you are the Son of God.”
text.DRB: And they that were in the boat, came and adored him, saying: Indeed thou art the Son of God.

text.NRSV: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
text.Rotherham: And, going across, they came up the land, into Gennesaret.
text.KJV: ¶ And when they were gone ouer, they came into þ land of Genesaret.
text.NABRE: The Healings at Gennesaret. After making the crossing, they came to land at Gennesaret.
text.DRB: And having passed the water, they came into the country of Genesar.

text.NRSV: After the people of that place recognized him, they sent word throughout the region and brought all who were sick to him,
text.Rotherham: And, recognizing him, the men of that place, sent out into all that region, and they brought unto him all who were sick;
text.KJV: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that countrey round about, and brought vnto him al that were diseased,
text.NABRE: When the men of that place recognized him, they sent word to all the surrounding country. People brought to him all those who were sick
text.DRB: And when the men of that place understood of him, they sent into all that country, and brought unto him all that were ill at ease:

text.NRSV: and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.
text.Rotherham: and were beseeching [him], that they might, only, touch the border of his mantle, and, as many as touched, were made quite well.
text.KJV: And besought him, that they might onely touch the hemme of his garment; and as many as touched, were made perfectly whole.
text.NABRE: and begged him that they might touch only the tassel on his cloak, and as many as touched it were healed.
text.DRB: and they besought him that they might touch but the hem of his garment, and whosoever did touch, were made whole.

text.NRSV: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
text.Rotherham: Then, there come unto Jesus from Jerusalem Pharisees and Scribes, saying—
text.KJV: Then came to Iesus Scribes and Pharisees, which were of Hierusalem, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 The Tradition of the Elders. Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said,
text.DRB: Then came to him from Jerusalem Scribes and Pharisees, saying:

text.NRSV: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands before they eat.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands, when they eat bread!
text.KJV: Why do thy disciples transgresse the tradition of the Elders? for they wash not their handes when they eat bread.
text.NABRE: “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They do not wash [their] hands when they eat a meal.”
text.DRB: Why do thy Disciples transgress the tradition of the Ancients? For they wash not their hands when they eat bread.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “And why do you break the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—Wherefore do, ye also, transgress the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
text.KJV: But hee answered, and said vnto them, Why doe you also transgresse the Commandement of God by your tradition?
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “And why do you break the commandment of God for the sake of your tradition?
text.DRB: But he answering said to them: Why do you also transgress the commandment of God for your tradition? For God said:

text.NRSV: For God said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
text.Rotherham: For, God, said—Honour thy father and thy mother, and—He that revileth father or mother, let him, surely die!
text.KJV: For God commaunded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: And hee that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
text.NABRE: For God said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and ‘Whoever curses father or mother shall die.’
text.DRB: Honour father and mother. And: He that shall curse father or mother, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: But you say that whoever tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is given to God,’
text.Rotherham: But, ye, say—Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother—A gift! Whatsoever, out of me, thou mightest be profited,
text.KJV: But yee say, Whosoeuer shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift by whatsoeuer thou mightest bee profited by me,
text.NABRE: But you say, ‘Whoever says to father or mother, “Any support you might have had from me is dedicated to God,”
text.DRB: But you say: whosoever shall say to father or mother, the gift whatsoever proceedeth from me, shall profit thee:

text.NRSV: then that person need not honor the father. So, for the sake of your tradition, you make void the word of God.
text.Rotherham: in nowise, shall honour his father or his mother—and so ye have cancelled, the word of God, for the sake of your, tradition.
text.KJV: And honour not his father or his mother, hee shall be free. Thus haue yee made the Commaundement of God of none effect by your tradition.
text.NABRE: need not honor his father.’ You have nullified the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
text.DRB: And shall not honour his father or his mother: & you have made frustrate the commandment of God for your own tradition.

text.NRSV: You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied rightly about you when he said:
text.Rotherham: Hypocrites! well prophesied concerning you, Isaiah, saying—
text.KJV: Yee hypocrites, well did Esaias prophecie of you, saying,
text.NABRE: Hypocrites, well did Isaiah prophesy about you when he said:
text.DRB: Hypocrites, well hath Esay Prophesied of you, saying:

text.NRSV: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.Rotherham: This people, with the lips, do, honour, me, while, their heart, far off, holdeth from me;
text.KJV: This people draweth nigh vnto mee with their mouth, and honoureth mee with their lips: but their heart is farre from me.
text.NABRE: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.DRB: This People honoureth me with their lips: but their heart is far from me.

text.NRSV: in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.’”
text.Rotherham: But, in vain, do they pay devotions unto me, teaching, for teachings, the commandments of men.
text.KJV: But in vaine they do worship me, teaching for doctrines, the commandements of men.
text.NABRE: in vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines human precepts.’”
text.DRB: And in vain do they worship me, teaching doctrines and commandments of men.

text.NRSV: Then he called the crowd to him and said to them, “Listen and understand:
text.Rotherham: And, calling near the multitude, he said to them—Hear and understand!
text.KJV: ¶ And he called the multitude, and said vnto them, Heare and vnderstand.
text.NABRE: He summoned the crowd and said to them, “Hear and understand.
text.DRB: And having called together the multitudes unto him, he said to them: Hear ye and understand.

text.NRSV: it is not what goes into the mouth that defiles a person, but it is what comes out of the mouth that defiles.”
