text.NRSV: Some Pharisees came to him, and to test him they asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any cause?”
text.Rotherham: And there came unto him Pharisees, testing him, and saying,—Whether is it allowed a man to divorce his wife, for every cause?
text.KJV: ¶ The Pharisees also came vnto him, tempting him, and saying vnto him, Is it lawfull for a man to put away his wife for euery cause?
text.NABRE: Some Pharisees approached him, and tested him, saying, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any cause whatever?”
text.DRB: And there came to him the Pharisees tempting him, and saying: Is it lawful for a man to dismiss his wife for every cause?

text.NRSV: He answered, “Have you not read that the one who made them at the beginning ‘made them male and female,’
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Did ye never read—He who created at the beginning, Male and female, made them,—
text.KJV: And hee answered, and said vnto them, Haue ye not read, that he which made them at the beginning, made them male and female?
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “Have you not read that from the beginning the Creator ‘made them male and female’
text.DRB: Who answering, said to them: Have ye not read, that he which did make from the beginning, made them male & female? And he said:

text.NRSV: and said, ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh’?
text.Rotherham: and said—For this cause, will a man leave his father and his mother, and be united to his wife,—and, the two, will become, one flesh;
text.KJV: And said, For this cause shall a man leaue father and mother, and shall cleaue to his wife: and they twaine shalbe one flesh.
text.NABRE: and said, ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh’?
text.DRB: For this cause, man shall leave father & mother, & shall cleave to his wife, and they two shall be in one flesh.

text.NRSV: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
text.Rotherham: So that, the longer, are they, two, but, one flesh, What, therefore, God, hath yoked together, Let not, a man, put asunder.
text.KJV: Wherefore they are no more twaine, but one flesh. What therefore God hath ioyned together, let not man put asunder.
text.NABRE: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has joined together, no human being must separate.”
text.DRB: Therefore now they are not two, but one flesh. That therefore which God hath joined together, let not man separate.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Why then did Moses command us to give a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her?”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Why then did, Moses, command, to give a writing of repudiation and to divorce?
text.KJV: They say vnto him, Why did Moses then command to giue a writing of diuorcement, and to put her away?
text.NABRE: They said to him, “Then why did Moses command that the man give the woman a bill of divorce and dismiss [her]?”
text.DRB: They say to him: Why then did Moyses command to give a bill of divorce, and to dismiss her?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It was because you were so hard-hearted that Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—Moses, in view of your hardness of heart, permitted you to divorce your wives; From the beginning however, hath it not been done, thus.
text.KJV: Hee saith vnto them, Moses, because of the hardnesse of your hearts, suffered you to put away your wiues: but from the beginning it was not so.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Because of the hardness of your hearts Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
text.DRB: He saith to them: Because Moyses for the hardness of your heart permitted you to dismiss your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.

text.NRSV: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for unchastity, and marries another commits adultery.”
text.Rotherham: And I say unto you—Whosoever shall divorce his wife, saving for unfaithfulness, and shall marry another, committeth adultery.
text.KJV: And I say vnto you, Whosoeuer shall put away his wife, except it be for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away, doth commit adultery.
text.NABRE: I say to you, whoever divorces his wife (unless the marriage is unlawful) and marries another commits adultery.”
text.DRB: And I say to you, that whosoever shall dismiss his wife, but for fornication, and shall marry another, doth commit adultery: and he that shall marry her that is dismissed, committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: His disciples said to him, “If such is the case of a man with his wife, it is better not to marry.”
text.Rotherham: The disciples say unto him—If, so, is the cause of the husband with the wife, it is not expedient to marry.
text.KJV: ¶ His disciples say vnto him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marrie.
text.NABRE: [His] disciples said to him, “If that is the case of a man with his wife, it is better not to marry.”
text.DRB: His Disciples say unto him: If the case of a man with his wife be so, it is not expedient to marry.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “Not everyone can accept this teaching, but only those to whom it is given.
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—Not all, find room for he word, save they to whom it hath been given;
text.KJV: But hee said vnto them, All men cannot receiue this saying, saue they to whom it is giuen.
text.NABRE: He answered, “Not all can accept [this] word, but only those to whom that is granted.
text.DRB: Who said to them: Not all take this word, but they to whom it is given.

text.NRSV: For there are eunuchs who have been so from birth, and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by others, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Let anyone accept this who can.”
text.Rotherham: For there, are, eunuchs, who, from their mother’s womb, were born so, and there, are, eunuchs, who were made eunuchs, by men,—and there, are, eunuchs, who have made, themselves, eunuchs, for the sake the kingdom of the heavens: He that is able to find room, let him find room.
text.KJV: For there are some Eunuches, which were so borne from their mothers wombe: and there are some Eunuches, which were made Eunuches of men: and there be Eunuches, which haue made themselues Eunuches for the kingdome of heauens sake. He that is able to receiue it, let him receiue it.
text.NABRE: Some are incapable of marriage because they were born so; some, because they were made so by others; some, because they have renounced marriage for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Whoever can accept this ought to accept it.” Blessing of the Children.
text.DRB: For there are Eunuchs which were born so from their mother's womb: and there are Eunuchs, which were made by men: and there are Eunuchs, which have gelded themselves for the Kingdom of Heaven. He that can take, let him take.

text.NRSV: Then little children were being brought to him in order that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples spoke sternly to those who brought them;
text.Rotherham: Then, were brought unto him children,—that he might lay his hands upon them, and pray. And, the disciples, rebuked them.
text.KJV: ¶ Then were there brought vnto him little children, that he should put his hands on them, and pray: and the disciples rebuked them.
text.NABRE: Then children were brought to him that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples rebuked them,
text.DRB: Then were little children presented to him, that he should impose hands upon them & pray. And the Disciples rebuked them.

text.NRSV: but Jesus said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of heaven belongs.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Suffer the children—and do not hinder them—to come unto me,—for, of such, is the kingdom of the heavens.
text.KJV: But Iesus said, Suffer little children, and forbid them not to come vnto me: for of such is þ kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: but Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and do not prevent them; for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.”
text.DRB: But Jesus said to them: Suffer the little children, and stay them not from coming unto me: for the Kingdom of Heaven is for such.

text.NRSV: And he laid his hands on them and went on his way.
text.Rotherham: And, laying his hands upon them, he went his way from thence.
text.KJV: And he laide his hands on them, and departed thence.
text.NABRE: After he placed his hands on them, he went away. The Rich Young Man.
text.DRB: And when he had imposed hands upon them, he departed from thence.

text.NRSV: Then someone came to him and said, “Teacher, what good deed must I do to have eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And lo! one coming near unto him, said, Teacher! what good thing shall I do, that I may have life age-abiding?
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, one came and said vnto him, Good master, what good thing shall I do, that I may haue eternall life?
text.NABRE: Now someone approached him and said, “Teacher, what good must I do to gain eternal life?”
text.DRB: And behold one came and said to him: Good Master, what good shall I do that I may have life everlasting?

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. If you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Why dost thou question me concerning that which is good? There is, One, that is good! But, if thou desirest, into life, to enter, be keeping the commandments.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is God: but if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandements.
text.NABRE: He answered him, “Why do you ask me about the good? There is only One who is good. If you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
text.DRB: Who said to him: What asketh thou me of good? One is good, God. But if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments.

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Which ones?” And Jesus said, “You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness;
text.Rotherham: He saith unto him, Which? Jesus, said—These: Thou shalt not commit murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal,—Thou shalt not bear false witness;
text.KJV: He saith vnto him, Which? Iesus said, Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steale, Thou shalt not beare false witnesse,
text.NABRE: He asked him, “Which ones?” And Jesus replied, “ ‘You shall not kill; you shall not commit adultery; you shall not steal; you shall not bear false witness;
text.DRB: He saith to him, which? And Jesus said: Thou shalt not murder, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness.

text.NRSV: Honor your father and mother; also, You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
text.Rotherham: Honour thy father and thy mother,—and—Thou shalt love thy neighbour as, thyself.
text.KJV: Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt loue thy neighbour as thy selfe.
text.NABRE: honor your father and your mother’; and ‘you shall love your neighbor as yourself.’”
text.DRB: Honour thy father & thy mother, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

text.NRSV: The young man said to him, “I have kept all these; what do I still lack?”
text.Rotherham: The young man saith unto him—These all, have I kept,—What, further, do I lack?
text.KJV: The young man saith vnto him, All these things haue I kept from my youth vp: what lacke I yet?
text.NABRE: The young man said to him, “All of these I have observed. What do I still lack?”
text.DRB: The young man saith to him: All these have I kept from my youth; what is yet wanting unto me?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “If you wish to be perfect, go, sell your possessions, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto him—If thou desirest to be perfect, Withdraw! sell thy substance, and give to the destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in the heavens; and come! be following me.
text.KJV: Iesus said vnto him, If thou wilt be perfect, goe and sell that thou hast, and giue to the poore, and thou shalt haue treasure in heauen: and come and follow me.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “If you wish to be perfect, go, sell what you have and give to [the] poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: If thou wilt be perfect, go, sell the things that thou hast, & give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven: and come, follow me.

text.NRSV: When the young man heard this word, he went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
text.Rotherham: And the young man, hearing, this word, went away sorrowing,—for he was holding large possessions.
text.KJV: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowfull: for he had great possessions.
text.NABRE: When the young man heard this statement, he went away sad, for he had many possessions.
text.DRB: And when the young man had heard this word, he went away sad: for he had many possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto his disciples—Verily, I say unto you, A rich man, with difficulty, shall enter into the kingdom of the heavens.
text.KJV: ¶ Then said Iesus vnto his disciples, Uerely I say vnto you, that a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdome of heauen.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Amen, I say to you, it will be hard for one who is rich to enter the kingdom of heaven.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to his Disciples: Amen I say to you, that a rich man shall hardly enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: Again I say unto you—Easier, is it for, a camel, through the eye of a needle, to enter, than a rich man—into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: And againe I say vnto you, It is easier for a camel to goe thorow the eye of a needle, then for a rich man to enter into the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: Again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for one who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: And again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

text.NRSV: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astounded and said, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing it, the disciples were being struck with the greatest astonishment, saying—Who then can be saved?
text.KJV: When his disciples heard it, they were exceedingly amazed, saying, Who then can be saued?
text.NABRE: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and said, “Who then can be saved?”
text.DRB: And when they had heard this, the Disciples marveled very much, saying: Who then can be saved?

text.NRSV: But Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but for God all things are possible.”
text.Rotherham: And, looking intently, Jesus said unto them—With men, this is, impossible, but, with God, all things are possible.
text.KJV: But Iesus beheld them, and said vnto them, With men this is vnpossible, but with God al things are possible.
text.NABRE: Jesus looked at them and said, “For human beings this is impossible, but for God all things are possible.”
text.DRB: And Jesus beholding, said to them: With men this is impossible: but with God all things are possible.

text.NRSV: Then Peter said in reply, “Look, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?”
text.Rotherham: Then, making answer, Peter said unto him—Lo! we, have left all, and followed thee,—What then shall there be, for us?
text.KJV: ¶ Then answered Peter, and said vnto him, Behold, we haue forsaken all, and followed thee, what shall we haue therefore?
text.NABRE: Then Peter said to him in reply, “We have given up everything and followed you. What will there be for us?”
text.DRB: Then Peter answering, said to him: Behold we have left all things, & have followed thee: what therefore shall we have?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man is seated on the throne of his glory, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you, As for you who followed me in the regeneration, When the Son of Man shall take his seat on his throne of glory, ye also, shall be seated upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto them, Uerily I say vnto you, that ye which haue followed me, in the regeneration when the Sonne of man shal sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shal sit vpon twelue thrones, iudging the twelue tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Amen, I say to you that you who have followed me, in the new age, when the Son of Man is seated on his throne of glory, will yourselves sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: Amen I say to you, that you which have followed me, in the regeneration, when the Son of man shall sit in the seat of his majesty, you also shall sit upon twelve seats, judging the twelve Tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields, for my name’s sake, will receive a hundredfold, and will inherit eternal life.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever left houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or children, or lands, for sake of my name, manifold, shall receive, and life, age-abiding, shall inherit.
text.KJV: And euery one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my Names sake, shall receiue an hundred fold, and shall inherite euerlasting life.
text.NABRE: And everyone who has given up houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or lands for the sake of my name will receive a hundred times more, and will inherit eternal life.
text.DRB: And every one that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands for my name's sake: shall receive an hundred-fold, & shall possess life everlasting.

text.NRSV: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.
text.Rotherham: But many shall be—first-last, and last-first.
text.KJV: But many that are first, shall be last, and the last shall be first.
text.NABRE: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.
text.DRB: And many shall be first, that are last; and last, that are first.

text.NRSV: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.
text.Rotherham: For the kingdom of the heavens, is like, a man, a householder,—who went forth with the morning, to hire labourers into his vineyard;
text.KJV: For the kingdome of heauen is like vnto a man that is an housholder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 The Workers in the Vineyard. “The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out at dawn to hire laborers for his vineyard.
text.DRB: The Kingdom of Heaven is like to a man that is an householder which went forth early in the morning to hire workmen into his vineyard.

text.NRSV: After agreeing with the laborers for the usual daily wage, he sent them into his vineyard.
text.Rotherham: and, when he had agreed with the labourers for a denary the day, he sent them into his vineyard.
text.KJV: And when hee had agreed with the labourers for a peny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
text.NABRE: After agreeing with them for the usual daily wage, he sent them into his vineyard.
text.DRB: And having made covenant with the workmen for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

text.NRSV: When he went out about nine o’clock, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace;
text.Rotherham: And, going forth about the third hour, he saw others, standing in the market-place, unemployed;
text.KJV: And he went out about the third houre, and saw others standing idle in the market place,
text.NABRE: Going out about nine o’clock, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
text.DRB: And going forth about the third hour, he saw other standing in the market place idle,

text.NRSV: and he said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.’ So they went.
text.Rotherham: and, to them, he said—ye also, go your way into the vineyard, and, whatsoever may be right, I will give you;
text.KJV: And said vnto them, Go ye also into the vineyard, & whatsoeuer is right, I wil giue you. And they went their way.
text.NABRE: and he said to them, ‘You too go into my vineyard, and I will give you what is just.’
text.DRB: and he said to them: Go you also into the vineyard, and that which shall be just, I will give you.

text.NRSV: When he went out again about noon and about three o’clock, he did the same.
text.Rotherham: and, they, departed. And, again, going forth about the sixth and ninth hour, he did likewise.
text.KJV: Againe he went out about the sixth and ninth houre, and did likewise.
text.NABRE: So they went off. [And] he went out again around noon, and around three o’clock, and did likewise.
text.DRB: And they went their way. And again he went forth about the sixth & the ninth hour: and did likewise.

text.NRSV: And about five o’clock he went out and found others standing around; and he said to them, ‘Why are you standing here idle all day?’
text.Rotherham: And, about the eleventh, going forth, he found others, standing, and saith unto them—Why, here, stand ye, all the day, unemployed?
text.KJV: And about the eleuenth houre, he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith vnto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?
text.NABRE: Going out about five o’clock, he found others standing around, and said to them, ‘Why do you stand here idle all day?’
text.DRB: But about the eleventh hour he went forth and found other standing, & he saith to them: What stand you here all the day idle?

text.NRSV: They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard.’
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Because, no one, hath hired, us. He saith unto them—Ye also, go your way into the vineyard.
text.KJV: They say vnto him, Because no man hath hired vs. He saith vnto them, Go ye also into the vineyard: and whatsoeuer is right, that shall ye receiue.
text.NABRE: They answered, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ He said to them, ‘You too go into my vineyard.’
text.DRB: They say to him: Because no man hath hired us. He saith to them: Go you also into the vineyard.

text.NRSV: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and give them their pay, beginning with the last and then going to the first.’
text.Rotherham: And, when evening came, the master of the vineyard saith unto his steward—Call the labourers, and pay the hire,—beginning from the last, unto the first.
text.KJV: So when euen was come, the lord of the vineyard saith vnto his Steward, Call the labourers, and giue them their hire, beginning from the last, vnto the first.
text.NABRE: When it was evening the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, ‘Summon the laborers and give them their pay, beginning with the last and ending with the first.’
text.DRB: And when evening was come, the Lord of the vineyard saith to his bailiff, Call the workmen, and pay them their hire, beginning from the last even to the first.

text.NRSV: When those hired about five o’clock came, each of them received the usual daily wage.
text.Rotherham: And they of the eleventh hour, coming, received severally a denary.
text.KJV: And when they came that were hired about the eleuenth houre, they receiued euery man a penie.
text.NABRE: When those who had started about five o’clock came, each received the usual daily wage.
text.DRB: Therefore when they were come that came about the eleventh hour, they received every one a penny.

text.NRSV: Now when the first came, they thought they would receive more; but each of them also received the usual daily wage.
text.Rotherham: And, when the first came, they supposed that, more, they should receive,—and, they also, received severally a denary.
text.KJV: But when the first came, they supposed that they should haue receiued more, and they likewise receiued euery man a penie.
text.NABRE: So when the first came, they thought that they would receive more, but each of them also got the usual wage.
text.DRB: But when the first also came, they thought that they should receive more: and they also received every one a penny.

text.NRSV: And when they received it, they grumbled against the landowner,
text.Rotherham: And, having received it, they began to murmur against the householder, saying—
text.KJV: And when they had receiued it, they murmured against the good man of the house,
text.NABRE: And on receiving it they grumbled against the landowner,
text.DRB: And receiving it they murmured against the good-man of the house.

text.NRSV: saying, ‘These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.’
text.Rotherham: These last, one hour, wrought, and thou hast made them, equal unto us, who have borne the burden of the day, and the scorching heat.
text.KJV: Saying, These last haue wrought but one houre, and thou hast made them equall vnto vs, which haue borne the burden, and heat of the day.
text.NABRE: saying, ‘These last ones worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us, who bore the day’s burden and the heat.’
text.DRB: Saying: These last have continued one hour, and thou hast made them equal to us that have borne the burden of the day & the heats.

text.NRSV: But he replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong; did you not agree with me for the usual daily wage?
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, unto one of them, said,—Friend! I wrong thee not: Was it not, for a denary, thou didst agree with me?
text.KJV: But he answered one of them and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penie?
text.NABRE: He said to one of them in reply, ‘My friend, I am not cheating you. Did you not agree with me for the usual daily wage?
text.DRB: But he answering said to one of them, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst thou not covenant with me for a penny?

text.NRSV: Take what belongs to you and go; I choose to give to this last the same as I give to you.
text.Rotherham: Take thine own, and go thy way; but I please, unto this last, to give, as also to thee:
text.KJV: Take that thine is, and goe thy way, I will giue vnto this last, euen as vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Take what is yours and go. What if I wish to give this last one the same as you?
text.DRB: Take that is thine, and go: I will also give to this last even as to thee also.

text.NRSV: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or are you envious because I am generous?’
text.Rotherham: Is it not allowed me to do, what I please, with my own? or is, thine eye, evil, because I good?
text.KJV: Is it not lawfull for mee to doe what I wil with mine owne? Is thine eye euill, because I am good?
text.NABRE: [Or] am I not free to do as I wish with my own money? Are you envious because I am generous?’
text.DRB: Or, is it not lawful for me to do that I will? Is thine eye naught, because I am good?

text.NRSV: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall be—The last first, and the first last.
text.KJV: So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many bee called, but fewe chosen.
text.NABRE: Thus, the last will be first, and the first will be last.” The Third Prediction of the Passion.
text.DRB: So shall the last be first, and the first, last. For many be called, but few elect.

text.NRSV: While Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside by themselves, and said to them on the way,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, being about to go up unto Jerusalem, took unto him the twelve [disciples], apart, and, in the way, he said unto them—
text.KJV: ¶ And Iesus going vp to Hierusalem, tooke the twelue disciples apart in the way, and said vnto them,
text.NABRE: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve [disciples] aside by themselves, and said to them on the way,
text.DRB: And Jesus going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve Disciples secretly, and said to them:

text.NRSV: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death;
text.Rotherham: Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem; and, the Son of Man, will be delivered up unto the chief-priests and Scribes, and they will condemn him, [to death],
text.KJV: Behold, we goe vp to Hierusalem, and the Sonne of man shall be betraied vnto the chiefe Priests, and vnto the Scribes, and they shall condemne him to death,
text.NABRE: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death,
text.DRB: Behold we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be delivered to the chief Priests and to the Scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,

text.NRSV: then they will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified; and on the third day he will be raised.”
text.Rotherham: And deliver him up unto the nations, to mock and to scourge and to crucify,—and, on the third day, he will, arise.
text.KJV: And shal deliuer him to the Gentiles to mocke, and to scourge, and to crucifie him: and the third day he shall rise againe.
text.NABRE: and hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and scourged and crucified, and he will be raised on the third day.” The Request of James and John.
text.DRB: and shall deliver him to the Gentiles to be mocked, and scourged, and crucified, and the third day he shall rise again.

text.NRSV: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, and kneeling before him, she asked a favor of him.
text.Rotherham: Then came unto him the mother of the sons of Zebedee, with her sons, bowing down, and asking something from him.
text.KJV: ¶ Then came to him the mother of Zebedees children, with her sonnes, worshipping him, and desiring a certain thing of him.
text.NABRE: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee approached him with her sons and did him homage, wishing to ask him for something.
text.DRB: Then came to him the mother of the sons of Zebedee with her sons, adoring and desiring something of him.

text.NRSV: And he said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Declare that these two sons of mine will sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—What desirest thou? She saith unto him—Bid, that these my two sons may sit, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left, in thy kingdom.
text.KJV: And he said vnto her, What wilt thou? She saith vnto him, Grant, that these my two sonnes may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left in thy kingdome.
text.NABRE: He said to her, “What do you wish?” She answered him, “Command that these two sons of mine sit, one at your right and the other at your left, in your kingdom.”
text.DRB: Who said to her: What wilt thou? She saith to him: Say that these my two sons may sit, one at thy right hand, & one at thy left hand in thy Kingdom.

text.NRSV: But Jesus answered, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?” They said to him, “We are able.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, answering, said—Ye know not what ye are asking: Are ye able to drink the cup, which, I, am about to drink; They say unto him—We are able.
text.KJV: But Iesus answered, and said, Ye know not what ye aske. Are ye able to drinke of the cup that I shall drinke of, and to be baptized with the baptisme that I am baptized with? They say vnto him, We are able.
text.NABRE: Jesus said in reply, “You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I am going to drink?” They said to him, “We can.”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said: You know not what you desire. Can you drink of the cup that I shall drink of? They say to him: We can.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left, this is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—My cup, indeed, ye shall drink; but, to sit on my right hand and on my left, is not mine to give,—except unto those for whom it hath been prepared by my Father.
text.KJV: And he saith vnto them, Yee shall drinke indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptisme that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to giue, but it shall be giuen to them for whom it is prepared of my father.
text.NABRE: He replied, “My cup you will indeed drink, but to sit at my right and at my left [, this] is not mine to give but is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
text.DRB: He saith to them: My cup indeed you shall drink of: but to sit at my right hand and left, is not mine to give to you: but to whom it is prepared of my Father.

text.NRSV: When the ten heard it, they were angry with the two brothers.
text.Rotherham: And the ten, hearing, were sorely displeased, concerning the two brethren.
text.KJV: And when the ten heard it, they were moued with indignation against the two brethren.
text.NABRE: When the ten heard this, they became indignant at the two brothers.
text.DRB: And the ten hearing it, were displeased at the two brethren.

text.NRSV: But Jesus called them to him and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, calling them near, said—Ye know that, the rulers of the nations, lord it over them, and, the great ones, wield authority over them:
text.KJV: But Iesus called them vnto him, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion ouer them, and they that are great, exercise authoritie vpon them.
text.NABRE: But Jesus summoned them and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and the great ones make their authority over them felt.
text.DRB: And Jesus calleth them unto him, and said: You know that the princes of the Gentiles overrule them: and they that are the greater, exercise power against them.

text.NRSV: It will not be so among you; but whoever wishes to be great among you must be your servant,
text.Rotherham: Not so, is it, among you,—but, whosoever shall desire, among you, to become, great, shall be, your minister;
text.KJV: But it shall not be so among you: But whosoeuer will bee great among you, let him be your minister.
text.NABRE: But it shall not be so among you. Rather, whoever wishes to be great among you shall be your servant;
text.DRB: It shall not be so among you, but whosoever will be the greater among you, let him be your minister:

text.NRSV: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be your slave;
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall desire, among you, to be first, shall be, your servant:—
text.KJV: And whosoeuer will be chiefe among you, let him be your seruant.
text.NABRE: whoever wishes to be first among you shall be your slave.
text.DRB: And he that will be first among you, shall be your servant.

text.NRSV: just as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
text.Rotherham: Just as, the Son of Man, came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom instead of many.
text.KJV: Euen as the Sonne of man came not to be ministred vnto, but minister, and to giue his life a ransome for many.
text.NABRE: Just so, the Son of Man did not come to be served but to serve and to give his life as a ransom for many.” The Healing of Two Blind Men.
text.DRB: Even as the Son of man is not come to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a redemption for many.

text.NRSV: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were going forth from Jericho, there followed him a great multitude;
text.KJV: And as they departed from Hiericho, a great multitude followed him.
text.NABRE: As they left Jericho, a great crowd followed him.
text.DRB: And when they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

text.NRSV: There were two blind men sitting by the roadside. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: and lo! two blind men, sitting beside the road, hearing that Jesus was passing by, cried aloud saying—Lord! have mercy on us! Son of David!
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Iesus passed by, cried out, saying, Haue mercie on vs, O Lord, thou sonne of Dauid.
text.NABRE: Two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “[Lord,] Son of David, have pity on us!”
text.DRB: And behold two blind men sitting by the way side, heard that Jesus passed by, and they cried out saying: Lord, have mercy upon us, Son of David.

text.NRSV: The crowd sternly ordered them to be quiet; but they shouted even louder, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
text.Rotherham: But the multitude rebuked them, that they might hold their peace. But, they, the more, cried aloud, saying—Lord, have mercy on us! Son of David!
text.KJV: And the multitude rebuked them, because they should holde their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Haue mercie on vs, O Lord, thou sonne of Dauid.
text.NABRE: The crowd warned them to be silent, but they called out all the more, “Lord, Son of David, have pity on us!”
text.DRB: And the multitude rebuked them that they should hold their peace. But they cried out the more, saying: Lord, have mercy upon us, Son of David.

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and called them, saying, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text.Rotherham: And, standing still, Jesus called them, and said—What desire ye I should do for you?
text.KJV: And Iesus stood still, and called them, and saide, What will yee that I shall doe vnto you?
text.NABRE: Jesus stopped and called them and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text.DRB: And Jesus stood, and called them, and said: What will ye that I do to you?

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Lord! that, our eyes, may open.
text.KJV: They say vnto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
text.NABRE: They answered him, “Lord, let our eyes be opened.”
text.DRB: They say to him: Lord, that our eyes may be opened.

text.NRSV: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they regained their sight and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes,—and, straightway, they recovered sight, and followed him.
text.KJV: So Iesus had compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediatly their eyes receiued sight, and they followed him.
text.NABRE: Moved with pity, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they received their sight, and followed him.
text.DRB: And Jesus having compassion on them, touched their eyes. And immediately they saw, and followed him.

text.NRSV: When they had come near Jerusalem and had reached Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
text.Rotherham: And, when they drew near unto Jerusalem, and came unto Bethphage, unto the Mount of Olives, then, Jesus sent forth two disciples;
text.KJV: And when they drewe nigh vnto Hierusalem, and were come to Bethphage, vnto the mount of Oliues, then sent Iesus two Disciples,
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 The Entry into Jerusalem. When they drew near Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
text.DRB: And when they drew nigh to Jerusalem, and were come to Beth-phagee unto Mount-olivet, then Jesus sent two Disciples,

text.NRSV: saying to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to me.
text.Rotherham: saying unto them—Be going into the village that is over against you, and, straightway, ye shall find an ass, bound, and a colt with her,—loose them, and lead them unto me.
text.KJV: Saying vnto them, Goe into the village ouer against you, and straightway yee shall find an Asse tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them vnto me.
text.NABRE: saying to them, “Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find an ass tethered, and a colt with her. Untie them and bring them here to me.
text.DRB: saying to them: Go ye into the town that is against you, and immediately you shall find an ass tied and a colt with her: loose them & bring them to me:

text.NRSV: If anyone says anything to you, just say this, ‘The Lord needs them.’ And he will send them immediately.”
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone, unto you, say aught, ye shall say—Their Lord, hath, need,—and, straightway, he will send them.
text.KJV: And if any man say ought vnto you, yee shall say, The Lord hath need of them, and straightway hee will send them.
text.NABRE: And if anyone should say anything to you, reply, ‘The master has need of them.’ Then he will send them at once.”
text.DRB: And if any man shall say ought unto you, say ye, that our Lord hath need of them: and forthwith he will let them go.

text.NRSV: This took place to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet, saying,
text.Rotherham: But, this, hath come to pass, that it might be fulfilled, which was spoken through the prophet, saying:
text.KJV: All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet, saying,
text.NABRE: This happened so that what had been spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled:
text.DRB: And this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet, saying:

text.NRSV: “Tell the daughter of Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
text.Rotherham: Tell ye the daughter of Zion, Lo! thy King, is coming unto thee, meek and mounted upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of a toiling ass.
text.KJV: Tell yee the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy king commeth vnto thee, meeke, and sitting vpon an Asse, and a colt, the foale of an Asse.
text.NABRE: “Say to daughter Zion, ‘Behold, your king comes to you, meek and riding on an ass, and on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.’”
text.DRB: Say ye to the daughter of Sion: Behold thy King cometh to thee, meek, & sitting upon an ass and a colt the foal of her that is used to the yoke.

text.NRSV: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them;
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, went, and did as Jesus directed them;
text.KJV: And the Disciples went, and did as Iesus commanded them,
text.NABRE: The disciples went and did as Jesus had ordered them.
text.DRB: And the Disciples going, did as Jesus commanded them.

text.NRSV: they brought the donkey and the colt, and put their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.
text.Rotherham: and led the ass and the colt, and laid upon them their garments, and he took his seat upon them.
text.KJV: And brought the Asse, and the colt, and put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon.
text.NABRE: They brought the ass and the colt and laid their cloaks over them, and he sat upon them.
text.DRB: And they brought the ass and the colt: and laid their garments upon them, and made him to sit thereon.

text.NRSV: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
text.Rotherham: And, the chief multitude, spread their own mantles in the way, and, others, were cutting off young branches from the trees, and spreading them in the way;
text.KJV: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way, others cut downe branches from the trees, and strawed them in the way.
text.NABRE: The very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and strewed them on the road.
text.DRB: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way: and others did cut boughs from the trees, and strawed them in the way:

text.NRSV: The crowds that went ahead of him and that followed were shouting, “Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
text.Rotherham: and the multitudes, they who were going before him and they who were following after, were crying aloud, saying—Hosanna! to the Son of David, Blessed is he that is coming in the name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest.
text.KJV: And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna to the sonne of Dauid: Blessed is he that commeth in the Name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest.
text.NABRE: The crowds preceding him and those following kept crying out and saying: “Hosanna to the Son of David; blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord; hosanna in the highest.”
text.DRB: and the multitudes that went before and that followed, cried, saying: Hosanna to the Son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord. Hosanna in the highest.

text.NRSV: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in turmoil, asking, “Who is this?”
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered into Jerusalem, all the city was startled, saying—Who is this?
text.KJV: And when hee was come into Hierusalem, all the citie was mooued, saying, Who is this?
text.NABRE: And when he entered Jerusalem the whole city was shaken and asked, “Who is this?”
text.DRB: And when he was entered Jerusalem, the whole city was moved, saying: Who is this?

text.NRSV: The crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, were saying—This, is the prophet Jesus,—He from Nazareth of Galilee.
text.KJV: And the multitude said, This is Iesus the Prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
text.NABRE: And the crowds replied, “This is Jesus the prophet, from Nazareth in Galilee.” The Cleansing of the Temple.
text.DRB: And the People said: This is Jesus the Prophet, of Nazareth in Galilee.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus entered the temple and drove out all who were selling and buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus entered into the temple, and cast out all who were selling and buying in the temple,—and, the tables of the money-changers, he overthrew, and the seats of them who were selling doves;
text.KJV: ¶ And Iesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the Temple, and ouerthrew the tables of the money changers, and the seats of them that solde doues,
text.NABRE: Jesus entered the temple area and drove out all those engaged in selling and buying there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
text.DRB: And Jesus entered into the temple of God, and cast out all that sold and bought in the temple, and the tables of the bankers, & the chairs of them that sold pigeons he overthrew:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer’; but you are making it a den of robbers.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—It is written, My house, a house of prayer, shall be called; but, ye, are making it, A den of robbers!
text.KJV: And said vnto them, It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer, but yee haue made it a denne of theeues.
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “It is written: ‘My house shall be a house of prayer,’ but you are making it a den of thieves.”
text.DRB: and he saith to them: It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer: but you have made it a den of thieves.

text.NRSV: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he cured them.
text.Rotherham: And there came unto him blind and lame, in the temple, and he cured them.
text.KJV: And the blind and the lame came to him in the Temple, & he healed them.
text.NABRE: The blind and the lame approached him in the temple area, and he cured them.
text.DRB: And there came to him the blind, and the lame in the temple; and he healed them.

text.NRSV: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the amazing things that he did, and heard the children crying out in the temple, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they became angry
text.Rotherham: But the Chief-priests and the Scribes, seeing, the marvels that he wrought, and the boys who were crying aloud in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David! were greatly displeased;
text.KJV: And when the chiefe Priests and Scribes saw the wonderfull things that he did, & the children crying in the temple, & saying, Hosanna to the sonne of Dauid, they were sore displeased,
text.NABRE: When the chief priests and the scribes saw the wondrous things he was doing, and the children crying out in the temple area, “Hosanna to the Son of David,” they were indignant
text.DRB: And the chief Priests & Scribes seeing the marvelous things that he did, and the children crying in the temple, & saying, Hosanna to the Son of David; they had indignation,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes; have you never read, ‘Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise for yourself’?”
text.Rotherham: and said unto him—Hearest thou what these are saying? And, Jesus, saith unto them—Yea: Have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings, hast thou prepared praise?
text.KJV: And said vnto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Iesus saith vnto them, Yea, haue yee neuer read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
text.NABRE: and said to him, “Do you hear what they are saying?” Jesus said to them, “Yes; and have you never read the text, ‘Out of the mouths of infants and nurslings you have brought forth praise’?”
text.DRB: and said to him: Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus said to them: Very well. Have you never read: That out of the mouth of infants and sucklings thou hast perfited praise?

text.NRSV: He left them, went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
text.Rotherham: And, leaving them behind, he went forth outside the city into Bethany, and spent the night there.
text.KJV: ¶ And he left them, and went out of the citie into Bethany, and he lodged there.
text.NABRE: And leaving them, he went out of the city to Bethany, and there he spent the night. The Cursing of the Fig Tree.
text.DRB: And leaving them, he went forth out of the city into Bethania, and remained there.

text.NRSV: In the morning, when he was returning to the city, he was hungry.
text.Rotherham: And, early, returning into the city, he hungered;
text.KJV: Now in the morning, as hee returned into the citie, he hungred.
text.NABRE: When he was going back to the city in the morning, he was hungry.
text.DRB: And in the morning returning into the city, he was an hungered.

text.NRSV: And seeing a fig tree by the side of the road, he went to it and found nothing at all on it but leaves. Then he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” And the fig tree withered at once.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing one fig-tree by the way, he came up to it, and nothing, found he thereon, save leaves only,—and he saith unto it—No more, from thee, let fruit spring forth, unto times age-abiding,—and the fig-tree, instantly withered away.
text.KJV: And when he saw a figge tree in the way, hee came to it, and found nothing thereon but leaues only, and said vnto it, Let no fruite growe on thee hence forward for euer. And presently the figge tree withered away.
text.NABRE: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he went over to it, but found nothing on it except leaves. And he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again.” And immediately the fig tree withered.
text.DRB: And seeing a certain figtree by the way side, he came to it, and found nothing on it but leaves only, and he saith to it: Never grow there fruit of thee for ever. And incontinent the figtree was withered.

text.NRSV: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed, saying, “How did the fig tree wither at once?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, seeing it, marvelled, saying—How instantly, did the fig-tree wither away.
text.KJV: And when the Disciples saw it, they marueiled, saying, How soone is the figge tree withered away?
text.NABRE: When the disciples saw this, they were amazed and said, “How was it that the fig tree withered immediately?”
text.DRB: And the Disciples seeing it, marveled saying: How is it withered incontinent?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you, If ye have faith, and do not doubt, not only, this of the fig-tree, shall ye do,—but, even if, unto this mountain, ye shall say, Be lifted up, and be cast into the sea, it shall be done.
text.KJV: Iesus answered, and said vnto them, Uerily I say vnto you, if yee haue faith, and doubt not, yee shall not onely doe this which is done to the figge tree, but also, if ye shall say vnto this mountaine, Be thou remoued, and be thou cast into the Sea, it shall be done.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Amen, I say to you, if you have faith and do not waver, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to them: Amen I say to you, if you shall have faith, and stagger not, not only that of the figtree shall you do, but and if you shall say to this mountain, Take up and throw thyself into the sea, it shall be done.

text.NRSV: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.”
text.Rotherham: And, all things whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.
text.KJV: And all things whatsoeuer yee shall aske in prayer, beleeuing, ye shall receiue.
text.NABRE: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.” The Authority of Jesus Questioned.
text.DRB: And all things whatsoever you shall ask in prayer believing, you shall receive.

text.NRSV: When he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?”
text.Rotherham: And, when he, entered, the temple, the Chief-priests and the Elders of the people, came unto him as he was teaching, saying, By what authority, these things, art thou doing? and, who, to thee, gave, this authority?
text.KJV: ¶ And when he was come into the temple, the chiefe Priests and the Elders of the people came vnto him as he was teaching, and said, By what authoritie doest thou these things? and who gaue thee this authoritie?
text.NABRE: When he had come into the temple area, the chief priests and the elders of the people approached him as he was teaching and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? And who gave you this authority?”
text.DRB: And when he was come into the temple, there came to him as he was teaching, the chief Priests and ancients of the people, saying: In what power doest thou these things? and who hath given thee this power?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I will also ask you one question; if you tell me the answer, then I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Jesus said unto them—I also, will ask, you, one thing,—which if ye tell me, I also, will tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing:—
text.KJV: And Iesus answered, and said vnto them, I also will aske you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authoritie I doe these things.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “I shall ask you one question, and if you answer it for me, then I shall tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.DRB: Jesus answering said to them: I also will ask you one word, which if you shall tell me, I also will tell you in what power I do these things.

text.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?” And they argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
text.Rotherham: The immersion by John, whence was it? Of heaven, or of men? But, they, began to deliberate among themselves, saying—If we say, Of heaven; He will say unto us, Wherefore then did ye not believe him?
text.KJV: The baptisme of Iohn, whence was it? from heauen, or of men? and they reasoned with themselues saying, If we shall say, From heauen, hee will say vnto vs, Why did ye not then beleeue him?
text.NABRE: Where was John’s baptism from? Was it of heavenly or of human origin?” They discussed this among themselves and said, “If we say ‘Of heavenly origin,’ he will say to us, ‘Then why did you not believe him?’
text.DRB: The Baptism of John whence was it? from Heaven, or from men? But they thought within themselves, saying:

text.NRSV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we are afraid of the crowd; for all regard John as a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: But, if we say, Of men, we fear the multitude, for, all, as a prophet, are holding John.
text.KJV: But if we shall say, Of men, we feare the people, for all hold Iohn as a Prophet.
text.NABRE: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we fear the crowd, for they all regard John as a prophet.”
text.DRB: If we shall say from Heaven, he will say to us, why then did you not believe him? But if we shall say from men, we fear the multitude, for all hold John as a Prophet.

text.NRSV: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
text.Rotherham: And, making answer to Jesus, they said—We know not. He also, said unto them—Neither do, I, tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing.
text.KJV: And they answered Iesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said vnto them, Neither tell I you by what authoritie I doe these things.
text.NABRE: So they said to Jesus in reply, “We do not know.” He himself said to them, “Neither shall I tell you by what authority I do these things. The Parable of the Two Sons.
text.DRB: And answering to Jesus they said: We know not. He also said to them: Neither do I tell you in what power I do these things.

text.NRSV: “What do you think? A man had two sons; he went to the first and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
text.Rotherham: But how, to you, doth it seem? A man, had two sons: coming unto the first, he said, Son! go thy way, to-day, be working in the vineyard.
text.KJV: ¶ But what thinke you? A certaine man had two sonnes, and he came to the first, and said, Sonne, goe worke to day in my vineyard.
text.NABRE: “What is your opinion? A man had two sons. He came to the first and said, ‘Son, go out and work in the vineyard today.’
text.DRB: But what is your opinion? A certain man had two sons, and coming to the first, he said: Son, go work today in my vineyard.

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘I will not’; but later he changed his mind and went.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said, I, sir! and went not;
text.KJV: He answered, & said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, ‘I will not,’ but afterwards he changed his mind and went.
text.DRB: And he answering, said: I will not. But afterward moved with repentance he went.

text.NRSV: The father went to the second and said the same; and he answered, ‘I go, sir’; but he did not go.
text.Rotherham: And, coming unto the second, he spake, in like manner,—and, he, answering, said, I will not: afterwards, smitten with regret, he went.
text.KJV: And hee came to the second, and said likewise: and hee answered, and said, I goe sir, and went not.
text.NABRE: The man came to the other son and gave the same order. He said in reply, ‘Yes, sir,’ but did not go.
text.DRB: And coming to the other, he said likewise. And he answering, said: I go Lord, and he went not:

text.NRSV: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are going into the kingdom of God ahead of you.
text.Rotherham: Which of the two, did the will of the father? They say, The latter. Jesus saith unto them—Verily, I say unto you, the tax-collectors and the harlots, are going before you, into the kingdom of God;
text.KJV: Whether of them twaine did the will of his father? They say vnto him, The first. Iesus saith vnto them, Uerely I say vnto you, that the Publicanes and the harlots go into the kingdome of God before you.
text.NABRE: Which of the two did his father’s will?” They answered, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Amen, I say to you, tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God before you.
text.DRB: Which of the two did the father's will? They say to him: The first. Jesus saith to them: Amen I say to you, that the Publicans and whores go before you into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: For John came to you in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him; and even after you saw it, you did not change your minds and believe him.
text.Rotherham: For John came unto you, in a way of righteousness, and ye believed him not,—but, the tax-collectors and the harlots, believed him; and, ye, seeing it, were not even smitten with regret, afterwards, so as to believe him.
text.KJV: For Iohn came vnto you in the way of righteousnesse, and ye beleeued him not: but the Publicanes and the harlots beleeued him. And ye when ye had seene it, repented not afterward, that ye might beleeue him.
text.NABRE: When John came to you in the way of righteousness, you did not believe him; but tax collectors and prostitutes did. Yet even when you saw that, you did not later change your minds and believe him. The Parable of the Tenants.
text.DRB: For John came to you in the way of justice, and you did not believe him. But the Publicans and whores did believe him: but you seeing it, neither have ye had repentance afterward, to believe him.

text.NRSV: “Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a wine press in it, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
text.Rotherham: Another parable, hear ye:—A man there was, a householder, who planted a vineyard, and, a wall around it, placed, and digged in it a wine-vat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen,—and left home.
text.KJV: ¶ Heare another parable. There was a certaine house-holder, which planted a Uineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a wine-presse in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a farre countrey.
text.NABRE: “Hear another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a wine press in it, and built a tower. Then he leased it to tenants and went on a journey.
text.DRB: Another parable hear ye: A man there was an householder who planted a vineyard, and made a hedge round about it, and digged in it a press, and builded a tower, and let it out to husbandmen: and went forth into a strange country.

text.NRSV: When the harvest time had come, he sent his slaves to the tenants to collect his produce.
text.Rotherham: And, when the season of fruits drew near, He sent forth his servants unto the husbandmen to receive his fruits.
text.KJV: And when the time of the fruite drew neere, he sent his seruants to the husbandmen, that they might receiue the fruits of it.
text.NABRE: When vintage time drew near, he sent his servants to the tenants to obtain his produce.
text.DRB: And when the time of fruits drew nigh, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, to receive the fruits thereof.

text.NRSV: But the tenants seized his slaves and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
text.Rotherham: And the husbandmen, taking his servants, one, indeed, they beat, and, another, slew,—and, another, stoned.
text.KJV: And the husbandmen tooke his seruants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.
text.NABRE: But the tenants seized the servants and one they beat, another they killed, and a third they stoned.
text.DRB: And the husbandmen apprehending his servants, one they beat, another they killed, and another they stoned.

text.NRSV: Again he sent other slaves, more than the first; and they treated them in the same way.
text.Rotherham: Again, sent he forth other servants, more than the first, and they did unto them, likewise.
text.KJV: Againe hee sent other seruants, moe then the first, and they did vnto them likewise.
text.NABRE: Again he sent other servants, more numerous than the first ones, but they treated them in the same way.
text.DRB: Again he sent other servants more than the former: and they did to them likewise.

text.NRSV: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, however, he sent forth unto them his son, saying,—They will pay deference unto my son!
text.KJV: But last of all, he sent vnto them his sonne, saying, They will reuerence my sonne.
text.NABRE: Finally, he sent his son to them, thinking, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.DRB: And last of all he sent to them his son, saying: They will reverence my son.

text.NRSV: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him and get his inheritance.’
text.Rotherham: But, the husbandmen, seeing the son, said among themselves,—This, is, the heir: Come on! Let us slay him, and have his inheritance.
text.KJV: But when the husbandmen saw the sonne, they said among themselues, This is the heire, come, let vs kill him, and let vs sease on his inheritance.
text.NABRE: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and acquire his inheritance.’
text.DRB: But the husbandmen seeing the son, said within themselves: This is the heir, come, let us kill him, and we shall have his inheritance.

text.NRSV: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text.Rotherham: And, taking him, they cast him forth outside the vineyard,—and slew him.
text.KJV: And they caught him, and cast him out of the Uineyard, and slew him.
text.NABRE: They seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text.DRB: And apprehending him they cast him forth out of the vineyard, & killed him.

text.NRSV: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?”
text.Rotherham: When therefore the master of the vineyard shall come, what will he do unto those husbandmen?
text.KJV: When the Lord therefore of the Uineyard commeth, what will he doe vnto those husbandmen?
text.NABRE: What will the owner of the vineyard do to those tenants when he comes?”
text.DRB: When therefore the Lord of the vineyard shall come, what will he do to those husbandmen?

text.NRSV: They said to him, “He will put those wretches to a miserable death, and lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the produce at the harvest time.”
text.Rotherham: They say unto him—Miserable men! Miserably, will he destroy them; and will let out, the vineyard, to other husbandmen, who will render unto him the fruits in their seasons.
text.KJV: They say vnto him, He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out his Uineyard vnto other husbandmen, which shall render him the fruits in their seasons.
text.NABRE: They answered him, “He will put those wretched men to a wretched death and lease his vineyard to other tenants who will give him the produce at the proper times.”
text.DRB: They say to him: The naughty men he will bring to naught: and his vineyard he will let out to other husbandmen, that shall render him the fruit in their seasons.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the scriptures: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto them—Have ye, never, read in the Scriptures, A stone which the builders rejected, the same, hath become, head of the corner: From the Lord, hath this come to pass, and is marvellous in our eyes.
text.KJV: Iesus saith vnto them, Did ye neuer reade in the Scriptures, The stone which the builders reiected, the same is become the head of the corner? This is the Lords doing, and it is marueilous in our eyes.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Did you never read in the scriptures: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; by the Lord has this been done, and it is wonderful in our eyes’?
text.DRB: Jesus saith to them: Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone which the builders rejected, the same is made into the head of the corner? By our Lord was this done, and it is marvelous in our eyes.

text.NRSV: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people that produces the fruits of the kingdom.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore I say unto you, The kingdom of God, will be taken away from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof:
text.KJV: Therefore say I vnto you, the kingdome of God shall be taken from you, and giuen to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I say to you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people that will produce its fruit.
text.DRB: Therefore I say to you, that the Kingdom of God shall be taken away from you, and shall be given to a Nation yielding the fruits thereof.

text.NRSV: The one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
text.Rotherham: [And, he that falleth on this stone, will be sorely bruised; but, on whomsoever it may fall, it will utterly destroy him.]
text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall fall on this stone, shalbe broken: but on whom soeuer it shall fall, it will grinde him to powder.
text.NABRE: [ The one who falls on this stone will be dashed to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.]”
text.DRB: And he that falleth upon this stone, shall be broken: and on whom it falleth, it shall all to bruise him.

text.NRSV: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized that he was speaking about them.
text.Rotherham: And the Chief-priests and the Pharisees, hearing his parables, took note, that, concerning them, he was speaking.
text.KJV: And when the chiefe Priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they perceiued that he spake of them.
text.NABRE: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they knew that he was speaking about them.
text.DRB: And when the chief Priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they knew that he spake of them.

text.NRSV: They wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, because they regarded him as a prophet.
text.Rotherham: And, seeking to secure him, they feared the multitudes; since, for a prophet, were they holding him.
text.KJV: But when they sought to lay hands on him, they feared the multitude, because they tooke him for a Prophet.
text.NABRE: And although they were attempting to arrest him, they feared the crowds, for they regarded him as a prophet.
text.DRB: And seeking to lay hands upon him, they feared the multitudes: because they held him as a Prophet.

text.NRSV: Once more Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying:
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus, again, spake in parables unto them, saying:—
text.KJV: And Iesus answered, and spake vnto them againe by parables, and said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 The Parable of the Wedding Feast. Jesus again in reply spoke to them in parables, saying,
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, spake again in parables to them, saying:

text.NRSV: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text.Rotherham: The kingdom of the heavens hath become like a man, a king,—who made a marriage-feast for his son;
text.KJV: The Kingdome of heauen is like vnto a certaine King, which made a marriage for his sonne,
text.NABRE: “The kingdom of heaven may be likened to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son.
text.DRB: The Kingdom of Heaven is likened to a man being a king, which made a marriage to his son.

text.NRSV: He sent his slaves to call those who had been invited to the wedding banquet, but they would not come.
text.Rotherham: And sent his servants, to call the invited into the marriage-feast,—and they would not come.
text.KJV: And sent forth his seruants to call them that were bidden to the wedding, and they would not come.
text.NABRE: He dispatched his servants to summon the invited guests to the feast, but they refused to come.
text.DRB: And he sent his servants to call them that were invited to the marriage: and they would not come.

text.NRSV: Again he sent other slaves, saying, ‘Tell those who have been invited: Look, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready; come to the wedding banquet.’
text.Rotherham: Again, sent he other servants, saying—Say to the invited, Lo! my dinner, have I prepared, mine oxen and my fatlings, are slain,—and, all things, are, ready: Come ye into the marriage-feast.
text.KJV: Againe, hee sent foorth other seruants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Beholde, I haue prepared my dinner; my oxen, and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come vnto the marriage.
text.NABRE: A second time he sent other servants, saying, ‘Tell those invited: “Behold, I have prepared my banquet, my calves and fattened cattle are killed, and everything is ready; come to the feast.”’
text.DRB: Again he sent other servants, saying: Tell them that were invited, Behold I have prepared my dinner; my beeves & fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come ye to the marriage.

text.NRSV: But they made light of it and went off, one to his farm, another to his business,
text.Rotherham: And, they, slighting it, went off, one, indeed, into his own field, and, another, unto his merchandise,—
text.KJV: But they made light of it, and went their wayes, one to his farme, another to his merchandize:
text.NABRE: Some ignored the invitation and went away, one to his farm, another to his business.
text.DRB: But they neglected and went their ways, one to his farm, and another to his merchandise:

text.NRSV: while the rest seized his slaves, mistreated them, and killed them.
text.Rotherham: And, the rest, securing his servants, ill-treated and slew them.
text.KJV: And the remnant tooke his seruants, and intreated them spitefully, and slew them.
text.NABRE: The rest laid hold of his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.
text.DRB: and the rest laid hands upon his servants, and spitefully entreating them, murdered them.

text.NRSV: The king was enraged. He sent his troops, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
text.Rotherham: And, the king, was provoked to anger,—and, sending his armies, destroyed those murderers, and, their city, set on fire.
text.KJV: But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth, and hee sent foorth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burnt vp their citie.
text.NABRE: The king was enraged and sent his troops, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
text.DRB: But when the king had heard of it, he was wroth, and sending his hosts, destroyed those murderers, and burnt their city.

text.NRSV: Then he said to his slaves, ‘The wedding is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto his servants—The marriage, indeed, is ready,—but, the invited, were not, worthy;
text.KJV: Then saith hee to his seruants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden, were not worthy.
text.NABRE: Then he said to his servants, ‘The feast is ready, but those who were invited were not worthy to come.
text.DRB: Then he saith to his servants: The marriage indeed is ready: but they that were invited, were not worthy.

text.NRSV: Go therefore into the main streets, and invite everyone you find to the wedding banquet.’
text.Rotherham: Be going, therefore, into the crossways of the roads, and, as many as ye shall find, call ye into the marriage-feast.
text.KJV: Goe yee therefore into the high wayes, and as many as yee shall finde, bid to the marriage.
text.NABRE: Go out, therefore, into the main roads and invite to the feast whomever you find.’
text.DRB: Go ye therefore into the high ways; and whosoever you shall find, call to the marriage.

text.NRSV: Those slaves went out into the streets and gathered all whom they found, both good and bad; so the wedding hall was filled with guests.
text.Rotherham: And those servants, going forth, into the roads, gathered together all whom they found, both bad and good,—and filled was the bride-chamber with guests.
text.KJV: So those seruants went out into the high wayes, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good, and the wedding was furnished with ghests.
text.NABRE: The servants went out into the streets and gathered all they found, bad and good alike, and the hall was filled with guests.
text.DRB: And his servants going forth into the ways, gathered together all that they found, bad and good: and the marriage was filled with guests.

text.NRSV: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing a wedding robe,
text.Rotherham: But the king, entering to view the guests, saw there a man, who had not put on a wedding-garment,—
text.KJV: ¶ And when the King came in to see the guests, hee sawe there a man, which had not on a wedding garment,
text.NABRE: But when the king came in to meet the guests he saw a man there not dressed in a wedding garment.
text.DRB: And the king went in to see the guests: and he saw there a man not attired in a wedding garment.

text.NRSV: and he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding robe?’ And he was speechless.
text.Rotherham: And saith unto him—Friend! how camest thou in here, not having a wedding-garment? And, he, was put to silence.
text.KJV: And hee sayth vnto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither, not hauing a wedding garment? And hee was speechlesse.
text.NABRE: He said to him, ‘My friend, how is it that you came in here without a wedding garment?’ But he was reduced to silence.
text.DRB: And he saith to him: Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? But he was dumb.

text.NRSV: Then the king said to the attendants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
