text.Rotherham: Then, the king said unto the ministers, Binding him feet and hands, cast him forth into the darkness, outside: There, shall be wafting and gnashing of teeth.
text.KJV: Then said the king to the seruants, Binde him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkenesse, there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: Then the king said to his attendants, ‘Bind his hands and feet, and cast him into the darkness outside, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.’
text.DRB: Then the king said to the waiters: Bind his hands and feet, and cast him into the utter darkness: there shall be weeping & gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: For many are called, but few are chosen.”
text.Rotherham: For, many, are called, but, few, chosen.
text.KJV: For many are called, but few are chosen.
text.NABRE: Many are invited, but few are chosen.” Paying Taxes to the Emperor.
text.DRB: For many be called, but few elect.

text.NRSV: Then the Pharisees went and plotted to entrap him in what he said.
text.Rotherham: Then, went the Pharisees and took, counsel, that they might ensnare him, in discourse.
text.KJV: ¶ Then went the Pharises, and tooke counsell, how they might intangle him in his talke.
text.NABRE: Then the Pharisees went off and plotted how they might entrap him in speech.
text.DRB: Then the Pharisees departing, consulted among themselves for to entrap him in his talk.

text.NRSV: So they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and teach the way of God in accordance with truth, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality.
text.Rotherham: And they sent forth to him their disciples, with the Herodians, saying, Teacher! we know that, true, thou art, and, the way of God, in truth, dost teach, and it concerneth thee not about anyone,—for thou lookest not unto the face of men:
text.KJV: And they sent out vnto him their disciples, with the Herodians, saying, Master, wee know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in trueth, neither carest thou for any man; for thou regardest not the person of men.
text.NABRE: They sent their disciples to him, with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are a truthful man and that you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. And you are not concerned with anyone’s opinion, for you do not regard a person’s status.
text.DRB: And they send to him their disciples with the Herodians, saying: Master, we know that thou art a true speaker, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest thou for any man. For thou dost not respect the person of men:

text.NRSV: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?”
text.Rotherham: Tell us then, how, to thee, it seemeth? Is it allowable to give tax unto Caesar, or not?
text.KJV: Tell vs therefore, what thinkest thou? Is it lawfull to giue tribute vnto Cesar, or not?
text.NABRE: Tell us, then, what is your opinion: Is it lawful to pay the census tax to Caesar or not?”
text.DRB: Tell us therefore what is thy opinion, is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?

text.NRSV: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, “Why are you putting me to the test, you hypocrites?
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, taking note of their wickedness, said—Why are ye tempting me, hypocrites?
text.KJV: But Iesus perceiued their wickednesse, and said, Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?
text.NABRE: Knowing their malice, Jesus said, “Why are you testing me, you hypocrites?
text.DRB: But Jesus knowing their naughtiness, said: What do you tempt me Hypocrites?

text.NRSV: Show me the coin used for the tax.” And they brought him a denarius.
text.Rotherham: Shew me the coin appointed for the tax. And, they, brought unto him a denary.
text.KJV: Shew me the tribute money. And they brought vnto him a peny.
text.NABRE: Show me the coin that pays the census tax.” Then they handed him the Roman coin.
text.DRB: Shew me the tribute coin. And they offered him a penny.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Whose, is this image and the inscription?
text.KJV: And he sayth vnto them, Whose is this image and superscription?
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Whose image is this and whose inscription?”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to them: Whose is this image and superscription?

text.NRSV: They answered, “The emperor’s.” Then he said to them, “Give therefore to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
text.Rotherham: They say—Caesar’s. Then, saith he unto them—Render, therefore, the things of Caesar, unto Caesar,—and, the things of God, unto God.
text.KJV: They say vnto him, Cesars. Then sayth he vnto them, Render therefore vnto Cesar, the things which are Cesars: and vnto God, the things that are Gods.
text.NABRE: They replied, “Caesar’s.” At that he said to them, “Then repay to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.”
text.DRB: They say to him, Cæsar's. Then he saith to them: Render therefore the things that are Cæsar's, to Cæsar: and the things that are God's, to God.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, they were amazed; and they left him and went away.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing, they marvelled,—and, leaving him, departed.
text.KJV: When they had heard these wordes, they marueiled, and left him, and went their way.
text.NABRE: When they heard this they were amazed, and leaving him they went away. The Question About the Resurrection.
text.DRB: And hearing it they marveled, and leaving him went their ways.

text.NRSV: The same day some Sadducees came to him, saying there is no resurrection; and they asked him a question, saying,
text.Rotherham: On that day, there came unto him Sadducees, who say, there is, no resurrection—and they questioned him,
text.KJV: ¶ The same day came to him the Sadduces, which say that there is no resurrection, and asked him,
text.NABRE: On that day Sadducees approached him, saying that there is no resurrection. They put this question to him,
text.DRB: That day there came to him the Sadducees, that say there is no resurrection, and asked him,

text.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies childless, his brother shall marry the widow, and raise up children for his brother.’
text.Rotherham: saying—Teacher! Moses, said, If any man die not having children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
text.KJV: Saying, Master, Moses said, If a man die, hauing no children, his brother shall marrie his wife, and raise vp seed vnto his brother.
text.NABRE: saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies without children, his brother shall marry his wife and raise up descendants for his brother.’
text.DRB: saying: Master, Moyses said, If a man die not having a child, that his brother marry his wife, and raise up seed to his brother.

text.NRSV: Now there were seven brothers among us; the first married, and died childless, leaving the widow to his brother.
text.Rotherham: Now there were, with us, seven brethren; and, the first, marrying, died, and not having seed, left his wife unto his brother.
text.KJV: Now there were with vs seuen brethren, and the first when he had maried a wife, deceased, and hauing no issue, left his wife vnto his brother.
text.NABRE: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first married and died and, having no descendants, left his wife to his brother.
text.DRB: And there were with us seven brethren: and the first having married a wife, died; and not having issue, left his wife to his brother.

text.NRSV: The second did the same, so also the third, down to the seventh.
text.Rotherham: Likewise, the second also, and the third,—unto the seven.
text.KJV: Likewise the second also, and the third, vnto the seuenth.
text.NABRE: The same happened with the second and the third, through all seven.
text.DRB: In like manner the second and the third even to the seventh.

text.NRSV: Last of all, the woman herself died.
text.Rotherham: And, last of all, died, the wife.
text.KJV: And last of al the woman died also.
text.NABRE: Finally the woman died.
text.DRB: And last of all the woman died also.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection, then, whose wife of the seven will she be? For all of them had married her.”
text.Rotherham: In the resurrection, therefore,—Of which of the seven, shall she be wife? For, all, had her.
text.KJV: Therefore, in the resurrection, whose wife shall she be of the seuen? for they all had her.
text.NABRE: Now at the resurrection, of the seven, whose wife will she be? For they all had been married to her.”
text.DRB: In the Resurrection therefore whose wife of the seven shall she be? For they all had her.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “You are wrong, because you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus answering, said—Ye are deceiving yourselves, knowing neither the Scriptures, nor yet the power of God.
text.KJV: Iesus answered, and said vnto them, Yee doe erre, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “You are misled because you do not know the scriptures or the power of God.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: You do err, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God.

text.NRSV: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
text.Rotherham: For, in the resurrection, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but, as messengers in the heaven, are they.
text.KJV: For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are giuen in marriage, but are as the Angels of God in heauen.
text.NABRE: At the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are like the angels in heaven.
text.DRB: For in the resurrection neither shall they marry nor be married: but are as the Angels of God in Heaven.

text.NRSV: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God,
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the resurrection of the dead—Have ye not read what was spoken unto you by God, saying—
text.KJV: But as touching the resurrection of the dead, haue ye not read that which was spoken vnto you by God, saying,
text.NABRE: And concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God,
text.DRB: And concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read that which was spoken of God saying to you.

text.NRSV: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is God not of the dead, but of the living.”
text.Rotherham: I, am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?—He is not God, of the dead, but, of the living.
text.KJV: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Iacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the liuing.
text.NABRE: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead but of the living.”
text.DRB: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He is not God of the dead, but of the living.

text.NRSV: And when the crowd heard it, they were astounded at his teaching.
text.Rotherham: And, the multitudes, hearing, were being struck with astonishment at his teaching.
text.KJV: And when the multitude heard this, they were astonished at his doctrine.
text.NABRE: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at his teaching. The Greatest Commandment.
text.DRB: And the multitudes hearing it, marveled at his doctrine.

text.NRSV: When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together,
text.Rotherham: Now, the Pharisees, hearing that he had silenced the Sadducees, were brought together with one accord;
text.KJV: ¶ But when the Pharises had heard that he had put the Sadduces to silence, they were gathered together.
text.NABRE: When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together,
text.DRB: But the Pharisees hearing that he had put the Sadducees to silence, came together:

text.NRSV: and one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.
text.Rotherham: and one from among them, a lawyer, proposed a question, putting him to the test:
text.KJV: Then one of them, which was a Lawyer, asked him a question, tempting him, and saying,
text.NABRE: and one of them [a scholar of the law] tested him by asking,
text.DRB: and one of them a doctor of law asked of him, tempting him:

text.NRSV: “Teacher, which commandment in the law is the greatest?”
text.Rotherham: Teacher! which commandment, is greatest in the law?
text.KJV: Master, which is the great Commandement in the Law?
text.NABRE: “Teacher, which commandment in the law is the greatest?”
text.DRB: Master, which is the great commandment in the law?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Thou shalt love the Lord thy God—with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind:
text.KJV: Iesus sayd vnto him, Thou shalt loue the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soule, and with all thy minde.
text.NABRE: He said to him, “You shall love the Lord, your God, with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God from thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole mind.

text.NRSV: This is the greatest and first commandment.
text.Rotherham: This, is the great and first commandment.
text.KJV: This is the first and great Commandement.
text.NABRE: This is the greatest and the first commandment.
text.DRB: This is the greatest & the first commandment.

text.NRSV: And a second is like it: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
text.Rotherham: The second, like it, is, this: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
text.KJV: And the second is like vnto it, Thou shalt loue thy neighbour as thy selfe.
text.NABRE: The second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
text.DRB: And the second is like to this: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

text.NRSV: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: In these two commandments, all the law, is contained, and the prophets.
text.KJV: On these two Commandements hang all the Law and the Prophets.
text.NABRE: The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.” The Question About David’s Son.
text.DRB: On these two commandments dependeth the whole Law and the Prophets.

text.NRSV: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them this question:
text.Rotherham: Now, the Pharisees having come together, Jesus questioned them, saying—
text.KJV: ¶ While the Pharises were gathered together, Iesus asked them,
text.NABRE: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus questioned them,
text.DRB: And the Pharisees being assembled, Jesus asked them

text.NRSV: “What do you think of the Messiah? Whose son is he?” They said to him, “The son of David.”
text.Rotherham: How, to you, doth it seem, concerning the Christ? Whose son, is he? They say unto him—David’s.
text.KJV: Saying, What thinke yee of Christ? whose sonne is hee? They say vnto him, The sonne of Dauid.
text.NABRE: saying, “What is your opinion about the Messiah? Whose son is he?” They replied, “David’s.”
text.DRB: saying: What is your opinion of Christ? whose son is he? They say to him, David's.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “How is it then that David by the Spirit calls him Lord, saying,
text.Rotherham: He saith unto them—How then doth, David, in spirit, call him, Lord, saying—
text.KJV: He saith vnto them, How then doth Dauid in spirit call him Lord, saying,
text.NABRE: He said to them, “How, then, does David, inspired by the Spirit, call him ‘lord,’ saying:
text.DRB: He saith to them: How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying:

text.NRSV: ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet”’?
text.Rotherham: The Lord, hath said unto, my Lord,—Sit thou on my right hand, until I make thy foes thy footstool?
text.KJV: The Lord said vnto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstoole?
text.NABRE: ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand until I place your enemies under your feet”’?
text.DRB: The Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand, until I put thine enemies the foot-stool of thy feet?

text.NRSV: If David thus calls him Lord, how can he be his son?”
text.Rotherham: If then, David, calleth him, Lord, How, is he, his son?
text.KJV: If Dauid then call him Lord, how is he his sonne?
text.NABRE: If David calls him ‘lord,’ how can he be his son?”
text.DRB: If David therefore call him Lord, how is he his son?

text.NRSV: No one was able to give him an answer, nor from that day did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, was able to answer him a word, neither durst anyone, from that day, question him, any more.
text.KJV: And no man was able to answere him a word, neither durst any man (from that day foorth) aske him any moe questions.
text.NABRE: No one was able to answer him a word, nor from that day on did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.
text.DRB: And no man could answer him a word: neither durst any man from that day ask him any more.

text.KJV: Then spake Iesus to the multitude, and to his disciples,
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Denunciation of the Scribes and Pharisees. Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to his disciples,
text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples,
text.DRB: Then Jesus spake to the multitudes and to his Disciples,
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus spake unto the multitudes and unto his disciples,

text.KJV: Saying, The Scribes and the Pharises sit in Moses seate:
text.NABRE: saying, “The scribes and the Pharisees have taken their seat on the chair of Moses.
text.NRSV: “The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses’ seat;
text.DRB: saying: Upon the chair of Moyses have sitten the Scribes and the Pharisees.
text.Rotherham: saying—Upon Moses’ seat, have sat down, the Scribes and the Pharisees:

text.KJV: All therefore whatsoeuer they bid you obserue, that obserue and doe, but doe not ye after their workes: for they say, and doe not.
text.NABRE: Therefore, do and observe all things whatsoever they tell you, but do not follow their example. For they preach but they do not practice.
text.NRSV: therefore, do whatever they teach you and follow it; but do not do as they do, for they do not practice what they teach.
text.DRB: All things therefore whatsoever they shall say to you, observe ye and do ye: but according to their works do ye not, for they say and do not.
text.Rotherham: All things, therefore, whatsoever they tell you, do and observe,—but, according to their works, do ye not, for they, say, and do not, perform.

text.KJV: For they binde heauie burdens, and grieuous to be borne, and lay them on mens shoulders, but they themselues will not mooue them with one of their fingers.
text.NABRE: They tie up heavy burdens [hard to carry] and lay them on people’s shoulders, but they will not lift a finger to move them.
text.NRSV: They tie up heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on the shoulders of others; but they themselves are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
text.DRB: For they bind heavy burdens & importable: & put them upon men's shoulders: but with a finger of their own they will not move them.
text.Rotherham: But they bind together heavy burdens, and lay upon men’s shoulders, whereas, they, with their finger, are not willing to move them.

text.KJV: But all their workes they doe, for to be seene of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,
text.NABRE: All their works are performed to be seen. They widen their phylacteries and lengthen their tassels.
text.NRSV: They do all their deeds to be seen by others; for they make their phylacteries broad and their fringes long.
text.DRB: But they do all their works for to be seen of men. For they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge their fringes.
text.Rotherham: But, all their works, they do, to be gazed at, by men,—for they make broad their amulets, and make large their fringes,

text.KJV: And loue the vppermost roomes at feasts, and the chiefe seats in the Synagogues,
text.NABRE: They love places of honor at banquets, seats of honor in synagogues,
text.NRSV: They love to have the place of honor at banquets and the best seats in the synagogues,
text.DRB: And they love the first places at suppers, and the first chairs in the Synagogues,
text.Rotherham: And dearly love the first couch in the chief meals, and the first seats in the synagogues,

text.KJV: And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.
text.NABRE: greetings in marketplaces, and the salutation ‘Rabbi.’
text.NRSV: and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have people call them rabbi.
text.DRB: and salutations in the market-place, and to be called of men, Rabbi.
text.Rotherham: And the salutations in the market-places, and to be called by men, Rabbi.

text.KJV: But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, euen Christ, and all ye are brethren.
text.NABRE: As for you, do not be called ‘Rabbi.’ You have but one teacher, and you are all brothers.
text.NRSV: But you are not to be called rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all students.
text.DRB: But be not you called Rabbi. For one is your Master, and all you are brethren.
text.Rotherham: But, ye,—do not be called Rabbi,—for, one, is your Teacher, and, all ye, are brethren;

text.KJV: And call no man your father vpon the earth: for one is your father which is in heauen.
text.NABRE: Call no one on earth your father; you have but one Father in heaven.
text.NRSV: And call no one your father on earth, for you have one Father—the one in heaven.
text.DRB: And call none Father to yourself upon earth: for one is your Father, he that is in Heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, father, be none of you called, upon the earth, for, one, is your Father, the Heavenly;

text.KJV: Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, euen Christ.
text.NABRE: Do not be called ‘Master’; you have but one master, the Messiah.
text.NRSV: Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Messiah.
text.DRB: Neither be ye called Masters: for one is your Master, Christ.
text.Rotherham: Neither be called leaders, for, your leader, is one, the Christ;

text.KJV: But hee that is greatest among you, shall be your seruant.
text.NABRE: The greatest among you must be your servant.
text.NRSV: The greatest among you will be your servant.
text.DRB: He that is the greater of you, shall be your servitor.
text.Rotherham: And, the greatest of you, shall be to you, a minister;

text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall exalt himselfe, shall be abased: and he that shall humble himselfe, shall be exalted.
text.NABRE: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled; but whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
text.NRSV: All who exalt themselves will be humbled, and all who humble themselves will be exalted.
text.DRB: And he that exalteth himself, shall be humbled: and he that humbleth himself, shall be exalted.
text.Rotherham: And, whoever shall exalt himself, shall be, abased, and, whoever shall abase himself, shall be, exalted,

text.KJV: ¶ But woe vnto you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; for yee shut vp the kingdom of heauen against men: For yee neither goe in your selues, neither suffer ye them that are entring, to goe in.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You lock the kingdom of heaven before human beings. You do not enter yourselves, nor do you allow entrance to those trying to enter.
text.NRSV: “But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. For you do not go in yourselves, and when others are going in, you stop them.
text.DRB: But woe to you Scribes & Pharisees, hypocrites: because you shut the Kingdom of Heaven before men. For yourselves do not enter in: & those that are going in, you suffer not to enter.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; for yee deuoure widowes houses, and for a pretence make long prayer; therefore ye shall receiue the greater damnation.
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: because you devour widows' houses, praying long prayers. For this you shall receive the greater judgement.
text.Rotherham: But alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye are locking up the kingdom of the heavens before men,—for, ye, are not entering, neither, them who are entering, suffer ye to enter.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharises, hypocrites; for yee compasse Sea and land to make one Proselyte, and when hee is made, yee make him two fold more the childe of hell then your selues.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You traverse sea and land to make one convert, and when that happens you make him a child of Gehenna twice as much as yourselves.
text.NRSV: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cross sea and land to make a single convert, and you make the new convert twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: because you go round about the sea and the land, to make one proselyte: and when he is made, you make him the child of hell double more than yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: because ye compass sea and dry land, to make one convert—and, when it is done, ye make him a son of gehenna, twofold more than ye.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you, yee blind guides, which say, Whosoeuer shall sweare by the Temple, it is nothing: but whosoeuer shal sweare by the gold of the Temple, he is a debter.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, blind guides, who say, ‘If one swears by the temple, it means nothing, but if one swears by the gold of the temple, one is obligated.’
text.NRSV: “Woe to you, blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever swears by the sanctuary, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the sanctuary is bound by the oath.’
text.DRB: Woe to you blind guides, that say, whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing: but he that shall swear by the gold of the temple, is bound.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, blind guides! that say—Whosoever shall swear by the Temple, it is, nothing, but, whosoever shall swear by the gold of the Temple, is bound:

text.KJV: Ye fooles and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the Temple that sanctifieth the gold?
text.NABRE: Blind fools, which is greater, the gold, or the temple that made the gold sacred?
text.NRSV: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the sanctuary that has made the gold holy?
text.DRB: Ye foolish and blind; for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?
text.Rotherham: Foolish and blind! for which is, greater, The gold, or the Temple that hath hallowed the gold?

text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall sweare by the Altar, it is nothing: but whosoeuer sweareth by the gift that is vpon it, he is guiltie.
text.NABRE: And you say, ‘If one swears by the altar, it means nothing, but if one swears by the gift on the altar, one is obligated.’
text.NRSV: And you say, ‘Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on the altar is bound by the oath.’
text.DRB: And whosoever shall swear by the Altar, it is nothing: but whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it, is bound.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is, nothing, but, whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it, is bound:

text.KJV: Ye fooles and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the Altar that sanctifieth the gift?
text.NABRE: You blind ones, which is greater, the gift, or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text.NRSV: How blind you are! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?
text.DRB: Ye blind; for whether is greater, the gift, or the Altar that sanctifieth the gift?
text.Rotherham: Blind! for which is greater, The gift, or the altar that halloweth the gift?

text.KJV: Who so therefore shall sweare by the Altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.
text.NABRE: One who swears by the altar swears by it and all that is upon it;
text.NRSV: So whoever swears by the altar, swears by it and by everything on it;
text.DRB: He therefore that sweareth by the Altar, sweareth by it, and by all things that are upon it:
text.Rotherham: He therefore that hath sworn by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all that is upon it;

text.KJV: And who so shall sweare by the Temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.
text.NABRE: one who swears by the temple swears by it and by him who dwells in it;
text.NRSV: and whoever swears by the sanctuary, swears by it and by the one who dwells in it;
text.DRB: And whosoever shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it and by him that dwelleth in it:
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath sworn by the Temple, sweareth by it, and by him who dwelleth therein;

text.KJV: And he that shall sweare by heauen, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.
text.NABRE: one who swears by heaven swears by the throne of God and by him who is seated on it.
text.NRSV: and whoever swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by the one who is seated upon it.
text.DRB: And he that sweareth by Heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath sworn by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him who sitteth thereupon.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; for yee pay tithe of mint, and annise, and cummine, and haue omitted the weightier matters of the Law, iudgement, mercie and faith: these ought ye to haue done, and not to leaue the other vndone.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You pay tithes of mint and dill and cummin, and have neglected the weightier things of the law: judgment and mercy and fidelity. [But] these you should have done, without neglecting the others.
text.NRSV: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faith. It is these you ought to have practiced without neglecting the others.
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: because you tithe mint, and anise, and cummin, and have left the weightier things of the law, judgement, and mercy, and faith. These things you ought to have done, & not to have omitted those.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye tithe the mint and the anise and the cummin,—and have dismissed the weightier matters of the law—the justice, the mercy, and the faith; whereas, these, it was binding, to do, and, those, not to, dismiss.

text.KJV: Ye blind guides, which straine at a gnat, and swallow a camel.
text.NABRE: Blind guides, who strain out the gnat and swallow the camel!
text.NRSV: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel!
text.DRB: Blind guides, that strain a gnat, and swallow a camel.
text.Rotherham: Blind guides! Straining out the gnat, but, the camel, swallowing.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; for yee make cleane the outside of the cup, and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excesse.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You cleanse the outside of cup and dish, but inside they are full of plunder and self-indulgence.
text.NRSV: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the plate, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, Hypocrites: because you make clean that on the outside of the cup and dish, but within you are full of rapine and uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye cleanse the outside of the cup and of the dish,—while, within, they are full of plunder and intemperance.

text.KJV: Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may bee cleane also.
text.NABRE: Blind Pharisee, cleanse first the inside of the cup, so that the outside also may be clean.
text.NRSV: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup, so that the outside also may become clean.
text.DRB: Thou blind Pharisee, first make clean the inside of the cup and the dish that the outside may become clean.
text.Rotherham: Blind Pharisee! cleanse, first, the inside, of the cup [and of the dish], that, the outside thereof, may become, clean.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for yee are like vnto whited sepulchres, which indeed appeare beautifull outward, but are within full of dead mens bones, and of all vncleannesse.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You are like whitewashed tombs, which appear beautiful on the outside, but inside are full of dead men’s bones and every kind of filth.
text.NRSV: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which on the outside look beautiful, but inside they are full of the bones of the dead and of all kinds of filth.
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, Hypocrites: because you are like to whited sepulchres, which outwardly appear unto men beautiful, but within are full of dead men's bones, and all filthiness.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye make yourselves like sepulchres whitewashed, which, outside, indeed, appear, beautiful, but, within, are full, of dead men’s bones and all uncleanness,—

text.KJV: Euen so, yee also outwardly appeare righteous vnto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisie and iniquitie.
text.NABRE: Even so, on the outside you appear righteous, but inside you are filled with hypocrisy and evildoing.
text.NRSV: So you also on the outside look righteous to others, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
text.DRB: So you also outwardly indeed appear to men just; but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, outside, indeed, appear to men, righteous, but, within, are full, of hypocrisy and lawlessness.

text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because ye build the tombes of the Prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites. You build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the memorials of the righteous,
text.NRSV: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous,
text.DRB: Woe to you Scribes and Pharisees, Hypocrites: because you build the Prophets' sepulchres, and garnish the monuments of just men,
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites; because ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and adorn the monuments of the righteous,

text.KJV: And say, If wee had beene in the dayes of our fathers, wee would not haue bene partakers with them in the blood of the Prophets.
text.NABRE: and you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we would not have joined them in shedding the prophets’ blood.’
text.NRSV: and you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.’
text.DRB: and say: If we had been in our fathers' days, we had not been their fellows in the blood of the Prophets.
text.Rotherham: and say—If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been their partners in the blood of the prophets:

text.KJV: Wherefore ye bee witnesses vnto your selues, that yee are the children of them which killed the Prophets.
text.NABRE: Thus you bear witness against yourselves that you are the children of those who murdered the prophets;
text.NRSV: Thus you testify against yourselves that you are descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
text.DRB: Therefore you are a testimony to your own selves, that you are the sons of them that killed the Prophets.
text.Rotherham: So that ye bear witness against yourselves, that ye are, sons, of them who murdered the prophets.

text.KJV: Fil ye vp then the measure of your fathers.
text.NABRE: now fill up what your ancestors measured out!
text.NRSV: Fill up, then, the measure of your ancestors.
text.DRB: And fill you up the measure of your fathers.
text.Rotherham: And, ye, fill ye up the measure of your fathers!

text.KJV: Yee serpents, yee generation of vipers, How can yee escape the damnation of hell?
text.NABRE: You serpents, you brood of vipers, how can you flee from the judgment of Gehenna?
text.NRSV: You snakes, you brood of vipers! How can you escape being sentenced to hell?
text.DRB: You serpents, viper's broods, how will you flee from the judgement of Hell?
text.Rotherham: Serpents! broods of vipers! how should ye flee from the judgment of gehenna?

text.KJV: ¶ Wherefore behold, I send vnto you Prophets, and wisemen, and Scribes, and some of them yee shall kill and crucifie, and some of them shall yee scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from citie to citie:
text.NABRE: Therefore, behold, I send to you prophets and wise men and scribes; some of them you will kill and crucify, some of them you will scourge in your synagogues and pursue from town to town,
text.NRSV: Therefore I send you prophets, sages, and scribes, some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town;
text.DRB: Therefore behold I send unto you Prophets, and wise men, and Scribes, and of them you shall kill & crucify, and of them you shall scourge in your Synagogues, and persecute from city into city:
text.Rotherham: For this cause, lo! I, send unto you, prophets and wise men and scribes,—some from among them, ye will slay and crucify, And some from among them ye will—scourge in your synagogues, and pursue from city to city:

text.KJV: That vpon you may come all the righteous blood shed vpon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel, vnto the blood of Zacharias, sonne of Barachias, whom yee slew betweene the temple and the altar.
text.NABRE: so that there may come upon you all the righteous blood shed upon earth, from the righteous blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, the son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
text.NRSV: so that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
text.DRB: that upon you may come all the just blood that was shed upon the earth, from the blood of Abel the just, even unto the blood of Zacharias the son of Barachias, whom you murdered between the temple & the altar.
text.Rotherham: That there may come upon you—all righteous blood poured out upon the earth, from the blood of Abel the righteous, unto the blood of Zachariah, son of Barachiah, whom ye murdered between the Temple and the altar.

text.KJV: Uerily I say vnto you, All these things shal come vpon this generation.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation. The Lament over Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, all this will come upon this generation.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, all these things shall come upon this generation.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—All these things, will have come, upon this generation.

text.KJV: O Hierusalem, Hierusalem, thou that killest the Prophets, and stonest them which are sent vnto thee, how often would I haue gathered thy children together, euen as a hen gathereth her chickens vnder her wings, and yee would not?
text.NABRE: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how many times I yearned to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her young under her wings, but you were unwilling!
text.NRSV: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to it! How often have I desired to gather your children together as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you were not willing!
text.DRB: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the Prophets, and stonest them that were sent to thee, how often would I gather together thy children as the hen doth gather together her chickens under her wings, and thou wouldest not?
text.Rotherham: Jerusalem! Jerusalem! that slayeth the prophets, and stoneth them that have been sent unto her,—how often, would I have gathered thy children, like as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings,—and ye would not!

text.KJV: Behold, your house is left vnto you desolate.
text.NABRE: Behold, your house will be abandoned, desolate.
text.NRSV: See, your house is left to you, desolate.
text.DRB: Behold, your house shall be left desert to you.
text.Rotherham: Lo! your house is left to you;

text.KJV: For I say vnto you, yee shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that commeth in the Name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.’”
text.NRSV: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, ‘Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.’”
text.DRB: For I say to you, you shall not see me from hence forth till you say: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord.
text.Rotherham: For I say unto you—in no wise, may ye see me, henceforth, until ye say, Blessed, is he that cometh, in the name of the Lord!

text.NRSV: As Jesus was leaving the temple and was walking away, his disciples came to him to point out the buildings of the temple.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus coming forth, from the temple, was taking his departure, when his disciples came forward to point out to him the buildings of the temple.
text.KJV: And Iesus went out, and departed from the temple, and his Disciples came to him, for to shew him the buildings of the temple.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 The Destruction of the Temple Foretold. Jesus left the temple area and was going away, when his disciples approached him to point out the temple buildings.
text.DRB: And Jesus being gone out of the temple, went. And his Disciples came to shew him the buildings of the temple.

text.NRSV: Then he asked them, “You see all these, do you not? Truly I tell you, not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—Are ye not beholding all these things? Verily, I say unto you—in no wise, shall there be left here, stone upon stone, which shall not be thrown down.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto them, See yee not all these things? Uerily I say vnto you, there shall not be left heere one stone vpon another, that shall not be throwen downe.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “You see all these things, do you not? Amen, I say to you, there will not be left here a stone upon another stone that will not be thrown down.”
text.DRB: And he answering said to them: Do you see all these things? Amen I say to you, there shall not be left here a stone upon a stone that shall not be destroyed.

text.NRSV: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was sitting upon the Mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him, privately, saying—Tell us, when these things shall be,—and what the sign of thy presence, and the conclusion of the age.
text.KJV: ¶ And as he sate vpon the mount of Oliues, the Disciples came vnto him priuately, saying, Tell vs, when shall these things be? And what shall be the signe of thy coming, and of the end of the world?
text.NABRE: The Beginning of Calamities. As he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples approached him privately and said, “Tell us, when will this happen, and what sign will there be of your coming, and of the end of the age?”
text.DRB: And when he was sitting upon Mount-olivet, the Disciples came to him secretly, saying: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign of thy coming, and of the consummation of the world?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Beware that no one leads you astray.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—Be taking heed, lest anyone, deceive, you;
text.KJV: And Iesus answered, and said vnto them, Take heed that no man deceiue you.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “See that no one deceives you.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: Beware that no man seduce you:

text.NRSV: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Messiah!’ and they will lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: For, many, will come upon my name, saying—I, am the Christ,—and will, deceive many.
text.KJV: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ: and shall deceiue many.
text.NABRE: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Messiah,’ and they will deceive many.
text.DRB: for many shall come in my name saying, I am Christ; and they shall seduce many.

text.NRSV: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that you are not alarmed; for this must take place, but the end is not yet.
text.Rotherham: Moreover ye will be sure to be hearing of wars, and rumours of wars: Mind! be not alarmed, for it must needs happen,—but, not yet, is, the end;
text.KJV: And yee shall heare of warres, and rumors of warres: See that yee be not troubled: for all these things must come to passe, but the end is not yet.
text.NABRE: You will hear of wars and reports of wars; see that you are not alarmed, for these things must happen, but it will not yet be the end.
text.DRB: For you shall hear of wars, & bruits of wars. See that ye be not troubled. For these things must be done, but the end is not yet.

text.NRSV: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places:
text.Rotherham: For there will arise—nation against nation, and kingdom against kingdom,—and there will be famines and earthquakes, in places.
text.KJV: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdome against kingdome, and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes in diuers places.
text.NABRE: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be famines and earthquakes from place to place.
text.DRB: For Nation shall rise against Nation, and Kingdom against Kingdom; and there shall be pestilences, and famines, and earth-quakes in places;

text.NRSV: all this is but the beginning of the birthpangs.
text.Rotherham: But, all these things, are a beginning of birth-pangs.
text.KJV: All these are the beginning of sorrowes.
text.NABRE: All these are the beginning of the labor pains.
text.DRB: and all these things are the beginnings of sorrows.

text.NRSV: “Then they will hand you over to be tortured and will put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of my name.
text.Rotherham: Then, will they deliver you up into tribulation, and will slay you,—and ye will be men hated by all the nations, because of my name
text.KJV: Then shall they deliuer you vp to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and yee shall bee hated of all nations for my names sake.
text.NABRE: Then they will hand you over to persecution, and they will kill you. You will be hated by all nations because of my name.
text.DRB: Then shall they deliver you into tribulation, and shall kill you: and you shall be odious to all Nations for my name's sake.

text.NRSV: Then many will fall away, and they will betray one another and hate one another.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will many be caused to stumble, and, one another, will deliver up, and will hate one another;
text.KJV: And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
text.NABRE: And then many will be led into sin; they will betray and hate one another.
text.DRB: And then many shall be scandalized: and they shall deliver up one another: and they shall hate one another.

text.NRSV: And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: And, many false prophets, will arise, and deceive, many;
text.KJV: And many false Prophets shall rise, and shall deceiue many.
text.NABRE: Many false prophets will arise and deceive many;
text.DRB: And many false-prophets shall rise, and shall seduce many.

text.NRSV: And because lawlessness is increased, the love of many will grow cold.
text.Rotherham: And, because of lawlessness being brought to the full, the love of the many, will grew cold.
text.KJV: And because iniquitie shal abound, the loue of many shall waxe cold.
text.NABRE: and because of the increase of evildoing, the love of many will grow cold.
text.DRB: And because iniquity shall abound, the charity of many shall wax cold.

text.NRSV: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath endured throughout, the same, shall be saved.
text.KJV: But he that shall endure vnto the end, the same shall be saued.
text.NABRE: But the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.
text.DRB: But he that shall persevere to the end, he shall be saved.

text.NRSV: And this good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world, as a testimony to all the nations; and then the end will come.
text.Rotherham: And this glad message of the kingdom will be proclaimed in all the inhabited earth, for a witness unto all the nations,—and, then, will have come, the end.
text.KJV: And this Gospell of the kingdome shall be preached in all the world, for a witnesse vnto al nations, and then shall the end come.
text.NABRE: And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached throughout the world as a witness to all nations, and then the end will come. The Great Tribulation.
text.DRB: And this Gospel of the Kingdom shall be preached in the whole world, for a testimony to all nations, and then shall come the consummation.

text.NRSV: “So when you see the desolating sacrilege standing in the holy place, as was spoken of by the prophet Daniel (let the reader understand),
text.Rotherham: Whensoever, therefore, ye shall see the abomination desolation, that was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in a holy place,—he that readeth, let him think,
text.KJV: When yee therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, stand in the holy place, (who so readeth, let him vnderstand.)
text.NABRE: “When you see the desolating abomination spoken of through Daniel the prophet standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
text.DRB: Therefore when you shall see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, standing in the holy place (he that readeth, let him understand)

text.NRSV: then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;
text.Rotherham: Then, they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains;
text.KJV: Then let them which be in Iudea, flee into the mountaines.
text.NABRE: then those in Judea must flee to the mountains,
text.DRB: then they that are in Jewry, let them flee to the mountains:

text.NRSV: the one on the housetop must not go down to take what is in the house;
text.Rotherham: And, he that is on the house-top, let him not come down, to take away the things out of his house;
text.KJV: Let him which is on the house top, not come downe, to take any thing out of his house:
text.NABRE: a person on the housetop must not go down to get things out of his house,
text.DRB: and he that is on the house-top let him not come down to take any thing out of his house:

text.NRSV: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.
text.Rotherham: And, he that is in the field, let him not turn back, to take away his mantle.
text.KJV: Neither let him which is in the field, returne backe to take his clothes.
text.NABRE: a person in the field must not return to get his cloak.
text.DRB: and he that is in the field, let him not go back to take his coat.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!
text.Rotherham: But alas! for the women with child, and for them that are giving suck, in those days;
text.KJV: And woe vnto them that are with child, and to them that giue sucke in those dayes.
text.NABRE: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days.
text.DRB: And woe to them that are with child, and that give suck in those days.

text.NRSV: Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a sabbath.
text.Rotherham: But be praying that your flight, may not happen in winter, nor on sabbath;
text.KJV: But pray yee that your flight bee not in the winter, neither on the Sabbath day:
text.NABRE: Pray that your flight not be in winter or on the sabbath,
text.DRB: But pray that your flight be not in the winter, or on the Sabboth,

text.NRSV: For at that time there will be great suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, and never will be.
text.Rotherham: For there will be then, Great tribulation, such as hath not happened, from the beginning of the world, until the present time, neither in any wise shall happen.
text.KJV: For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor euer shall be.
text.NABRE: for at that time there will be great tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the world until now, nor ever will be.
text.DRB: For there shall be then great tribulation, such as hath not been from the beginning of the world until now, neither shall be.

text.NRSV: And if those days had not been cut short, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
text.Rotherham: And, except those days had been shortened, no flesh had been saved; but, for the sake of the chosen, those days shall be shortened.
text.KJV: And except those dayes should be shortned, there should no flesh be saued: but for the elects sake, those dayes shall be shortned.
text.NABRE: And if those days had not been shortened, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect they will be shortened.
text.DRB: And unless those days had been shortened, no flesh should be saved: but for the Elect the days shall be shortened.

text.NRSV: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look! Here is the Messiah!’ or ‘There he is!’—do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: Then, if any, unto you, say—Lo! here, is the Christ, or, there, Do not believe it;
text.KJV: Then if any man shall say vnto you, Loe, heere is Christ, or there: beleeue it not.
text.NABRE: If anyone says to you then, ‘Look, here is the Messiah!’ or, ‘There he is!’ do not believe it.
text.DRB: Then if any man shall say unto you: Lo here is Christ, or there; do not believe him.

text.NRSV: For false messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce great signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise, false Christs, and false prophets,—and they will show great signs and wonders, so that, if possible, even the chosen, should be deceived:—
text.KJV: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shal shew great signes and wonders: insomuch that (if it were possible,) they shall deceiue the very elect.
text.NABRE: False messiahs and false prophets will arise, and they will perform signs and wonders so great as to deceive, if that were possible, even the elect.
text.DRB: For there shall rise false Christs and false-Prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders, so that the Elect also (if it be possible) may be induced into error.

text.NRSV: Take note, I have told you beforehand.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have foretold you.
text.KJV: Behold, I haue told you before.
text.NABRE: Behold, I have told it to you beforehand.
text.DRB: Lo I have foretold you.

text.NRSV: So, if they say to you, ‘Look! He is in the wilderness,’ do not go out. If they say, ‘Look! He is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: If therefore they should say unto you, Lo! in the desert, he is, Do not go forth; Lo! in the chambers, Do not believe it.
text.KJV: Wherefore, if they shall say vnto you, Behold, he is in the desert, goe not foorth: Behold, he is in the secret chambers, beleeue it not.
text.NABRE: So if they say to you, ‘He is in the desert,’ do not go out there; if they say, ‘He is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
text.DRB: If therefore they shall say unto you: Behold he is in the desert; go ye not out: Behold in the closets, believe it not.

text.NRSV: For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the lightning goeth forth from the east, and shineth unto the west, so, shall be the presence of the Son of Man.
text.KJV: For as the lightening commeth out of the East, and shineth euen vnto the West: so shall also the coming of the Sonne of man be.
text.NABRE: For just as lightning comes from the east and is seen as far as the west, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
text.DRB: For as lightning cometh out of the east, and appeareth even into the west, so shall also the advent of the Son of man be.

text.NRSV: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever, the corpse, shall be, there, shall be gathered, the vultures!
text.KJV: For wheresoeuer the carkeise is, there will the Eagles bee gathered together.
text.NABRE: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
text.DRB: Wheresoever the body is, thither shall the Eagles also be gathered together.

text.NRSV: “Immediately after the suffering of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of heaven will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: But, straightway after the tribulation of those days, the sun, will be darkened, and, the moon, will not give her brightness, and, the stars, will fall from heaven,—and, the powers of the heavens, will be shaken;
text.KJV: ¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those dayes, shall the Sunne be darkned, and the Moone shall not giue her light, and the starres shall fall from heauen, and the powers of the heauens shall be shaken.
text.NABRE: The Coming of the Son of Man. “Immediately after the tribulation of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, and the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
text.DRB: And immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from Heaven, and the powers of Heaven shall be moved:

text.NRSV: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will be displayed—The sign of the Son of Man in heaven, and, then, will smite their breasts—all the tribes of the earth; and they will see the Son of Man—coming upon the clouds of heaven, with great power and glory.
text.KJV: And then shall appeare the signe of the Sonne of man in heauen: and then shall all the Tribes of the earth mourne, and they shall see the Sonne of man coming in the clouds of heauen, with power and great glory.
text.NABRE: And then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming upon the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
text.DRB: and then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in Heaven: and then shall all tribes of the earth bewail: and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of Heaven with much power and majesty.

text.NRSV: And he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
text.Rotherham: And he will send forth his messengers, with a great trumpet, and they will gather together his chosen—Out of the four winds, from heavens’ bounds, unto their bounds.
text.KJV: And hee shall send his Angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his Elect from the foure windes, from one end of heauen to the other.
text.NABRE: And he will send out his angels with a trumpet blast, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other. The Lesson of the Fig Tree.
text.DRB: And he shall send his Angels with a trumpet, and a great voice: and they shall gather together his Elect from the four winds, from the furthest parts of Heaven even to the ends thereof.

text.NRSV: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.Rotherham: Now, from the fig-tree, learn ye, the parable:—When, already, her young branch, becometh tender, and the leaves, may be sprouting, ye observe, that, near, is, the summer:
text.KJV: Now learne a parable of the figtree: when his branch is yet tender, and putteth foorth leaues, yee know that Summer is nigh:
text.NABRE: “Learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branch becomes tender and sprouts leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.DRB: And of the figtree learn a parable: When now the bough thereof is tender, and the leaves come forth, you know that summer is nigh.

text.NRSV: So also, when you see all these things, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, when ye shall see all these things, observe ye, that, near, he is, at the doors.
text.KJV: So likewise yee, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is neere, euen at the doores.
text.NABRE: In the same way, when you see all these things, know that he is near, at the gates.
text.DRB: So you also, when you shall see these things, know ye that it is nigh even at the doors.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise, shall this generation, pass away, until all these things, shall happen:
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, this generation shall not passe, till all these things be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: The heaven and the earth, shall pass away, but, my words, shall, in nowise, pass away.
text.KJV: Heauen and earth shall passe away, but my wordes shall not passe away.
text.NABRE: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away. The Unknown Day and Hour.
text.DRB: Heaven and earth shall pass, but my words shall not pass.

text.NRSV: “But about that day and hour no one knows, neither the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning that day and hour, no one, knoweth, neither the messengers of the heavens, nor the Son,—save the Father only.
text.KJV: ¶ But of that day and houre knoweth no man, no, not the Angels of heauen, but my Father onely.
text.NABRE: “But of that day and hour no one knows, neither the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but the Father alone.
text.DRB: But of that day and hour nobody knoweth, neither the Angels of Heaven, but the Father alone.

text.NRSV: For as the days of Noah were, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: For, just as the days of Noah, so, will be the presence of the Son of Man;
text.KJV: But as the dayes of Noe were, so shall also the comming of the Sonne of man be.
text.NABRE: For as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.
text.DRB: And as in the days of Noe, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

text.NRSV: For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark,
text.Rotherham: For, as they were in those days that were before the flood, feeding and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage,—until the day Noah entered into the ark;
text.KJV: For as in the dayes that were before the Flood, they were eating, and drinking, marrying, and giuing in mariage, vntill the day that Noe entred into the Arke,
text.NABRE: In [those] days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, up to the day that Noah entered the ark.
text.DRB: For as they were in the days before the flood, eating and drinking, marrying and giving to marriage, even unto that day in which Noe entered into the ark,

text.NRSV: and they knew nothing until the flood came and swept them all away, so too will be the coming of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: And they observed not, until the flood came and took away all together, so, will be, the presence of the Son of Man.
text.KJV: And knew not vntill the Flood came, and tooke them all away: so shall also the comming of the Sonne of man be.
text.NABRE: They did not know until the flood came and carried them all away. So will it be [also] at the coming of the Son of Man.
text.DRB: and knew not till the flood came, & took them all: so also shall the coming of the Son of man be.

text.NRSV: Then two will be in the field; one will be taken and one will be left.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall there be two men in the field,—one, is taken near, and, one, is left behind:
text.KJV: Then shall two be in the field, the one shalbe taken, and the other left.
text.NABRE: Two men will be out in the field; one will be taken, and one will be left.
text.DRB: Then two shall be in the field: one shall be taken, & one shall be left.

text.NRSV: Two women will be grinding meal together; one will be taken and one will be left.
text.Rotherham: Two women grinding at the mill,—one, is taken near, and, one, is left behind.
text.KJV: Two women shall be grinding at the mill: the one shall be taken, and the other left.
text.NABRE: Two women will be grinding at the mill; one will be taken, and one will be left.
text.DRB: Two women grinding in the mill: one shall be taken, & one shall be left.

text.NRSV: Keep awake therefore, for you do not know on what day your Lord is coming.
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, for ye know not, on what manner of day, your Lord is coming.
text.KJV: ¶ Watch therfore, for ye know not what houre your Lord doth come.
text.NABRE: Therefore, stay awake! For you do not know on which day your Lord will come.
text.DRB: Watch therefore because you know not what hour your Lord will come.

text.NRSV: But understand this: if the owner of the house had known in what part of the night the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and would not have let his house be broken into.
text.Rotherham: But there is, one thing, ye know—That, if the householder, had known, in what watch, the thief was coming, he would have been on the alert, and not have suffered his house to be dug through.
text.KJV: But know this, that if the good man of the house had knowen in what watch the thiefe would come, he would haue watched, and would not haue suffered his house to be broken vp.
text.NABRE: Be sure of this: if the master of the house had known the hour of night when the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and not let his house be broken into.
text.DRB: But this know ye, that if the good-man of the house did know what hour the thief would come, he would surely watch, and would not suffer his house to be broken up.

text.NRSV: Therefore you also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an unexpected hour.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, ye also, be getting ready, because, in what hour ye are, not thinking, The Son of Man doth come.
text.KJV: Therefore be yee also ready: for in such an houre as you thinke not, the sonne of man commeth.
text.NABRE: So too, you also must be prepared, for at an hour you do not expect, the Son of Man will come. The Faithful or the Unfaithful Servant.
text.DRB: Therefore be you also ready, because at what hour you know not, the Son of man will come.

text.NRSV: “Who then is the faithful and wise slave, whom his master has put in charge of his household, to give the other slaves their food at the proper time?
text.Rotherham: Who then is the faithful and prudent servant, whom the master hath appointed over his household, to give them food in season?
text.KJV: Who then is a faithfull and wise seruant, whom his Lord hath made ruler ouer his houshold, to giue them meat in due season?
text.NABRE: “Who, then, is the faithful and prudent servant, whom the master has put in charge of his household to distribute to them their food at the proper time?
text.DRB: Who, thinkest thou, is a faithful and wise servant, whom his Lord hath appointed over his family, to give them meat in season?

text.NRSV: Blessed is that slave whom his master will find at work when he arrives.
text.Rotherham: Happy! that servant, whom his master, when he cometh, shall find so doing!
text.KJV: Blessed is that seruant, whome his Lord when he commeth, shall finde so doing.
text.NABRE: Blessed is that servant whom his master on his arrival finds doing so.
text.DRB: Blessed is that servant, whom when his Lord cometh, he shall find so doing.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, he will put that one in charge of all his possessions.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Over all his substance, will he appoint him.
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, that hee shal make him ruler ouer all his goods.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, he will put him in charge of all his property.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that over all his goods shall he appoint him.

text.NRSV: But if that wicked slave says to himself, ‘My master is delayed,’
text.Rotherham: But, if that wicked servant should say in his heart—My master, delayeth,
text.KJV: But and if that euill seruant shal say in his heart, My Lord delayeth his comming,
text.NABRE: But if that wicked servant says to himself, ‘My master is long delayed,’
text.DRB: But if that naughty servant shall say in his heart: My Lord is long a coming:

text.NRSV: and begins to beat his fellow slaves, and eats and drinks with drunkards,
text.Rotherham: And begin to be striking his fellow-servants, and eating and drinking with the drunken,
text.KJV: And shall begin to smite his fellow seruants, and to eate and drinke with the drunken:
text.NABRE: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eat and drink with drunkards,
text.DRB: and shall begin to strike his fellow-servants, and eateth, & drinketh with drunkards:

text.NRSV: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour that he does not know.
text.Rotherham: The master of that servant, will have come, on a day when he is not expecting, and in an hour when he is not observing,—
text.KJV: The Lord of that seruant shall come in a day when hee looketh not for him, and in an houre that hee is not ware of:
text.NABRE: the servant’s master will come on an unexpected day and at an unknown hour
text.DRB: the Lord of that servant shall come in a day that he hopeth not, & an hour that he knoweth not,

text.NRSV: He will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.Rotherham: And will cut him asunder; and, his part, with the hypocrites, will appoint: There, shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth!
text.KJV: And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: and will punish him severely and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.
text.DRB: and shall divide him, & appoint his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: “Then the kingdom of heaven will be like this. Ten bridesmaids took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.
text.Rotherham: Then, will the kingdom of the heavens become like unto, ten virgins, who, taking their torches, went forth to meet the bridegroom.
text.KJV: Then shall the kingdome of heauen be likened vnto ten Uirgins, which tooke their lamps, & went forth to meet the bridegrome.
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 The Parable of the Ten Virgins. “Then the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
text.DRB: Then shall the Kingdom of Heaven be like to ten virgins, which taking their lamps went forth to meet the bridegroom and the bride.

text.NRSV: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.
text.Rotherham: Now, five of them, were foolish, and, five, prudent.
text.KJV: And fiue of them were wise, and fiue were foolish.
text.NABRE: Five of them were foolish and five were wise.
text.DRB: And five of them were foolish, and five wise.

text.NRSV: When the foolish took their lamps, they took no oil with them;
text.Rotherham: For, the foolish—though they took, their torches, took not with them, oil:
text.KJV: They that were foolish tooke their lampes, and tooke no oyle with them:
text.NABRE: The foolish ones, when taking their lamps, brought no oil with them,
text.DRB: But the five foolish, having taken their lamps, did not take oil with them:

text.NRSV: but the wise took flasks of oil with their lamps.
text.Rotherham: But, the prudent, took oil in their vessels, with their torches.
text.KJV: But the wise tooke oyle in their vessels with their lampes.
text.NABRE: but the wise brought flasks of oil with their lamps.
text.DRB: but the wise did take oil in their vessels with the lamps.

text.NRSV: As the bridegroom was delayed, all of them became drowsy and slept.
text.Rotherham: Now, the bridegroom, delaying, they all became drowsy, and were sleeping.
text.KJV: While the bridegrome taried, they all slumbred and slept.
text.NABRE: Since the bridegroom was long delayed, they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
text.DRB: And the bridegroom tarrying long they slumbered all and slept.

text.NRSV: But at midnight there was a shout, ‘Look! Here is the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.’
text.Rotherham: And, at midnight, an outcry hath been made—Lo! the bridegroom! Be going forth to meet him!
text.KJV: And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegrome commeth, goe ye out to meet him.
text.NABRE: At midnight, there was a cry, ‘Behold, the bridegroom! Come out to meet him!’
text.DRB: And at midnight there was a clamour made: Behold the bridegroom cometh, go ye forth to meet him.

text.NRSV: Then all those bridesmaids got up and trimmed their lamps.
text.Rotherham: Then, arose all those virgins, and trimmed their torches.
text.KJV: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lampes.
text.NABRE: Then all those virgins got up and trimmed their lamps.
text.DRB: Then arose all those virgins, and they trimmed their lamps.

text.NRSV: The foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
text.Rotherham: And, the foolish, unto the prudent, said—Give us of your oil, because, our torches, are going out.
text.KJV: And the foolish said vnto the wise, Giue vs of your oyle, for our lampes are gone out.
text.NABRE: The foolish ones said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
text.DRB: And the foolish said to the wise: Give us of your oil, because our lamps are going out.

text.NRSV: But the wise replied, ‘No! there will not be enough for you and for us; you had better go to the dealers and buy some for yourselves.’
text.Rotherham: But the prudent, answered, saying—Lest once, by any means, there be not enough for us and you, be going rather unto them that sell, and buy for yourselves
text.KJV: But the wise answered, saying, Not so, lest there be not ynough for vs and you, but goe ye rather to them that sell, and buy for your selues.
text.NABRE: But the wise ones replied, ‘No, for there may not be enough for us and you. Go instead to the merchants and buy some for yourselves.’
text.DRB: The wise answered, saying: Lest peradventure there suffice not for us and you, go rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.

text.NRSV: And while they went to buy it, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went with him into the wedding banquet; and the door was shut.
text.Rotherham: But, as they were going away to buy, the bridegroom came; and, they who were ready, went in with him, into the marriage-feast, and, the door was locked.
text.KJV: And while they went to buy, the bridegrome came, and they that were ready, went in with him to the marriage, and the doore was shut.
text.NABRE: While they went off to buy it, the bridegroom came and those who were ready went into the wedding feast with him. Then the door was locked.
text.DRB: And whiles they went to buy, the bridegroom was come: and they that were ready, entered with him to the marriage, and the gate was shut.

text.NRSV: Later the other bridesmaids came also, saying, ‘Lord, lord, open to us.’
text.Rotherham: But, afterwards, came the other virgins also, saying—Lord! Lord! open unto us!
text.KJV: Afterward came also the other virgines, saying, Lord, Lord, open to vs.
text.NABRE: Afterwards the other virgins came and said, ‘Lord, Lord, open the door for us!’
text.DRB: But last of all come also the other virgins saying: Lord, Lord, open to us.

text.NRSV: But he replied, ‘Truly I tell you, I do not know you.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Verily, I say unto you, I know you not.
text.KJV: But he answered, and said, Uerely I say vnto you, I know you not.
text.NABRE: But he said in reply, ‘Amen, I say to you, I do not know you.’
text.DRB: But he answering said: Amen I say to you, I know you not.

text.NRSV: Keep awake therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour.
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, because ye know, neither the day nor the hour.
text.KJV: Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day, nor the houre, wherein the Sonne of man commeth.
text.NABRE: Therefore, stay awake, for you know neither the day nor the hour. The Parable of the Talents.
text.DRB: Watch ye therefore, because you know not the day nor the hour.

text.NRSV: “For it is as if a man, going on a journey, summoned his slaves and entrusted his property to them;
text.Rotherham: For it is, just as a man, who, going from home, called his own servants, and delivered up to them his substance;
text.KJV: ¶ For the kingdome of heauen is as a man trauailing into a farre countrey, who called his owne seruants, and deliuered vnto them his goods:
text.NABRE: “It will be as when a man who was going on a journey called in his servants and entrusted his possessions to them.
text.DRB: For even as a man going into a strange country, called his servants, and delivered them his goods.

text.NRSV: to one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his ability. Then he went away.
text.Rotherham: And, to one, indeed, gave he five talents, and, to another, two, and, to another, one,—to each according to his particular ability, and went from home.
text.KJV: And vnto one he gaue fiue talents, to another two, and to another one, to euery man according to his seuerall ability, & straightway tooke his iourney.
text.NABRE: To one he gave five talents; to another, two; to a third, one—to each according to his ability. Then he went away. Immediately
text.DRB: And to one he gave five talents, and to another two, and to another one, to every one according to his proper faculty: and immediately he took his journey.
