text.NRSV: The one who had received the five talents went off at once and traded with them, and made five more talents.
text.Rotherham: Straightway, he who, the five talents, had received, went and traded with them, and gained other five:
text.KJV: Then hee that had receiued the fiue talents, went and traded with the same, and made them other fiue talents.
text.NABRE: the one who received five talents went and traded with them, and made another five.
text.DRB: And he that had received the five talents, went his way, and occupied with the same, and gained other five.

text.NRSV: In the same way, the one who had the two talents made two more talents.
text.Rotherham: Likewise, he of the two, gained other two:
text.KJV: And likewise he that had receiued two, he also gained other two.
text.NABRE: Likewise, the one who received two made another two.
text.DRB: Likewise also he that had received the two, gained other two.

text.NRSV: But the one who had received the one talent went off and dug a hole in the ground and hid his master’s money.
text.Rotherham: But, he who the one had received, went away, and digged up ground, and hid the silver of his lord.
text.KJV: But hee that had receiued one, went and digged in the earth, and hid his lordes money.
text.NABRE: But the man who received one went off and dug a hole in the ground and buried his master’s money.
text.DRB: But he that had received the one, going his way digged into the earth, and hid his lord's money.

text.NRSV: After a long time the master of those slaves came and settled accounts with them.
text.Rotherham: And, after a long time, cometh the lord of those servants, and reckoneth with them.
text.KJV: After a long time, the lord of those seruants commeth, and reckoneth with them.
text.NABRE: After a long time the master of those servants came back and settled accounts with them.
text.DRB: But after much time the lord of those servants cometh, & made account with them.

text.NRSV: Then the one who had received the five talents came forward, bringing five more talents, saying, ‘Master, you entrusted to me five talents; see, I have made five more talents.’
text.Rotherham: And he who the five talents had received, coming forward, brought other five talents, saying,—Lord! five talents, to me, thou didst deliver up: See! other five talents, I gained.
text.KJV: And so hee that had receiued fiue talents, came and brought other fiue talents, saying, Lord, thou deliueredst vnto me fiue talents, behold, I haue gained besides them, fiue talents moe.
text.NABRE: The one who had received five talents came forward bringing the additional five. He said, ‘Master, you gave me five talents. See, I have made five more.’
text.DRB: And there came he that had received the five talents, & offered other five talents, saying: Lord five talents thou didst deliver me, behold I have gained other five besides.

text.NRSV: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and trustworthy slave; you have been trustworthy in a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.’
text.Rotherham: His lord said unto him—Well-done! good and faithful servant,—Over a few things wast thou faithful, over many things, will I appoint thee: Enter into the joy of thy lord.
text.KJV: His lord said vnto him, Well done, thou good and faithfull seruant, thou hast been faithfull ouer a few things, I wil make thee ruler ouer many things: enter thou into the ioy of thy lord.
text.NABRE: His master said to him, ‘Well done, my good and faithful servant. Since you were faithful in small matters, I will give you great responsibilities. Come, share your master’s joy.’
text.DRB: His lord said unto him: Well-fare thee good and faithful servant, because thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will place thee over many things: enter into the joy of thy lord.

text.NRSV: And the one with the two talents also came forward, saying, ‘Master, you entrusted to me two talents; see, I have made two more talents.’
text.Rotherham: He also of the two talents, coming forward, said—Lord! two talents, to me, didst thou deliver up: See! other two talents, I gained.
text.KJV: He also that had receiued two talents, came and said, Lord, thou deliueredst vnto me two talents: behold, I haue gained two other talents besides them.
text.NABRE: [Then] the one who had received two talents also came forward and said, ‘Master, you gave me two talents. See, I have made two more.’
text.DRB: And there came also he that had received the two talents, & said: Lord two talents thou didst deliver me: behold I have gained other two.

text.NRSV: His master said to him, ‘Well done, good and trustworthy slave; you have been trustworthy in a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; enter into the joy of your master.’
text.Rotherham: His lord said unto him—Well-done! good and faithful servant,—Over a few things, wast thou faithful, over many things, will I appoint thee: Enter into the joy of thy lord.
text.KJV: His lord said vnto him, Well done, good and faithfull seruant, thou hast beene faithfull ouer a few things, I wil make thee ruler ouer many things: enter thou into the ioy of thy lord.
text.NABRE: His master said to him, ‘Well done, my good and faithful servant. Since you were faithful in small matters, I will give you great responsibilities. Come, share your master’s joy.’
text.DRB: His lord said to him: Well-fare thee good & faithful servant: because thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will place thee over many things, enter into the joy of thy lord.

text.NRSV: Then the one who had received the one talent also came forward, saying, ‘Master, I knew that you were a harsh man, reaping where you did not sow, and gathering where you did not scatter seed;
text.Rotherham: But he also who, the one talent, had received, coming forward, said—Lord I knew thee, that thou art a, hard, man, reaping, where thou hast not sown,—and gathering, whence thou hast not winnowed;
text.KJV: Then he which had receiued the one talent, came & said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sowen, & gathering where thou hast not strawed:
text.NABRE: Then the one who had received the one talent came forward and said, ‘Master, I knew you were a demanding person, harvesting where you did not plant and gathering where you did not scatter;
text.DRB: And he also that had received the one talent, came forth, and said: Lord, I know that thou art a hard man; thou reapest where thou didst not sow, and gatherest where thou strawedst not:

text.NRSV: so I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground. Here you have what is yours.’
text.Rotherham: And, overcome with fear, I went away, and hid thy talent in the ground: See! thou hast what is thine!
text.KJV: And I was afraid, and went and hidde thy talent in the earth: loe, there thou hast that is thine.
text.NABRE: so out of fear I went off and buried your talent in the ground. Here it is back.’
text.DRB: and being afraid I went, and hid thy talent in the earth: behold lo here thou hast that which thine is.

text.NRSV: But his master replied, ‘You wicked and lazy slave! You knew, did you, that I reap where I did not sow, and gather where I did not scatter?
text.Rotherham: And his lord, answering, said unto him—O wicked servant, and cowardly, knewest thou that I reap, where I have not sown, and gather, whence I have not winnowed?
text.KJV: His lord answered, and said vnto him, Thou wicked and slouthfull seruant, thou knewest that I reape where I sowed not, and gather where I haue not strawed:
text.NABRE: His master said to him in reply, ‘You wicked, lazy servant! So you knew that I harvest where I did not plant and gather where I did not scatter?
text.DRB: And his lord answering, said to him: Naughty and slothful servant, thou didst know that I reap where I sow not, & gather where I strawed not:

text.NRSV: Then you ought to have invested my money with the bankers, and on my return I would have received what was my own with interest.
text.Rotherham: It was binding on thee, therefore, to cast my silver into the money-changers,—and, I, when I, came, might have obtained what was mine, with interest.
text.KJV: Thou oughtest therefore to haue put my money to the exchangers, and then at my comming I should haue receiued mine owne with vsurie.
text.NABRE: Should you not then have put my money in the bank so that I could have got it back with interest on my return?
text.DRB: thou oughtest therefore to have committed my money to the bankers, and coming I might have received mine own with usury.

text.NRSV: So take the talent from him, and give it to the one with the ten talents.
text.Rotherham: Therefore take away, from him, the talent, and give unto him that hath the ten talents;
text.KJV: Take therefore the talent from him, and giue it vnto him which hath ten talents.
text.NABRE: Now then! Take the talent from him and give it to the one with ten.
text.DRB: Take ye away therefore the talent from him, and give it him that hath ten talents.

text.NRSV: For to all those who have, more will be given, and they will have an abundance; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: For, to every one that hath, shall be given, and he shall be made to abound; but, from him that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken away from him:
text.KJV: For vnto euery one that hath shall be giuen, and he shall haue abundance: but from him that hath not, shal be taken away, euen that which he hath.
text.NABRE: For to everyone who has, more will be given and he will grow rich; but from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
text.DRB: For to every one that hath shall be given, and he shall abound: but from him that hath not, that also which he seemeth to have, shall be taken away from him.

text.NRSV: As for this worthless slave, throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
text.Rotherham: And, the unprofitable servant, cast ye forth into the darkness, outside: There, shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
text.KJV: And cast yee the vnprofitable seruant into outer darkenesse, there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
text.NABRE: And throw this useless servant into the darkness outside, where there will be wailing and grinding of teeth.’ The Judgment of the Nations.
text.DRB: And the unprofitable servant cast ye out into the utter darkness. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

text.NRSV: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever the Son of Man shall come in his glory, and all the messengers with him, then will he sit on his throne of glory;
text.KJV: ¶ When the Sonne of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy Angels with him, then shall hee sit vpon the throne of his glory:
text.NABRE: “When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, he will sit upon his glorious throne,
text.DRB: And when the Son of man shall come in his majesty, and all the Angels with him, then shall he sit upon the seat of his majesty:

text.NRSV: All the nations will be gathered before him, and he will separate people one from another as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats,
text.Rotherham: And there will be gathered before him, all the nations, and he will separate them one from another, just as, the shepherd! separateth the sheep from the goats,—
text.KJV: And before him shall be gathered all nations, and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepheard diuideth his sheepe from the goats.
text.NABRE: and all the nations will be assembled before him. And he will separate them one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
text.DRB: and all nations shall be gathered together before him, and he shall separate them one from another, as the pastor separateth the sheep from the goats:

text.NRSV: and he will put the sheep at his right hand and the goats at the left.
text.Rotherham: And he will set, the sheep, indeed, on his right hand, but, the goats, on the left.
text.KJV: And he shall set the sheepe on his right hand, but the goats on the left.
text.NABRE: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text.DRB: and shall set the sheep at his right hand, but the goats at his left.

text.NRSV: Then the king will say to those at his right hand, ‘Come, you that are blessed by my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;
text.Rotherham: Then, will the king say to those on his right hand: Come ye, the blessed of my Father! Inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;
text.KJV: Then shall the King say vnto them on his right hand, Come ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdome prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
text.NABRE: Then the king will say to those on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father. Inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
text.DRB: Then shall the King say to them that shall be at his right hand: Come ye blessed of my Father, possess you the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.

text.NRSV: for I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me something to drink, I was a foreigner and you welcomed me,
text.Rotherham: For I hungered, and ye gave me to eat, I thirsted, and ye gave me drink, a stranger, was I, and ye took me home,
text.KJV: For I was an hungred, and yee gaue me meate: I was thirstie, and ye gaue me drinke: I was a stranger, and ye tooke me in:
text.NABRE: For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, a stranger and you welcomed me,
text.DRB: For I was an hungered, and you gave me to eat: I was a thirst, and you gave me to drink.

text.NRSV: I was naked and you gave me clothing, I was sick and you took care of me, I was in prison and you visited me.’
text.Rotherham: Naked, and ye clothed me, sick, and ye visited me, in prison, was I, and ye came unto me.
text.KJV: Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sicke, and yee visited me: I was in prison, and ye came vnto me.
text.NABRE: naked and you clothed me, ill and you cared for me, in prison and you visited me.’
text.DRB: I was a stranger, and you took me in: naked, and you covered me: sick, and you visited me. I was in prison, and you came to me.

text.NRSV: Then the righteous will answer him, ‘Lord, when was it that we saw you hungry and gave you food, or thirsty and gave you something to drink?
text.Rotherham: Then, will the righteous answer him, saying: Lord! when, saw we thee—hungry, and fed thee, or thirsty, and gave thee drink?
text.KJV: Then shal the righteous answere him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fedde thee? or thirstie, and gaue thee drinke?
text.NABRE: Then the righteous will answer him and say, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you drink?
text.DRB: Then shall the just answer him, saying: Lord, when did we see thee an hungered, and fed thee, a thirst, and gave thee drink?

text.NRSV: And when was it that we saw you a foreigner and welcomed you, or naked and gave you clothing?
text.Rotherham: And, when, saw we thee—a stranger, and took thee home, or naked, and clothed thee?
text.KJV: When saw wee thee a stranger, and tooke thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?
text.NABRE: When did we see you a stranger and welcome you, or naked and clothe you?
text.DRB: and when did we see thee a stranger, and took thee in? Or naked, and covered thee?

text.NRSV: And when was it that we saw you sick or in prison and visited you?’
text.Rotherham: And, when, saw we thee—sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?
text.KJV: Or when saw we thee sicke, or in prison, and came vnto thee?
text.NABRE: When did we see you ill or in prison, and visit you?’
text.DRB: Or when did we see thee sick or in prison, and came to thee?

text.NRSV: And the king will answer them, ‘Truly I tell you, just as you did it to one of the least of these who are members of my family, you did it to me.’
text.Rotherham: And, answering, the king will say unto them: Verily, I say unto you—Inasmuch as ye did it unto one of these my least brethren, unto me, ye did it.
text.KJV: And the King shall answere, and say vnto them, Uerely I say vnto you, in as much as ye haue done it vnto one of the least of these my brethren, ye haue done it vnto me.
text.NABRE: And the king will say to them in reply, ‘Amen, I say to you, whatever you did for one of these least brothers of mine, you did for me.’
text.DRB: And the King answering, shall say to them: Amen I say to you, as long as you did it to one of these my least brethren, you did it to me.

text.NRSV: Then he will say to those at his left hand, ‘You that are accursed, depart from me into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels;
text.Rotherham: Then, will he say unto those also, on his left hand: Depart ye from me, accursed ones! Into the age-abiding fire, which hath been prepared for the adversary and his messengers;
text.KJV: Then shall he say also vnto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into euerlasting fire, prepared for the deuill and his angels.
text.NABRE: Then he will say to those on his left, ‘Depart from me, you accursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.
text.DRB: Then he shall say to them also that shall be at his left hand: Get ye away from me you cursed into fire everlasting, which was prepared for the devil and his angels.

text.NRSV: for I was hungry and you gave me no food, I was thirsty and you gave me nothing to drink,
text.Rotherham: For I hungered, and ye gave me not to eat, [and] I was thirsty, and ye gave me not to drink,
text.KJV: For I was an hungred, and yee gaue me no meat: I was thirstie, and ye gaue me no drinke:
text.NABRE: For I was hungry and you gave me no food, I was thirsty and you gave me no drink,
text.DRB: For I was an hungered, & you gave me not to eat, I was a thirst, & you gave me not to drink.

text.NRSV: I was a foreigner and you did not welcome me, naked and you did not give me clothing, sick and in prison and you did not visit me.’
text.Rotherham: A stranger, was I, and ye took me not home, naked, and ye clothed me not, sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.
text.KJV: I was a stranger, and yee tooke me not in: naked, and ye clothed mee not: sicke, and in prison, and yee visited me not.
text.NABRE: a stranger and you gave me no welcome, naked and you gave me no clothing, ill and in prison, and you did not care for me.’
text.DRB: I was a stranger, and you took me not in: naked, and you covered me not: sick, & in prison, and you did not visit me.

text.NRSV: Then they also will answer, ‘Lord, when was it that we saw you hungry or thirsty or a foreigner or naked or sick or in prison, and did not take care of you?’
text.Rotherham: Then, will, they also, answer, saying: Lord! when saw we thee—hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and ministered not unto thee?
text.KJV: Then shall they also answere him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sicke, or in prison, and did not minister vnto thee?
text.NABRE: Then they will answer and say, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or ill or in prison, and not minister to your needs?’
text.DRB: Then they also shall answer him, saying: Lord, when did we see thee an hungered, or a thirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, & did not minister to thee?

text.NRSV: Then he will answer them, ‘Truly I tell you, just as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to me.’
text.Rotherham: Then, will he answer them, saying: Verily, I say unto you—Inasmuch as ye did it not, unto one of these least, neither unto me, did ye it.
text.KJV: Then shall he answere them, saying, Uerely, I say vnto you, in as much as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.
text.NABRE: He will answer them, ‘Amen, I say to you, what you did not do for one of these least ones, you did not do for me.’
text.DRB: Then he shall answer them, saying, Amen I say to you, as long as you did it not to one of these lesser, neither did you it to me.

text.NRSV: And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: And, these, shall go away, into, age-abiding, correction, but, the righteous, into, age-abiding, life.
text.KJV: And these shall goe away into euerlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternall.
text.NABRE: And these will go off to eternal punishment, but the righteous to eternal life.”
text.DRB: And these shall go into punishment everlasting: but the just, into life everlasting.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he said to his disciples,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended all these words, he said unto his disciples:
text.KJV: And it came to passe, when Iesus had finished al these sayings, hee said vnto his disciples,
text.NABRE: VII. The Passion and Resurrection Chapter 26 The Conspiracy Against Jesus. When Jesus finished all these words, he said to his disciples,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended all these words, he said to his Disciples:

text.NRSV: “You know that after two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.”
text.Rotherham: Ye know that, after two days, the passover, taketh place,—and, the Son of Man, is to be delivered up, to be crucified.
text.KJV: Ye know that after two dayes is the feast of the Passeouer, and the Sonne of man is betrayed to be crucified.
text.NABRE: “You know that in two days’ time it will be Passover, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.”
text.DRB: You know that after two days shall be Pasch, and the Son of man shall be delivered to be crucified.

text.NRSV: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people gathered in the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
text.Rotherham: Then, were gathered together, the High-priests and the Elders of the people, into the court of the High-priest who was called Caiaphas;
text.KJV: Then assembled together the chiefe Priests, and the Scribes, and the Elders of the people, vnto the palace of the high Priest, who was called Caiaphas,
text.NABRE: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people assembled in the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
text.DRB: Then were gathered together the chief Priests and Ancients of the people into the court of the high Priest, who was called Caiphas:

text.NRSV: and they conspired to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
text.Rotherham: and they took counsel together, in order that, Jesus, by guile, they might secure, and slay.
text.KJV: And consulted that they might take Iesus by subtiltie, and kill him.
text.NABRE: and they consulted together to arrest Jesus by treachery and put him to death.
text.DRB: and they consulted how they might by some wile apprehend Jesus, and kill him.

text.NRSV: But they said, “Not during the festival, or there may be a riot among the people.”
text.Rotherham: They were saying, however: Not during the feast, lest, an uproar, arise among the people.
text.KJV: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there bee an vproare among the people.
text.NABRE: But they said, “Not during the festival, that there may not be a riot among the people.” The Anointing at Bethany.
text.DRB: But they said: Not on the festival day, lest perhaps there might be a tumult among the people.

text.NRSV: Now while Jesus was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper,
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, happening to be in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
text.KJV: ¶ Now when Iesus was in Bethanie, in the house of Simon the leper,
text.NABRE: Now when Jesus was in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper,
text.DRB: And when Jesus was in Bethania in the house of Simon the Leper

text.NRSV: a woman came to him with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.
text.Rotherham: there came unto him a woman, holding an alabaster-jar of costly perfume, and she poured it down upon his head, as he was reclining.
text.KJV: There came vnto him a woman, hauing an alabaster boxe of very precious ointment, and powred it on his head, as he sate at meat.
text.NABRE: a woman came up to him with an alabaster jar of costly perfumed oil, and poured it on his head while he was reclining at table.
text.DRB: there came to him a woman having an alabaster-box of precious ointment, and poured it out upon his head as he sat at the table.

text.NRSV: But when the disciples saw it, they were angry and said, “Why this waste?
text.Rotherham: And the disciples, seeing it, were greatly displeased, saying—To what end, this loss?
text.KJV: But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this waste?
text.NABRE: When the disciples saw this, they were indignant and said, “Why this waste?
text.DRB: And the Disciples seeing it, had indignation saying: Whereto is this waste?

text.NRSV: For this ointment could have been sold for a large sum, and the money given to the poor.”
text.Rotherham: For this could have been sold for much, and given to the destitute.
text.KJV: For this ointment might haue bin sold for much, and giuen to the poore.
text.NABRE: It could have been sold for much, and the money given to the poor.”
text.DRB: For this might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.

text.NRSV: But Jesus, aware of this, said to them, “Why do you trouble the woman? She has performed a good service for me.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, taking note, said unto them—Why vex ye the woman? for, a seemly work, hath she wrought for me;
text.KJV: When Iesus vnderstood it, he said vnto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good worke vpon me.
text.NABRE: Since Jesus knew this, he said to them, “Why do you make trouble for the woman? She has done a good thing for me.
text.DRB: And Jesus knowing it, said to them: Why do you molest this woman? For she hath wrought a good work upon me.

text.NRSV: For you always have the poor with you, but you will not always have me.
text.Rotherham: For, always, the destitute, have ye with you,—but, me, not always, have ye;
text.KJV: For ye haue the poore alwayes with you, but me ye haue not alwayes.
text.NABRE: The poor you will always have with you; but you will not always have me.
text.DRB: For the poor you have always with you: but me you have not always.

text.NRSV: By pouring this ointment on my body she has prepared me for burial.
text.Rotherham: For, she, pouring this perfume upon my body, so as to prepare me for burial, did it.
text.KJV: For in that she hath powred this ointment on my body, shee did it for my buriall.
text.NABRE: In pouring this perfumed oil upon my body, she did it to prepare me for burial.
text.DRB: For she in pouring this ointment upon my body hath done it to bury me.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, wherever this good news is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Wheresoever this glad-message shall be proclaimed in all the world, also what she did, will be told, as a memorial of her.
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, Wheresoeuer this Gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memoriall of her.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, wherever this gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be spoken of, in memory of her.”
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, wheresoever this Gospel shall be preached in the whole world, that also which she hath done, shall be reported for a memory of her.

text.NRSV: Then one of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
text.Rotherham: Then, went one of the twelve, the one called Judas Iscariot, unto the High-priests,
text.KJV: ¶ Then one of the twelue, called Iudas Iscariot, went vnto the chiefe Priests,
text.NABRE: The Betrayal by Judas. Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
text.DRB: Then went one of the Twelve, which was called Judas Iscarioth, to the chief Priests,

text.NRSV: and said, “What will you give me if I betray him to you?” They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
text.Rotherham: and said—What are ye willing to give unto me? and, I, unto you, will deliver him up. And they appointed him thirty pieces of silver.
text.KJV: And said vnto them, What will ye giue me, and I will deliuer him vnto you? and they couenanted with him for thirtie pieces of siluer.
text.NABRE: and said, “What are you willing to give me if I hand him over to you?” They paid him thirty pieces of silver,
text.DRB: & said to them: What will you give me, and I will deliver him unto you? But they appointed unto him thirty pieces of silver.

text.NRSV: And from that moment he began to look for an opportunity to betray him.
text.Rotherham: And, from that time, was he seeking a favourable opportunity that he might, deliver him up.
text.KJV: And from that time he sought opportunitie to betray him.
text.NABRE: and from that time on he looked for an opportunity to hand him over.
text.DRB: And from thenceforth he sought opportunity to betray him.

text.NRSV: On the first day of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Where do you want us to make the preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
text.Rotherham: And, on the first of the days of unleavened bread, the disciples came unto Jesus, saying—Where wilt thou, that we make ready for thee to eat the passover?
text.KJV: ¶ Now the first day of the feast of vnleauened bread, the disciples came to Iesus, saying vnto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the Passeouer?
text.NABRE: Preparations for the Passover. On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the disciples approached Jesus and said, “Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
text.DRB: And the first day of the Azymes the Disciples came to Jesus saying: Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the Pasch?

text.NRSV: He said, “Go into the city to a certain man, and say to him, ‘The Teacher says, My time is near; I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.’”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Go your way into the city, unto such-a-one, and say to him, The teacher, saith, My season, is, near, with thee, will I keep the passover, with my disciples.
text.KJV: And he said, Goe into the citie to such a man, and say vnto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand, I will keepe the Passeouer at thy house with my disciples.
text.NABRE: He said, “Go into the city to a certain man and tell him, ‘The teacher says, “My appointed time draws near; in your house I shall celebrate the Passover with my disciples.”’”
text.DRB: But Jesus said: Go ye into the city to a certain man, and say to him: The Master saith, my time is at hand, with thee do I make the Pasch with my Disciples.

text.NRSV: So the disciples did as Jesus had directed them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And the disciples did, as Jesus directed them, and made ready the passover.
text.KJV: And the disciples did, as Iesus had appointed them, and they made ready the Passeouer.
text.NABRE: The disciples then did as Jesus had ordered, and prepared the Passover.
text.DRB: And the Disciples did as Jesus appointed them, and they prepared the Pasch.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, he took his place with the twelve;
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, came, he was reclining with the twelve [disciples];
text.KJV: Now when the euen was come, he sate downe with the twelue.
text.NABRE: The Betrayer. When it was evening, he reclined at table with the Twelve.
text.DRB: But when it was even, he sat down with his twelve Disciples.

text.NRSV: and while they were eating, he said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me.”
text.Rotherham: and, as they were eating, he said—Verily, I say unto you, One from among you, will deliver me up.
text.KJV: And as they did eate, he said, Uerely I say vnto you, that one of you shal betray me.
text.NABRE: And while they were eating, he said, “Amen, I say to you, one of you will betray me.”
text.DRB: And while they were eating, he said: Amen I say to you, that one of you shall betray me.

text.NRSV: And they became greatly distressed and began to say to him one after another, “Surely not I, Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And, being exceedingly grieved, they began to be saying to him, each one—Can it be, I, Lord?
text.KJV: And they were exceeding sorowfull, and began euery one of them to say vnto him, Lord, Is it I?
text.NABRE: Deeply distressed at this, they began to say to him one after another, “Surely it is not I, Lord?”
text.DRB: And they being very sad, began every one to say: Is it I Lord?

text.NRSV: He answered, “The one who has dipped his hand into the bowl with me will betray me.
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—He who hath dipped with me his hand in the bowl, the same, will deliver me up.
text.KJV: And he answered and said, Hee that dippeth his hand with mee in the dish, the same shall betray me.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “He who has dipped his hand into the dish with me is the one who will betray me.
text.DRB: But he answering said: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, he shall betray me.

text.NRSV: The Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that one by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that one not to have been born.”
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man, indeed, goeth his way, according as it is written concerning him,—But alas! for that man, through whom the Son of Man, is being delivered up: Well, had it been for him, if, that man, had not been born!
text.KJV: The sonne of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe vnto that man by whom the sonne of man is betrayed: It had bin good for that man, if hee had not bene borne.
text.NABRE: The Son of Man indeed goes, as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed. It would be better for that man if he had never been born.”
text.DRB: The Son of man indeed goeth as it is written of him: but woe be to that man, by whom the Son of man shall be betrayed. It were good for him, if that man had not been born.

text.NRSV: Judas, who betrayed him, said, “Surely not I, Rabbi?” He replied, “You have said so.”
text.Rotherham: And Judas, who was delivering him up, answering, said—Can it be, I, Rabbi? He saith unto him—Thou, hast said.
text.KJV: Then Iudas, which betrayed him, answered, and said, Master, Is it I? He said vnto him, Thou hast said.
text.NABRE: Then Judas, his betrayer, said in reply, “Surely it is not I, Rabbi?” He answered, “You have said so.”
text.DRB: And Judas that betrayed him, answering said: Is it I Rabbi? He saith to him: Thou hast said.

text.NRSV: While they were eating, Jesus took a loaf of bread, and after blessing it he broke it, gave it to the disciples, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were eating, Jesus, taking a loaf and blessing, brake,—and, giving to his disciples, said—Take, eat! This, is, my body.
text.KJV: ¶ And as they were eating, Iesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gaue it to the Disciples, and said, Take, eate, this is my body.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Supper. While they were eating, Jesus took bread, said the blessing, broke it, and giving it to his disciples said, “Take and eat; this is my body.”
text.DRB: And whiles they were at supper, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake: and he gave to his Disciples, and said: Take ye, and eat: This is my Body.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he gave it to them, saying, “Drink from it, all of you;
text.Rotherham: And, taking a cup, and giving thanks, he gave unto them, saying—Drink of it, all of you;
text.KJV: And he tooke the cup, and gaue thankes, and gaue it to them, saying, Drinke ye all of it:
text.NABRE: Then he took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, “Drink from it, all of you,
text.DRB: And taking the chalice, he gave thanks: and gave to them, saying: Drink ye all of this.

text.NRSV: for this is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: For, this, is my blood of the covenant, which, for many, is to be poured out, for remission of sins.
text.KJV: For this is my blood of the new Testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sinnes.
text.NABRE: for this is my blood of the covenant, which will be shed on behalf of many for the forgiveness of sins.
text.DRB: For This is my Blood of The New Testament, Which Shall be Shed For Many Unto Remission of Sins.

text.NRSV: I tell you, I will never again drink of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, I say unto you—In nowise, will I drink, henceforth, of this produce of the vine, until that day, whensoever I shall drink it with you, new, in the kingdom of my Father.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, I will not drinke henceforth of this fruite of the vine, vntill that day when I drinke it new with you in my fathers kingdom.
text.NABRE: I tell you, from now on I shall not drink this fruit of the vine until the day when I drink it with you new in the kingdom of my Father.”
text.DRB: And I say to you, I will not drink from henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I shall drink it with you new in the Kingdom of my Father.

text.NRSV: When they had sung the hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: And, having sung praise, they went forth unto the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: And when they had sung an hymne, they went out into the mount of Oliues.
text.NABRE: Then, after singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
text.DRB: And an hymn being said, they went forth unto Mount-olivet.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “You will all become deserters because of me this night; for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus saith unto them—All ye, will find cause of stumbling in me, during this night; for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and, the sheep of the flock, will be scattered abroad;
text.KJV: Then saith Iesus vnto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night, For it is written, I will smite the Shepheard, and the sheepe of the flocke shall be scattered abroad.
text.NABRE: Peter’s Denial Foretold. Then Jesus said to them, “This night all of you will have your faith in me shaken, for it is written: ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be dispersed’;
text.DRB: Then Jesus saith to them: All you shall be scandalized in me in this night. For it is written: I will strike the Pastor, and the sheep of the flock shall be dispersed.

text.NRSV: But after I am raised up, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: But, after my arising, I will go before you into Galilee.
text.KJV: But after I am risen againe, I will goe before you into Galilee.
text.NABRE: but after I have been raised up, I shall go before you to Galilee.”
text.DRB: But after I shall be risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Though all become deserters because of you, I will never desert you.”
text.Rotherham: Now Peter, answering, said unto him—Though, all, shall find cause of stumbling in thee, I, shall never find cause of stumbling.
text.KJV: Peter answered, and said vnto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I neuer be offended.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him in reply, “Though all may have their faith in you shaken, mine will never be.”
text.DRB: And Peter answering, said to him: Although all shall be scandalized in thee, I will never be scandalized.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, this very night, before the cock crows, you will deny me three times.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him: Verily, I say unto thee—During this night, before a cock crow, Thrice, wilt thou deny me.
text.KJV: Iesus said vnto him, Uerily I say vnto thee, that this might before the cocke crow, thou shalt denie me thrise.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Amen, I say to you, this very night before the cock crows, you will deny me three times.”
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: Amen I say to thee, that in this night before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” And so said all the disciples.
text.Rotherham: Peter saith unto him: Even though it be needful for me, with thee to die, in nowise, will I deny thee. Likewise, all the disciples also, said.
text.KJV: Peter said vnto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not denie thee. Likewise also said all the Disciples.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him, “Even though I should have to die with you, I will not deny you.” And all the disciples spoke likewise.
text.DRB: Peter saith to him: Yea though I should die with thee, I will not deny thee. Likewise also said all the Disciples.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus went with them to a place called Gethsemane; and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jesus cometh with them, into a place called Gethsemane,—and he saith unto his disciples, Sit ye here, while I go yonder and pray.
text.KJV: ¶ Then commeth Iesus with them vnto a place called Gethsemane, and saith vnto the Disciples, Sit yee heere, while I goe and pray yonder.
text.NABRE: The Agony in the Garden. Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text.DRB: Then Jesus cometh with them into a village called Gethsemani: and he said to his Disciples: Sit you here till I go yonder, and pray.

text.NRSV: He took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be grieved and agitated.
text.Rotherham: And, taking with him, Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to be grieved and to be in great distress.
text.KJV: And hee tooke with him Peter, and the two sonnes of Zebedee, and beganne to be sorrowful, and very heauie.
text.NABRE: He took along Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to feel sorrow and distress.
text.DRB: And taking to him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to wax sorrowful and to be sad.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “I am deeply grieved, even to death; remain here, and stay awake with me.”
text.Rotherham: Then, saith he unto them: Encompassed with grief, is my soul, unto death: Abide ye here, and be watching with me.
text.KJV: Then saith he vnto them, My soule is exceeding sorrowfull, euen vnto death: tary ye heere, & watch with me.
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “My soul is sorrowful even to death. Remain here and keep watch with me.”
text.DRB: Then he saith to them: My soul is sorrowful even unto death: stay here, and watch with me.

text.NRSV: And going a little farther, he threw himself on the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me; yet not what I want but what you want.”
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he fell upon his face, offering prayer, and saying—My Father! If it is possible, let this cup pass from me,—Nevertheless, not as, I, will, but as, thou, wilt.
text.KJV: And he went a little further, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, O my father, if it be possible, let this cup passe from me: neuerthelesse, not as I will, but as thou wilt.
text.NABRE: He advanced a little and fell prostrate in prayer, saying, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me; yet, not as I will, but as you will.”
text.DRB: And being gone forward a little, he fell upon his face, praying, and saying: My Father, if it be possible, let this chalice pass from me. Nevertheless not as I will, but as thou.

text.NRSV: Then he came to the disciples and found them sleeping; and he said to Peter, “So, could you not stay awake with me one hour?
text.Rotherham: And he cometh unto his disciples, and findeth them sleeping, and said unto Peter—Thus, powerless are ye, one hour, to watch with me?
text.KJV: And he commeth vnto the Disciples, and findeth them asleepe, and saith vnto Peter, What, could ye not watch with me one houre?
text.NABRE: When he returned to his disciples he found them asleep. He said to Peter, “So you could not keep watch with me for one hour?
text.DRB: And he cometh to his Disciples, and findeth them sleeping, and he saith to Peter: Even so? Could you not watch one hour with me?

text.NRSV: Stay awake and pray that you may not come into the time of trial; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
text.Rotherham: Be watching and praying, that ye may not enter into temptation: The spirit, indeed, is, eager, but, the flesh, weak.
text.KJV: Watch and pray, that yee enter not into temptation: The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weake.
text.NABRE: Watch and pray that you may not undergo the test. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
text.DRB: Watch ye, & pray that ye enter not into tentation. The Spirit indeed is prompt, but the flesh weak.

text.NRSV: Again he went away for the second time and prayed, “My Father, if this cannot pass unless I drink it, your will be done.”
text.Rotherham: Again, a second time, departing he prayed, saying: My Father! If it is impossible for this to pass, except I, drink, it, Accomplished, be, thy will.
text.KJV: He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, O my father, if this cup may not passe away from me, except I drinke it, thy will be done.
text.NABRE: Withdrawing a second time, he prayed again, “My Father, if it is not possible that this cup pass without my drinking it, your will be done!”
text.DRB: Again the second time he went, and prayed, saying: My Father, if this chalice may not pass, but I must drink it, thy will be done.

text.NRSV: Again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
text.Rotherham: And coming, he, again, found them sleeping, for their eyes had become heavy.
text.KJV: And he came and found them asleep againe: For their eies were heauie.
text.NABRE: Then he returned once more and found them asleep, for they could not keep their eyes open.
text.DRB: And he cometh again, and findeth them sleeping, for their eyes were become heavy.

text.NRSV: So leaving them again, he went away and prayed for the third time, saying the same words.
text.Rotherham: And, leaving them, he, again, departed, and prayed a third time, the same word, saying again.
text.KJV: And he left them, and went away againe, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.
text.NABRE: He left them and withdrew again and prayed a third time, saying the same thing again.
text.DRB: And leaving them, he went again: and he prayed the third time, saying the selfsame word.

text.NRSV: Then he came to the disciples and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? See, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.Rotherham: Then, cometh he unto the disciples, and saith unto them,—Ye are sleeping what time remaineth, and taking your rest: Lo! the hour hath drawn near, and, the Son of Man, is being delivered up into the hands of sinners.
text.KJV: Then commeth he to his Disciples, and saith vnto them, Sleepe on now, and take your rest, behold, the houre is at hand, and the sonne of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.NABRE: Then he returned to his disciples and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? Behold, the hour is at hand when the Son of Man is to be handed over to sinners.
text.DRB: Then he cometh to his Disciples, and saith to them: Sleep ye now and take rest. Behold the hour approacheth, and the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of sinners.

text.NRSV: Get up, let us be going. See, my betrayer is at hand.”
text.Rotherham: Arise! let us be going,—Lo! he that delivereth me up, hath drawn near.
text.KJV: Rise, let vs be going: behold, he is at hand that doeth betray me.
text.NABRE: Get up, let us go. Look, my betrayer is at hand.”
text.DRB: Rise, let us go: behold he approacheth that shall betray me.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived; with him was a large crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and the elders of the people.
text.Rotherham: And, while, yet, he was speaking, lo! Judas, one of the twelve, came, and, with him, a large multitude, with swords and clubs, from the High-priests and Elders of the people.
text.KJV: ¶ And while yet spake, loe, Iudas one of the twelue came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staues from the chiefe Priests and Elders of the people.
text.NABRE: The Betrayal and Arrest of Jesus. While he was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, accompanied by a large crowd, with swords and clubs, who had come from the chief priests and the elders of the people.
text.DRB: As he yet spake, behold Judas one of the Twelve came, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, sent from the chief Priests and the ancients of the people.

text.NRSV: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I will kiss is the man; arrest him.”
text.Rotherham: And, he who was delivering him up, gave them a sign, saying—Whomsoever I shall kiss, he, it is,—secure him.
text.KJV: Now he that betrayed him, gaue them a signe, saying, Whomsoeuer I shall kisse, that same is he, hold him fast.
text.NABRE: His betrayer had arranged a sign with them, saying, “The man I shall kiss is the one; arrest him.”
text.DRB: And he that betrayed him, gave them a sign, saying: Whomsoever I shall kiss, that is he, hold him.

text.NRSV: At once he came up to Jesus and said, “Greetings, Rabbi!” and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, coming unto Jesus, he said—Joy to thee! Rabbi, and eagerly kissed him.
text.KJV: And forthwith hee came to Iesus, and said, Haile master, and kissed him.
text.NABRE: Immediately he went over to Jesus and said, “Hail, Rabbi!” and he kissed him.
text.DRB: And forthwith coming to Jesus, he said: Hail Rabbi. And he kissed him.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Friend, do what you are here to do.” Then they came and laid hands on Jesus and arrested him.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto him—Friend! wherefore art thou here? Then, coming forward, they thrust their hands upon Jesus, and secured him.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Friend, Wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid handes on Iesus, and tooke him.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Friend, do what you have come for.” Then stepping forward they laid hands on Jesus and arrested him.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Friend, whereto art thou come? Then they drew near, and laid hands on Jesus, and held him.

text.NRSV: Suddenly, one of those with Jesus put his hand on his sword, drew it, and struck the slave of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
text.Rotherham: And lo! one of those with Jesus, stretching forth his hand, grasped his sword, and smiting the servant of the High-priest, cut off his ear.
text.KJV: And behold, one of them which were with Iesus, stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and stroke a seruant of the high Priests, and smote off his eare.
text.NABRE: And behold, one of those who accompanied Jesus put his hand to his sword, drew it, and struck the high priest’s servant, cutting off his ear.
text.DRB: And behold one of them that were with Jesus, stretching forth his hand, drew out his sword; and striking the servant of the high Priest, cut off his ear.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place; for all who take the sword will perish by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus saith unto him—Put back thy sword into its place, for, all they who take a sword, by a sword, shall perish.
text.KJV: Then said Iesus vnto him, Put vp againe thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword, shall perish with the sword.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its sheath, for all who take the sword will perish by the sword.
text.DRB: Then Jesus saith to him: Return thy sword into his place: for all that take the sword shall perish with the sword.

text.NRSV: Do you think that I cannot appeal to my Father, and he will at once send me more than twelve legions of angels?
text.Rotherham: Or thinkest thou that I cannot call upon my Father, and he will set near me, even now, more than twelve legions of messengers?
text.KJV: Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my father, and he shall presently giue me more then twelue legions of Angels?
text.NABRE: Do you think that I cannot call upon my Father and he will not provide me at this moment with more than twelve legions of angels?
text.DRB: Thinkest thou that I cannot ask my Father, and he will give me presently more than twelve legions of Angels?

text.NRSV: But how then would the scriptures be fulfilled, which say it must happen in this way?”
text.Rotherham: How then should the Scriptures be fulfilled, that, thus, it must needs come to pass?
text.KJV: But how then shall the Scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?
text.NABRE: But then how would the scriptures be fulfilled which say that it must come to pass in this way?”
text.DRB: How then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that so it must be done?

text.NRSV: At that hour Jesus said to the crowds, “Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as though I were a robber? Day after day I sat in the temple teaching, and you did not arrest me.
text.Rotherham: In that hour, said Jesus unto the multitudes: As against a robber, came ye forth, with swords and clubs, to arrest me? Daily in the temple, used I to sit teaching, and ye secured me not;
text.KJV: In that same houre said Iesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thiefe with swords and staues for to take mee? I sate daily with you teaching in the Temple, and ye laide no hold on me.
text.NABRE: At that hour Jesus said to the crowds, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me? Day after day I sat teaching in the temple area, yet you did not arrest me.
text.DRB: In that hour Jesus said to the multitudes: You are come out as it were to a thief with swords and clubs to apprehend me. I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and you laid no hands on me.

text.NRSV: But all this has taken place, so that the scriptures of the prophets may be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples deserted him and fled.
text.Rotherham: But, this, hath, wholly, come to pass, that, the Scriptures of the prophets, may be fulfilled. Then, the disciples, all forsaking him, fled.
text.KJV: But all this was done, that the Scriptures of the Prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the Disciples forsooke him, and fled:
text.NABRE: But all this has come to pass that the writings of the prophets may be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples left him and fled. Jesus Before the Sanhedrin.
text.DRB: And all this was done, that the scriptures of the Prophets might be fulfilled. Then the Disciples all leaving him, fled.

text.NRSV: Those who had arrested Jesus took him to Caiaphas the high priest, in whose house the scribes and the elders had gathered.
text.Rotherham: And, they who secured Jesus, led him away unto Caiaphas the High-priest, where the Scribes and the Elders gathered themselves together.
text.KJV: ¶ And they that had laid hold on Iesus, led him away to Caiaphas the high Priest, where the Scribes and the Elders were assembled.
text.NABRE: Those who had arrested Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.
text.DRB: But they taking hold of Jesus, led him to Caiphas the high Priest, where the Scribes and ancients were assembled.

text.NRSV: But Peter was following him at a distance, as far as the courtyard of the high priest; and going inside, he sat with the guards in order to see how this would end.
text.Rotherham: Now, Peter, was following him afar off, unto the court of the High-priest,—and, entering within, was sitting with the attendants, to see the end.
text.KJV: But Peter followed him afarre off, vnto the high Priests palace, and went in, and sate with the seruants to see the end.
text.NABRE: Peter was following him at a distance as far as the high priest’s courtyard, and going inside he sat down with the servants to see the outcome.
text.DRB: And Peter followed him a far off, even to the court of the high Priest. And going in he sat with the servants, that he might see the end.

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they might put him to death,
text.Rotherham: Now, the High-priests and all the High-council, were seeking false-witness against Jesus, that they might, put him to death;
text.KJV: Now the chiefe Priests and Elders, and all the councell, sought false witnesse against Iesus to put him to death,
text.NABRE: The chief priests and the entire Sanhedrin kept trying to obtain false testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death,
text.DRB: And the chief Priests and the whole Council sought false witness against Jesus, that they might put him to death:

text.NRSV: but they found none, though many false witnesses came forward. At last two came forward
text.Rotherham: and found not any, though many came forward as false-witnesses. At length, however, there came forward, two,
text.KJV: But found none: yea, though many false witnesses came, yet found they none. At the last came two false witnesses,
text.NABRE: but they found none, though many false witnesses came forward. Finally two came forward
text.DRB: and they found not, whereas many false witnesses had come in. And last of all there came two false witnesses;

text.NRSV: and said, “This fellow said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God and to build it in three days.’”
text.Rotherham: and said: This, man said—I am able to pull down the shrine of God, and, in three days, to, build, it.
text.KJV: And said, This fellow said, I am able to destroy the Temple of God, and to build it in three dayes.
text.NABRE: who stated, “This man said, ‘I can destroy the temple of God and within three days rebuild it.’”
text.DRB: and they said: This man said, I am able to destroy the temple of God, and after three days to re-edify it.

text.NRSV: The high priest stood up and said, “Have you no answer? What is it that they testify against you?”
text.Rotherham: And the High-priest, arising, said unto him—Nothing, answerest thou? What are these, against thee bearing witness?
text.KJV: And the high Priest arose, and said vnto him, Answerest thou nothing? what is it, which these witnesse against thee?
text.NABRE: The high priest rose and addressed him, “Have you no answer? What are these men testifying against you?”
text.DRB: And the high Priest rising up, said to him: Answerest thou nothing to the things which these do testify against thee?

text.NRSV: But Jesus was silent. Then the high priest said to him, “I put you under oath before the living God, tell us if you are the Messiah, the Son of God.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, was silent. And, the High-priest, said unto him: I put thee on oath, by the Living God, that, to us, thou say—Whether, thou, art the Christ, the Son of God.
text.KJV: But Iesus held his peace. And the high Priest answered, and said vnto him, I adiure thee by the liuing God, that thou tell vs, whether thou bee the Christ the Sonne of God.
text.NABRE: But Jesus was silent. Then the high priest said to him, “I order you to tell us under oath before the living God whether you are the Messiah, the Son of God.”
text.DRB: But Jesus held his peace. And the high Priest said to him: I adjure thee by the living God, that thou tell us if thou be Christ the Son of God.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “You have said so. But I tell you, From now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Jesus saith unto him: Thou, hast said; Moreover, I say unto you—Hereafter, ye will see the Son of Man, sitting on the right hand of power, and coming upon the clouds of heaven.
text.KJV: Iesus saith vnto him, Thou hast saide: Neuerthelesse I say vnto you, Hereafter shall yee see the Sonne of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heauen.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “You have said so. But I tell you: From now on you will see ‘the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power’ and ‘coming on the clouds of heaven.’”
text.DRB: Jesus saith to him: Thou hast said. Nevertheless I say to you, hereafter you shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of the power of God, and coming in the clouds of Heaven.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, “He has blasphemed! Why do we still need witnesses? You have now heard his blasphemy.
text.Rotherham: Then, the High-priest rent asunder his garments, saying—He hath spoken profanely! What further need have we, of witnesses? Lo! now, ye have heard the profanity!
text.KJV: Then the high Priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken blasphemie: what further need haue wee of witnesses? Behold, now ye haue heard his blasphemie.
text.NABRE: Then the high priest tore his robes and said, “He has blasphemed! What further need have we of witnesses? You have now heard the blasphemy;
text.DRB: Then the high Priest rent his garments, saying: He hath blasphemed, what need we witnesses any further? Behold, now you have heard the blasphemy;

text.NRSV: What is your verdict?” They answered, “He deserves death.”
text.Rotherham: How, to you, doth it seem? And, they, answering, said: Guilty of death, he is.
text.KJV: What thinke ye? They answered and said, He is guiltie of death.
text.NABRE: what is your opinion?” They said in reply, “He deserves to die!”
text.DRB: how think you? But they answering said: He is guilty of death.

text.NRSV: Then they spat in his face and struck him; and some slapped him,
text.Rotherham: Then, spat they into his face, and buffeted him; and, others, struck him smartly,
text.KJV: Then did they spit in his face, and buffeted him, and others smote him with the palmes of their hands,
text.NABRE: Then they spat in his face and struck him, while some slapped him,
text.DRB: Then did they spit on his face, and buffeted him, & other smote his face with the palms of their hands,

text.NRSV: saying, “Prophesy to us, you Messiah! Who is it that struck you?”
text.Rotherham: saying—Prophesy unto us, O Christ! Who is he that struck thee?
text.KJV: Saying, Prophecie vnto vs, thou Christ, who is he that smote thee?
text.NABRE: saying, “Prophesy for us, Messiah: who is it that struck you?”
text.DRB: saying: Prophesy unto us O Christ; who is he that strook thee?

text.NRSV: Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant-girl came to him and said, “You also were with Jesus the Galilean.”
text.Rotherham: Now, Peter, was sitting without, in the court; and there came unto him a certain female servant, saying—Thou also, wast with Jesus, the Galilaean.
text.KJV: ¶ Now Peter sate without in the palace: and a damosell came vnto him, saying, Thou also wast with Iesus of Galilee.
text.NABRE: Peter’s Denial of Jesus. Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. One of the maids came over to him and said, “You too were with Jesus the Galilean.”
text.DRB: But Peter sat without in the court; and there came to him one wench, saying: Thou also wast with Jesus the Galilean.

text.NRSV: But he denied it before all of them, saying, “I do not know what you are talking about.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, denied before all, saying—I know not what thou sayest.
text.KJV: But hee denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou saiest.
text.NABRE: But he denied it in front of everyone, saying, “I do not know what you are talking about!”
text.DRB: But he denied before them all, saying: I wot not what thou sayest.

text.NRSV: When he went out to the porch, another servant-girl saw him, and she said to the bystanders, “This man was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he went out into the porch, another female saw him, and said unto them who were there—This, one was with Jesus the Nazarene.
text.KJV: And when he was gone out into the porch, another maide saw him, and saide vnto them that were there, This fellow was also with Iesus of Nazareth.
text.NABRE: As he went out to the gate, another girl saw him and said to those who were there, “This man was with Jesus the Nazorean.”
text.DRB: And as he went out of the gate, another wench saw him, and she saith to them that were there: And this fellow also was with Jesus the Nazarite.

text.NRSV: Again he denied it with an oath, “I do not know the man.”
text.Rotherham: And, again, he denied, with an oath—I know not the man.
text.KJV: And againe hee denied with an oath, I doe not know the man.
text.NABRE: Again he denied it with an oath, “I do not know the man!”
text.DRB: And again he denied with an oath: That I know not the man.

text.NRSV: After a little while the bystanders came up and said to Peter, “Certainly you are also one of them, for your accent betrays you.”
text.Rotherham: And, after a little, the by-standers, coming forward, said unto Peter: Truly, thou also, art, from among them, for, thy very speech, maketh thee, manifest.
text.KJV: And after a while came vnto him they that stood by, and saide to Peter, Surely thou also art one of them, for thy speech bewrayeth thee.
text.NABRE: A little later the bystanders came over and said to Peter, “Surely you too are one of them; even your speech gives you away.”
text.DRB: And after a little they came that stood by, and said to Peter: Surely thou also art of them: for even thy speech doth bewray thee.

text.NRSV: Then he began to curse, and he swore an oath, “I do not know the man!” At that moment the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: Then, began he to be cursing and swearing—I know not the man! And, straightway, a cock crowed.
text.KJV: Then beganne hee to curse and to sweare, saying, I know not the man. And immediatly the cocke crew.
text.NABRE: At that he began to curse and to swear, “I do not know the man.” And immediately a cock crowed.
text.DRB: Then he began to curse and to swear that he knew not the man. And incontinent the cock crew.

text.NRSV: Then Peter remembered the saying of Jesus, “Before the cock crows, you will deny me three times.” And he went out and wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: And Peter was put in mind of the declaration of Jesus, of his having said—Before a cock crow, thrice, wilt thou utterly deny me; and, going forth outside, he wept bitterly.
text.KJV: And Peter remembred the words of Iesus, which said vnto him, Before the cocke crow, thou shalt denie mee thrice. And hee went out, and wept bitterly.
text.NABRE: Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken: “Before the cock crows you will deny me three times.” He went out and began to weep bitterly.
text.DRB: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus which he had said: Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice. And going forth, he wept bitterly.

text.NRSV: When morning came, all the chief priests and the elders of the people conferred together against Jesus in order to bring about his death.
text.Rotherham: Now, when morning came, all the High-priests and Elders of the people took, counsel, against Jesus, so as to put him to death:
text.KJV: When the morning was come, all the chiefe Priests and Elders of the people, tooke counsell against Iesus to put him to death.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 Jesus Before Pilate. When it was morning, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death.
text.DRB: And when morning was come; all the chief Priests and ancients of the people consulted together against Jesus, that they might put him to death.

text.NRSV: They bound him, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate the governor.
text.Rotherham: and, binding him, they led him away, and delivered him up unto Pilate the governor.
text.KJV: And when they had bound him, they led him away, and deliuered him to Pontius Pilate the gouernour.
text.NABRE: They bound him, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate, the governor.
text.DRB: And they brought him bound and delivered him to Ponce Pilate the President.

text.NRSV: When Judas, his betrayer, saw that Jesus was condemned, he repented and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders.
text.Rotherham: Then, Judas who had delivered him up, seeing that he was condemned, being smitten with remorse, returned the thirty pieces of silver unto the High-priests and Elders,
text.KJV: ¶ Then Iudas, which had betraied him, when he saw that hee was condemned, repented himselfe, and brought againe the thirtie pieces of siluer to the chiefe Priests and Elders,
text.NABRE: The Death of Judas. Then Judas, his betrayer, seeing that Jesus had been condemned, deeply regretted what he had done. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
text.DRB: Then Judas that betrayed him, seeing that he was condemned, repenting him, returned the thirty silver pieces to the chief Priests and ancients,

text.NRSV: He said, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” But they said, “What is that to us? See to it yourself.”
text.Rotherham: saying—I have sinned, in delivering up righteous blood. But, they, said—What is that to us? thou, shalt see to it for thyself.
text.KJV: Saying, I haue sinned, in that I haue betraied the innocent blood. And they said, What is that to vs? see thou to that.
text.NABRE: saying, “I have sinned in betraying innocent blood.” They said, “What is that to us? Look to it yourself.”
text.DRB: saying: I have sinned, betraying just blood. But they said: What is that to us? look thou to it.

text.NRSV: Throwing down the pieces of silver in the temple, he departed; and he went and hanged himself.
text.Rotherham: And, throwing the pieces of silver into the Temple, he withdrew, and, going away, hanged himself.
text.KJV: And hee cast downe the pieces of siluer in the Temple, and departed, and went and hanged himselfe.
text.NABRE: Flinging the money into the temple, he departed and went off and hanged himself.
text.DRB: And casting down the silver pieces in the temple, he departed: and went and hanged himself with an halter.

text.NRSV: But the chief priests, taking the pieces of silver, said, “It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, since they are blood money.”
text.Rotherham: But, the High-priests, taking the pieces of silver, said—It is not allowed, to cast them into the treasury; since, a price of blood, they are.
text.KJV: And the chiefe Priests tooke the siluer pieces, and said, It is not lawfull for to put them into the treasurie, because it is the price of blood.
text.NABRE: The chief priests gathered up the money, but said, “It is not lawful to deposit this in the temple treasury, for it is the price of blood.”
text.DRB: And the chief Priests having taken the silver pieces, said: It is not lawful to cast them into the Corbana, because it is the price of blood.

text.NRSV: After conferring together, they used them to buy the potter’s field as a place to bury foreigners.
text.Rotherham: And, taking counsel, they bought with them, the field of the potter, as a burial-place for strangers.
text.KJV: And they tooke counsell, and bought with them the potters field, to burie strangers in.
text.NABRE: After consultation, they used it to buy the potter’s field as a burial place for foreigners.
text.DRB: And after they had consulted together, they bought with them the potter's field, to be a burying place for strangers.

text.NRSV: Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, that field hath been called Field of Blood, until this day.
text.KJV: Wherefore that field was called, The field of blood vnto this day.
text.NABRE: That is why that field even today is called the Field of Blood.
text.DRB: For this cause that field was called Haceldama, that is, the field of blood, even to this present day.

text.NRSV: Then was fulfilled what had been spoken through the prophet Jeremiah, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of the one on whom a price had been set, on whom some of the people of Israel had set a price,
text.Rotherham: Then, was fulfilled, that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying: And they took the thirty pieces of silver, as the value of him whom they had valued, whom they had valued, of Israel’s sons,—
text.KJV: (Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Ieremie the Prophet, saying, And they tooke the thirtie pieces of siluer, the price of him that was valued, whom they of the children of Israel did value:
text.NABRE: Then was fulfilled what had been said through Jeremiah the prophet, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the value of a man with a price on his head, a price set by some of the Israelites,
text.DRB: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremie the Prophet, saying: And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of the priced, whom they did price of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: and they gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord commanded me.”
text.Rotherham: And gave them for the field of the potter, as, the Lord, directed me.
text.KJV: And gaue them for the potters field, as the Lord appointed me.)
text.NABRE: and they paid it out for the potter’s field just as the Lord had commanded me.”
text.DRB: And they gave them into the potter's field, as our Lord did appoint to me.

text.NRSV: Now Jesus stood before the governor; and the governor asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” Jesus said, “You say so.”
text.Rotherham: Now, Jesus, stood before the governor; and the governor questioned him, saying—Thou, art the king of the Jews? And, Jesus, said—Thou, sayest.
text.KJV: And Iesus stood before the gouernour, and the gouernour asked him, saying; Art thou the King of the Iewes? And Iesus sayd vnto him, Thou sayest.
text.NABRE: Jesus Questioned by Pilate. Now Jesus stood before the governor, and he questioned him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” Jesus said, “You say so.”
text.DRB: And Jesus stood before the President, and the President asked him, saying: Art thou the King of the Jews? Jesus saith to him: Thou sayest.

text.NRSV: But when he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he gave no answer.
text.Rotherham: And, while he was being accused by the High-priests and Elders, he answered nothing.
text.KJV: And when hee was accused of the chiefe Priests and Elders, he answered nothing.
text.NABRE: And when he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he made no answer.
text.DRB: And when he was accused of the chief Priests and Ancients, he answered nothing.

text.NRSV: Then Pilate said to him, “Do you not hear how many accusations they make against you?”
text.Rotherham: Then, Pilate saith unto him—Hearest thou not how many things against thee they are bearing witness?
text.KJV: Then saith Pilate vnto him, Hearest thou not how many things they witnesse against thee?
text.NABRE: Then Pilate said to him, “Do you not hear how many things they are testifying against you?”
text.DRB: Then Pilate saith to him: Doest thou not hear how many testimonies they allege against thee?

text.NRSV: But he gave him no answer, not even to a single charge, so that the governor was greatly amazed.
text.Rotherham: And he answered him, no not so much as a single word, so that the governor was marvelling exceedingly.
text.KJV: And he answered him to neuer a word: insomuch that the Gouernour marueiled greatly.
text.NABRE: But he did not answer him one word, so that the governor was greatly amazed.
text.DRB: And he answered him not to any word: so that the President did marvel exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Now at the festival the governor was accustomed to release a prisoner for the crowd, anyone whom they wanted.
text.Rotherham: But, feast by feast, the governor had been wont to release one unto the multitude, a prisoner whom they were desiring.
text.KJV: Now at that feast the Gouernor was woont to release vnto the people a prisoner, whom they would.
text.NABRE: The Sentence of Death. Now on the occasion of the feast the governor was accustomed to release to the crowd one prisoner whom they wished.
text.DRB: And upon the solemn day the President had accustomed to release unto the People one prisoner, whom they would.

text.NRSV: At that time they had a notorious prisoner, called Jesus Barabbas.
text.Rotherham: Now they had at that time a distinguished prisoner, called, Barabbas.
text.KJV: And they had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
text.NABRE: And at that time they had a notorious prisoner called [Jesus] Barabbas.
text.DRB: And he had then a notorious prisoner, that was called Barabbas.

text.NRSV: So after they had gathered, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release for you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called the Messiah?”
text.Rotherham: They therefore having come together, Pilate, said to them—Whom will ye I should release unto you? Barabbas? or Jesus, who is called Christ?
text.KJV: Therefore when they were gathered together, Pilate said vnto them, Whom will ye that I release vnto you? Barabbas, or Iesus, which is called Christ?
text.NABRE: So when they had assembled, Pilate said to them, “Which one do you want me to release to you, [Jesus] Barabbas, or Jesus called Messiah?”
text.DRB: They therefore being gathered together, Pilate said: Whom will you that I release to you, Barabbas, or Jesus that is called Christ?

text.NRSV: For he realized that it was out of jealousy that they had handed him over.
text.Rotherham: For he knew that, for envy, they had delivered him up.
text.KJV: For hee knew that for enuie they had deliuered him.
text.NABRE: For he knew that it was out of envy that they had handed him over.
text.DRB: For he knew that for envy they had delivered him.

text.NRSV: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent word to him, “Have nothing to do with that innocent man, for today I have suffered a great deal because of a dream about him.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was sitting upon the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying: Have thou nothing to do with that righteous man, for, many things, have I suffered this day, by dream, because of him.
text.KJV: ¶ When he was set downe on the Iudgement seate, his wife sent vnto him, saying, Haue thou nothing to doe with that iust man: for I haue suffered many things this day in a dreame, because of him.
text.NABRE: While he was still seated on the bench, his wife sent him a message, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man. I suffered much in a dream today because of him.”
text.DRB: And as he was sitting in place of judgement, his wife sent unto him, saying: Have thou nothing to do with that just man. For I have suffered many things this day in my sleep for him.

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus killed.
text.Rotherham: But, the High-priests and the elders, persuaded the multitudes, that they should claim Barabbas, but, Jesus, should, destroy.
text.KJV: But the chiefe Priestes and Elders perswaded the multitude that they should aske Barabbas, & destroy Iesus.
text.NABRE: The chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas but to destroy Jesus.
