text.DRB: But the chief Priests and ancients persuaded the people, that they should ask Barabbas, and make Jesus away.

text.NRSV: The governor again said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release for you?” And they said, “Barabbas.”
text.Rotherham: Now the governor, answering, said unto them—Which of the two desire ye I should release unto you? And they said—Barabbas!
text.KJV: The Gouernour answered, and said vnto them, Whether of the twaine will ye that I release vnto you? They said, Barabbas.
text.NABRE: The governor said to them in reply, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” They answered, “Barabbas!”
text.DRB: And the President answering, said to them: Whether will you of the two to be released unto you? But they said, Barabbas.

text.NRSV: Pilate said to them, “Then what should I do with Jesus who is called the Messiah?” All of them said, “Let him be crucified!”
text.Rotherham: Pilate saith unto them—What then shall I do unto Jesus, who is called Christ? They all say—Let him be crucified!
text.KJV: Pilate said vnto them, What shall I doe then with Iesus, which is called Christ? They all sayde vnto him, Let him be crucified.
text.NABRE: Pilate said to them, “Then what shall I do with Jesus called Messiah?” They all said, “Let him be crucified!”
text.DRB: Pilate saith to them: What shall I do then with Jesus that is called Christ? They say all: Let him be crucified.

text.NRSV: Then he asked, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Let him be crucified!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Indeed! what evil hath he done? But, they, vehemently, were crying aloud, saying—Let him be crucified!
text.KJV: And the Gouernour said, Why, what euil hath he done? But they cried out þ more, saying, Let him be crucified.
text.NABRE: But he said, “Why? What evil has he done?” They only shouted the louder, “Let him be crucified!”
text.DRB: The President said to them: Why what evil hath he done? But they cried the more, saying: Let him be crucified.

text.NRSV: So when Pilate saw that he could do nothing, but rather that a riot was beginning, he took some water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this man’s blood; see to it yourselves.”
text.Rotherham: And Pilate, seeing, that, nothing, it availed, but rather, a tumult, was arising, taking water, washed his hands of it before the multitude, saying—Innocent, am I, of the blood of this man,—Ye, shall see to it for yourselves.
text.KJV: ¶ When Pilate saw that he could preuaile nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, hee tooke water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this iust person: see yee to it.
text.NABRE: When Pilate saw that he was not succeeding at all, but that a riot was breaking out instead, he took water and washed his hands in the sight of the crowd, saying, “I am innocent of this man’s blood. Look to it yourselves.”
text.DRB: And Pilate seeing that he nothing prevailed, but rather tumult was toward; taking water he washed his hands before the People, saying: I am innocent of the blood of this just man: look you to it.

text.NRSV: Then the people as a whole answered, “His blood be on us and on our children!”
text.Rotherham: And all the people, answering, said—His blood be upon us and upon our children!
text.KJV: Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on vs, and on our children.
text.NABRE: And the whole people said in reply, “His blood be upon us and upon our children.”
text.DRB: And the whole people answering, said: His blood be upon us, and upon our children.

text.NRSV: So he released Barabbas for them; and after flogging Jesus, he handed him over to be crucified.
text.Rotherham: Then released he unto them Barabbas, but, scourging, Jesus, delivered him up, that he might be crucified.
text.KJV: ¶ Then released hee Barabbas vnto them, and when he had scourged Iesus, he deliuered him to be crucified.
text.NABRE: Then he released Barabbas to them, but after he had Jesus scourged, he handed him over to be crucified.
text.DRB: Then he released to them Barabbas, and having scourged Jesus, delivered him unto them for to be crucified.

text.NRSV: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the governor’s headquarters, and they gathered the whole cohort around him.
text.Rotherham: Then the soldiers of the governor, taking Jesus aside into the judgment-hall, gathered unto him all the band;
text.KJV: Then the souldiers of the Gouernour tooke Iesus into the common hall, and gathered vnto him the whole band of souldiers.
text.NABRE: Mockery by the Soldiers. Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus inside the praetorium and gathered the whole cohort around him.
text.DRB: Then the President's soldiers taking Jesus into the Palace, gathered together unto him the whole band:

text.NRSV: They stripped him and put a scarlet robe on him,
text.Rotherham: and, unclothing him, a scarlet scarf, put they about him,—
text.KJV: And they stripped him, and put on him a scarlet robe.
text.NABRE: They stripped off his clothes and threw a scarlet military cloak about him.
text.DRB: and stripping him, put a scarlet cloak about him.

text.NRSV: and after twisting some thorns into a crown, they put it on his head. They put a reed in his right hand and knelt before him and mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
text.Rotherham: and, plaiting a crown out of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand,—and, kneeling before him, began to mock him, saying—Joy to thee, King of the Jews!
text.KJV: ¶ And when they had platted a crowne of thornes, they put it vpon his head, and a reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Haile king of the Iewes.
text.NABRE: Weaving a crown out of thorns, they placed it on his head, and a reed in his right hand. And kneeling before him, they mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
text.DRB: and platting a crown of thorns, put it upon his head, and a reed in his right hand. And bowing the knee before him, they mocked him, saying: Hail King of the Jews.

text.NRSV: They spat on him, and took the reed and struck him on the head.
text.Rotherham: And, spitting upon him, they took the reed, and began striking upon his head.
text.KJV: And they spit vpon him, and tooke the reed, and smote him on the head.
text.NABRE: They spat upon him and took the reed and kept striking him on the head.
text.DRB: And spitting upon him, they took the reed, and smote his head.

text.NRSV: After mocking him, they stripped him of the robe and put his own clothes on him. Then they led him away to crucify him.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had mocked him, they put off him the scarf, and put on him his garments, and led him away unto the crucifying.
text.KJV: And after that they had mocked him, they tooke the robe off from him, and put his owne raiment on him, and led him away to crucifie him.
text.NABRE: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the cloak, dressed him in his own clothes, and led him off to crucify him. The Way of the Cross.
text.DRB: And after they had mocked him, they took off the cloak from him, and put on him his own garments, and led him away to crucify him.

text.NRSV: As they went out, they came upon a man from Cyrene named Simon; they compelled this man to carry his cross.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were going forth, they found a man of Cyrene, by name, Simon,—the same, they impressed to bear his cross.
text.KJV: And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to beare his Crosse.
text.NABRE: As they were going out, they met a Cyrenian named Simon; this man they pressed into service to carry his cross.
text.DRB: And in going they found a man of Cyrene, named Simon: him they forced to take up his Cross.

text.NRSV: And when they came to a place called Golgotha (which means Place of a Skull),
text.Rotherham: And, coming into a place called Golgotha, that is to say, Skull-place,
text.KJV: And when they were come vnto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of a skull,
text.NABRE: The Crucifixion. And when they came to a place called Golgotha (which means Place of the Skull),
text.DRB: And they came into the place that is called Golgotha, which is, the place of Calvary.

text.NRSV: they offered him wine to drink, mixed with gall; but when he tasted it, he would not drink it.
text.Rotherham: they gave him to drink, wine, with gall, mingled, and, tasting, he would not drink.
text.KJV: ¶ They gaue him vineger to drinke, mingled with gall: and when hee had tasted thereof, hee would not drinke.
text.NABRE: they gave Jesus wine to drink mixed with gall. But when he had tasted it, he refused to drink.
text.DRB: And they gave him wine to drink mingled with gall. And when he had tasted, he would not drink.

text.NRSV: And when they had crucified him, they divided his clothes among themselves by casting lots;
text.Rotherham: And, having crucified him, they parted among themselves his garments, casting a cloth;
text.KJV: And they crucified him, and parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet, They parted my garments among them, and vpon my vesture did they cast lots.
text.NABRE: After they had crucified him, they divided his garments by casting lots;
text.DRB: And after they had crucified him; they divided his garments, casting lots; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet, saying: They divided my garments among them; and upon my vesture they did cast lots.

text.NRSV: then they sat down there and kept watch over him.
text.Rotherham: and, being seated, were watching him there.
text.KJV: And sitting downe, they watched him there:
text.NABRE: then they sat down and kept watch over him there.
text.DRB: And they sat and watched him.

text.NRSV: Over his head they put the charge against him, which read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text.Rotherham: And they put up, over his head, his accusation, written—THIS, IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
text.KJV: And set vp ouer his head, his accusation written, THIS IS IESVS THE KING OF THE IEWES.
text.NABRE: And they placed over his head the written charge against him: This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.
text.DRB: And they put over his head his cause written: This is Jesus The King of The Jews.

text.NRSV: Then two bandits were crucified with him, one on his right and one on his left.
text.Rotherham: Then, are crucified with him, two robbers,—one on the right hand, and one on the left.
text.KJV: Then were there two theeues crucified with him: one on the right hand, and another on the left.
text.NABRE: Two revolutionaries were crucified with him, one on his right and the other on his left.
text.DRB: Then were crucified with him two thieves; one on the right hand, and one on the left.

text.NRSV: Those who passed by derided him, shaking their heads
text.Rotherham: And, they who were passing by, were reviling him, shaking their heads,
text.KJV: ¶ And they that passed by, reuiled him, wagging their heads,
text.NABRE: Those passing by reviled him, shaking their heads
text.DRB: And they that passed by, blasphemed him, wagging their heads,

text.NRSV: and saying, “You who would destroy the temple and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross.”
text.Rotherham: and saying—The man that taketh down the Temple and in three days buildeth it! Save, thyself! If thou art God’s, Son, come down from the cross.
text.KJV: And saying, Thou that destroyest the Temple, & buildest it in three dayes, saue thy selfe: If thou be the Sonne of God, come downe from the Crosse.
text.NABRE: and saying, “You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save yourself, if you are the Son of God, [and] come down from the cross!”
text.DRB: and saying: Vah, thou that destroyest the Temple of God and in three days doest re-edify it; save thine own self: if thou be the Son of God, come down from the Cross.

text.NRSV: In the same way the chief priests also, along with the scribes and elders, were mocking him, saying,
text.Rotherham: Likewise, the High-priests, [also], mocking, with the Scribes and Elders, were saying: Others, he saved,—Himself, he cannot save:
text.KJV: Likewise also the chiefe Priests mocking him, with the Scribes and Elders, said,
text.NABRE: Likewise the chief priests with the scribes and elders mocked him and said,
text.DRB: In like manner also the chief Priests with the Scribes and ancients mocking, said:

text.NRSV: “He saved others; he cannot save himself. He is the King of Israel; let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him.
text.Rotherham: Israel’s King, he is! Let him come down, now, from the cross,—and we will believe on him!
text.KJV: He saued others, himselfe he cannot saue: If he be the King of Israel, let him now come downe from the Crosse, and we will beleeue him.
text.NABRE: “He saved others; he cannot save himself. So he is the king of Israel! Let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him.
text.DRB: He saved other; himself he cannot save: if he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the Cross, and we will believe him.

text.NRSV: He trusts in God; let God deliver him now, if he wants to; for he said, ‘I am God’s Son.’”
text.Rotherham: He hath put confidence upon God, Let him rescue him, now, if he desireth him; for he said, I am, God’s Son.
text.KJV: He trusted in God, let him deliuer him now if hee will haue him: for he said, I am the Sonne of God.
text.NABRE: He trusted in God; let him deliver him now if he wants him. For he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’”
text.DRB: He trusted in God; let him now deliver him if he will: for he said, that I am the Son of God.

text.NRSV: The bandits who were crucified with him also taunted him in the same way.
text.Rotherham: And, the same thing, the robbers also, who were crucified with him, were casting in his teeth.
text.KJV: The thieues also which were crucified with him, cast þ same in his teeth.
text.NABRE: The revolutionaries who were crucified with him also kept abusing him in the same way.
text.DRB: And the self same thing the thieves also that were crucified with him, reproached him withal.

text.NRSV: From noon on, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
text.Rotherham: Now, from the sixth hour, darkness, came upon all the land—until the ninth hour.
text.KJV: Now from the sixth houre there was darkenesse ouer all the land vnto the ninth houre.
text.NABRE: The Death of Jesus. From noon onward, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
text.DRB: And from the sixth hour, there was darkness made upon the whole earth, until the ninth hour.

text.NRSV: And about three o’clock Jesus cried with a loud voice, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” that is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
text.Rotherham: And, about the ninth hour, Jesus uttered a cry, with a loud voice, saying—Eloi! Eloi! lema sabachthanei? that is, My God! my God! to what end hast thou forsaken me?
text.KJV: And about the ninth houre, Iesus cried with a loud voyce, saying, Eli, Eli, Lamasabachthani, that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken mee?
text.NABRE: And about three o’clock Jesus cried out in a loud voice, “ Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani? ” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
text.DRB: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a mighty voice, saying: Eli, Eli, lamma-sabacthani? that is, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

text.NRSV: When some of the bystanders heard it, they said, “This man is calling for Elijah.”
text.Rotherham: Now certain of them who were there standing, when they heard, began to say—This man, calleth, Elijah.
text.KJV: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.
text.NABRE: Some of the bystanders who heard it said, “This one is calling for Elijah.”
text.DRB: And certain that stood there and heard, said: He calleth Elias.

text.NRSV: At once one of them ran and got a sponge, filled it with sour wine, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, one from among them, running, and taking a sponge, and filling it with vinegar and putting it on a reed, was giving him to drink;
text.KJV: And straightway one of them ran, and tooke a spunge, and filled it with vineger, and put it on a reede, and gaue him to drinke.
text.NABRE: Immediately one of them ran to get a sponge; he soaked it in wine, and putting it on a reed, gave it to him to drink.
text.DRB: And incontinent one of them running, took a sponge, & filled it with vinegar; and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.

text.NRSV: But the others said, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to save him.”
text.Rotherham: but, the rest, said—Stay! let us see whether Elijah is coming, and will save him. [[But, another, taking a spear, pierced his side, and there came forth water and blood]].
text.KJV: The rest said, Let bee, let vs see whether Elias will come to saue him.
text.NABRE: But the rest said, “Wait, let us see if Elijah comes to save him.”
text.DRB: And other said: Let be, let us see whether Elias come to deliver him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus cried again with a loud voice and breathed his last.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, again crying out with a loud voice, dismissed the spirit.
text.KJV: ¶ Iesus, when hee had cried againe with a loud voice, yeelded vp the ghost.
text.NABRE: But Jesus cried out again in a loud voice, and gave up his spirit.
text.DRB: And Jesus again crying with a mighty voice, yielded up the ghost.

text.NRSV: At that moment the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. The earth shook, and the rocks were split.
text.Rotherham: And lo! the veil of the Temple, was rent, from top to bottom, into two; and, the earth, was shaken, and, the rocks, were rent;
text.KJV: And behold, the vaile of the Temple was rent in twaine, from the top to the bottome, and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent.
text.NABRE: And behold, the veil of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth quaked, rocks were split,
text.DRB: And behold the veil of the Temple was rent in two pieces, from the top even to the bottom, and the earth did quake, and the rocks were rent,

text.NRSV: The tombs also were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised.
text.Rotherham: and, the tombs, were opened and, many bodies of the holy ones who had fallen asleep, arose,—
text.KJV: And the graues were opened, and many bodies of Saints which slept, arose,
text.NABRE: tombs were opened, and the bodies of many saints who had fallen asleep were raised.
text.DRB: And the graves were opened: and many bodies of the saints that had slept, rose.

text.NRSV: After his resurrection they came out of the tombs and entered the holy city and appeared to many.
text.Rotherham: and, coming forth out of the tombs after his arising, entered into, the holy city, and plainly appeared unto many.
text.KJV: And came out of the graues after his resurrection, and went into the holy citie, and appeared vnto many.
text.NABRE: And coming forth from their tombs after his resurrection, they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
text.DRB: And they going forth out of the graves after his resurrection, came into the holy city; and appeared to many.

text.NRSV: Now when the centurion and those with him, who were keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were terrified and said, “Truly this man was God’s Son!”
text.Rotherham: Now, the centurion, and those with him watching Jesus, seeing the earthquake and the things coming to pass, were violently affrighted, saying—Truly, God’s Son, was, he!
text.KJV: Now when the Centurion, and they that were with him, watching Iesus, saw the earthquake, & those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truely this was the Son of God.
text.NABRE: The centurion and the men with him who were keeping watch over Jesus feared greatly when they saw the earthquake and all that was happening, and they said, “Truly, this was the Son of God!”
text.DRB: And the Centurion and they that were with him watching Jesus, having seen the earth-quake and the things that were done, were sore afraid, saying: Indeed this was the Son of God.

text.NRSV: Many women were also there, looking on from a distance; they had followed Jesus from Galilee and had provided for him.
text.Rotherham: Now there were there, many women, from afar, beholding, who indeed had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him;
text.KJV: And many women were there (beholding afarre off) which followed Iesus from Galilee, ministring vnto him.
text.NABRE: There were many women there, looking on from a distance, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him.
text.DRB: And there were there many women a far off, which had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:

text.NRSV: Among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
text.Rotherham: among whom was Mary the Magdalene, also Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
text.KJV: Among which was Mary Magdalene, & Mary the mother of Iames and Ioses, and the mother of Zebedees children.
text.NABRE: Among them were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee. The Burial of Jesus.
text.DRB: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the Sons of Zebedee.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, arrived, there came a rich man from Arimathaea, whose name was Joseph, who also, himself, had been discipled unto Jesus.
text.KJV: When the Euen was come, there came a rich man of Arimathea, named Ioseph, who also himselfe was Iesus disciple:
text.NABRE: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea named Joseph, who was himself a disciple of Jesus.
text.DRB: And when it was evening, there came a certain rich man of Arimathæa, named Joseph, who also himself was disciple to Jesus.

text.NRSV: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus; then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
text.Rotherham: The same, going unto Pilate, claimed the body of Jesus. Then, Pilate commanded it to be given up.
text.KJV: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Iesus: then Pilate commanded the body to be deliuered.
text.NABRE: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus; then Pilate ordered it to be handed over.
text.DRB: He went to Pilate, and asked the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded that the body should be delivered.

text.NRSV: So Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth
text.Rotherham: And Joseph, taking the body, wrapped it up in a clean Indian-cloth,
text.KJV: And when Ioseph had taken the body, hee wrapped it in a cleane linnen cloth,
text.NABRE: Taking the body, Joseph wrapped it [in] clean linen
text.DRB: And Joseph taking the body, wrapt it in clean sindon,

text.NRSV: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn in the rock. He then rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb and went away.
text.Rotherham: and laid it in his new tomb, which he had hewn in the rock, and, rolling near a large stone unto the door of the tomb, departed.
text.KJV: And laide it in his owne newe tombe, which he had hewen out in the rocke: and he rolled a great stone to the doore of the sepulchre, and departed.
text.NABRE: and laid it in his new tomb that he had hewn in the rock. Then he rolled a huge stone across the entrance to the tomb and departed.
text.DRB: & laid it in his own new monument, which he had hewed out in a rock. And he rolled a great stone to the door of the monument, & went his way.

text.NRSV: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting opposite the tomb.
text.Rotherham: Now there were there, Mary the Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.
text.KJV: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting ouer against the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: But Mary Magdalene and the other Mary remained sitting there, facing the tomb. The Guard at the Tomb.
text.DRB: And there was there Mary Magdalene, & the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.

text.NRSV: The next day, that is, after the day of Preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate
text.Rotherham: And, on the morrow, which is after the preparation, the High-priests and the Pharisee were gathered together unto Pilate,
text.KJV: ¶ Now the next day that followed the day of the preparation, the chiefe Priests and Pharisees came together vnto Pilate,
text.NABRE: The next day, the one following the day of preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate
text.DRB: And the next day, which is after the Parasceve, the chief Priests and the Pharisees came together to Pilate,

text.NRSV: and said, “Sir, we remember what that impostor said while he was still alive, ‘After three days I will rise again.’
text.Rotherham: saying—Sir! we have been put in mind that, that deceiver, said, while yet living,—After three days, will I, arise.
text.KJV: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiuer said, while he was yet aliue, After three daies I wil rise againe.
text.NABRE: and said, “Sir, we remember that this impostor while still alive said, ‘After three days I will be raised up.’
text.DRB: saying: Sir, we have remembered, that that seducer said yet living, After three days I will rise again.

text.NRSV: Therefore give orders for the tomb to be made secure until the third day; otherwise his disciples may go and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He has been raised from the dead,’ and the last deception would be worse than the first.”
text.Rotherham: Command, therefore, that the sepulchre be made secure until the third day,—lest once the disciples should come and steal him, and say unto the people, He hath arisen from the dead! and, the last deception, shall be, worse than the first.
text.KJV: Command therfore that the sepulchre be made sure, vntill the third day, lest his disciples come by night, & steale him away, and say vnto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last errour shalbe worse then the first.
text.NABRE: Give orders, then, that the grave be secured until the third day, lest his disciples come and steal him and say to the people, ‘He has been raised from the dead.’ This last imposture would be worse than the first.”
text.DRB: Command therefore the sepulchre to be kept until the third day; lest perhaps his Disciples come, and steal him, & say to the people, he is risen from the dead: and the last error shall be worse than the first.

text.NRSV: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard of soldiers; go, make it as secure as you can.”
text.Rotherham: Pilate saith unto them—Ye have a guard: Go your way, secure it for yourselves, as ye know how.
text.KJV: Pilate said vnto them, Yee haue a watch, goe your way, make it as sure as you can.
text.NABRE: Pilate said to them, “The guard is yours; go secure it as best you can.”
text.DRB: Pilate said to them: You have a guard: go, guard it as you know.

text.NRSV: So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone.
text.Rotherham: And, they, went and secured for themselves the sepulchre, sealing the stone, with the guard.
text.KJV: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.
text.NABRE: So they went and secured the tomb by fixing a seal to the stone and setting the guard.
text.DRB: And they departing, made the sepulchre sure; sealing up the stone, with watchmen.

text.NRSV: After the sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, late in the week, when it was on the point of dawning into the first of the week, came Mary the Magdalene, and the other Mary, to view the sepulchre.
text.KJV: In the ende of the Sabbath, as it began to dawne towards the first day of the weeke, came Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, to see the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: Chapter 28 The Resurrection of Jesus. After the sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.
text.DRB: And in the evening of the Sabboth which dawneth on the first of the Sabboth, came Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.

text.NRSV: And suddenly there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord, descending from heaven, came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a great commotion occurred,—for, a messenger of the Lord, descending out of heaven, and coming near, had rolled away the stone, and was sitting upon it.
text.KJV: And behold, there was a great earthquake, for the Angel of the Lord descended from heauen, and came and rolled backe the stone from the doore, and sate vpon it.
text.NABRE: And behold, there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, approached, rolled back the stone, and sat upon it.
text.DRB: And behold there was made a great earth-quake. For an Angel of our Lord descended from Heaven: and coming, rolled back the stone, and sat upon it:

text.NRSV: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
text.Rotherham: Now his appearance, was, as lightning, and, his clothing, white as snow;
text.KJV: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snowe.
text.NABRE: His appearance was like lightning and his clothing was white as snow.
text.DRB: and his countenance was as lightning, and his garment as snow.

text.NRSV: For fear of him the guards shook and became like dead men.
text.Rotherham: and, for fear of him, they who were keeping watch were thrown into a commotion, and became as dead men.
text.KJV: And for feare of him, the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.
text.NABRE: The guards were shaken with fear of him and became like dead men.
text.DRB: And for fear of him, the watchmen were frighted, and became as dead.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to the women, “Do not be afraid; I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
text.Rotherham: But the messenger, answering, said unto the women—Be not, ye, afraid! For I know that, Jesus the crucified, ye are seeking:
text.KJV: And the Angel answered, and said vnto the women, Feare not ye: for I know that ye seeke Iesus, which was crucified.
text.NABRE: Then the angel said to the women in reply, “Do not be afraid! I know that you are seeking Jesus the crucified.
text.DRB: And the Angel answering said to the women: Fear not you. For I know that you seek Jesus that was crucified.

text.NRSV: He is not here; for he has been raised, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
text.Rotherham: He is not here, for he hath arisen, as he said. Come! see the place where he lay;
text.KJV: He is not here: for he is risen, as hee said: Come, see the place where the Lord lay.
text.NABRE: He is not here, for he has been raised just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay.
text.DRB: He is not here, for he is risen, as he said. Come, and see the place where our Lord was laid.

text.NRSV: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has been raised from the dead, and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him.’ This is my message for you.”
text.Rotherham: And, quickly going, tell his disciples—He hath arisen from the dead, and lo! he is going before you into Galilee;—There, shall ye yourselves see him: Lo! I have told you.
text.KJV: And goe quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead. And behold, hee goeth before you into Galilee, there shall ye see him: loe, I haue told you.
text.NABRE: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has been raised from the dead, and he is going before you to Galilee; there you will see him.’ Behold, I have told you.”
text.DRB: And going quickly, tell ye his Disciples that he is risen: and behold he goeth before you into Galilee: there you shall see him. Lo I have foretold you.

text.NRSV: So they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
text.Rotherham: And, departing quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to bring tidings unto his disciples.
text.KJV: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre, with feare and great ioy, and did run to bring his disciples word.
text.NABRE: Then they went away quickly from the tomb, fearful yet overjoyed, and ran to announce this to his disciples.
text.DRB: And they went forth quickly out of the monument with fear and great joy, running to tell his Disciples.

text.NRSV: Suddenly Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” And they came to him, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! Jesus, met them, saying—Joy to you! And, they, going forward, held his feet, and worshipped him.
text.KJV: ¶ And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Iesus met them, saying, All haile. And they came, and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
text.NABRE: And behold, Jesus met them on their way and greeted them. They approached, embraced his feet, and did him homage.
text.DRB: And behold Jesus met them, saying: All hail. But they came near and took hold of his feet, and adored him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.”
text.Rotherham: Then, Jesus saith unto them—Be not afraid! Go your way, bear tidings unto my brethren, that they may depart into Galilee; and, there, shall they themselves see me.
text.KJV: Then said Iesus vnto them, Be not afraid: Goe tell my brethren that they goe into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to them, “Do not be afraid. Go tell my brothers to go to Galilee, and there they will see me.” The Report of the Guard.
text.DRB: Then Jesus said to them: Fear not. Go, tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, there they shall see me.

text.NRSV: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text.Rotherham: Now as they were, going, lo! certain of the guard, went into the city and reported unto the High-priests all the things that had come to pass;
text.KJV: ¶ Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the citie, and shewed vnto the chiefe Priests all the things that were done.
text.NABRE: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests all that had happened.
text.DRB: Who when they were departed, behold certain of the watchmen came into the city, and told the chief Priests all things that had been done.

text.NRSV: After the priests had assembled with the elders, they devised a plan to give a large sum of money to the soldiers,
text.Rotherham: and, being gathered together with the Elders and taking counsel, sufficient pieces of silver, gave they unto the soldiers,—
text.KJV: And when they were assembled with the Elders, and had taken counsell, they gaue large money vnto the souldiers,
text.NABRE: They assembled with the elders and took counsel; then they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
text.DRB: And being assembled together with the Ancients, taking counsel, they gave a great sum of money to the soldiers,

text.NRSV: telling them, “You must say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.’
text.Rotherham: saying—Say ye, His disciples, coming, by night, stole him, while we were sleeping;
text.KJV: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
text.NABRE: telling them, “You are to say, ‘His disciples came by night and stole him while we were asleep.’
text.DRB: saying: Say you, that his Disciples came by night, and stole him away when we were asleep.

text.NRSV: If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.”
text.Rotherham: And, if this be reported unto the governor, we, will persuade him, and will make, you, free from care.
text.KJV: And if this come to the gouernours eares, wee will perswade him, and secure you.
text.NABRE: And if this gets to the ears of the governor, we will satisfy [him] and keep you out of trouble.”
text.DRB: And if the President shall hear of this, we will persuade him, and make you secure.

text.NRSV: So they took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story is still told among the Jews to this day.
text.Rotherham: And, they, taking the pieces of silver, did as they were instructed. And this account was spread abroad among the Jews…until this [very] day.
text.KJV: So they tooke the money, and did as they were taught. And this saying is commonly reported among the Iewes vntill this day.
text.NABRE: The soldiers took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story has circulated among the Jews to the present [day]. The Commissioning of the Disciples.
text.DRB: But they taking the money, did as they were taught. And this word was bruited abroad among the Jews, even unto this day.

text.NRSV: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.
text.Rotherham: And, the eleven disciples, went into Galilee, unto the mountain which, Jesus, had appointed them;
text.KJV: ¶ Then the eleuen disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountaine where Iesus had appointed them.
text.NABRE: The eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had ordered them.
text.DRB: And the eleven Disciples went into Galilee, unto the mount where Jesus had appointed them.

text.NRSV: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing him, they worshipped, but, some, doubted.
text.KJV: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
text.NABRE: When they saw him, they worshiped, but they doubted.
text.DRB: And seeing him they adored, but some doubted.

text.NRSV: And Jesus came and said to them, “All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, coming near, spake unto them, saying—All authority, in heaven and on earth, hath been given unto me;
text.KJV: And Iesus came, and spake vnto them, saying, All power is giuen vnto me in heauen and in earth.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus approached and said to them, “All power in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
text.DRB: And Jesus coming near spake unto them, saying: All power is given to me in Heaven and in earth.

text.NRSV: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
text.Rotherham: Go ye, therefore, and disciple all the nations, Immersing them into the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit,—
text.KJV: ¶ Goe ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the Name of the Father, and of the Sonne, and of the holy Ghost:
text.NABRE: Go, therefore, and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the holy Spirit,
text.DRB: going therefore teach ye all Nations: Baptizing Them in The Name of The Father, And of The Son, And of The Holy Ghost,

text.NRSV: and teaching them to obey everything that I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
text.Rotherham: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I myself have commanded you, And lo! I, am, with you, all the days, until the conclusion of the age.
text.KJV: Teaching them to obserue all things, whatsoeuer I haue commanded you: and loe, I am with you alway, euen vnto the end of the world. Amen.
text.NABRE: teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, until the end of the age.”
text.DRB: teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and behold I am with you all days, even to the consummation of the world.
